src.what_is:
The Abbaye d'Aulps (or Abbaye Sainte-Marie d'Aulps) is a former Cistercian abbey situated at 807 m altitude in the Upper Chablais valley. Founded around 1094 by monks from the abbey of Molesme in Burgundy, it affiliated with the Cistercian order in 1136 and became one of the most powerful monasteries in medieval Savoy. Partially destroyed in 1823 after 700 years of monastic life, its majestic remains of the abbatial church have been listed as a Historic Monument since 1902.

The estate now houses the Aulps Valley Discovery Area, a unique interpretation centre in France located in the restored former monastic farm (2007). The scenography, entirely renovated in November 2020, tells the story of daily life among Cistercian monks in the Middle Ages, their roles within the community, relations between the abbey and Savoy, and the surprising history of its destruction.

The visit circuit includes the remains of the abbatial church, former cellars, gatehouse, medicinal herb garden, medieval vegetable garden and vegetated cloister. An augmented reality application allows visitors to discover the abbey reconstructed in 3D as it was between the 16th and 18th centuries, using a virtual reality headset or digital tablet.

segments.src: (or Abbaye Sainte-Marie d'Aulps) is a former Cistercian abbey situated at 807 m altitude in the Upper Chablais valley. Founded around 1094 by monks from the abbey of Molesme in Burgundy, it affiliated with the Cistercian order in 1136 and became one of the most powerful monasteries in medieval Savoy. Partially destroyed in 1823 after 700 years of monastic life, its majestic remains of the abbatial church have been listed as a Historic Monument since 1902.
segments.mt: (lub Abbaye Sainte- Marie d 'Aulps) - były opactwo cysterskie położone na wysokości 807 m w dolinie Górnego Chablais. Założony około 1094 roku przez mnichów z opactwa Molesme w Burgundii, w 1136 roku związany z zakonem cystersów i stał się jednym z najpotężniejszych klasztorów w średniowiecznym Savoy. Częściowo zniszczony w 1823 roku po 700 latach życia zakonnego, jego majestatyczne szczątki kościoła abbatyckiego zostały wymienione jako zabytek historyczny od 1902 roku.

segments.src: , a unique interpretation centre in France located in the restored former monastic farm (2007). The scenography, entirely renovated in November 2020, tells the story of daily life among Cistercian monks in the Middle Ages, their roles within the community, relations between the abbey and Savoy, and the surprising history of its destruction.
segments.mt: , unikalne centrum tłumaczeń ustnych we Francji znajduje się w odrestaurowanym dawnym gospodarstwie klasztornym (2007). Scenografia, całkowicie odnowiona w listopadzie 2020 roku, opowiada historię codziennego życia mnichów cysterskich w średniowieczu, ich ról w społeczności, relacji pomiędzy opactwem a Savoyem oraz zaskakującej historii jego zniszczenia.

segments.src: The visit circuit includes the remains of the abbatial church, former cellars, gatehouse, medicinal herb garden, medieval vegetable garden and vegetated cloister. An augmented reality application allows visitors to discover the abbey reconstructed in 3D as it was between the 16th and 18th centuries, using a virtual reality headset or digital tablet.
segments.mt: W obwodzie odwiedzin znajdują się pozostałości kościoła abbacjalnego, byłych piwnic, bram, ogrodu ziół leczniczych, średniowiecznego ogrodu warzywnego i wegetowanego klasztoru. Aplikacja rozszerzonej rzeczywistości pozwala zwiedzającym odkryć opactwo zrekonstruowane w 3D, jak to było między XVI a XVIII wiekiem, przy użyciu zestawu słuchawkowego wirtualnej rzeczywistości lub tabletu cyfrowego.

src.title: Visit the interpretation centre
src.description: Browse the permanent exhibition in the former monastic farm: interactive scenography on the daily life of Cistercian monks, their roles and the site's history.

src.title: Discover the abbey in 3D
src.description: Experience an augmented reality reconstruction of the abbey with a virtual reality headset or tablet, to see the abbey as it was between the 16th and 18th centuries.

src.title: Explore the monumental remains
src.description: Walk through the classified ruins of the abbatial church, admire the monumental façade, discover the cellars, gatehouse and former conventional spaces.

src.title: Stroll through the medieval gardens
src.description: Walk through the medicinal herb garden, medieval vegetable garden, orchard and heritage rose garden, and the vegetated cloister.

src.title: Participate in cultural events
src.description: Attend the Storytelling Festival, Flamb'Aulps (torchlit evening visits), concerts, the Monastic Market and contemporary art exhibitions.

src.title: Visit the contemporary art space
src.description: Discover temporary contemporary art exhibitions hosted in the abbey's spaces, resonating with the site's history and architecture.

src.car: From Thonon-les-Bains (25 km, ~30 min), take the D902 towards Morzine/Aulps Valley to Saint-Jean-d'Aulps. From Cluses (35 km, ~40 min), take the D902 towards Taninges then Saint-Jean-d'Aulps. Free parking at the abbey.
src.public_transport: Coach service from Thonon-les-Bains or Cluses (SAT Co., tel. 04 50 71 85 55). Balad'Aulps Bus valley shuttle in summer and winter season, Abbey stop.

fields.src: The abbey is open year-round with varying hours by season. Summer (mid-June to mid-September) offers the best hours (10am–7pm daily) and allows full enjoyment of the medieval gardens in bloom. Spring and autumn are ideal for discovering the site in a quieter atmosphere, though closed Saturday. Winter (January to March) limits opening to weekday afternoons. Flamb'Aulps (torchlit evening visits) and cultural events (Storytelling Festival, concerts) punctuate the summer calendar.

segments.src: The abbey is open year-round with varying hours by season. Summer (mid-June to mid-September) offers the best hours (10am–7pm daily) and allows full enjoyment of the medieval gardens in bloom. Spring and autumn are ideal for discovering the site in a quieter atmosphere, though closed Saturday. Winter (January to March) limits opening to weekday afternoons. Flamb'Aulps (torchlit evening visits) and cultural events (Storytelling Festival, concerts) punctuate the summer calendar.
segments.mt: Opactwo jest otwarte rok-runda z różnych godzin w sezonie. Lato (od połowy czerwca do połowy września) oferuje najlepsze godziny (codziennie od 10 do 19 rano) i pozwala na pełne korzystanie z średniowiecznych ogrodów w rozkwicie. Wiosna i jesień są idealne do odkrywania miejsca w spokojniejszej atmosferze, choć zamknięte w sobotę. Zima (styczeń-marzec) ogranicza otwarcie do popołudnia tygodnia. FLamb 'Aulps (wycieczki wieczorne z pochodniami) i wydarzenia kulturalne (Festiwal Storytelling, koncerty) interpunkują letni kalendarz.

src.q: How do I visit Abbaye d'Aulps?
src.a: Abbaye d'Aulps can be visited freely on site without reservation for individual visitors. Tickets are purchased at the reception. Group booking is required at 04 50 04 52 63.

src.q: What is the admission price for Abbaye d'Aulps?
src.a: The adult rate for Abbaye d'Aulps is €7. Child 6–16 years: €3.50. Adult group (min. 20 persons): €5.60. Free for under 6 and with Multi Pass Portes du Soleil or Pass Léman France. Verified May 14, 2026.

src.q: What are the hours for Abbaye d'Aulps?
src.a: Abbaye d'Aulps is open in summer (June 15 – Sept 15) daily from 10am to 7pm, and in winter (Jan–Mar) Monday to Friday from 2pm to 6pm. Spring and autumn: afternoon opening except Saturday. Verified on the official website on May 14, 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

src.q: Is the 3D visit at Abbaye d'Aulps included in the ticket?
src.a: Yes, the Abbaye d'Aulps 3D application (augmented reality) is included in the admission price for Abbaye d'Aulps. Visit with virtual reality headset or digital tablet to discover the abbey reconstructed in the 16th–18th centuries.

src.q: Can I bring a dog to Abbaye d'Aulps?
src.a: No, dogs and other pets are not permitted on the Abbaye d'Aulps estate, including in the gardens and ruins.

src.q: Is Abbaye d'Aulps wheelchair accessible?
src.a: Yes, Abbaye d'Aulps is accessible to people using wheelchairs with assistance. The site has slopes greater than 5% but they are reasonable. Accommodations for hearing, mental and visual disabilities are available.

src.q: How much time should I plan for visiting Abbaye d'Aulps?
src.a: Plan 1 h 30 to 2 h 30 for a complete visit to Abbaye d'Aulps: interpretation centre, 3D visit, remains of the abbatial church, medieval gardens and conventional spaces.

src.q: Is there a restaurant or tavern at Abbaye d'Aulps?
src.a: Yes, the Tavern La Croix Blanche is located on the Abbaye d'Aulps estate. In summer, the Priory Menu is offered for lunch. A gift shop completes the offering.

src.what_is:
The Abbaye de Sixt is a former abbey of regular canons of the order of Saint Augustine, founded in 1144 in the upper Giffre valley. A dependency of the prestigious Abbey of Saint-Maurice d'Agaune in the Valais, it contributed for centuries to permanent human presence in this isolated high mountain area, developing agro-pastoralism and structuring the valley's life.

The abbey complex comprises a 13th-century abbey church, now the parish church dedicated to Saint Mary Magdalene, an abbots' lodge to the south built between the 12th and 18th centuries, a presbytery and a granary to the east. Closed in 1792 following the French invasion of Savoy, the abbey was sold as national property and later converted into a hotel at the end of the 19th century. The abbots' lodge was acquired by the Haute-Savoie Department in 2000.

Listed as a Historic Monument in 1997, the abbey was the subject of major archaeological research in the 2010s and European restoration (ALCOTRA PHENIX project). Each summer, the abbots' lodge opens its doors for a free thematic exhibition. The church is freely accessible year-round.

segments.src: is a former abbey of regular canons of the order of Saint Augustine, founded in 1144 in the upper Giffre valley. A dependency of the prestigious Abbey of Saint-Maurice d'Agaune in the Valais, it contributed for centuries to permanent human presence in this isolated high mountain area, developing agro-pastoralism and structuring the valley's life.
segments.mt: jest byłym opactwem stałych kanonów zakonu św. Augustyna, założonym w 1144 roku w górnej dolinie Giffre. Zależność prestiżowego Opactwa Saint- Maurice d 'Agaune w Valais, przyczyniła się przez wieki do stałej obecności człowieka w tym odizolowanym obszarze wysokich gór, rozwijając agropastoralizm i organizując życie doliny.

segments.src: The abbey complex comprises a 13th-century abbey church, now the parish church dedicated to Saint Mary Magdalene, an abbots' lodge to the south built between the 12th and 18th centuries, a presbytery and a granary to the east. Closed in 1792 following the French invasion of Savoy, the abbey was sold as national property and later converted into a hotel at the end of the 19th century. The abbots' lodge was acquired by the Haute-Savoie Department in 2000.
segments.mt: Kompleks opactwa składa się z 13.-wiecznego kościoła opactwa, obecnie kościoła parafialnego poświęconego św. Marii Magdalenie, domku opatów na południu zbudowanego między XII a XVIII wiekiem, prezbiterium i spichlerza na wschodzie. Zamknięty w 1792 roku po francuskiej inwazji na Savoy, opactwo zostało sprzedane jako własność narodowa, a później przekształcone w hotel pod koniec XIX wieku. Dom opatów został zakupiony przez Departament Haute-Savoie w 2000 roku.

segments.src: Listed as a Historic Monument in 1997, the abbey was the subject of major archaeological research in the 2010s and European restoration (ALCOTRA PHENIX project). Each summer, the abbots' lodge opens its doors for a free thematic exhibition. The church is freely accessible year-round.
segments.mt: W 1997 r. opactwo było wymienione jako zabytek historyczny, a w 2010 r. było przedmiotem głównych badań archeologicznych i odbudowy europejskiej (projekt ALCOTRA PHENIX). Każdego lata dom opatów otwiera drzwi do darmowej wystawy tematycznej. Kościół jest dostępny przez cały rok.

src.title: Visit the summer exhibition in the abbots' lodge
src.description: Each summer (mid-June to mid-September), the Haute-Savoie Department presents a themed cultural exhibition in the restored abbots' lodge. Free entry.

src.title: Discover the 13th-century abbey church
src.description: Church dedicated to Saint Mary Magdalene, featuring a medieval choir with ribbed vaults on sculptured corbels, a treasure protected as a Historic Monument, and a 19th-century bulbous steeple.

src.title: Admire the sculpted elements of the medieval cloister
src.description: More than 140 reused stones from the Romanesque cloister (capitals, column shafts, abacuses) are visible in the north wall of the lodge, unique in Haute-Savoie.

src.title: Explore the site of Blessed Ponce de Faucigny
src.description: First abbot of Sixt (12th century), Ponce de Faucigny is venerated locally. His cenotaph and bust are preserved in the abbey church.

src.title: Stroll through the historic centre of Sixt
src.description: The abbey stands at the heart of the village, ranked among the Most Beautiful Villages of France. Fountains, shrines and mountain chalets testify to alpine heritage.

src.title: Hike to the Fer-à-Cheval Cirque on foot
src.description: From the village, several marked trails lead to the famous glacial cirque and the Sixt-Passy National Nature Reserve.

segments.mt: Odwiedź letnią wystawę w domku opatów

segments.mt: Pierwszy opat Sixt (XII wiek), Ponce de Faucigny jest czczony lokalnie. Jego cenotaf i biust są zachowane w kościele opactwa.

segments.src: Stroll through the historic centre of Sixt
The abbey stands at the heart of the village, ranked among the Most Beautiful Villages of France. Fountains, shrines and mountain chalets testify to alpine heritage.
segments.mt: Opactwo znajduje się w samym sercu wsi, w rankingu najpiękniejszych wsi Francji. Fontanny, sanktuarium i schroniska górskie świadczą o alpejskim dziedzictwie.

segments.src: From the village, several marked trails lead to the famous glacial cirque and the Sixt-Passy National Nature Reserve.
segments.mt: Z wioski kilka oznakowanych szlaków prowadzi do słynnego cyrku lodowcowego i Sixt- Passy National Nature Reserve.

src.what_is:
The Forêt d'Aventures de Manigod is a treetop adventure park perched at the Col de la Croix Fry, at 1,477 m altitude in the Aravis range. Ziplines run above the alpine pastures, with trails suited to the whole family (children from age 4, intermediate and sporty levels) and an exceptional panorama of the Aravis range with Lake Annecy in the background.

The park is the official local recommendation under the 'Indian Forest Annecy-Les Aravis Manigod' label. The Indian Forest network has no other site in the Haute-Savoie department, so this Manigod park is the reference for the area, in a mid-mountain setting overlooking the Aravis.

2026 prices: adult £20 / €20, child €15 (ages 4–12). Season: May to October. Weekends and bank holidays 10am–6pm in May–June and September–October, daily 9am–7pm in July and August. Not suitable for reduced-mobility visitors (high-ropes trails).

segments.mt: w zasięgu Aravis. Zipliny biegną ponad pastwiskami alpejskimi, z trasami dostosowanymi do całej rodziny (dzieci w wieku 4 lat, poziom pośredni i sportowy) oraz wyjątkową panoramą aravis

fields.src: Family treetop adventure park in Rocailles forest at Reignier-Ésery. Suspended Acro'Net, courses from age 1 m. 10 km from Annemasse, 20 km from Geneva. From €16.

src.title: Grand Adult and Youth Course
src.description: 2h30 course from 1m35 with 4 progressively difficult loops, Tarzan swing and return zip-lines of 200 m. Adult required for under 11s or under 1m40.

src.title: Junior Course
src.description: 2h course from 1m20 with 5 loops including a zip-line course. Adult required if under 1m30. Ideal for children and young teenagers.

src.title: Children's Course
src.description: 1h course from 1 m with 4 loops and zip-lines. Adult required for under 6s or under 1m10. Playful introduction to rope courses.

src.title: Acro'Net
src.description: 12 m high structure with two levels of suspended netting: giant ball zones, ball pit, trampolines and slide. No harness. All ages, 1 to 2h.

src.title: Treasure Hunt
src.description: From age 3. Equipped with a map, search for clues to open the treasure chest. Fun forest activity adapted for the youngest.

src.title: Orienteering Course
src.description: From age 7, supervisor required for under 15s. Search for 20 markers in the Rocailles forest with map and compass. €6.

src.title: Birthday parties and groups
src.description: Children's birthday packages with cake and activities. Group services: seminars, holiday camps, school groups, bachelor and bachelorette parties. Group rate from 8 people.

src.title: Activity passes and subscriptions
src.description: Multi-activity pass to combine several courses in one day. Season subscriptions available for regular users. Discount for Reignier-Ésery residents.

fields.src: The park is open year-round with hours varying by season. High season (April to August) offers the widest time slots with evening openings until 7:30 pm on weekends. In winter, the park operates Wednesdays and weekends with daily opening during school holidays. Spring and autumn are ideal periods to enjoy the forest with less crowding. Allow 1 to 2h30 depending on the course chosen. Avoid heavy rain or strong wind days. Online booking required, possible until the last minute.

segments.src: The park is open year-round with hours varying by season. High season (April to August) offers the widest time slots with evening openings until 7:30 pm on weekends. In winter, the park operates Wednesdays and weekends with daily opening during school holidays. Spring and autumn are ideal periods to enjoy the forest with less crowding. segments.mt: Park jest otwarty przez cały rok z godzinami zmiennymi w zależności od sezonu. Najdłuższy sezon (od kwietnia do sierpnia) oferuje najszersze sloty czasowe z wieczornymi otwarciami do 19: 30 w weekendy. W zimie park działa w środy i weekendy z codziennym otwarciem podczas wakacji szkolnych. Wiosna i jesień to idealne okresy, aby cieszyć się lasem z mniejszym tłokiem. Pozwolić 1 do 2h30 w zależności od wybranego kursu. Unikać silnego deszczu lub silnych dni wiatru. Rezerwacja online wymagane, możliwe do ostatniej minuty.

src.q: How do I book Acro'Aventures Reignier?
src.a: Booking at Acro'Aventures Reignier is mandatory and done exclusively online at acro-aventures.com/reignier/. You can book until the last minute. By phone at 06 43 16 27 59, leave an SMS if voicemail.

src.q: What is the price for Acro'Aventures Reignier?
src.a: The price for Acro'Aventures Reignier varies by course: Grand Course adult (16+) €26, youth (under 16) €21, Junior Course €20, Children's Course €16, Acro'Net €16, Orienteering course €6. Group rate from 8 people. Verified May 14, 2026.

src.q: What are the hours for Acro'Aventures Reignier?
src.a: Acro'Aventures Reignier is open year-round with varying hours. In winter (Dec–Mar): Wed/Sat/Sun 1 pm–5 pm, school holidays daily 11 am–5 pm. In season (Apr–Aug): Mon–Fri 1 pm–5:30 pm, weekends 10 am–7:30 pm. Verified on official website May 14, 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

src.q: From what age can you do Acro'Aventures Reignier?
src.a: Acro'Aventures Reignier is accessible from 1 m tall (approximately age 3) for the Children's Course and Acro'Net. The Junior Course is accessible from 1m20 (approximately age 6) and the Grand Course from 1m35 (approximately ages 9–10). Adult required depending on age and height.

src.q: Is parking free at Acro'Aventures Reignier?
src.a: Yes, parking at Acro'Aventures Reignier is free and located directly on site at the park entrance in the Rocailles forest.

src.q: What should I wear for Acro'Aventures Reignier?
src.a: For Acro'Aventures Reignier, wear sportswear and closed shoes that must cover heel and toes. Crocs, flip-flops and sandals are prohibited. Secure long hair and glasses, remove scarves and jewelry. Equipment (harness, pulley) is provided.

src.q: Can we come as a group to Acro'Aventures Reignier?
src.a: Yes, Acro'Aventures Reignier welcomes groups (schools, companies, birthdays, bachelor/bachelorette parties) with reduced rates from 8 people. Children's birthday packages with cake and activities available by booking.

src.q: Is there a maximum age for treetop courses at Reignier?
src.a: No, there is no maximum age. Courses are adapted according to height and physical condition. The Grand Course requires good fitness, while the Acro'Net suits all ages without harness.

src.v: Forest Road, Planfait, 74290 Talloires-Montmin
Approximately 900 m (site in forest above Talloires)

Open 28 March to 15 November 2026. Daily departures July-August 10am to 5:30pm. Weekends and school holidays only outside July-August (mid-April to end October). See detailed calendar on official website. Verified 5 June 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

Advanced Adult Course €27, Junior (under 16) €22. Junior Course €22. Children's Course €17. Acro'Ninja €17. Hop Up Challenge €17. Acro'Filet €17 (free under 3). Sensory Forest €5 (free under 3). Treasure Hunt €10 (accompanying adult €7). Orienteering €6. Multi-activity pass available. Group rates on request. Verified 5 June 2026.
Online booking at acro-aventures.com/talloires. Recommended during peak season (July-August). Payment by card, cheque, holiday cheques or cash accepted.
Planfait municipal car park in immediate vicinity (paid 2 May to 30 September under municipal order).
Not permitted on tree-top courses (safety reasons).

The Sensory Forest is accessible to pushchairs and wheelchairs (except balance workshop and barefoot trail). Children's and Junior courses may suit some prosthesis wearers (contact park first). Free companion for any person with disabilities unable to complete the activity independently.

src.what_is:
The Acroparc de Bellavallis is a treetop adventure park set at 950 m altitude at the foot of the Cascade de la Diomaz, in the commune of Bellevaux in the Brevon valley (Haut-Chablais). Run by GR Aventure, it offers 8 trails and 70 workshops organised by level and age, with many ziplines threading through the forest.

The park is divided into three zones: the Île aux enfants from age 3 (low-height trails, no minimum size, €10), the children's trails for ages 6–16 (min. 1.10 m, €17) and the adult trails for ages 16 and up (€23). Each ticket gives access to the trails suited to the participant's age and size, and every trail can be repeated several times.

GR Aventure also rents out via ferrata equipment to tackle the nearby Via Ferrata de la Diomaz (from age 12, 1.40 m, 40–120 kg): full kit without pulley €20, with pulley €25. The park is open from April to October, closed from 1 November to 30 April.

src.q: Comment réserver à l'Acroparc de Bellavallis ?
src.a: Réservation à l'Acroparc de Bellavallis en ligne sur gr-aventure.com ou par téléphone au +33 (0)4 50 94 64 76. Réservation obligatoire pour les groupes ≥8 personnes ; conseillée en haute saison estivale pour les visites individuelles.

src.q: Quel est le tarif de l'Acroparc de Bellavallis ?
src.a: Acroparc de Bellavallis : Île aux enfants 3-6 ans 10 €. Parcours enfants 6-16 ans 17 €. Parcours adulte 16 ans et + 23 €. Tarifs groupe (dès 8 pers.) 10 €/15 €/19 €. Location via ferrata 20-25 €. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.

src.q: Quelles sont les horaires de l'Acroparc de Bellavallis ?
src.a: Acroparc de Bellavallis ouvre de mai à octobre. Juillet-août tous les jours 9h–18h30. Mai-juin et septembre-octobre week-ends et jours fériés 14h–18h. Fermé du 1er novembre au 30 avril. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

src.q: À partir de quel âge à l'Acroparc de Bellavallis ?
src.a: L'Acroparc de Bellavallis accueille les enfants dès 3 ans sur l'Île aux enfants (sans minimum de taille), dès 6 ans (1,10 m min.) sur les parcours enfants, et 16 ans et + sur les parcours adulte. Femmes enceintes : activité interdite par sécurité.

src.q: L'Acroparc de Bellavallis loue-t-il le matériel via ferrata ?
src.a: Oui. L'Acroparc de Bellavallis loue le matériel pour la via ferrata de la Diomaz adjacente : kit complet sans poulie 20 €, avec poulie 25 €, casque ou poulie seul 10 €. Dès 12 ans, 1,40 m, 40-120 kg.

src.q: L'Acroparc de Bellavallis est-il ouvert par temps de pluie ?
src.a: Non. L'Acroparc de Bellavallis peut fermer sans préavis par mauvais temps (pluie soutenue, orage, vent fort). Appeler le +33 (0)4 50 94 64 76 le jour même pour vérifier l'ouverture en cas de météo incertaine.

src.q: Y a-t-il une cascade près de l'Acroparc de Bellavallis ?
src.a: Oui. L'Acroparc de Bellavallis est implanté au pied de la cascade de la Diomaz et de sa via ferrata, dans la vallée du Brevon. Site naturel remarquable du Haut-Chablais, accessible à pied depuis le parking du parc.

src.q: Comment venir à l'Acroparc de Bellavallis depuis Thonon ?
src.a: L'Acroparc de Bellavallis est à 30 min de Thonon-les-Bains par D26 puis D32 vers Bellevaux. En bus : ligne Cars Région Y03 arrêt 'Bellevaux Chef-lieu' (tarif 1,60 €) puis ≈1 km à pied. Pas de gare SNCF à proximité ; gare la plus proche Thonon-les-Bains.

fields.src: Former ramparts of the Château de Lullin · Lullin (74) | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Former ramparts of the Château de Lullin · Lullin (74) | Loisirs 74

src.what_is:
The former ramparts of the Château de Lullin are the last surviving traces of a major medieval fortress built in 1304 by Hugues du Faucigny on a natural mound that overlooks the Follaz valley by 65 metres, in the village of Lullin (74470), halfway between the Chablais and the Faucigny. Allied with the Count of Geneva against the House of Savoy, the Dauphin of Faucigny fortified the site: 300 metres of ramparts were raised or renovated by 892 workers, turning the village into a fully walled town.

The threat was taken seriously by Amadeus V of Savoy, who sent his son Edward to besiege the fortress. After eleven days of siege in 1305, Lullin fell to the Savoyards, who in turn built permanent stone fortifications. The castle continued to grow until the 16th century, reaching imposing dimensions — 92 metres long by 45 wide — before being destroyed by a fire in 1564. Quickly falling into ruin, it was gradually dismantled and the stone reused for the parish church and several village houses.

Today only a single fragment of the enclosure wall survives, leaning against the back of the presbytery in the village centre. Pierced with arrow slits and built of rough-cut stone, this short stretch of wall is the only physical reminder of the medieval fortress of Lullin and of the dramatic episode in Faucigny–Savoy relations that preceded the final union of the region under Savoy.

src.q: What remains today of the old ramparts of the Château de Lullin?
src.a: What remains in Lullin is a curtain wall set against the parish rectory, on which arrow slits can still be made out. It is the last visible vestige of the 92 m-long fortress built in 1304 by Hugh of Faucigny, dismantled after the 1564 fire to be used as a stone quarry.

src.q: How can I visit the old ramparts of the Château de Lullin?
src.a: Access to the old ramparts of the Château de Lullin is free and open year-round. The remains lie in the heart of the village, against the rectory, a few steps from the church and the town hall. No reservation, no fixed hours: simply stroll through the village.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the old ramparts of the Château de Lullin?
src.a: The remains of the Château de Lullin are visible 24/7, free access year-round. Daytime is preferable to read the arrow slits and the curtain wall properly. Checked on 10 June 2026.

src.q: How much does it cost to visit the old ramparts of the Château de Lullin?
src.a: Visiting the old ramparts of the Château de Lullin is entirely free: these are remains in a public space, with no ticket desk or regular guided tour. Checked on 10 June 2026.

src.q: What is the history of the Château de Lullin?
src.a: The Château de Lullin was built in 1304 by Hugh of Faucigny on the Motte de Lullin to counter Savoy. Edward of Savoy besieged it for eleven days in 1305 and took it. The castle flourished until the 16th century (92 m long) before being burned down in 1564 and turned into a stone quarry.

src.q: Where can I park to visit the old ramparts of the Château de Lullin?
src.a: Free parking in the village of Lullin, near the town hall and the church. The ramparts of the Château de Lullin are a few minutes' walk away. No parking constraints in the low season.

src.q: How long should I allow to visit the old ramparts of the Château de Lullin?
src.a: Allow 20 to 45 minutes to walk through the remains of the ramparts of the Château de Lullin and tour the old village. With an extended walk through the village and its surroundings, allow up to 1 hr 30.

src.q: What is the best season to visit the old ramparts of the Château de Lullin?
src.a: The remains of the Château de Lullin can be visited year-round, but spring and summer remain the most pleasant seasons — the village of Lullin (820 m altitude) can be covered in snow in winter. Autumn offers beautiful light on the medieval stones.

src.what_is:
The Château Bleu Aquatic Centre is the main public aquatic facility of Annemasse Agglomération, located just steps from the SNCF train station and Léman Express. This modern complex features a 50-metre sports pool (convertible into two 25 m pools with a movable wall), a 25-metre leisure pool with water jets and bubbling baths, and a 100 m² paddling pool for toddlers.

The wellness area includes a hammam, sauna, jacuzzi, and an 80 m² fitness room equipped with 16 cardio and strength-training machines. Large glass windows open in summer to over 7,600 m² of outdoor spaces: 1,000 m² of mineral decking and 6,000 m² of green lawns, transforming the centre into a true water park.

Managed by Annemasse Agglomération, Château Bleu stands out from other aquatic centres in Haute-Savoie with its affordable public pricing (€5.10 adults / €3.60 children), 4 to 5 times cheaper than private water parks in the region like Vitam Parc (€22-27). Located 5 minutes on foot from the train station, it's easily accessible from Geneva and Greater Geneva via Léman Express or TAC bus line 8 (Château Bleu stop).

2026 Rates: Adults €5.10, Children €3.60. Season: Year-round. Hours: Mon 12pm-8:30pm, Tue 12pm-8pm, Wed 9am-8pm, Thu 12pm-8pm, Fri 12pm-8pm, Sat 10am-6pm, Sun 8am-1:30pm. Wheelchair accessible: Yes.

segments.src: Are pets allowed at Château Bleu Aquatic Centre?
segments.mt: Czy zwierzęta domowe są dozwolone w Château Bleu Aquatic Centre?

segments.src: No, pets are not allowed at Château Bleu Aquatic Centre (indoor). Aquatic centre regulations.
segments.mt: Nie, zwierzęta nie są dozwolone w Château Bleu Aquatic Centre (kryty). Przepisy dotyczące centrum wodnego.

segments.src: How long to visit Château Bleu Aquatic Centre?
segments.mt: Jak długo można odwiedzić Château Bleu Aquatic Centre?

segments.src: Average duration at Château Bleu Aquatic Centre: 1-2h for the aquatic pools. Allow 2-3h if you wish to enjoy the wellness area (hammam, sauna, jacuzzi) and fitness room.
segments.mt: Średni czas trwania w Château Bleu Aquatic Centre: 1- 2h dla basenów wodnych. Pozwolić 2- 3h, jeśli chcesz cieszyć się wellness (hammam, sauna, jacuzzi) i sala fitness.

segments.src: How to get to Château Bleu Aquatic Centre?
segments.mt: Jak dostać się do Château Bleu Aquatic Centre?

segments.src: Château Bleu Aquatic Centre is located at 2, route de Bonneville, 74100 Annemasse. By car: motorway exit no.14 at 1.2 km, free parking. By public transport: Léman Express Annemasse station (5 min walk) or TAC line 8 stop Château Bleu. Phone: 04 50 87 52 30.
segments.mt: Château Bleu Aquatic Centre znajduje się na 2, route de Bonneville, 74100 Annemasse. Samochodem: zjazd z autostrady nr 14 przy 1.2 km, bezpłatny parking. Transport publiczny: Léman Express stacja Annemasse (5 min pieszo) lub linia TAC 8 przystanek Château Bleu. Telefon: 04 50 87 52 30.

segments.src: Where to park at Château Bleu Aquatic Centre?
segments.mt: Gdzie zaparkować w Château Bleu Aquatic Centre?

segments.src: Château Bleu Aquatic Centre has free on-site parking (Château Bleu car park). Motorway exit no.14 at 1.2 km. Check availability during high season.
segments.mt: Château Bleu Aquatic Centre posiada bezpłatny parking na miejscu (Château Bleu parking). Autostrada zjazd nr 14 przy 1,2 km. Sprawdź dostępność w sezonie.

segments.src: Do I need to book for Château Bleu Aquatic Centre?
segments.mt: Czy muszę zarezerwować dla Château Bleu Aquatic Centre?

segments.src: No booking required for open access to pools and wellness area. Booking recommended for classes (aquabike, aquagym, swimming, baby swimming). Information and registration on chateau-bleu.com or at 04 50 87 52 30.
segments.mt: Brak rezerwacji dla otwartego dostępu do basenów i wellness. Rezerwacja polecana dla klas (aquabike, aquagym, pływanie, pływanie dla dzieci). Informacje i rejestracja na chateau- bleu.com lub na 04 50 87 52 30.

fields.src: 50-metre Olympic outdoor pool on the shore of Lake Geneva at Thonon-les-Bains, with diving boards, beach and children's pool. Major public facility, adult entry €4.

segments.src: 50-metre Olympic outdoor pool on the shore of Lake Geneva at Thonon-les-Bains, with diving boards, beach and children's pool. Major public facility, adult entry €4.
segments.mt: 50- metrowy odkryty basen olimpijski nad jeziorem Genewa w Thonon-les-Bains, z deskami do nurkowania, plażą i basenem dla dzieci. Główny obiekt publiczny, wejście dla dorosłych 4 EUR.

src.what_is:
The Olympic Pool of Thonon is a large aquatic facility set right on the shore of Lake Geneva at Thonon-les-Bains. It combines a 50-metre Olympic outdoor pool, a children's pool and several diving boards, doubled with a beach facing the lake. A major public facility of Thonon Agglomération, it opens for the summer season, from May to September.

The complex offers direct access to the Lake Geneva shoreline, so swimmers can switch between the pool and open-water bathing in the lake itself, with a view across to the Vaud Alps on the Swiss side.

2026 prices: adult €4, child €2.80. Season: May to September (outdoor season). Hours: daily 10am–7pm, with variations depending on the weather. Accessible for reduced-mobility visitors.

fields.src: Aquariaz is open two seasons: in winter (December to mid-April) for skiers and holidaymakers, and in summer (late June to late August) for hikers and mountain bikers. In winter, the centre is very busy during school holidays and weekends (check the Affluences app to avoid crowds). In summer, the atmosphere is quieter and Avoriaz resort is often empty, making for a more intimate experience. The outdoor hot tub is particularly popular in winter after a day of skiing, with views of snow-capped peaks. Aquafitness sessions at 10:30 am allow you to enjoy the pools in the morning before afternoon crowds. In season, last entry at 6:45 pm, pool evacuation at 7:15 pm.

segments.src: Aquariaz is open two seasons: in winter (December to mid-April) for skiers and holidaymakers, and in summer (late June to late August) for hikers and mountain bikers. In winter, the centre is very busy during school holidays and weekends (check the Affluences app to avoid crowds). In summer, the atmosphere is quieter and Avoriaz resort is often empty, making for a more intimate experience. The outdoor hot tub is particularly popular in winter after a day of skiing, with views of snow-capped peaks. Aquafitness sessions at 10:30 am allow you to enjoy the pools in the morning before afternoon crowds. In season, last entry at 6:45 pm, pool evacuation at 7:15 pm.
segments.mt: Aquariaz jest otwarty dwa sezony: zimą (od grudnia do połowy kwietnia) dla narciarzy i urlopowiczów, a latem (od czerwca do końca sierpnia) dla turystów i rowerzystów górskich. W zimie ośrodek jest bardzo zajęty podczas wakacji szkolnych i weekendów (sprawdź aplikację Affluences, aby uniknąć tłumów). Latem atmosfera jest cichsza, a ośrodek Avoriaz jest często pusty, co zapewnia bardziej intymne doświadczenie. Jacuzzi na świeżym powietrzu jest szczególnie popularne w zimie po dniu jazdy na nartach, z widokiem na śnieżne szczyty. Sesje Aquafitness o 10: 30 pozwalają cieszyć się basenem rano przed popołudniem tłumy. W sezonie, ostatni wpis o 18: 45, ewakuacja basenu o 19: 15.

src.q: How do I book Aquariaz?
src.a: No reservation is necessary to access Aquariaz. Simply arrive at the reception during opening hours (1:30 pm–7:30 pm, last entry 6:45 pm). Groups over 20 people must complete the online form on avoriaz.com.

src.q: What are Aquariaz's rates?
src.a: Aquariaz winter rate for adults is €12. Child 3-11 years: €8. Senior 65+: €11. Summer adult rate: €11. Free under 2 years. Free entry with the Avoriaz MultiPass, -10% with Alpine Express. Verified 6 June 2026.

src.q: What are Aquariaz's opening hours?
src.a: Aquariaz is open Sunday to Friday 1:30 pm–7:30 pm in winter (12 Dec 2025 – 17 Apr 2026, closed Saturday except exceptions) and daily 1:30 pm–7:30 pm in summer (26 Jun – 28 Aug 2026). Last entry 6:45 pm, pool evacuation 7:15 pm. Verified 6 June 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

src.q: Are there activities for children at Aquariaz?
src.a: Yes, Aquariaz has a fully dedicated children's area for ages 0-12: heated paddling pool at 32 °C, water tree with geysers and suspended water games, slides, water guns, tipping bucket. Colourful, fun and secure play area. Children under 12 must be accompanied by an adult (18 years and over).

src.q: Can I access the outdoor hot tub at Aquariaz in winter?
src.a: Yes, Aquariaz's outdoor hot tub is heated to 33 °C and accessible in winter and summer. It offers breathtaking views of the Portes du Soleil peaks. A unique mountain experience, particularly appreciated after a day of skiing.

src.q: Where can I park for Aquariaz?
src.a: Avoriaz is a car-free resort. Park at the paid car parks in Prodains (with cable car) or at Avoriaz car parks on the village edge. Tracked shuttle buses connect the car parks to the resort centre. Aquariaz is located in the centre, within walking distance of all accommodation.

src.q: Do I need to bring my own swimsuit to Aquariaz?
src.a: You can bring your own swimsuit. If needed, swimsuit and towel rental is available on site (€6 + deposit, subject to availability). Swim shorts are allowed. Consider bringing swimming goggles for children.

src.q: When is the best time to visit Aquariaz to avoid crowds?
src.a: To avoid crowds, visit in the morning (arrive at 1:30 pm opening), outside school holidays, or in summer (June-August) which is quieter. Check the Affluences app for real-time crowd levels before you go.

fields.src: Art of Pinball Poisy — Pinball & arcade venue | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Art of Pinball Poisy — Pinball & arcade venue | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Art of Pinball Poisy - Pinball & arcade miejsce 124; Loisirs 74

src.what_is:
Art of Pinball Poisy is a retro and modern pinball & arcade venue located in Poisy (74330), 10 minutes north of Annecy. The venue gathers an eclectic collection of iconic pinball machines (from 1980s-1990s machines to the latest Stern Pinball, Jersey Jack Pinball releases, etc.) and classic arcade cabinets (80s-90s games, fighting games, racing games). All games are free to play once inside: you pay an entry based on duration (1h, 2h, 3h or day-pass), then play unlimited on all available machines.

Who is it for? Art of Pinball is aimed at pinball and arcade nostalgics, retro video game fans, families with children from age 6-8 (depending on games), groups of friends and students. It's also a popular spot for birthdays (kids or adults), original stag/hen parties and informal corporate outings. The atmosphere is relaxed and friendly, with period soundtracks and evocative lighting. Bar/snack often available to wait, take a break or share a drink between games.

Practical info and access. No booking required for free access — just turn up. Expect around €10-15 for 2-3 hours of unlimited play, with preferential rates for kids, students and pass holders. For groups (birthdays, stag/hen, private events), privatisation is generally possible by booking. Parking nearby, quick access from Annecy or Sillingy. Check exact hours and prices on artofpinball.fr or by phone.

Wszystkie gry są darmowe do gry raz wewnątrz: płacisz wpis w zależności od czasu trwania (1h, 2h, 3h lub day- pass), a następnie grać nieograniczony na wszystkich dostępnych maszynach.

segments.mt: Brak rezerwacji dla bezpłatnego dostępu - po prostu się pojaw. Oczekuj około €10- 15 na 2-3 godziny nieograniczonej gry, z preferencyjnymi stawkami dla dzieci, studentów i posiadaczy przepustek. W przypadku grup (urodziny, jelenia / kury, imprezy prywatne) prywatyzacja jest ogólnie możliwa poprzez rezerwację. Parking w pobliżu, szybki dostęp od Annecy lub Sillingy. Sprawdź dokładne godziny i ceny na artofpinball.fr lub telefonicznie.

src.q: Where is Art of Pinball Poisy?
src.a: Art of Pinball is in Poisy (74330), 10 minutes north of Annecy. Exact address to confirm on artofpinball.fr. Access by car, bike or Sibra bus.

src.q: How does it work? Are there tokens?
src.a: No, no tokens or coins. Entry is paid by duration (1h, 2h, 3h or day pass) and gives unlimited access to all machines for that time. Once inside, you play freely.

src.q: How much is entry?
src.a: Expect around €10-15 for 2-3 hours of unlimited play, with preferential rates for kids, students and pass holders. Exact 2026 prices to confirm on artofpinball.fr.

src.q: From what age can my kids play?
src.a: Access is generally possible from age 6-8 depending on games. Classic arcade cabinets suit all ages; some modern pinball machines may have more adult themes (assess based on your child).

src.q: What pinball machines are in the collection?
src.a: The collection is eclectic and regularly refreshed: vintage pinball machines from 1980s-1990s (Bally, Williams) and modern (Stern Pinball, Jersey Jack Pinball, Spooky Pinball). Up-to-date catalogue on artofpinball.fr.

src.q: Can I organise a birthday or stag/hen?
src.a: Yes, Art of Pinball is a popular venue for birthdays (kids or adults) and original stag/hen parties. Privatisation generally possible by booking. Contact the venue for a quote.

src.q: Is there a bar/snack on site?
src.a: Yes, a bar/snack is often available to wait, take a break or share a drink between games. Relaxed and friendly atmosphere.

fields.src: Atelier de poterie du Prunier — Pottery in Thônes, Aravis | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Atelier de poterie du Prunier — Pottery in Thônes, Aravis | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Atelier de poterie du Prunier - Pottery in Thônes, Aravis AIR124; Loisirs 74

src.q: Do I need experience to take part at the Atelier de poterie du Prunier?
src.a: No, the workshop primarily welcomes beginners. The potter guides you step by step through your first movements on the wheel or in hand-building. The termly cycles allow the most motivated participants to progress week by week.

src.q: How much does a workshop at the Atelier de poterie du Prunier cost?
src.a: Allow ~€50-70 for a 2-hour introductory session, ~€70-90 for a half-day, ~€100-130 for a full day. Children's courses ~€30-50. The price includes clay, use of materials and firing of the pieces. 2026 prices to be confirmed.

src.q: When can I collect my pieces after the Atelier de poterie du Prunier?
src.a: Allow 3 to 4 weeks between the workshop and collecting your pieces: drying (1-2 weeks), first bisque firing (~950 °C), glazing and final firing (~1240 °C for stoneware). The workshop lets you know when the pieces are ready.

src.q: From what age can a child take part at the Atelier de poterie du Prunier?
src.a: Children's courses are offered from age 6-7, mainly in hand-building (handling clay, free creation, small objects). The wheel is generally introduced from age 10-12, depending on the child's coordination. Birthdays possible.

src.q: How should I dress to attend the Atelier de poterie du Prunier?
src.a: Bring work clothes that can take stains (clay colours and soaks in) — old jeans, sweatshirt or T-shirt. Avoid loose long sleeves at the wheel. Closed shoes. Aprons provided by the workshop.

src.q: Is the Atelier de poterie du Prunier accessible to people with reduced mobility?
src.a: Accessibility depends on the specific workshop layout (floors, access width, wheel height). We recommend calling ahead to discuss your needs and adapt the welcome.

src.q: Can I offer a course at the Atelier de poterie du Prunier as a gift?
src.a: Yes, the workshop offers gift cards to give an introduction or a half- or full-day course. The card is valid for around 1 year. Ideal for a birthday, Mother's or Father's Day, Christmas or a wedding anniversary.

segments.src: Do I need experience to take part at the Atelier de poterie du Prunier?
segments.mt: Czy potrzebuję doświadczenia, aby wziąć udział w Atelier de poterie du Prunier?

segments.src: No, the workshop primarily welcomes beginners. The potter guides you step by step through your first movements on the wheel or in hand-building. The termly cycles allow the most motivated participants to progress week by week.
segments.mt: Nie, warsztat wita przede wszystkim początkujących. Garncarz prowadzi krok po kroku przez swoje pierwsze ruchy na kole lub w budowie ręcznej. Krótkie cykle pozwalają najbardziej zmotywowanym uczestnikom rozwijać się tydzień po tygodniu.

segments.src: How much does a workshop at the Atelier de poterie du Prunier cost?
segments.mt: Ile kosztuje warsztat w Atelier de poterie du Prunier?

segments.src: Allow ~€50-70 for a 2-hour introductory session, ~€70-90 for a half-day, ~€100-130 for a full day. Children's courses ~€30-50. The price includes clay, use of materials and firing of the pieces. 2026 prices to be confirmed.
segments.mt: Pozwól ~ €50- 70 na dwugodzinną sesję wprowadzającą, ~ €70- 90 na pół dnia, ~ €100- 130 na cały dzień. Kursy dla dzieci ~ €30- 50. Cena obejmuje glinę, wykorzystanie materiałów i wypalanie kawałków. Ceny do potwierdzenia w 2026 r.

segments.src: When can I collect my pieces after the Atelier de poterie du Prunier?
segments.mt: Kiedy mogę zebrać swoje dzieła po Atelier de poterie du Prunier?

segments.src: Allow 3 to 4 weeks between the workshop and collecting your pieces: drying (1-2 weeks), first bisque firing (~950 °C), glazing and final firing (~1240 °C for stoneware). The workshop lets you know when the pieces are ready.
segments.mt: Pozwolić 3 do 4 tygodni pomiędzy warsztatem i zbierać swoje kawałki: suszenie (1-2 tygodni), pierwszy bisque wypalanie (~ 950 ° C), oszklenia i końcowe wypalanie (~ 1240 ° C dla kamionek). Warsztat informuje, kiedy będą gotowe.

segments.src: From what age can a child take part at the Atelier de poterie du Prunier?
segments.mt: W jakim wieku dziecko może uczestniczyć w Atelier de poterie du Prunier?

segments.src: Children's courses are offered from age 6-7, mainly in hand-building (handling clay, free creation, small objects). The wheel is generally introduced from age 10-12, depending on the child's coordination. Birthdays possible.
segments.mt: Kursy dla dzieci oferowane są w wieku od 6 do 7 lat, głównie w budowie ręcznej (obsługa gliny, darmowe tworzenie, małe obiekty). Koło jest zazwyczaj wprowadzane od 10- 12 roku życia, w zależności od koordynacji dziecka. Możliwe urodziny.

segments.src: How should I dress to attend the Atelier de poterie du Prunier?
segments.mt: Jak mam się ubrać, aby uczestniczyć w Atelier de poterie du Prunier?

segments.src: Bring work clothes that can take stains (clay colours and soaks in) — old jeans, sweatshirt or T-shirt. Avoid loose long sleeves at the wheel. Closed shoes. Aprons provided by the workshop.
segments.mt: Przynieś ubrania robocze, które mogą wziąć plamy (gliniane kolory i mydło w) - stare dżinsy, bluza lub T-shirt. Unikać luźnych długich rękawów przy kole. Zamknięte buty. Fartuchy dostarczone przez warsztat.

segments.src: Is the Atelier de poterie du Prunier accessible to people with reduced mobility?
segments.mt: Czy Atelier de poterie du Prunier jest dostępny dla osób o ograniczonej sprawności ruchowej?

segments.src: Accessibility depends on the specific workshop layout (floors, access width, wheel height). We recommend calling ahead to discuss your needs and adapt the welcome.
segments.mt: Dostępność zależy od konkretnego układu warsztatowego (podłogi, szerokość dostępu, wysokość koła). Polecamy połączenie do przodu, aby omówić swoje potrzeby i dostosować powitanie.

segments.src: Can I offer a course at the Atelier de poterie du Prunier as a gift?
segments.mt: Czy mogę zaoferować kurs w Atelier de poterie du Prunier jako prezent?

segments.src: Yes, the workshop offers gift cards to give an introduction or a half- or full-day course. The card is valid for around 1 year. Ideal for a birthday, Mother's or Father's Day, Christmas or a wedding anniversary.
segments.mt: Tak, warsztat oferuje karty podarunkowe, aby dać wprowadzenie lub pół-lub całodniowy kurs. Karta jest ważna przez około 1 rok. Idealny na urodziny, Dzień Matki lub Ojca, Boże Narodzenie lub rocznicę ślubu.

src.v: Plan d'eau Biotope, Route de Prairy, 74920 Combloux
Approximately 1,000 m (Combloux village)
Open 13 June to 6 September 2026. High season (4 July - 30 August): daily 10:30 AM - 7 PM. Low season: variable hours, see official website.
High season: adult €7.50, child 5-14 years €6. Low season: adult €6.50, child €5.50. Free under 5 (ticket required). 12-entry cards and season passes available.
+33 4 57 44 02 99 — plandeau@mairie-combloux.fr
combloux.com/profiter/eau/se-baigner/plan-eau-biotope-combloux — online booking at boutique.combloux.com
Parking des Cristaux or Théâtre de Verdure (Route de Prairy), 2 min walk. From A40 take Sallanches exit, then towards Combloux/Megève (20 min).
Changing rooms and showers available at the entrance. Lockers for personal belongings (deposit or ID required depending on attendance).
Swimming supervised by qualified lifeguards (MNS) throughout the open season. Enhanced presence in high season (July-August).

fields.src: The leisure complex is open daily in July and August, the busiest period with all activities running and permanent swimming supervision. In June, opening is limited to weekends and public holidays. The lake water, naturally temperate, is most pleasant from late June to late August. Outside high season, the complex offers afternoon opening from 1:30pm for unsupervised swimming and adventure course. The restaurant "À la Belle Étoile" remains accessible year-round Thursday to Sunday (except July-August when open daily), allowing you to enjoy the lake setting even outside summer. Autumn and spring offer a quieter setting for hikers wishing to discover the site without the summer crowds.

segments.mt: Kompleks rekreacyjny jest otwarty codziennie w lipcu i sierpniu, najbardziej pracowity okres ze wszystkimi działaniami bieżącymi i stałego nadzoru pływania. W czerwcu otwarcie jest ograniczone do weekendów i świąt publicznych. Woda w jeziorze, naturalnie umiarkowana, jest najprzyjemniejsza od końca czerwca do końca sierpnia. Poza wysokim sezonem, kompleks oferuje popołudniowe otwarcie od 13: 30 na nienadzorowane pływanie i kurs przygody. Restauracja "La Belle Étoile" pozostaje dostępna przez cały rok od czwartku do niedzieli (z wyjątkiem July- August, gdy codziennie otwarte), co pozwala cieszyć się widokiem jeziora nawet poza latem. Jesień i wiosna oferują spokojniejsze ustawienie dla turystów chcących odkryć miejsce bez letnich tłumów.

src.q: How do I book at Base de loisirs de la Beunaz?
src.a: Online booking at indianaventures.com or on-site purchase at the entry to Base de loisirs de la Beunaz. Reservation is advised in July-August for activities (adventure course, relaxation areas) and mandatory for unusual accommodation.

src.q: What are the rates at Base de loisirs de la Beunaz?
src.a: Swimming entry alone costs €3.80 (from age 6, free under 6). The Pass'Beun multi-activity is €18 (unlimited access to water and land activities). Indiana Parc course is €23 adult. Complete package (Pass + Course) €39. Verified 14 May 2026.

src.q: What are the hours at Base de loisirs de la Beunaz?
src.a: In June: Saturday-Sunday and public holidays 10am–6pm. In July-August: daily 10am–6pm. Off-season: afternoon opening from 1:30pm for unsupervised swimming and adventure course. Hours verified on the official website 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

src.q: Is there parking at Base de loisirs de la Beunaz?
src.a: Yes. Base de loisirs de la Beunaz has free on-site parking, directly accessible from the route de la Plage (locality La Beunaz).

src.q: Can I swim in Lake Beunaz?
src.a: Yes. Lake Beunaz offers a supervised swimming zone in July-August with pool fed by lake water, diving boards, slide and inflatable structures. The water is naturally temperate around 16 °C.

src.q: Are dogs accepted at Base de loisirs de la Beunaz?
src.a: No. Animals are not permitted at Base de loisirs de la Beunaz (internal regulations in effect).

src.q: What is the Pass'Beun?
src.a: The Pass'Beun is a day package at €18 giving unlimited access to all Base de loisirs de la Beunaz activities: swimming, canoe, pedalo, stand-up paddle, Télébeune (aquatic zip-line), Wibit (inflatable structures), trampolines, sports fields. Indiana Parc adventure course is not included.

src.q: Can I stay overnight at Base de loisirs de la Beunaz?
src.a: Yes. Base de loisirs de la Beunaz offers unusual accommodation: tree houses (4 people), tipis and yurts (10 people), wigwam (2 people). Overnight stay with breakfast included, facilities on the complex. Mandatory booking at indianaventures.com.

src.q: Is access to the Base de loisirs des Ilettes free?
src.a: Yes. Access to the Base de loisirs des Ilettes is free and unrestricted year-round, as is parking on site.

src.q: Are dogs allowed at the Base de loisirs des Ilettes?
src.a: Dogs are prohibited at lakes 1 and 2 (fishing and swimming) for public health reasons. They are allowed on lead at lake 3 (windsurfing) and on the rustic path along the Arve. Dog swimming is strictly prohibited throughout the site. Dog waste bins are available.

src.q: Is swimming supervised at the Base de loisirs des Ilettes?
src.a: Yes. Swimming at lake 2 (swimming lake, 450 m long) is supervised by lifeguards in July and August from 12pm to 6pm. Outside this period, swimming remains permitted but unsupervised.

src.q: Where can you fish at the Base de loisirs des Ilettes?
src.a: Lake 1 (242 m) is exclusively reserved for category 1 fishing (wild and rainbow trout, perch, carp, pike). Lakes 2 and 3 are classified as category 2 for fishing outside summer season. Fishing permits available at Passion Pêche shop or Sallanches tourist office. Night carp fishing in 'no kill' format organised by AAPPMA (registration required).

src.q: Is there a restaurant at the Base de loisirs des Ilettes?
src.a: Yes. A café and snack bar (Transat restaurant) operate at the Base de loisirs des Ilettes during the summer season, approximately mid-April to mid-September. Contact the tourist office for exact opening times.

src.q: Can you windsurf at the Base de loisirs des Ilettes?
src.a: Yes. Lake 3 (515 m long) is one of the best windsurfing and wingfoil spots in Haute-Savoie. The southern section of the lake is reserved for windsurfers, with pontoons and launching ramps. The northern section is dedicated to category 2 fishing.

src.q: What facilities are available at the Base de loisirs des Ilettes?
src.a: The Base de loisirs des Ilettes has outdoor showers near the first aid post, volleyball and pétanque courts, table tennis tables, a fitness trail with 23 apparatus, equipped picnic areas, public toilets, free parking and dog waste bins. A bird observatory is located at the northern end of lake 3.

src.q: Are there any works planned at the Base de loisirs des Ilettes?
src.a: Yes. Refurbishment works at the Base de loisirs des Ilettes are underway from September 2025 to June 2026. During this period, paths around the fishing and swimming lakes are closed, as are the north and central car parks. The northern lake (windsurfing) and the Arve embankment remain accessible. For your safety, respect barriers in place.

segments.src: Lake 1 (242 m) is exclusively reserved for category 1 fishing (wild and rainbow trout, perch, carp, pike). Lakes 2 and 3 are classified as category 2 for fishing outside summer season. Fishing permits available at Passion Pêche shop or Sallanches tourist office. Night carp fishing in 'no kill' format organised by AAPPMA (registration required).
segments.mt: Jezioro 1 (242 m) jest zastrzeżone wyłącznie dla połowów kategorii 1 (pstrąg dziki i tęczowy, okoń, karp, szczupak). Jeziora 2 i 3 są klasyfikowane jako kategoria 2 do połowów poza sezonem letnim. Zezwolenia na połowy dostępne w sklepie Passion Pęche lub w biurze turystycznym Sallanches. Nocne połowy karpia w formacie "no kill" organizowanym przez AAPPPMA (wymagana rejestracja).

segments.src: Is there a restaurant at the Base de loisirs des Ilettes?
segments.mt: Czy jest restauracja w Base de loisirs des Ilettes?

segments.src: Yes. A café and snack bar (Transat restaurant) operate at the Base de loisirs des Ilettes during the summer season, approximately mid-April to mid-September. Contact the tourist office for exact opening times.
segments.mt: Tak. Kawiarnia i bar przekąskowy (restauracja Transat) działają w Base de loisirs des Ilettes w sezonie letnim, około połowy kwietnia do połowy września. Skontaktuj się z biurem turystycznym dla dokładnych godzin otwarcia.

segments.src: Can you windsurf at the Base de loisirs des Ilettes?
segments.mt: Możesz surfować w Base de loisirs des Ilettes?

segments.src: Yes. Lake 3 (515 m long) is one of the best windsurfing and wingfoil spots in Haute-Savoie. The southern section of the lake is reserved for windsurfers, with pontoons and launching ramps. The northern section is dedicated to category 2 fishing.
segments.mt: Tak. Jezioro 3 (515 m długości) jest jednym z najlepszych miejsc windsurfingu i folii skrzydłowej w Haute-Savoie. Południowa część jeziora jest zarezerwowana dla windsurferów, z pontonami i rampami startowymi. Część północna poświęcona jest rybołówstwu kategorii 2.

segments.src: What facilities are available at the Base de loisirs des Ilettes?
segments.mt: Jakie udogodnienia są dostępne w Base de loisirs des Ilettes?

segments.src: The Base de loisirs des Ilettes has outdoor showers near the first aid post, volleyball and pétanque courts, table tennis tables, a fitness trail with 23 apparatus, equipped picnic areas, public toilets, free parking and dog waste bins. A bird observatory is located at the northern end of lake 3.
segments.mt: Base de loisirs des Ilettes posiada prysznice na świeżym powietrzu w pobliżu pierwszej pomocy, boiska do siatkówki i pétanque, stoły do tenisa stołowego, szlak fitness z 23 aparatury, wyposażone miejsca piknikowe, toalety publiczne, bezpłatny parking i kosze na śmieci dla psów. Obserwatorium ptaków znajduje się na północnym końcu jeziora 3.

segments.src: Are there any works planned at the Base de loisirs des Ilettes?
segments.mt: Czy w Base de loisirs des Ilettes planowane są jakieś prace?

segments.src: Yes. Refurbishment works at the Base de loisirs des Ilettes are underway from September 2025 to June 2026. During this period, paths around the fishing and swimming lakes are closed, as are the north and central car parks. The northern lake (windsurfing) and the Arve embankment remain accessible. For your safety, respect barriers in place.
segments.mt: Tak. Prace remontowe w Base de loisirs des Ilettes trwają od września 2025 r. do czerwca 2026 r. W tym okresie ścieżki wokół łowisk i jezior są zamknięte, podobnie jak parkingi północne i centralne. Północne jezioro (windsurfing) i nabrzeże Arve pozostają dostępne. Dla waszego bezpieczeństwa, szanujcie bariery.

src.title: Swim in the lake
src.description: Supervised swimming July 6 to September 1, 12pm to 6pm. Equipped beach with gradual access. Free.

src.title: Play in children's playground
src.description: Large secure play area in the heart of the base. Slide, climbing structures, swings. Free.

src.title: Play team sports
src.description: Football, basketball, beach volleyball, pétanque fields in free access. Tennis, padel and beach tennis for a fee (rental via Tennis du Giffre).

src.title: Walk around the lakes
src.description: Easy 2 km trail around the two lakes. Suitable for pushchairs and people with reduced mobility. Starting point for hikes to Morillon and the Giffre riverside.

src.title: Fish
src.description: Fishing allowed in both lakes (regulations by municipal by-law, consult the town hall website). Fishing licence required.

src.title: Stand-up paddling
src.description: Paddle rental on site. Activity accessible to beginners, ideal for discovering the lakes peacefully.

src.title: Picnic
src.description: Several picnic areas equipped with tables, benches and bins. Public sanitary facilities on site. Free.

src.title: Dine
src.description: Two bars-restaurants on the base: L'Entre 2 Lacs and the Espace Le Bois aux Dames bar. Terraces with lake views.

src.what_is:
The Base de loisirs du Lac Bleu is a public recreational complex located at 670 m elevation in Morillon, in the Giffre valley. The site is organised around a small 2.5-hectare mountain lake, surrounded by shaded lawns, a sandy beach, and large open spaces designed for families. Access to the park is free and open year-round.

The lake has a supervised swimming zone in summer (July–August, 1–7 pm), with shallow areas cordoned off for young children. Around the lake, several leisure providers offer paid activities: tree-top course, equestrian centre, Big Air Bag, inflatable water structures (Le Splash), pumptrack, trampolines, kayaking, paddleboarding, and beach volleyball. A free children's play area (slides, swings, wooden trail course) is accessible to all.

The site regularly hosts summer events: children's shows, open-air concerts, trout-fishing contests. La Covagne bar-restaurant on the lake shore. Public toilets, benches, and picnic tables. Mountain-bike wash area. Located in the Grand Massif domain, Lac Bleu is a landing point for paragliders. Morillon is served by the intercommunal Haut-Giffre Tourisme office.

src.what_is:
The Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles, also called Lake of Passy, is an artificial lake of 50 hectares created in the 1970s when excavating materials for the A40 motorway. Over one kilometre long, it offers a frontal view of the Mont-Blanc massif and the Fiz range, at approximately 550 m altitude in the Arve valley.

The site features a fine sandy beach with supervised swimming in July–August (depth zones < 1.20 m and > 1.20 m), grassy coves, a 2.5 km walking trail around the lake, a fitness course in the woods (15 stations over 800 m), picnic areas and several activity providers: mini-golf, go-kart, rope course, small electric boats, pedal boats, stand-up paddle, windsurfing. Fishing is permitted on designated pontoons.

Accessible year-round, the leisure park is a popular destination in fine weather for swimming, water activities and family walks. In winter, the site becomes a peaceful place to stroll with spectacular views of snow-capped peaks. The lake is managed by Passy municipality with a commitment to sustainable tourism and environmental preservation.

fields.src: The lake is accessible year-round. High season runs from June to August: the beach is supervised in July–August (12 noon–6 pm), activity providers open daily, parking becomes paid. May–June and September are quieter periods, ideal for walks and water activities without crowds. In autumn and winter, the site becomes a popular place for peaceful strolls with spectacular views of snow-capped Mont-Blanc. The fitness trail and lake loop remain passable year-round except during heavy snowfall.

src.q: Is entry to Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles paid?
src.a: No. Entry to Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles is free year-round. Only parking is paid in July–August (€3 per day). Activities offered by providers (go-kart, rope course, pedal boats, mini-golf) are paid.

src.q: Is swimming supervised at Lake Îles?
src.a: Yes. Swimming at Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles is supervised in July and August only, from 12 noon to 6 pm, on the sandy beach with two delimited zones (< 1.20 m and > 1.20 m). Water quality is monitored regularly.

src.q: Are dogs allowed at Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles?
src.a: Not on the beach and coves. Dogs are strictly prohibited on the beach, grassy coves and riverside paths at Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles. They are allowed on leash on upper trails around the lake and on the south shore between fishing pontoons and the play area.

src.q: How long should I allow to visit Lake Îles?
src.a: Allow a minimum of 2 hours to walk around the lake (2.5 km) and enjoy a picnic. For swimming and water activities, allow a half day or even a full day at Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles.

src.q: Can I hire pedal boats and paddleboards at Lake Îles?
src.a: Yes. Several independent providers offer pedal boat, small electric boat, stand-up paddle and windsurfing equipment hire at Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles. Rentals are arranged directly on site, at the miniature port or on the beach.

src.q: Are there restaurants or snack bars at Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles?
src.a: Yes. A drinks stand (Buvette des Criques) and snack bar open in summer at Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles. Several picnic areas also allow you to bring your own food. Tables and benches are available around the lake.

segments.src: Are dogs allowed at Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles?
segments.mt: Czy psy są dozwolone w Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles?

segments.src: Not on the beach and coves. Dogs are strictly prohibited on the beach, grassy coves and riverside paths at Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles. They are allowed on leash on upper trails around the lake and on the south shore between fishing pontoons and the play area.
segments.mt: Nie na plaży i zatoczkach. Psy są surowo zabronione na plaży, trawiaste zatoczki i ścieżek rzecznych w Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles. Są one dozwolone na smyczy na górnych szlakach wokół jeziora i na południowym brzegu między pontony rybackie i plac zabaw.

segments.src: How long should I allow to visit Lake Îles?
segments.mt: Jak długo mam pozwolić na odwiedzenie jeziora?

segments.src: Allow a minimum of 2 hours to walk around the lake (2.5 km) and enjoy a picnic. For swimming and water activities, allow a half day or even a full day at Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles.
segments.mt: Pozostawić minimum 2 godziny na spacer po jeziorze (2,5 km) i cieszyć się piknikiem. Dla pływania i aktywności wodnej, niech pół dnia lub nawet cały dzień w Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles.

segments.src: Can I hire pedal boats and paddleboards at Lake Îles?
segments.mt: Czy mogę wynająć pedałowe łodzie i deski wiosłowe nad jeziorem?

segments.src: Yes. Several independent providers offer pedal boat, small electric boat, stand-up paddle and windsurfing equipment hire at Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles. Rentals are arranged directly on site, at the miniature port or on the beach.
segments.mt: Tak. Kilku niezależnych dostawców oferuje łodzi pedałowej, małej łodzi elektrycznej, stojący wiosła i sprzęt windsurfing wynajem w Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles. Wynajmuje się bezpośrednio na miejscu, w porcie miniaturowym lub na plaży.

segments.src: Are there restaurants or snack bars at Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles?
segments.mt: Czy w Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles są restauracje lub bary z przekąskami?

segments.src: Yes. A drinks stand (Buvette des Criques) and snack bar open in summer at Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles. Several picnic areas also allow you to bring your own food. Tables and benches are available around the lake.
segments.mt: Tak. Stojak na napoje (Buvette des Criques) i bar przekąskowy otwarty latem w Base de loisirs du Lac des Îles. Kilka obszarów piknikowych pozwala również przynieść własne jedzenie. Stoły i ławki dostępne są wokół jeziora.

src.what_is:
The Doussard Watersports Centre occupies the southern port of Lake Annecy, on the Doussard / Bout-du-Lac shore. Several private operators share the infrastructure: Skiwake74 for wakeboard and water-skiing, sailing schools and paddle rentals. The southern shore is directly linked to the lakeside Voie verte cycle path, making the site a practical starting point for water sports.

The centre covers all main disciplines: sailing (Optimist, dinghy), towed wakeboard, water-skiing, stand-up paddle (SUP) and kayak. The flat water of the early morning makes the spot particularly suited to technical disciplines and beginner courses, while the southern end of the lake gives easy onshore access via the Voie verte.

2026 prices: adult €30–80 depending on activity, child courses for ages 6–15. Season: May to September. Hours: rentals and lessons 9am–7pm in summer season. Partially accessible for reduced-mobility visitors.

src.what_is:
The Évian-les-Bains watersports centre is one of the main access points to water activities on the southern shore of Lake Geneva. Set on the Évian beach area, on avenue du Général Dupas, it offers both self-guided rentals (paddle, single or double kayak, pedalo, rowing boats) and supervised sessions run by the instructors of Évian's historic sailing club.

Practical info: the centre is open every day in summer from 1 pm to 7 pm, with a late session on Thursday until 9 pm. Supervised sessions run all summer: kayak and stand-up paddle on Wednesday and Saturday evenings, rowing on Monday/Wednesday/Friday afternoons, sailing on Wednesday afternoon from early July to late August. A supervised swimming area is also open on the adjacent beach from June to September (12 pm–6 pm, 11 am–7 pm in July–August).

The centre lets visitors and locals alike enjoy Lake Geneva in a unique setting — the Léman opening onto the Swiss shore, Évian's belle-époque promenade, the panorama of the Vaud mountains — at affordable group rates (paddle from €15/h, kayak €20/h, supervised sessions around €25/session).

src.q: What activities does the Évian-les-Bains water sports base offer?
src.a: The Évian-les-Bains water sports base offers paddle, kayak (single or double), sailing, rowing, diving, freediving, pedalos and rowing boats. Activities are self-guided or supervised by the Évian Sailing Club, on Lake Geneva.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Évian-les-Bains water sports base?
src.a: The Évian-les-Bains water sports base is open in the summer season daily from 1 pm to 7 pm, with a late opening until 9 pm on Thursdays. Lifeguarded beach June-September 12 pm-6 pm (11 am-7 pm in July-August). Checked on 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the prices at the Évian-les-Bains water sports base?
src.a: Prices at the Évian-les-Bains water sports base vary by activity, duration and level of supervision (free rental vs. supervised session). See ville-evian.fr/loisirs/centre-nautique/ or the Évian Sailing Club. Checked on 10 June 2026.

src.q: Do I need to book at the Évian-les-Bains water sports base?
src.a: Supervised sessions (sailing, rowing, kayak/SUP) at the Évian water sports base require booking with the Sailing Club or the water sports centre. Self-guided rentals (paddle, pedalos) are available without booking, subject to availability.

src.q: Where is the Évian-les-Bains water sports base?
src.a: The Évian-les-Bains water sports base is on avenue du Général Dupas, on Évian beach by Lake Geneva, 10 minutes' walk from Évian SNCF station. GPS: 46.400° N · 6.583° E.

src.q: Is the Évian-les-Bains water sports base open year-round?
src.a: No, the Évian-les-Bains water sports base operates mainly in the summer season, peaking from mid-June to the end of August. The adjacent beach is lifeguarded from June to September. Check the calendar on ville-evian.fr.

src.q: What is the best season for the Évian-les-Bains water sports base?
src.a: Summer (July-August) is the best time to enjoy all the activities at the Évian-les-Bains water sports base: mild Lake Geneva waters, supervised sessions on the programme and a lifeguarded beach.

src.q: How do I get to the Évian-les-Bains water sports base?
src.a: The Évian-les-Bains water sports base is a few minutes' walk from Évian town centre, from Évian SNCF station (Léman Express to Geneva via Annemasse) and from the quay car parks. CGN boats dock at the neighbouring landing stage.

src.what_is:
The Marquisats Watersports Centre, in the centre of Annecy, brings together two emblematic clubs: the SRVA (Société des Régates à Voile d'Annecy), the historic sailing club, and the Club des Sports Nautiques (rowing, kayak, paddle). Its central beach at the foot of the Old Town makes it a particularly well-placed venue for beginner lessons, week-long courses and training sessions.

The centre covers all watersports: sailing (Optimist, Laser, catamaran), rowing, kayak and stand-up paddle (SUP). Children's, teenage and adult courses run through the summer season, and equipment is also available to rent. The Marquisats beach is open to the public and offers direct access to Lake Annecy from the city centre.

2026 prices: adult €30–60 depending on activity, children's courses available. Season: March to October. Hours: lessons and rentals according to schedule – check the website. Partially accessible for reduced-mobility visitors (with adapted sailing).

src.what_is:
The Voile Léman Sciez Watersports Centre is a multi-discipline Lake Geneva sailing centre based at Sciez-Plage (74140), on the southern shore of Lake Geneva. The location is renowned for its optimal thermal winds in the afternoon (Vaudaire, Bornan), making it an ideal spot for sailing and windsurfing.

The school offers a wide range of activities: kitesurfing (approved school), dinghy sailing (Optimist for children, Laser and 420 for teens and adults), catamaran (Hobie Cat 15/16) and windsurfing. Five-day courses run all summer for all levels, with private lessons and equipment hire also available.

2026 prices: adult courses €200–400 depending on discipline, children's courses available. Season: May to September. Hours: daily 9am–6pm in season; out of season by reservation. Partially accessible for reduced-mobility visitors.

fields.src: Panoramic platform at 1254 m, remnant of the former Mont Veyrier cable car. Sweeping 180° view over Lake Annecy, Bauges and Mont-Blanc. Free access year-round.

src.lead: Concrete panoramic platform at 1,254 m altitude, the sole remaining vestige of the former Mont Veyrier cable car, offering sweeping views over Lake Annecy and a 180° panorama from Salève to the Bauges.

src.title: Admire the panorama from the platform
src.description: 180° view from the former cable car arrival: Lake Annecy below, Bauges massif facing south, Semnoz to the west, Mont-Blanc and Aravis to the east. Orientation table.

src.title: Continue to the summit of Mont Baron (1,299 m)
src.description: 10 minutes of climbing from the platform to reach the summit marked by a cairn. Expanded panorama over La Tournette, Dents de Lanfon, Bornes and Aravis ranges.

src.title: Follow the ridge path to Mont Veyrier
src.description: 30-minute ridge walk from the viewpoint heading north, along the ridge line, with continuous views over Lake Annecy. Arrival at the summit of Mont Veyrier (1,291 m).

src.title: Watch the sunset
src.description: One of the finest sunsets in the region. Many bivouac campers pitch tents to witness the spectacular nocturnal display over the lake. Clear westward views.

src.title: Photograph Lake Annecy
src.description: Iconic viewpoint for photographers. Vertical framing of the lake below, horizontal framing of the mountainous panorama. Optimal light in late afternoon.

src.title: Discover the cable car history

src.q: Is access to Belvédère du Mont Baron free?
src.a: Yes. Access to Belvédère du Mont Baron is free and open year-round. It is a hiking trail in free access with no admission fee or toll.

src.q: How do I reach Belvédère du Mont Baron from Annecy?
src.a: Since 29 July 2024, road access to Col des Contrebandiers is closed to vehicles. You must park by the lake (Veyrier-du-Lac or Annecy-le-Vieux) and hike up the marked Mont Baron trail. Allow 1 h 30 to 2 h of walking.

src.q: What is the altitude of Belvédère du Mont Baron?
src.a: Belvédère du Mont Baron (former cable car platform) sits at 1,254 m altitude. Mont Baron summit, 10 minutes higher, reaches 1,299 m.

src.q: Can you bivouac at Belvédère du Mont Baron?
src.a: Yes. Wild bivouacking is tolerated at Belvédère du Mont Baron and widely practiced for sunset viewing. The platform is a popular bivouac spot. Respect guidelines: tent pitched after 19:00, taken down before 09:00, no fires, pack out all waste.

src.q: Is the Belvédère du Mont Baron trail suitable for children?
src.a: Yes, from age 5 with supervision. The Belvédère du Mont Baron trail has rocky sections and some exposed passages secured by cables. Not suitable for young children or strollers.

src.q: What is the best season to visit Belvédère du Mont Baron?
src.a: April to October. Summer (June–September) offers the best weather but high crowds. Spring and autumn are quieter. In winter, the Belvédère du Mont Baron trail is icy and slippery; reserved for experienced hikers with proper equipment.

src.q: Why is the Col des Contrebandiers road closed?
src.a: Since 29 July 2024, the road has been closed to motor vehicles as part of the Mont Veyrier Massif Management Plan 2024-2029. The closure aims to facilitate emergency vehicle access (often blocked by chaotic parking), protect biodiversity and regulate growing visitor numbers.

src.q: What is the history of the Mont Veyrier cable car?
src.a: The Mont Veyrier cable car was inaugurated on 29 July 1934 by banker Léon Laydernier. It transported 2 million passengers over 50 years, reaching 70,000 passengers in 1948. It held two world records: 1,525 m cable span and 808 m elevation gain without intermediate pylons. Closed in 1984 for economic reasons (cost of safety upgrades), the upper station was demolished in 2001 after a fatal accident in 1998.

segments.src: What is the history of the Mont Veyrier cable car?
segments.mt: Jaka jest historia kolejki linowej Mont Veyrier?

segments.src: The Mont Veyrier cable car was inaugurated on 29 July 1934 by banker Léon Laydernier. It transported 2 million passengers over 50 years, reaching 70,000 passengers in 1948. It held two world records: 1,525 m cable span and 808 m elevation gain without intermediate pylons. Closed in 1984 for economic reasons (cost of safety upgrades), the upper station was demolished in 2001 after a fatal accident in 1998.
segments.mt: Stacja kolejowa Mont Veyrier została zainaugurowana 29 lipca 1934 roku przez bank Léon Laydernier. Przewozi 2 miliony pasażerów w ciągu 50 lat, osiągając 70 tysięcy pasażerów w 1948 roku. Posiada dwa rekordy świata: 1,525 m szerokości kabla i 808 m przyrostu wysokości bez pośrednich pylonów. Zamknięty w 1984 roku ze względów ekonomicznych (wzrost kosztów bezpieczeństwa), górna stacja została zburzona w 2001 roku po śmiertelnym wypadku w 1998 roku.
