src.what_is:
The Vuache Castle boundary stone is a commemorative stone dated 1579, sealed at the foot of the steps near the church of Vulbens (74520). Discreet but history-rich, it is today the only tangible remnant of the seigneurial castle of Vuache, which once overlooked the village from a rise about fifteen metres above the current church.

The castle, built in the early 14th century when the region belonged to the County of Geneva, played a triple role: military fortress, temporary residence of the lords of Vuache, and administrative centre of the mandement (seigneurial territory). It was progressively abandoned in the mid-17th century, when the seigneury's seat was moved to the nearby hamlet of Faramaz. The stones were reused in village constructions and the castle quickly vanished from the landscape.

For the history-curious visitor, the 1579 stone is a discreet but evocative starting point: read the engraved date, imagine the silhouette of the castle perched above the church, enjoy the panorama over the Genevois plain and the Vuache range. Free year-round visit.

src.q: What is the boundary stone of the Château du Vuache?
src.a: The boundary stone of the Château du Vuache is a stone dated 1579, set at the foot of the steps near the church of Vulbens. It is today the only tangible remnant of the seigneurial Château du Vuache, which dominated the village in the Middle Ages.

src.q: Where is the boundary stone of the Château du Vuache?
src.a: The boundary stone of the Château du Vuache is in the heart of the village of Vulbens (74520), at the foot of the steps immediately next to the church. GPS: 46.101° N · 5.927° E.

src.q: How much does it cost to visit the boundary stone of the Château du Vuache?
src.a: Access to the boundary stone of the Château du Vuache is entirely free: it is a remnant visible in the public space of Vulbens village. No ticketing. Checked on 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the boundary stone of the Château du Vuache?
src.a: The boundary stone of the Château du Vuache is accessible 24/7, year-round, with free access. Daytime is preferable to read the 1579 date clearly. Checked on 10 June 2026.

src.q: How long should I allow to visit the boundary stone of the Château du Vuache?
src.a: Allow 15 to 20 minutes to find the boundary stone of the Château du Vuache and take a quick tour of Vulbens village. Longer if combined with a walk in the Pays du Vuache.

src.q: What is the history of the Château du Vuache?
src.a: The Château du Vuache, of which the 1579 stone is the last witness, was a 14th-century seigneurial stronghold in the County of Geneva. It was abandoned in the mid-17th century when the seat of the seigneury was moved to Faramaz, and its stones were reused.

src.q: What is there to see around the boundary stone of the Château du Vuache?
src.a: Around the boundary stone of the Château du Vuache, you can visit the church of Vulbens, the Vuache Nature Trail (Jonzier-Épagny), the Tour du Vuache, and the neighbouring villages of Chevrier, Dingy-en-Vuache and Savigny in the Pays du Vuache.

src.q: Where can I park to visit the boundary stone of the Château du Vuache?
src.a: Free parking in the village of Vulbens near the church. The boundary stone of the Château du Vuache is a few steps from the car park, at the foot of the steps.

src.what_is:
The Château de Chaumont, whose romantic ruins still crown a rocky spur above the Rhône, was one of the strongholds of the County of Geneva. Until the early 15th century it housed a châtellenie — an administrative and military district — controlling a vast territory in the south-west Genevois. The sale of the County of Geneva to the Count of Savoy in 1401 was a decisive blow: stripped of its strategic role, the fortress was no longer maintained.

The killing blow came around 1630, during the War of the Mantuan Succession: the royal troops of Louis XIII attacked and razed the castle. Since then the ruins have crumbled, but the site retains a strong atmosphere, perched above the village, with a wide panorama over the Rhône valley, the Vuache range and (on clear days) the Jura.

The Heritage Guides of the Pays d'art et d'histoire run themed walks ("Castles and lords of the Genevois") that include Chaumont alongside other châtellenies. Free walk around the village; the ruins themselves are visible from marked paths.

Since then the ruins have crumbled, but the site retains a strong atmosphere, perched above the village, with a wide panorama over the Rhône valley, the Vuache range and (on clear days) the Jura.
segments.mt: , podczas wojny o Mantuan sukcesji: wojska królewskie Ludwika XIII zaatakowały i zrównały zamek. Od tego czasu ruiny rozpadły się, ale miejsce zachowuje silną atmosferę, wzniesioną nad wioską, z szeroką panoramą nad doliną Rhône, Vuache zakres i (w dni jasne) Jura.

segments.src: The Heritage Guides of the Pays d'art et d'histoire run themed walks ("Castles and lords of the Genevois") that include Chaumont alongside other châtellenies. Free walk around the village; the ruins themselves are visible from marked paths.
segments.mt: The Heritage Guides of the Pays d 'art et d' histoire run themed walks ("Castles and lords of the Genevois"), które obejmują Chaumont obok innych châtellenies. Wolny spacer po wsi; same ruiny są widoczne z zaznaczonych ścieżek.

src.what_is:
The Bowling de l'Aérodrome is a leisure complex at Annemasse, right next to the local airfield. It features twelve American and French pool tables, a video-games area (pinball, arcade, simulators) and a restaurant paired with a panoramic terrace overlooking Mont Blanc. The venue is easily reached from Geneva and is well suited to family evenings or group outings.

The restaurant and the panoramic terrace are a strong point of the venue, with classic brasserie cuisine and a particularly enjoyable view of the Mont-Blanc range at sunset. The bowling lanes, pool tables and arcade games make the complex a one-stop evening venue, combining game, drink and meal under one roof.

2026 prices: adult €8 per game, child €6. Season: year-round. Hours: Mon–Thu 2pm–1am, Fri–Sat 2pm–3am, Sun 2pm–midnight; school holidays open from noon. Partially accessible for reduced-mobility visitors (ground floor).

src.what_is:
Le Bowl Bowling Annecy is a leisure complex in central Annecy combining 16 bowling lanes, a restaurant and a nightclub. The unusual mix of bowling, dining and club venue under one roof makes it a one-stop evening destination, particularly popular with groups, students and night-out crowds.

The complex is located at 9 boulevard du Fier in Annecy, on the main axis linking the Old Town to the commercial activity zone. It is reachable on foot from the city centre, with free parking along the boulevard, making it accessible for visitors and locals alike.

2026 prices: adult €8 per game, child €6. Season: year-round. Hours: Mon–Thu 2pm–1am, Fri–Sat 2pm–4am, Sun 2pm–midnight. Accessible for reduced-mobility visitors (ground floor).

src.what_is:
The Bowling de Margencel is a leisure venue at Margencel (74200), in the Bas-Chablais. With 20 lanes it is the largest bowling alley near Thonon-les-Bains — only 10 minutes from the town centre — and is easily reached from the whole Bas-Chablais area and the southern shore of Lake Geneva.

The venue includes a traditional restaurant open at lunch and dinner, ideal for family or group evenings combining a meal with several games of bowling. The cuisine is classic brasserie style, designed for everyday dining and group bookings.

2026 prices: adult €8 per game, child €6. Season: year-round. Hours: Mon–Thu 4pm–1am, Fri–Sat 2pm–3am, Sun 2pm–11pm; school holidays open from noon. Accessible for reduced-mobility visitors (ground floor).

src.what_is:
The Bowling International d'Annecy-Sevrier has ten lanes paired with a restaurant. It is set on the route d'Albertville at Sevrier, on the western shore of Lake Annecy. The venue serves as an alternative to Le Bowl Annecy for residents and visitors on the southern half of the lake, particularly around Sevrier and Saint-Jorioz.

The on-site restaurant is a traditional brasserie, well suited to family evenings combining a meal with a few games. A bar and lounge area completes the offer, providing a space to wait between games or to round off the evening.

2026 prices: adult €8, child €6. Season: year-round. Hours: Tue–Thu 4pm–1am, Fri–Sat 4pm–3am, Sun 2pm–11pm; closed Monday. Accessible for reduced-mobility visitors.

src.what_is:
C5 Kids Party is an indoor play park for children aged 0 to 12, located at Ville-la-Grand (Haute-Savoie), a few minutes from Annemasse and the Swiss border, opposite the Leroy Merlin store. Charlie, the venue's mascot, welcomes families across 1,200 m² of covered, air-conditioned and secure space.

The park is divided into two worlds: a zone for ages 0–3 with sensory games, a ball pool, soft modules and a motor-skills course; and a large 4–12 zone with giant slides, suspension bridges, a maze, an adventure course and mini football and basketball pitches. The headline attractions are electric karting, a 15-metre indoor sledge run, arcade games, trampolines, a Power Tower and a zipline.

Simple day-rate pricing: €13 for ages 4–12, €8 for ages 1–3, free for under 1. Accompanying adults €2. Reduced rate for GIA Annemasse holders. Personalised birthday parties available in three packages from €15.90 to €19.90 per child.

src.q: Comment réserver au C5 Kids Party ?
src.a: L'entrée libre au C5 Kids Party ne nécessite pas de réservation — venir aux horaires d'ouverture. Réservation indispensable pour les anniversaires (8-20 enfants, 3 formules) et les sorties scolaires (Mar/Ven hors heures publiques).

src.q: Quel est le tarif du C5 Kids Party ?
src.a: C5 Kids Party : enfant 4-12 ans 13 €, enfant 1-3 ans 8 €, moins de 1 an gratuit, adulte accompagnateur 2 €. Tarif réduit GIA Annemasse sur contremarque : 11 € (4-12 ans), 6 € (1-3 ans), accompagnateur gratuit. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.

src.q: Quelles sont les horaires du C5 Kids Party ?
src.a: C5 Kids Party : toute l'année Mercredi, Samedi, Dimanche 10h–19h. Vacances automne, hiver et jours fériés Mardi-Dimanche 10h–19h. Été 1 juillet-31 août Mardi-Dimanche 10h–18h30. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

src.q: À partir de quel âge le C5 Kids Party est-il adapté ?
src.a: Le C5 Kids Party accueille les enfants dès la naissance jusqu'à 12 ans. Espace dédié 0-3 ans (jeux d'éveil, piscine à balles, modules souples) séparé de la zone 4-12 ans (toboggans géants, karting, luge indoor).

src.q: Le karting électrique du C5 Kids Party est-il inclus ?
src.a: Oui. Le karting électrique du C5 Kids Party est inclus dans le billet d'entrée, sans supplément de jetons (contrairement à d'autres parcs). La piste de luge indoor de 15 m est également incluse. Anniversaires personnalisables avec karting.

src.q: Combien de m² fait le C5 Kids Party ?
src.a: Le C5 Kids Party occupe 1 200 m² couverts, climatisés et sécurisés à Ville-la-Grand. Deux espaces distincts : 0-3 ans (éveil) et 4-12 ans (structures, karting, luge indoor, arcade, mini-terrains).

src.q: Quelle est la différence entre les formules anniversaire C5 Kids Party ?
src.a: Anniversaires C5 Kids Party en 3 formules de 2 h : Kids 15,90 €/enfant (entrée + animation), Kids Premium 17,90 € (avec goûter), Kids Premium Plus 19,90 € (avec gâteau et déco). 8 à 20 enfants, 3 adultes gratuits inclus.

src.q: Comment venir au C5 Kids Party depuis Annemasse ?
src.a: Le C5 Kids Party est à 5 min d'Annemasse Centre par la D903, face à Leroy Merlin Ville-la-Grand. Parking gratuit grande capacité. Bus TAC arrêt 'Leroy Merlin' à proximité, ou taxi depuis la gare SNCF Annemasse (2 km).

src.q: From what age can you go canyoning with Escape Canyon?
src.a: Beginner canyons (such as Angon or Belle-Anne) are accessible from 8-10 years depending on the instructor. Sporty canyons usually require a minimum of 12-14 years and good physical condition. Being able to swim is essential.

src.q: How much does a canyoning outing cost?
src.a: Plan for around €55 to 80 for a half-day and €85 to 110 for a full day, including gear and guiding. Exact 2026 prices to confirm on escape-canyon.com.

src.q: What gear should I bring?
src.a: All technical gear is provided (5 mm neoprene wetsuit, helmet, harness, boots). Bring swimwear, a towel, closed shoes that can get wet, a snack and water.

src.q: Do I need to know how to swim?
src.a: Yes, swimming is essential. Canyoning combines whitewater swimming, jumps and slides into pools. A declaration of ability to swim 25 m is generally required.

src.q: When should I book?
src.a: Booking is very strongly recommended several days ahead in July and August. In June or September, you can sometimes find places more easily, but plan ahead.

src.q: What happens in bad weather?
src.a: In case of storms, floods or unfavourable conditions, the outing can be postponed or cancelled for safety. A reschedule or refund is then offered according to conditions.

src.q: Can I organise a stag or hen party?
src.a: Yes, Escape Canyon offers dedicated stag/hen packages with possible privatisation from 6-8 people. Book several weeks in advance in peak season.

segments.src: From what age can you go canyoning with Escape Canyon?
segments.mt: Od jakiego wieku możesz iść kajakiem z Escape Canyon?

segments.src: Beginner canyons (such as Angon or Belle-Anne) are accessible from 8-10 years depending on the instructor. Sporty canyons usually require a minimum of 12-14 years and good physical condition. Being able to swim is essential.
segments.mt: Kaniony początkujących (takie jak Angon czy Belle- Anne) są dostępne od 8- 10 lat w zależności od instruktora. Sportowe kaniony zwykle wymagają minimum 12- 14 lat i dobrego stanu fizycznego. Zdolność do pływania jest niezbędna.

segments.src: How much does a canyoning outing cost?
Plan for around €55 to 80 for a half-day and €85 to 110 for a full day, including gear and guiding. Exact 2026 prices to confirm on escape-canyon.com.
segments.mt: Plan na około 55-80 EUR na pół dnia i 85-110 EUR na cały dzień, w tym narzędzia i przewodnictwo. Dokładne ceny 2026 do potwierdzenia na escape-canyon.com.

segments.src: All technical gear is provided (5 mm neoprene wetsuit, helmet, harness, boots). Bring swimwear, a towel, closed shoes that can get wet, a snack and water.
segments.mt: Wszystkie narzędzia techniczne są dostępne (5 mm kombinezon neoprenowy, kask, uprząż, buty). Przynieś strój kąpielowy, ręcznik, zamknięte buty, które mogą być mokre, przekąskę i wodę.

segments.src: Do I need to know how to swim?
Yes, swimming is essential. Canyoning combines whitewater swimming, jumps and slides into pools. A declaration of ability to swim 25 m is generally required.
segments.mt: Tak, pływanie jest niezbędne. Kanionizacja łączy pływanie w wodzie, skacze i ślizga się do basenów. Na ogół wymagana jest deklaracja zdolności pływania 25 m.

segments.src: Booking is very strongly recommended several days ahead in July and August. In June or September, you can sometimes find places more easily, but plan ahead.
segments.mt: Rezerwacja jest bardzo mocno zalecane kilka dni przed w lipcu i sierpniu. W czerwcu lub wrześniu, można czasem znaleźć miejsca łatwiej, ale planować przed.
Co się dzieje przy złej pogodzie?

segments.src: In case of storms, floods or unfavourable conditions, the outing can be postponed or cancelled for safety. A reschedule or refund is then offered according to conditions.
segments.mt: W przypadku burz, powodzi lub niekorzystnych warunków wyprawa może zostać odroczona lub odwołana ze względów bezpieczeństwa. Następnie proponuje się zmianę harmonogramu lub zwrot kosztów zgodnie z warunkami.

segments.src: Can I organise a stag or hen party?
segments.mt: Czy mogę zorganizować przyjęcie kawalerskie lub panieńskie?

segments.src: Yes, Escape Canyon offers dedicated stag/hen packages with possible privatisation from 6-8 people. Book several weeks in advance in peak season.
segments.mt: Tak, Escape Canyon oferuje dedykowane pakiety jelenia / kury z możliwością prywatyzacji od 6- 8 osób. Zarezerwuj kilka tygodni wcześniej w sezonie szczytowym.

fields.src: Yaute Canyon — Canyoning Aravis & Annecy | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Yaute Canyon — Canyoning Aravis & Annecy | Loisirs 74

src.title: Discovery canyons
src.description: Family-friendly canyons (Angon, Montmin): optional jumps up to 3 m, natural slides, small abseils. Ideal first experience from age 8-10.

src.title: Sporty canyons
src.description: Technical canyons (Grand Nant, Bionnassay, Aravis canyons) with 20-40 m abseils, 5-8 m jumps, whitewater swims. Sporty level required.

src.title: Half-day
src.description: Half-day format (~3-4 h on site + 1 h approach walk): great alongside other activities. Around €55-65.

src.title: Full day
src.description: Full day with picnic on site: 1 long canyon or 2 canyons in a row. Around €75-85.

src.title: Groups & stag/hen
src.description: Group packages (from 6-8 people), stag/hen parties, corporate team-building. Quote on request, full privatisation possible.

src.title: Multi-day course
src.description: 2-3 day courses to discover several canyons and improve technique (abseil, canyon reading).

src.title: State-qualified instructors
src.description: All instructors hold a DEJEPS or BEES Canyon qualification, guaranteeing safety and a quality experience.

src.title: Equipment supplied
src.description: Full 5 mm wetsuit, harness, helmet, lanyards and neoprene socks. Bring swimwear, towel and canyon shoes or worn-out trainers.

segments.src: Family-friendly canyons (Angon, Montmin): optional jumps up to 3 m, natural slides, small abseils. Ideal first experience from age 8-10.
segments.mt: Kaniony przyjazne rodzinie (Angon, Montmin): opcjonalne skoki do 3 m, naturalne slajdy, małe opaski. Idealne pierwsze doświadczenie od 8- 10 lat.

segments.src: Technical canyons (Grand Nant, Bionnassay, Aravis canyons) with 20-40 m abseils, 5-8 m jumps, whitewater swims. Sporty level required.
segments.mt: Kaniony techniczne (Grand Nant, Bionnatest, Aravis canyons) z 20- 40 m abseils, 5- 8 m skoki, białe wody pływa. Wymagany poziom sportowy.

segments.src: Half-day format (~3-4 h on site + 1 h approach walk): great alongside other activities. Around €55-65.
segments.mt: Format półdobowy (~ 3- 4 h na miejscu + 1 h podejścia): świetnie obok innych działań. Około 55- 65 EUR.

segments.src: Full day with picnic on site: 1 long canyon or 2 canyons in a row. Around €75-85.
segments.mt: Pełny dzień z piknikiem na miejscu: 1 długi kanion lub 2 kaniony z rzędu. Około 75- 85 euro.

segments.src: Group packages (from 6-8 people), stag/hen parties, corporate team-building. Quote on request, full privatisation possible.
segments.mt: Pakiety grupowe (od 6- 8 osób), imprezy jelenia / kur, korporacyjny team- building. Cytować na życzenie, pełna prywatyzacja możliwa.

segments.src: 2-3 day courses to discover several canyons and improve technique (abseil, canyon reading).
segments.mt: 2-3 dniowe kursy odkrywania kilku kanionów i doskonalenia techniki (abseil, czytanie kanionu).

segments.src: All instructors hold a DEJEPS or BEES Canyon qualification, guaranteeing safety and a quality experience.
segments.mt: Wszyscy instruktorzy posiadają kwalifikacje DEJEPS lub BEES Canyon, co gwarantuje bezpieczeństwo i jakość doświadczenia.

segments.src: Full 5 mm wetsuit, harness, helmet, lanyards and neoprene socks. Bring swimwear, towel and canyon shoes or worn-out trainers.
segments.mt: Pełen 5 mm kombinezon, uprząż, kask, smycze i skarpety neoprenowe. Przynieście stroje kąpielowe, ręczniki i buty z kanionu lub treserzy.

Rates not publicly displayed. Information and reservation at 06 83 19 21 18 or by email cascade.aventure@gmail.com. Various packages available: course only, ziplines only, canyoning, combined packages. Verified 14 May 2026.
Recommended, especially for canyoning (mandatory). Reservation by phone at 06 83 19 21 18 or via contact form on official website.

src.q: How do I book at Cascade Aventure?
src.a: Booking at Cascade Aventure is done by phone at 06 83 19 21 18 or via the contact form on the official website cascadeaventure.wixsite.com. Highly recommended in high season, it is mandatory for canyoning.

src.q: What are the rates at Cascade Aventure?
src.a: Cascade Aventure rates are not publicly displayed. To learn the prices for courses, ziplines, canyoning and combined packages, contact the park directly at 06 83 19 21 18. Verified 14 May 2026.

src.q: What are the hours at Cascade Aventure?
src.a: Cascade Aventure is open from 1 July to 31 August, daily from 10am to 6pm, closed Mondays and Tuesdays from 10am to 12pm. Verified on official website 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

src.q: From what age can children visit Cascade Aventure?
src.a: The Fairy Trail at Cascade Aventure is accessible from age 3 (under parental supervision). The main course is accessible from 1.20 m height (adult supervision mandatory for children under 12). Canyoning is accessible from age 8 with an adult.

src.q: Does Cascade Aventure offer canyoning?
src.a: Yes. Cascade Aventure offers guided canyoning in Nyon canyon led by a qualified instructor: jumps, rappels, natural slides. Sport level, reservation required. Wetsuit and equipment provided.

src.q: Is there parking at Cascade Aventure?
src.a: Yes. The Nyon waterfall parking near Cascade Aventure is free. Access via Old Mill Road (563 chemin sous le rocher).

src.q: What is the best time to visit Cascade Aventure?
src.a: The best time is mid-July to mid-August, when the park is open continuously every day and conditions are optimal. The shaded forest setting provides ideal comfort even on hot days.

src.q: Do I need to bring my own equipment to Cascade Aventure?
src.a: No, all safety equipment is provided by Cascade Aventure: harnesses, helmets, continuous safety line for treetop courses. For canyoning, wetsuit, helmet and technical equipment are also provided. Simply bring closed-toe shoes.

src.q: Is access to Cascade d'Angon paid?
src.a: No. Access to Cascade d'Angon is free and open year-round. Only parking is paid May to September (€2/hr, €18/day in 2023 at Vérel and Angon), free off-season.

src.q: How do I get to Cascade d'Angon?
src.a: Three starting points for Cascade d'Angon: from Vérel (10–15 min, shortest access), from Angon via Route des Vignes (30 min to 1 hr, steep climb), or from Talloires (1–1.25 hr, longest). Bus line 20 and F1 shuttle in summer.

src.q: What is the height of Cascade d'Angon?
src.a: The main Cascade d'Angon measures 60 meters in height. There are actually two waterfalls at the site: a smaller one the trail passes beneath, and the main 60 m cascade at the canyon's end.

src.q: Is hiking to Cascade d'Angon difficult?
src.a: The hike to Cascade d'Angon is moderately difficult. The final cliff-side section is exposed, secured by barriers and chains, but the ground can be slippery. Good hiking boots mandatory. Children must be held. Not recommended during rain.

src.q: Can you swim at Cascade d'Angon?
src.a: No, swimming is prohibited at Cascade d'Angon itself. Swimming is possible in the Nant d'Oy at Fairy Bridge level (10–15 min upstream from the waterfall) if flow is not too strong. Swimming recommended at Angon beach on the lake after the hike.

src.q: What is the best season to visit Cascade d'Angon?
src.a: The best season for Cascade d'Angon is spring (April–May) for maximal flow with snowmelt, or summer (June–September) for cool forest cover and canyoning. In winter, the cascade can be frozen, spectacular but access is slippery.

src.q: Is Cascade d'Angon accessible with children?
src.a: Yes, Cascade d'Angon is family-friendly from Vérel (10–15 min, minimal elevation), but the final section requires holding children by the hand at all times. The trail is equipped with barriers and chains. Slippery ground; good boots essential. Angon start steeper, less suitable for young children.

src.q: Is there canyoning at Cascade d'Angon?
src.a: Yes, the Angon canyon is a premier canyoning destination near Annecy. Eight waterfalls total, with main Cascade d'Angon (60 m rappel) as the finale. Accessible with professional guided instruction. Practiced in summer; Ice Canyoning possible in winter with appropriate gear.

src.what_is:
The Cascade de Chedde, also known as the Heart Cascade, is a natural waterfall located in the commune of Passy, in the hamlet of Chedde, at approximately 660 m elevation. It is formed by the Ugine torrent (locally called the Nant de l'Ugine), which emerges from the slopes of Platé mountain. A rock at the summit divides the waters into two branches that rejoin at the base, creating a characteristic heart silhouette.

Since the mid-18th century, the cascade has been a popular stop for travelers journeying from Geneva to Chamonix. Tourists organized their departures to arrive at sunrise, when light painted a rainbow in the water mist. The site once powered nearby mills and sawmills. Today, a discreet small hydroelectric plant is integrated at the top of the cascade. The site was designated a Natural Site of France in June 2024.

The cascade is accessible in 5 to 10 minutes' walk from the Parc Biolley parking area in Chedde, via a maintained trail leading to an observation platform. A second unmarked trail allows visitors to approach even closer to the waterfall. The flow is particularly spectacular after heavy rain or in spring during snowmelt.

segments.src: , is a natural waterfall located in the commune of Passy, in the hamlet of Chedde, at approximately 660 m elevation. It is formed by the Ugine torrent (locally called the Nant de l'Ugine), which emerges from the slopes of Platé mountain. A rock at the summit divides the waters into two branches that rejoin at the base, creating a characteristic heart silhouette.
segments.mt: , jest naturalnym wodospadem położonym w gminie Passy, w wiosce Chedde, na wysokości około 660 m. Tworzy go torrent Ugine (nazywany miejscowo Nant de l 'Ugine), który wyłania się ze zboczy Platé. Skała na szczycie dzieli wody na dwie gałęzie, które łączą się z bazą, tworząc charakterystyczną sylwetkę serca.

segments.src: Since the mid-18th century, the cascade has been a popular stop for travelers journeying from Geneva to Chamonix. Tourists organized their departures to arrive at sunrise, when light painted a rainbow in the water mist. The site once powered nearby mills and sawmills. Today, a discreet small hydroelectric plant is integrated at the top of the cascade. The site was designated a Natural Site of France in June 2024.
segments.mt: Od połowy XVIII wieku kaskada jest popularnym przystankiem dla podróżujących z Genewy do Chamonix. Turyści zorganizowali swoje wyjścia, aby dotrzeć o wschodzie słońca, kiedy światło malowało tęczę w mgle wodnej. Miejsce kiedyś zasiliło pobliskie młyny i tartaki. Obecnie na szczycie kaskady zintegrowana jest dyskretna mała hydroelektrownia. Strona została wyznaczona jako Natural Site of France w czerwcu 2024 roku.

segments.src: The cascade is accessible in 5 to 10 minutes' walk from the Parc Biolley parking area in Chedde, via a maintained trail leading to an observation platform. A second unmarked trail allows visitors to approach even closer to the waterfall. The flow is particularly spectacular after heavy rain or in spring during snowmelt.
segments.mt: Kaskada jest dostępna w odległości od 5 do 10 minut spacerem od parkingu Parc Biolley w Chedde, przez utrzymany szlak prowadzący do platformy obserwacyjnej. Drugi nieoznakowany szlak pozwala odwiedzającym zbliżyć się jeszcze bliżej wodospadu. Przepływ jest szczególnie spektakularny po silnym deszczu lub wiosną podczas roztopu śniegu.

src.q: Is access to Cascade de Chedde free?
src.a: Yes. Access to Cascade de Chedde is free and open year-round. Parking at Parc Biolley in Chedde is also free.

src.q: How do I reach Cascade de Chedde?
src.a: From the free Parc Biolley parking on Route des Soudans (D13) in Chedde, follow the maintained trail for 5 to 10 minutes. A platform allows you to admire the heart-shaped Cascade de Chedde.

src.q: What is the best season to visit Cascade de Chedde?
src.a: Spring (April–June) is ideal for Cascade de Chedde: flow is maximum thanks to snowmelt and the heart shape is spectacular. The site is also impressive after heavy rain.

src.q: Are dogs allowed at Cascade de Chedde?
src.a: Yes. Dogs are allowed on the trail to Cascade de Chedde, provided they remain on leash. The site has been a designated Natural Site of France since 2024; environmental regulations apply.

src.q: Is Cascade de Chedde accessible with a stroller?
src.a: No. The trail to Cascade de Chedde has elevation change and uneven surface that does not permit stroller or wheelchair access.

src.q: How much time should I allow to visit Cascade de Chedde?
src.a: Plan about 30 minutes round trip from Parc Biolley parking to the Cascade de Chedde platform, including viewing time. The unmarked trail adds 10–15 minutes.

src.q: Why is Cascade de Chedde called the Heart Cascade?
src.a: A rock at the top of Cascade de Chedde divides the waters of the Ugine torrent into two branches that form a heart when they rejoin at the base. This distinctive silhouette earned it the Heart Cascade nickname.

src.q: Is there a tourist history associated with Cascade de Chedde?
src.a: Yes. Since the 18th century, Cascade de Chedde has been a popular stop for travelers journeying from Geneva to Chamonix. They organized departures to arrive at sunrise and admire the rainbow in the mist.

segments.mt: Z bezpłatnego Parc Biolley parking na Route des Soudans (D13) w Chedde, śledzić utrzymany szlak przez 5 do 10 minut. Platforma pozwala podziwiać Cascade de Chedde w kształcie serca.

segments.src: What is the best season to visit Cascade de Chedde?
segments.mt: Jaki jest najlepszy sezon na wizytę w Cascade de Chedde?

segments.src: Spring (April–June) is ideal for Cascade de Chedde: flow is maximum thanks to snowmelt and the heart shape is spectacular. The site is also impressive after heavy rain.
segments.mt: Wiosna (April- June) jest idealnym rozwiązaniem dla Cascade de Chedde: przepływ jest maksymalny dzięki roztopie śniegu i kształt serca jest spektakularny. Strona jest również imponujące po silnym deszczu.

segments.src: Are dogs allowed at Cascade de Chedde?
segments.mt: Czy psy są dozwolone w Cascade de Chedde?

segments.src: Yes. Dogs are allowed on the trail to Cascade de Chedde, provided they remain on leash. The site has been a designated Natural Site of France since 2024; environmental regulations apply.
segments.mt: Tak. Psy są dozwolone na szlaku do Cascade de Chedde, pod warunkiem, że pozostają na smyczy. Od 2024 r. strona jest wyznaczona jako Natural Site of France; obowiązują przepisy środowiskowe.

segments.src: Is Cascade de Chedde accessible with a stroller?
segments.mt: Czy Cascade de Chedde jest dostępny z wózkiem?

segments.src: No. The trail to Cascade de Chedde has elevation change and uneven surface that does not permit stroller or wheelchair access.
segments.mt: Nie. Ścieżka do Cascade de Chedde ma zmianę wysokości i nierówną powierzchnię, która nie pozwala wózka lub wózka inwalidzkiego.

segments.src: How much time should I allow to visit Cascade de Chedde?
segments.mt: Ile czasu powinienem poświęcić na wizytę w Cascade de Chedde?

segments.src: Plan about 30 minutes round trip from Parc Biolley parking to the Cascade de Chedde platform, including viewing time. The unmarked trail adds 10–15 minutes.
segments.mt: Zaplanuj około 30 minut jazdy z Parc Biolley do platformy Cascade de Chedde, w tym czasu oglądania. Nieoznakowany ślad dodaje 10- 15 minut.

segments.src: Why is Cascade de Chedde called the Heart Cascade?
segments.mt: Dlaczego Cascade de Chedde nazywa się Kaskada Serca?

segments.src: A rock at the top of Cascade de Chedde divides the waters of the Ugine torrent into two branches that form a heart when they rejoin at the base. This distinctive silhouette earned it the Heart Cascade nickname.
segments.mt: Skała na szczycie Cascade de Chedde dzieli wody torrentu Ugine na dwie gałęzie, które tworzą serce, gdy dołączają do bazy. Ta charakterystyczna sylwetka zasłużyła na przezwisko Heart Cascade.

segments.src: Is there a tourist history associated with Cascade de Chedde?
segments.mt: Czy istnieje historia turystyczna związana z Cascade de Chedde?

segments.src: Yes. Since the 18th century, Cascade de Chedde has been a popular stop for travelers journeying from Geneva to Chamonix. They organized departures to arrive at sunrise and admire the rainbow in the mist.
segments.mt: Tak. Od XVIII wieku Cascade de Chedde jest popularnym przystankiem dla podróżujących z Genewy do Chamonix. Zorganizowali wyjazd o wschodzie słońca i podziwiali tęczę we mgle.

src.what_is:
The Cascade du Chinaillon, also called the Mysterious Cascade, is a natural waterfall located in the Aravis massif, at approximately 1,164 meters altitude above the Grand-Bornand Chinaillon resort. Fed by the Nant du Chinaillon, a tributary of the Borne, it plunges some thirty meters down a narrow gorge carved out by water. The site is accessible in about twenty minutes' walk from the main Chinaillon road, on a marked trail crossing meadows and beech forest.

The "mysterious" name of the waterfall originates from a local legend: it was at the foot of this waterfall that the first inhabitants of Grand-Bornand are said to have met, giving birth to the "Bornandins". This story passed down through generations adds a touch of poetry and enigma to this already impressive natural site. In spring, when snowmelt swells the torrent's flow, the sight is particularly striking. In autumn, the flamboyant colours of the surrounding beech forest add a different charm to the walk.

The trail first leads to the most spectacular part of the waterfall, then descends steeply towards the Chinaillon torrent where you discover a wild gorge. The terrain can be muddy and slippery — good hiking boots are essential. This easy and refreshing walk, ideal for families, also allows you to discover old Chinaillon with its typical alpine chalets and panoramic views over the Aravis range.

The site is accessible in about twenty minutes' walk from the main Chinaillon road, on a marked trail crossing meadows and beech forest.
segments.mt: , jest naturalnym wodospadem położonym w Masywie Aravisa, na wysokości około 1,164 metra nad ośrodkiem Grand- Bornand Chinaillon. Karmiony przez Nant du Chinaillon, dopływ Borne, zanurza się około 30 metrów w wąskim wąskim wąwozie wyrzeźbionym przez wodę. Obiekt jest dostępny w odległości około dwudziestu minut spacerem od głównej drogi chinaillońskiej, na szlaku łączącym łąki i las bukowy.

src.what_is:
The Casino Évian Resort, at Évian-les-Bains, is a historic casino founded in the late 19th century in the Belle Époque style, set on the shore of Lake Geneva at Quai Charles-Albert-Besson. Its exceptional architectural heritage is an integral part of the town's spa identity, and it houses both slot machines and gaming tables in a prestigious setting.

The casino is part of the Évian Resort group (formerly Royal Resort), a key heritage destination on the French side of Lake Geneva. The slot floor and table games occupy elegant Belle-Époque rooms that double as venues for events and entertainment throughout the year.

2026 prices: adult free entry, ages 18+ with ID; under-18s not admitted. Season: year-round. Hours: slot machine room 10am–3am; table game room 8.30pm–3am. Accessible for reduced-mobility visitors.

src.what_is:
The Casino de Saint-Julien-en-Genevois is a gaming establishment on the route d'Annecy, at the gates of Geneva. Its border location makes it one of the most accessible casinos from the Geneva metro area — less than 10 minutes by car. The venue combines slot floor, table-game lounge, poker room and three restaurants and bars, making it a full evening out, not only a destination for players.

The slot floor has 112 machines: 11 video pokers, 13 classic reels and 88 video reels from international brands. Slot hours: Sunday–Thursday 10 am–2 am, Friday–Saturday 10 am–3 am. The table-game lounge opens every evening from 8 pm: 2 blackjack tables, 3 traditional English roulettes, 4 Texas Hold'em poker tables and 15 electronic English roulettes.

Three on-site restaurants (gastronomic, brasserie and bar) complete the offer. Free parking. ID required (over 18, no exclusion order).

src.what_is:
The Cluses Intercommunal Aquatic Centre is the indoor swimming venue of the Cluses Arve et montagnes community (2CCAM). On rue Carnot, in central Cluses, it has a 25 m sports pool, a 15 × 15 m learning pool, a kids' paddling pool (1-5 yo) and a tube slide with landing pool.

It hosts schools, families, swimmers, clubs and learn-to-swim programmes. 2026 prices are affordable: €5 adult, €4.50 child 8-17 or student, €2.50 child 3-7, free under 3. 24-entry cards (€95 adult / €80 young / €45 kids 3-7) and annual cards (€180 adult / €105 young) make regular swimming cheaper.

Open 2 September 2025 to 30 June 2026, every day 11.50 am to 8 pm (some lanes reserved for schools/clubs during the day). Great for a rainy-day family outing, after school or as part of a Cluses stopover. Tel. 04 50 98 20 50.

fields.src: Forme d'O Châtel — Aquatic centre & spa in the resort | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Forme d'O Châtel — Aquatic centre & spa in the resort | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Forme d'O Châtel - Centrum Wodne & spa w ośrodku 124; Loisirs 74

src.what_is:
Forme d'O is the aquatic centre and spa of the Châtel resort (74390), in the Portes du Soleil ski area. The modern, light-filled building combines an aquatic area with swimming pool, kids' pool, massage jets and short slide depending on the configuration, plus a wellness area with sauna, hammam, jacuzzi and relaxation zone. Ideal alongside a ski day in winter, to cool off in summer and year-round for a relaxation break.

Who is it for? Forme d'O welcomes families with children, holidaymakers staying in Châtel or the Vallée d'Abondance, athletes (regular swimming, hydrotherapy) and wellness fans. The aquatic area is open to all, while the spa area (sauna/hammam/jacuzzi) is generally reserved for adults (16+). Aquagym and aquabike classes are sometimes offered depending on the season.

Practical info and access. No booking required for aquatic access (spa may require booking depending on demand). Expect €7-15 for aquatic entry by age and duration, €25-40 for the aquatic + spa pack. Extended hours in winter (school holidays) and reduced in mid-season. Changing rooms, showers, lockers, snack bar on site. The centre is reachable on foot from the Châtel centre, by shuttle or by car (resort car parks nearby).

src.q: Where is Forme d'O Châtel?
src.a: Forme d'O is in the heart of the Châtel resort (74390), reachable on foot from the centre, by resort shuttle or by car (resort car parks nearby).

src.q: What facilities does Forme d'O offer?
src.a: The centre combines a swimming pool, kids' pool, massage jets, plus a wellness area with sauna, hammam, jacuzzi and relaxation zone.

src.q: How much is entry?
src.a: Expect €7-15 for aquatic entry by age and duration, €25-40 for the aquatic + spa pack. Resident/holidaymaker special rates, passes available. Exact 2026 prices to confirm.

src.q: From what age?
src.a: The aquatic area is open to all (accompanied children). The spa area (sauna/hammam/jacuzzi) is generally reserved for adults from age 16.

src.q: Do I need to book?
src.a: Not required for aquatic access. For the spa area, booking may be needed depending on demand (especially in high season).

src.q: When is it best to go?
src.a: Year-round, with peaks in winter (ski holidays) and summer (cool-down after hiking). Prefer morning or end of day to avoid crowds.

src.q: Are there aquagym classes?
src.a: Yes, aquagym and aquabike classes are sometimes offered depending on the seasonal programme. Sign up at the counter or online. Programme on chatel-formedo.com.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the aquatic centre?
src.a: The centre is open year-round. Outside Zone A school holidays: Mon-Tue-Thu-Fri 8:30 am-8 pm (25 m pool closed to the public 1:45-4:15 pm for schools), Wed 10:30 am-8 pm, Sat 9 am-12 pm & 2 pm-6 pm, Sun 9 am-12:15 pm. Extended hours during holidays. Last admission 45 min before closing. Check current hours on the official site.

src.q: What are the entry prices?
src.a: Adult (18-64): €5.50. Child (5-17): €4.50. Seniors 65+, students and people with reduced mobility: €5. Free for under-5s. Wellness area (sauna-hammam, 18+): €10 extra. Subscriptions available: 10+1 entry card, 20+3 card, annual pass. Group rate from 10 people.

src.q: Can we bring young children?
src.a: Yes, the centre has a dedicated paddling pool for toddlers and offers baby-swimmer sessions from 6 months. Free for under-5s. In summer, the outdoor area offers a fun splashpad suitable for young children. Changing rooms with baby-changing tables.

src.q: Are bathing shorts allowed?
src.a: No, for hygiene reasons, only close-fitting swimwear (briefs, swim trunks) is allowed. Beach shorts, bermudas and boxers are not permitted. Swim cap is not required.

src.q: Is there a wellness area?
src.a: Yes, an adults-only wellness area (18+) with sauna, hammam and hydromassage showers. Access: €10 extra. Capacity limited to 20 people at a time. Direct access to the pools from the wellness area.

src.q: Is the aquatic centre accessible by public transport?
src.a: Yes, the Sallanches-Combloux-Megève SNCF station is 400 m away (5 min walk). Annecy-Saint-Gervais regional rail link. SAT Autocars Mont-Blanc buses serve the Gare and Charly Mottet stops. Free parking on site if you come by car.

src.q: What activities are offered at the aquatic centre?
src.a: Free swimming, swimming lessons at all levels (children and adults), baby swimmers, aquagym, aqua-power, aqua-mix, aquabike, aquatic playground, teen first-aid lessons, free-access aquafitness area. Detailed schedule and sign-up on the official site or on +33 4 50 58 12 32.

fields.src: Cité de l'Eau Publier — Pools, slides & wellness | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Cité de l'Eau Publier — Pools, slides & wellness | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Cité de l 'Eau Publier - Pools, suwaki i wellness

src.what_is:
The Cité de l'Eau aquatic centre in Publier-Amphion is the inter-municipal water complex of the Pays d'Évian Vallée d'Abondance community. Set just steps from Lake Geneva, it offers more than 4,500 m² of facilities: a 25m six-lane sports pool, a fun pool, a paddling pool for toddlers, slides, a current river and a wellness area with sauna and hammam. It is one of the main indoor aquatic facilities of the Évian area, used by both locals and holidaymakers.

Activities and audiences. The centre welcomes serious swimmers (lanes reserved early morning and at certain times), families with children (fun area, paddling pool, slides), seniors (aquagym, dedicated slots) and school groups. Programme of coached activities: aquagym, aquabike, swimming school, parent-and-baby classes, water garden. The wellness area (sauna, hammam, sensory showers) is available with an extra fee and open to over-16s. Lifeguards are present at all times around the pools.

Good to know. The centre is open year-round, with extended hours during school holidays. Public prices are around €5-8 for adults and €3-5 for children; 10-entry cards, monthly/annual passes and reduced rates are available. Swim caps compulsory, soapy shower before swimming. Free parking, bus access from Évian and Thonon. Perfect for an indoor activity alongside a day by the lake.

segments.src: is the inter-municipal water complex of the Pays d'Évian Vallée d'Abondance community. Set just steps from Lake Geneva, it offers more than 4,500 m² of facilities: a 25m six-lane sports pool, a fun pool, a paddling pool for toddlers, slides, a current river and a wellness area with sauna and hammam. It is one of the main indoor aquatic facilities of the Évian area, used by both locals and holidaymakers.
segments.mt: jest międzygminnym kompleksem wodnym społeczności Pays d 'Évian Vallée d' Abondance. Zaledwie kilka kroków od Jeziora Genewskiego oferuje ponad 4,500 m ² obiektów: basen sportowy o długości 25 m, basen rozrywkowy, basen dla dzieci, zjeżdżalnie, bieżącą rzekę oraz centrum odnowy biologicznej z sauną i hammamem. Jest to jeden z głównych wewnętrznych obiektów wodnych obszaru Évian, używanych zarówno przez miejscowych, jak i urlopowiczów.

segments.src: The centre welcomes serious swimmers (lanes reserved early morning and at certain times), families with children (fun area, paddling pool, slides), seniors (aquagym, dedicated slots) and school groups. Programme of coached activities: aquagym, aquabike, swimming school, parent-and-baby classes, water garden. The wellness area (sauna, hammam, sensory showers) is available with an extra fee and open to over-16s. Lifeguards are present at all times around the pools.
segments.mt: Centrum wita poważnych pływaków (pasy zarezerwowane wcześnie rano i w pewnych czasach), rodzin z dziećmi (plac zabaw, basen wiosłowania, zjeżdżalnie), seniorów (aquagym, dedykowane szczeliny) i grup szkolnych. Program zajęć dydaktycznych: aquagym, aquabike, szkoła pływania, zajęcia wychowawcze, ogród wodny. Obszar wellness (sauna, łaźnia, prysznice sensoryczne) jest dostępny za dodatkową opłatą i otwarty na ponad 16 osób. Strażnicy są obecni przez cały czas wokół basenów.

segments.src: The centre is open year-round, with extended hours during school holidays. Public prices are around €5-8 for adults and €3-5 for children; 10-entry cards, monthly/annual passes and reduced rates are available. Swim caps compulsory, soapy shower before swimming. Free parking, bus access from Évian and Thonon. Perfect for an indoor activity alongside a day by the lake.
segments.mt: Centrum jest otwarte przez cały rok, z wydłużonymi godzinami w czasie wakacji szkolnych. Ceny publiczne wynoszą około 5-8 EUR dla dorosłych i 3-5 EUR dla dzieci; dostępne są 10-karty wstępu, miesięczne / roczne przepustki i obniżone stawki. Swim czapki obowiązkowe, mydło prysznic przed pływaniem. Bezpłatny parking, dojazd autobusem z Évian i Thonon. Idealny do aktywności wewnątrz obok dnia nad jeziorem.

src.q: What pools are available?
src.a: The centre has a 25m six-lane sports pool, a fun pool with current river and massage jets, a paddling pool for toddlers and water slides.

src.q: How much is admission?
src.a: Adult admission is around €5-8, child €3-5, free for under-4s. The wellness area (sauna/hammam) is extra. 10-entry cards and annual passes available. 2026 prices to confirm on the website.

src.q: Are swim caps required?
src.a: Yes, swim caps are compulsory in all pools. They can be bought at reception. A soapy shower before swimming is also compulsory.

src.q: Is there a wellness area?
src.a: Yes, the centre has a wellness area with sauna, hammam and sensory showers, available with an extra fee. Reserved for teens and adults from age 16.

src.q: Can I enrol my children in lessons?
src.a: Yes, the swim school offers group or private lessons for children and adults (beginners, improvers). Baby swim and water garden for the youngest. Online or on-site enrolment.

src.q: Is the centre wheelchair-accessible?
src.a: Yes, the facility is wheelchair-accessible: equipped changing rooms and pool access. A pool hoist is available on request.

src.q: How to get there without a car?
src.a: The centre is served by CCPEVA bus lines from Évian and Thonon. The Lake Geneva cycle greenway runs nearby — ideal by bike from Évian-Amphion.

src.q: What are the opening hours of Centre Nautique Guy Châtel?
src.a: The centre is open year-round with different time slots depending on day and period (schools, clubs, public). The weekly schedule is published on the Bonneville town-hall website. An annual maintenance closure usually takes place in summer or autumn.

src.q: How much does entry cost?
src.a: Adult entry is around €3-6, with reduced rates for children, students and CCFG residents. Ticket books of 10 and quarterly/annual passes are available. 2026 prices to be confirmed at the town hall.

src.q: Is a swim cap mandatory?
src.a: Yes, a swim cap is mandatory in the sports pool. It is strongly recommended in the other pools. Caps are sold at reception if needed.

src.q: Is the outdoor area open all year?
src.a: No, the outdoor area (leisure pool, slide, solarium) is open only in summer, usually from June to late August depending on weather. The three indoor pools are open the rest of the year.

src.q: Are there swimming lessons?
src.a: Yes: lessons for children and adults, aqua-gym, aqua-bike, baby swim. Registration is at the town hall or directly at the centre's reception at the start of each term.

src.q: Is the centre wheelchair-accessible?
src.a: Yes, the centre has adapted changing rooms and a pool hoist for people with reduced mobility. Contact reception in advance to prepare your visit.

src.q: Is there a car park?
src.a: Yes, a large free car park is available on site, next to the entrance of the aquatic centre.

segments.src: What are the opening hours of Centre Nautique Guy Châtel?

segments.src: The centre is open year-round with different time slots depending on day and period (schools, clubs, public). The weekly schedule is published on the Bonneville town-hall website. An annual maintenance closure usually takes place in summer or autumn.

src.what_is:
The Château d'Avully at Brenthonne (74890) — also known as the Château Saint-Michel d'Avully, after the seigneurial family who held it from the 13th to the 18th century — is one of the most affecting castles in the Chablais. A fortified manor of the 13th–14th centuries, strengthened in the 14th on the order of the Count of Savoy, it lived through the Protestant Reformation, was confiscated at the Revolution and gradually fell into ruin in the 19th century. Listed as a Monument Historique in 1974 (Mérimée notice PA00118354).

Since 1971, the Guyon family has carried out an exceptional heritage restoration that continues to this day, over more than 50 years. The water-filled moat has been cleared, the drawbridge rebuilt using medieval techniques, the keep restored, and rare medieval wall paintings rediscovered and conserved. 18th-century-style gardens have been recreated from period plans and several rooms refitted for events. The work has been awarded several heritage prizes.

2026 prices: adult €10, student/jobseeker €8, child 5–12 €5, free under 5. Guided tour supplement €2. Medieval escape game €18/person, seasonal and by booking. 2026 opening: visits all year by appointment; from 1 July to 31 August, Monday–Friday 1.30pm–6pm without appointment, weekends by booking. Bed-and-breakfast and venue hire available. Summer festivals (early music, medieval theatre). Partial wheelchair access. Free parking.

src.what_is:
The Château de Beauregard at Saint-Jeoire-en-Faucigny (74490) is one of the most evocative fortified manors of the Faucigny. A 12th–13th-century fortified manor, it was owned by the La Fléchère family for 8 centuries — an exceptional case of family continuity in Savoyard heritage. The original rectangular keep, with walls 1.20 metres thick, is typical of Faucigny Savoyard military architecture.

The castle had an eventful history: burnt in 1589 during the Bernese invasion (Reformation), restored in the 17th–18th centuries; burnt again during the French Revolution, restored in the 19th. Given to the diocese of Annecy in 2004, the château was entrusted in 2008 to the Fraternité Eucharistein, a mixed Catholic community founded in 1973 (monastic life, spiritual guesthouse, retreats). The interior cannot be visited outside the European Heritage Days in mid-September.

The standout feature is that the château is illuminated at night — a lighting scheme that highlights the walls, the keep and the approaches, visible from the D907 road and the village of Saint-Jeoire. It is one of the few castles in Haute-Savoie to offer a free, unsupervised night-time approach, particularly striking in winter. Free admission to the surroundings year-round; interior only during the European Heritage Days. Car recommended (no direct public transport), A40 Bonneville exit 15 minutes away. Leashed pets welcome.

src.what_is:
The Château de Bellegarde at Thonon-les-Bains (74200) is the last surviving fragment of the town's medieval fortifications. Originally a square tower set into the city walls, it once guarded the Porte des Lombards, the northern gate of the fortified medieval town. It stands centrally on the Place de l'Hôtel de Ville, two minutes' walk from the Tourist Office, the Musée du Chablais (Château de Sonnaz) and the Port de Rives.

The name 'Bellegarde' comes from Janus Bellegarde, syndic and secretary of the syndic of Thonon in the 16th century, who lived in the tower. The château today houses Thonon's law courts (district court and commercial court), so the interior is not open outside the European Heritage Days in mid-September. Those days are also the only chance to see the castle's hidden treasure: remarkable 19th-century Rabelaisian friezes in carved wood — bawdy, comic scenes inspired by Rabelais, an exceptional example of heritage wood carving in the region.

Free admission to the exterior year-round; interior only during the European Heritage Days. The site is in the pedestrian zone, with town-centre car parks nearby. Star't bus lines A and B stop 'Hôtel de Ville' is 2 minutes away, with Thonon Léman Express station 1 km off. A natural combination: the exterior of the Château de Bellegarde, the Musée du Chablais and the Château de Ripaille make a full heritage day in Thonon.

src.lead: 15th-century fortified manor perched on a natural terrace at Lully (74): trapezoidal main building flanked by four round pepper-pot towers, traces of Genevan cannonballs (1590), listed as a Historic Monument in 1944.

segments.mt: Piętnastowieczny pałac ufortyfikowany na naturalnym tarasie Lully (74): główny budynek trapezoidalny otoczony czterema okrągłymi wieżami pepperowymi, ślady armatnich kul genewskich (1590), wymienionych jako zabytek historyczny w 1944 roku.

src.what_is:
The Château de Buffavent is a 15th-century fortified manor set on a natural terrace about 800 metres north-west of the village of Lully (74890), in the Chablais. Its evocative name reportedly comes from "bouffâ" — to blow in Savoyard dialect — pointing to a wind-exposed site. The trapezoidal main building is about 18.6 m long by 15.8 m wide, with four round corner towers crowned by conical "pepper-pot" roofs, characteristic of 15th-century Savoyard military architecture.

The castle was built by the lords of Langin as a fortified hunting residence, during the expansion phase that peaked in the 15th–16th centuries. In 1590, Genevan soldiers attacked the castle during the wars between Savoy and Genevan Protestantism: the arrow slits of the left tower still bear traces of cannon fire.

The château is private property, not open to the public, but is easily visible from the surrounding paths around Lully. Listed as a Monument Historique in 1944.

src.q: Can you visit the inside of the Château de Buffavent?
src.a: No, the Château de Buffavent is a private property closed to interior visits. The silhouette of the fortified manor and its four pepper-pot towers are clearly visible from the road and the local paths of Lully.

src.q: Where is the Château de Buffavent?
src.a: The Château de Buffavent stands about 800 m north-west of the village of Lully (74890), in the Bas-Chablais, 15 km from Thonon-les-Bains. GPS: 46.288° N · 6.406° E.

src.q: What is the history of the Château de Buffavent?
src.a: The Château de Buffavent is a 15th-century fortified manor built by the lords of Langin as a fortified hunting residence. Attacked and taken by the Genevans in 1590 (cannonball marks visible), it was listed as a Historic Monument in 1944.

src.q: How much does it cost to visit the Château de Buffavent?
src.a: Looking at the Château de Buffavent from outside is free from the public road. There is no interior visit, as the castle is privately owned. Checked on 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Château de Buffavent?
src.a: The exterior view of the Château de Buffavent is freely accessible year-round, 24/7, from the road and the local paths. No scheduled interior visit. Checked on 10 June 2026.

src.q: How long should I allow to see the Château de Buffavent?
src.a: Allow 15 to 30 minutes to admire the Château de Buffavent from outside, photograph the four pepper-pot towers and read about its history. Longer if you combine it with a country walk.

src.q: Where can I park to see the Château de Buffavent?
src.a: Free parking in the village of Lully. The Château de Buffavent is then reachable on foot via the road or the local paths, about 800 m from the village.

src.q: What else is there to see around the Château de Buffavent?
src.a: Around the Château de Buffavent, you can discover the Château d'Avully at Brenthonne (5 km away), the villages of the Bas-Chablais, and the paths leading to Thonon-les-Bains and Lake Geneva.

src.what_is:
The Château de Clermont at Clermont-en-Genevois (74270) is a little-known heritage gem: a rare example of Italian Renaissance architecture in the former Duchy of Savoy. Built between 1576 and 1580 by Monsignor Gallois de Regard (1512–1582), bishop of Bagnorea in Italy and chamberlain to Pope Paul IV, it draws directly on the Palazzo Farnese at Caprarola which he knew from his time in Italy. Listed as a Monument Historique in 1950 (façades and inner courtyard), with further protection in 1988 (interiors and roofs); owned by the Haute-Savoie Departmental Council since 1949.

The inner courtyard is the marvel of the Château de Clermont: three superposed levels of arcaded galleries in the Italian manner, with carved foliate ornament, friezes and mascarons and monumental staircases. This architectural treatment is unparalleled in Haute-Savoie and Savoy, where most local castles are either reworked medieval structures or 19th-century Neo-Gothic. Archaeological work is also ongoing near the château on the 13th-century medieval castle it succeeded.

2026 prices are exceptionally affordable: adult €3, reduced €2 (students, jobseekers, Pass'Région), free under 8 plus free on the first Sunday of the month and during the European Heritage Days in mid-September, school groups €1.50. Cultural season from 4 April to 1 November 2026: weekends and school holidays outside summer, Tuesday to Sunday in July and August, closed Monday (except JEP). Heat-wave closures may apply. Partially wheelchair accessible (courtyard and ground floor only). Free parking. One of the most affordable Renaissance châteaux in France for its heritage value.

src.what_is:
The Château Comtal de Ternier is one of the two medieval remnants crowning the hill of Ternier, a hamlet of the commune of Saint-Julien-en-Genevois (74160), about two kilometres south of the town centre. Built in the 13th century, it was the seat of the Châtellenie de Ternier, an important seigneury controlling the territories south of Geneva, between the Arve and the Laire.

The fortress originally belonged to the Ternier family, an influential lineage that sometimes rivalled the Counts of Geneva. The castle was then ceded to the Counts and became a comital residence and administrative seat — hence the name "Comtal". Not far away stood another fortification, La Poype, owned by the Ternier family proper: the two castles were separated by a mound and two defensive ditches still readable in the landscape.

The site is accessible by marked paths from the Ternier hamlet. Walls and earthworks visible; free access.

src.q: What is the Counts' Castle of Ternier?
src.a: The Counts' Castle of Ternier is one of the two medieval remains on the hill of Ternier at Saint-Julien-en-Genevois. It was the seat of the châtellenie of Ternier, ceded to the Counts of Geneva and then to the Counts of Savoy. Today: wall sections and ditches in open access.

src.q: Where is the Counts' Castle of Ternier?
src.a: The Counts' Castle of Ternier is on the hill of Ternier, a hamlet under the commune of Saint-Julien-en-Genevois (74160), about 2 km south of the town centre, in the Pays de Genève. GPS: 46.139° N · 6.089° E.

src.q: What is the history of the Counts' Castle of Ternier?
src.a: The Counts' Castle of Ternier dates from the 13th century. It originally belonged to the Ternier family before being ceded to the Counts of Geneva and then to the Counts of Savoy. Damaged by the Bernese and Savoyard wars, it was gradually abandoned.

src.q: How much does it cost to visit the Counts' Castle of Ternier?
src.a: Visiting the Counts' Castle of Ternier is entirely free: remains in open access on the hill. No ticket desk, no fixed hours. Checked on 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Counts' Castle of Ternier?
src.a: The Counts' Castle of Ternier site is open 24/7, year-round. Daytime is preferable to read the remains and enjoy the panorama. Checked on 10 June 2026.

src.q: What is the difference between the Counts' Castle and La Poype de Ternier?
src.a: The Counts' Castle of Ternier and La Poype are two fortified structures next to each other on the same hill. The Counts' Castle was a comital residence; La Poype was a fortified manor of the Ternier family. The two were separated by a mound and two ditches.

src.q: How long should I allow for the Counts' Castle of Ternier?
src.a: Allow 30 to 45 minutes to discover the remains of the Counts' Castle of Ternier; 1 hr to 1 hr 30 for a circuit combined with the neighbouring Poype de Ternier.

src.q: How do I get to the Counts' Castle of Ternier?
src.a: From Saint-Julien-en-Genevois, the Counts' Castle of Ternier is reachable on foot or by bike (≈ 2 km) via rural lanes. By car, free parking in the hamlet of Ternier. No direct public transport.

src.what_is:
The Château de la Croix is a historic fortified manor that overlooks the Arve valley from the commune of Scionzier (74950), a few kilometres downstream from Cluses, in the Faucigny. While local tradition mentions a medieval 12th-century core, the building as it stands today is essentially a 16th-century construction, built by the lords of La Croix, who later allied by marriage with the Rochette family. The castle's history is closely tied to that of the Faucigny and its noble lines.

The château preserves several remarkable decorative features: 17th-century wall paintings — exceptional in the region — and notably an 1840 panoramic wallpaper in one of the salons, a rare piece of 19th-century classical interior décor. Several parts of the castle were listed as Monument Historique in 1988, then more broadly in 1994.

The château is privately owned and not open to the public, but is clearly visible from the road through Scionzier.

segments.mt: Zewnętrzna wizyta w Château de la Croix łączy się z odkryciem wioski Scionzier i jej kościoła - mały, ale kompletny przystanek w samym sercu Faucigny.

src.q: Can you visit inside the Château de la Croix at Scionzier?
src.a: No, the Château de la Croix at Scionzier is private property and closed to the public. It can only be seen from the road. No interior visits are organised.

src.q: What is the history of the Château de la Croix?
src.a: The Château de la Croix is a fortified manor mainly built in the 16th century by the lords of La Croix, later linked to the Rochette family. Its history is tied to that of the Faucigny. Inscribed MH 1988, listed MH 1994.

src.q: Where is the Château de la Croix?
src.a: The Château de la Croix lies in the commune of Scionzier (74950), in the Arve valley (Faucigny), near Cluses. It is visible from the main road. GPS: 46.059° N · 6.552° E.

src.q: How much does it cost to visit the Château de la Croix?
src.a: The Château de la Croix at Scionzier cannot be visited inside. The external view from the road is free. No ticketing. Verified on 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening times of the Château de la Croix?
src.a: The Château de la Croix at Scionzier is visible from the road 24/7, year-round. No visiting hours (interior closed). Verified on 10 June 2026.

src.q: Why is the Château de la Croix listed as a Monument Historique?
src.a: Several parts of the Château de la Croix at Scionzier are listed Monument Historique (1994, PA00118442) for their architectural value and decoration: 17th-century wall paintings and an 1840 panoramic wallpaper in one of the salons.

src.q: Where can you park to see the Château de la Croix?
src.a: Free parking in the village of Scionzier, near the road from which the Château de la Croix is visible. No dedicated parking for the monument.

src.q: What else is there to see around the Château de la Croix?
src.a: Around the Château de la Croix at Scionzier, you can visit Cluses and its Museum of Watchmaking and Bar-Turning (5 km), head up to the Solaison plateau, or carry on to Sallanches and the Mont-Blanc country.

segments.src: Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard overlooking Lake Annecy, with its medieval towers and 19th-century Romantic architecture
segments.mt: Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard z widokiem na jezioro Annecy, ze średniowiecznymi wieżami i XIX-wieczną architekturą romantyczną

src.what_is:
The Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard is a medieval fortress transformed into a stately residence, dominating Lake Annecy from a rocky spur 200 metres above the water. Built from the 12th century onwards, it comprises today 105 rooms spread throughout an architectural ensemble blending medieval elements (three original square towers), Renaissance transformations, and 19th-century Romantic embellishments under the impetus of René de Menthon, great-grandfather of the current owners.

What distinguishes Château de Menthon is its exceptional family continuity: it has been inhabited by the same family since its foundation in the 11th century, spanning 23 generations. It is the birthplace of Saint Bernard of Menthon (1008), founder of the Grand-Saint-Bernard hospice and patron saint of alpinists. The castle retains its period furnishings, a library of over 12,000 rare books (including an Encyclopédie by Diderot and d'Alembert), a kitchen with a 15-metre serving hatch carved into the rock, and a chapel dedicated to Saint Bernard.

Local legend claims that Walt Disney drew inspiration from its turrets and romantic appearance for the castle in Sleeping Beauty — though this claim is undocumented. The castle is listed as a historic monument and has pursued an environmental preservation initiative since 2018: permaculture vegetable garden, biodynamic vineyards replanted on the hillside, and a cycle of lectures on these themes.

src.q: How do I book the Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard?
src.a: There is no online ticketing system. Tickets for the Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard are available only on site the day of your visit. For groups (10 people and above) and private tours, booking is mandatory by email contact@chateau-de-menthon.com or phone 07 81 74 39 72.

src.q: What is the admission price for the Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard?
src.a: The adult admission price (15+) for the guided tour of the Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard is €12. Child (5–14): €6. Free for children under 5. Gardens and terraces only ticket: €3. Group rate (10+ people): €10 per person. Verified 14 May 2026.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard?
src.a: The Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard is open April to mid-November. April, May, June, September, October: Friday, Saturday, Sunday and public holidays 1 pm to 6 pm (last entry 5:30 pm). July–August: daily 10 am to 7 pm (last entry 6:30 pm). Exceptional openings during Easter and All Saints' holidays: daily 1 pm–6 pm. Verified on the official website 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

src.q: Is the Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard wheelchair accessible?
src.a: No. The interior of the Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard is not wheelchair accessible due to numerous stairs and steps. The gardens and terraces are partially accessible. The site has no adapted PMR toilets.

src.q: How long does a visit to the Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard take?
src.a: The guided tour of the Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard takes approximately 45 minutes. Allow 1–2 hours in total including exploration of the gardens, terraces and vegetable garden, and a break at the gift shop or food truck.

src.q: Can I visit the gardens of the Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard without entering the castle?
src.a: Yes. The Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard offers a "gardens and terraces" ticket for €3 allowing access to the exterior without the indoor tour. Panoramic views over Lake Annecy, French gardens and permaculture vegetable garden.

src.q: Is there parking at the Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard?
src.a: Yes, the Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard has free parking on site, accessible via the castle avenue. Parking is limited in high season — early arrival recommended on July–August weekends.

src.q: Who lives in the Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard today?
src.a: The Château de Menthon-Saint-Bernard is still inhabited by the Menthon family, owners since the 11th century (23 generations). Count Olivier de Menthon is the current guardian. It is a private residence open to the public for guided tours.

src.what_is:
The Château de Thorens is a historic castle located in Fillière (formerly Thorens-Glières), in the Haute-Savoie department. Built in 1060 on the orders of Count Gérold of Geneva, this medieval fortress has been inhabited by the de Sales family since 1559. Today, it operates as a 4-star hotel and reception venue, managed by the de Roussy de Sales family.

The castle preserves architectural elements from the Middle Ages (keep, towers from the 12th and 13th centuries), the Baroque period, and the 19th century. It was the residence of Saint Francis of Sales (1567–1622), Prince-Bishop of Geneva, and the country retreat of Count Camille Benso di Cavour (1810–1861), Prime Minister of Piedmont and architect of Italian unity. The castle houses numerous mementos, artworks, and objects that belonged to these two major historical figures.

Since 2020, Château de Thorens no longer offers public guided tours. It welcomes only hotel guests, as well as clients for weddings, seminars, and private events. The site has been listed as a Historic Monument since 1990.

segments.src: is a historic castle located in Fillière (formerly Thorens-Glières), in the Haute-Savoie department. Built in 1060 on the orders of Count Gérold of Geneva, this medieval fortress has been inhabited by the de Sales family since 1559. Today, it operates as a 4-star hotel and reception venue, managed by the de Roussy de Sales family.
segments.mt: jest zabytkowym zamkiem położonym w Fillière (dawniej Thorens-Glières), w departamencie Haute-Savoie. Zbudowany w 1060 roku na rozkaz hrabiego Gérolda z Genewy, średniowieczna twierdza została zamieszkana przez rodzinę de Sales od 1559 roku. Obecnie działa jako czterogwiazdkowy hotel i miejsce odbioru, zarządzany przez rodzinę de Roussy de Sales.

fields.src: Château de Thorens operates as a 4-star hotel year-round. It is not open for free tourist visits — access is reserved for hotel guests, participants in private events (weddings, seminars), and restaurant diners (by reservation). The setting is particularly popular in spring and summer for outdoor weddings. Winter offers an intimate atmosphere with fireplaces and historic halls. To discover the castle, it is necessary to book a stay or event.

Nastawienie jest szczególnie popularne wiosną i latem na śluby na zewnątrz. Zima oferuje kameralną atmosferę z kominkami i historycznymi korytarzami. Aby odkryć zamek, należy zarezerwować pobyt lub wydarzenie.

src.q: How do I book a stay at Château de Thorens?
src.a: Accommodation at Château de Thorens can be reserved online via the castle's website through the direct-book.com platform, or by phone at +33 (0)4 50 22 42 02. Several period rooms and suites are available.

src.q: What is the nightly rate at Château de Thorens?
src.a: Accommodation rates at Château de Thorens are not publicly displayed. They vary depending on the room, season, and type of stay. Contact the establishment directly for a personalized quote.

src.q: Can Château de Thorens be visited freely?
src.a: No. Château de Thorens no longer offers public guided tours since 2020. Access to the castle is reserved for hotel guests, participants in private events, and restaurant diners by reservation.

src.q: Can I organize a wedding at Château de Thorens?
src.a: Yes. Château de Thorens is a sought-after wedding venue. It offers full or partial privatization, historic halls, a 5-hectare park, and bespoke services. Request a quote online or by phone.

src.q: Is Château de Thorens suitable for children?
src.a: Children aged 6 and over are welcome at Château de Thorens. Children under 6 may stay subject to availability of cots. Quiet hours are 11 p.m. to 6 a.m.

src.q: Are pets allowed at Château de Thorens?
src.a: No. Pets are not accepted at Château de Thorens, in accordance with the castle hotel's policy.

src.q: What is the history of Château de Thorens?
src.a: Château de Thorens was built in 1060 by Count Gérold of Geneva. Inhabited by the de Sales family since 1559, it was the residence of Saint Francis of Sales (1567–1622) and the country retreat of Count Camille di Cavour, architect of Italian unity.

src.q: Is there a restaurant at Château de Thorens?
src.a: Yes. La Table du Château offers gourmet French cuisine made from local and seasonal products. The restaurant is open by reservation for hotel guests and private events.

src.what_is:
The Château des Rubins at Sallanches (74700) — sometimes known locally as the 'Maison des Chattes Grises' — is an exceptional heritage building in the Mont-Blanc valley. A 14th-century fortified manor and former residence of the Loche family (bailiffs of Faucigny) in the 16th century, it is remarkable for having been spared by both the French Revolution and the great Sallanches fire of 1840, which destroyed almost the entire old town. It is one of the very few medieval buildings preserved at Sallanches.

From 2019 to 2021 the commune of Sallanches carried out a major heritage renovation to create the Observatoire des Alpes, an immersive interpretation centre on the mountain environment spread over 500 m² and 4 floors, with around fifty interactive modules. The thematic journey covers geology and glaciology, Alpine fauna and flora, the human history of the mountain and climate and adaptation. The route is family-friendly but scientifically rigorous, and works equally well for school groups.

2026 prices: adult €7.50, reduced €6.50 (child 5–18, student, jobseeker, senior 65+), family pass €23 (2 adults + 2 children, saving €5), free under 5 and for visitors with disabilities. Open year-round: Wed–Sun 2pm–6pm outside school holidays, daily 10am–6pm during school holidays and in July–August. Closed 25 December and 1 January. Fully wheelchair accessible thanks to the lift installed during the 2019–2021 renovation — a rarity for a historic castle. Sallanches-Combloux-Megève SNCF station is 10 minutes' walk; free parking nearby.

src.lead: 13th-century medieval keep at the heart of the castle, offering a panoramic 360° view over the town of Faverges and surrounding massifs after climbing 145 steps.

src.q: Is admission to the Château et Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges charged?
src.a: No. Access to the Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges is free, with the visit organised by the Association of Friends of Viuz-Faverges. The castle itself is a private events venue, but the keep and inner courtyard, listed as Historic Monuments, are open to the public.

src.q: How many steps are there to climb in the Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges?
src.a: The Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges has 145 steps to reach the summit and panoramic gallery. The climb takes approximately 10 minutes. Bring suitable shoes and avoid very hot weather.

src.q: What is the view like from the top of the Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges?
src.a: The summit of the Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges offers a 360° panoramic view over the Massif des Bauges (geopark), the Bornes massif, the town of Faverges and the surrounding basin. The wooden gallery (hourd) allows for detailed landscape interpretation.

src.q: Is the Château et Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges open year-round?
src.a: No. The Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges is open from mid-June to end of August (Monday to Friday 2:30 PM–6:30 PM, Saturday 10 AM–12 PM, closed Sundays). Outside season, visits are available during Heritage Days or by group request on 04 50 32 45 99.

src.q: Is the Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges accessible with a stroller or wheelchair?
src.a: No. The Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges is not accessible to people with reduced mobility due to the 145 steps without a lift. Heritage Guides remain available at the bottom to answer questions from visitors unable to climb.

src.q: What is the history of the Château et Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges?
src.a: Faverges Castle entered history in 1112, built by the lord Berlion. The Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges, a 13th-century round tower, was a strategic post between the counties of Geneva and Savoy. Restored in 2008, it has been listed as a Historic Monument since 1991.

src.what_is:
The Château de Fonbonne (sometimes spelled Fontbonne) is one of the discreet but singular heritage buildings at the heart of Évian-les-Bains. A fortified manor stood on this site from the 14th century, helping to defend the new Touvière quarter — the upper town outside Évian's enclosure, created by Amadeus V in 1303. The building was acquired in 1559 by Thomas Jacquerod and passed to the de Loys de Bonnevaux family.

In the late 18th century the wealthy English writer William Beckford stayed there and threw memorable parties. Transformed into a hotel around 1860, the building was reshaped by later restorations and today looks more 19th-century than medieval. Its garden, however, is the main draw.

In 2011 the Évian municipal services laid out the former garden as an Herbularius, a medieval-style herb garden featuring aromatic and medicinal plants (sage, rosemary, fennel, horseradish, lavender, hyssop). Open during city park hours; free access.

src.q: What is the Château de Fonbonne and its Herbularius?
src.a: The Château de Fonbonne is a heritage building in Évian-les-Bains, a former 14th-century fortified manor. Its garden, laid out in 2011 by the town as a Herbularius (medieval-inspired medicinal garden), is freely accessible and features sage, rosemary, fennel and other aromatic herbs.

src.q: Can you visit inside the Château de Fonbonne?
src.a: No, the Château de Fonbonne building at Évian-les-Bains is not open for interior visits. Only its Herbularius garden is accessible to the public.

src.a: Access to the Herbularius garden of the Château de Fonbonne is entirely free. No ticketing. Verified on 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening times of the Château de Fonbonne Herbularius garden?
src.a: The Herbularius garden at the Château de Fonbonne in Évian-les-Bains is freely accessible, especially during the growing season (spring to autumn). Check ville-evian.fr. Verified on 10 June 2026.
