src.a: The Château de Fonbonne lies in the historic La Touvière district of Évian-les-Bains, in the town centre, near the Thermes. GPS: 46.400° N · 6.593° E.

src.q: Which plants can you see in the Herbularius garden of the Château de Fonbonne?
src.a: The Herbularius garden of the Château de Fonbonne mainly features medieval-inspired aromatic and medicinal plants: sage, rosemary, fennel, horseradish, lemon balm, arranged in raised beds around a water feature.

src.q: What is the history of the Château de Fonbonne?
src.a: A fortified manor stood here in the 14th century, defending the new district of La Touvière. The Château de Fonbonne was bought in 1559 by Thomas Jacquerod, hosted William Beckford in the late 18th century, then was turned into a hotel around 1860.

src.a: Around the Château de Fonbonne, you can visit the Évian Thermes, the Palais Lumière, the Jardins de l'Eau du Pré Curieux, the Évian Resort Casino, or stroll along the Lake Geneva quays.

src.what_is:
The Château de la Rochette at Lully (74890) is one of the most affecting medieval remains of the Bas-Chablais. A 13th-century castle, built by the lords of Cervens (a cadet branch of the comital Faucigny family), it documents the rural Savoyard defensive system of the late Middle Ages on the plain of the Chablais between Annemasse and Thonon.

This geographical precision helps avoid the toponymic confusion frequent in the Chablais. The château is listed as a Monument Historique (1932), under Mérimée notice PA00118405, and benefits from full heritage protection.

The architecture is typical of the late 13th century: a central cylindrical keep (a military innovation over the older square towers), round corner towers and a partly preserved curtain wall. The site is in ruins, but the remains are visible from the outside of the private estate. NOTE: the château is privately owned — access from inside the property is NOT permitted without the owner's consent. Only the outer approaches (commune road and path) are freely and openly accessible year-round, via the Impasse du Château at Lully. Car recommended (no direct public transport); Bons-en-Chablais SNCF station (Léman Express) 5 km, A40 Bons-en-Chablais exit 10 minutes.

src.what_is:
The Château de Montrottier at Lovagny (74330) is one of the most striking castles in Haute-Savoie: a 13th-century fortified manor, remodelled in the 15th (cylindrical keep), the 18th (main residence) and finally restored in Neo-Gothic style in the 19th by Léon Marès, a wealthy collector who acquired the estate in 1906. At his death in 1916 he bequeathed the castle and its collections to the Académie Florimontane, which still manages the site. The spectacular setting overlooks the Fier Gorges just 800 metres away.

The highlight of the visit is the cabinet of curiosities, unique in the Pays de Savoie: over 1,500 pieces assembled by Léon Marès. 17th-century Japanese samurai armour, faience, antique African and Asian weapons, bronzes, manuscripts, ivories and ethnographic objects offer a journey across world civilisations. The 36-metre cylindrical keep (15th century) is climbed by a spiral staircase and gives an exceptional panorama over the Fier Gorges and the Semnoz range. A 4-hectare landscaped park with arboretum and formal gardens completes the visit.

2026 prices: adult €10, child 6–15 €6, free under 6, reduced €8 (students, jobseekers, seniors 65+), family pass €28 (2 adults + 2 children, saving €4), adult groups (15+) €8 with guided tour, school groups €4. Open from 29 March to 31 October 2026: Wed–Sun 2pm–6pm in March–May and September–October, daily 10am–6pm in July–August. Free parking, partial wheelchair access (ground floor and park accessible; keep not adapted). Car recommended (no direct public transport).

src.q: Qu'est-ce que le cabinet de curiosités du Château de Montrottier ?
src.a: Le cabinet de curiosités du Château de Montrottier rassemble 1500+ pièces réunies par Léon Marès (mort 1916) : armures samouraï japonaises XVIIe, faïences, armes anciennes, bronzes, manuscrits, ivoires. Unique en Pays de Savoie.

src.q: Quelle est l'histoire du Château de Montrottier ?
src.a: Le Château de Montrottier est une maison forte XIIIe siècle, donjon ajouté au XVe, logis remanié au XVIIIe, restauration néogothique XIXe par Léon Marès qui légua le château en 1916 à l'Académie Florimontane (gestionnaire actuel).

src.q: Comment combiner le Château de Montrottier avec les Gorges du Fier ?
src.a: Gorges du Fier à seulement 800 m du Château de Montrottier. Journée classique : Gorges du Fier matin (6 €) + Château de Montrottier après-midi (10 €) = patrimoine complet à 10 min d'Annecy.

src.q: Le Château de Montrottier est-il accessible aux PMR ?
src.a: Château de Montrottier partiellement accessible PMR : rez-de-chaussée du château et parc paysager 4 ha accessibles aux fauteuils roulants. Le donjon cylindrique n'est pas accessible PMR (escalier en colimaçon).

src.q: Comment venir au Château de Montrottier depuis Annecy ?
src.a: Le Château de Montrottier est à 10 min d'Annecy centre par D14 puis D116 vers Lovagny, 60 allée du Château. Sortie A41 Annecy-Sud à 5 min. Parking gratuit à l'entrée. Voiture conseillée (pas de transport public direct).

src.what_is:
The Château de Ripaille at Thonon-les-Bains (74200) is one of the major heritage sites of the Chablais: residence of the Dukes of Savoy from the 14th century, and the retreat of Duke Amadeus VIII (later Pope Felix V), who retired there in 1434 with 5 knights of the Order of Saint Maurice. A charterhouse founded by Amadeus VIII in 1410 completes the estate. Confiscated at the Revolution and bought in 1892 by the Mulhouse industrialist Frédéric Engel-Gros (DMC textiles), it received a sweeping Neo-medieval historicist restoration between 1892 and 1903 by architect Charles Schulé. Of the original seven towers, four still rise above the 14th-century walls.

The 53-hectare estate on the shore of Lake Geneva is unique in Haute-Savoie: a 19th–20th-century arboretum (rare species, giant sequoias), a reconstructed medieval garden, AOC Ripaille vineyards (white chasselas) across 23 hectares and the adjoining 350-hectare Ripaille state forest. The Fondation Ripaille (Necker-Engel family, recognised as a public-interest charity) manages the estate and its working vineyard. The original Engel-Gros Belle-Époque furniture is preserved in the apartments.

2026 prices: adult €12, reduced €9 (students, jobseekers, seniors 65+), family pass €29 (2 adults + 2 children, saving €5), child with disability + carer €5, audioguide €3 supplement (FR/EN/DE/IT), free under 7. Open 1 April to 2 November 2026, plus Christmas window (22 Dec 2025 – 4 Jan 2026) and Zone A school holidays. April–June and Sept–Oct: 2pm–5.30pm (Wed–Sun); July–August: 11am–5.30pm daily. Star't bus line A 'Ripaille' 5 min away, Thonon Léman Express station 3 km, free parking. Partial wheelchair access. 'Flash Back!' exhibition announced for 2026. AOC Ripaille wine tasting and sales on the way out.

src.what_is:
The Château des Sires de Faucigny at Bonneville (74130) — also known as the Château de Bonneville or Tour de Faucigny — is one of the major medieval castles of Haute-Savoie. Built around 1260 by Beatrice of Faucigny (daughter of Peter II of Savoy), it was the main fortress of the independent châtellenie of Faucigny in the face of Savoy. Its characteristic 'Savoyard square' plan originally featured four cylindrical corner towers, of which two survive today: the Black Tower (21 m, east) and the White Tower (15 m, west). Listed in the Inventaire des Monuments Historiques in 1987.

The castle had an eventful history: converted into the departmental prison in the 16th century, it kept that function for nearly 400 years, until 1934, when the town of Bonneville bought the monument from the State. A gradual heritage restoration has been ongoing ever since, with seasonal opening to the public via guided tours led by Faucigny Glières Tourisme.

2026 guided-tour prices: adult €5, child 10–18 €3, free under 10. Limited schedule (5–8 dates a year, generally May–September), booking required via ccfg.fr or tourisme-faucigny-glieres.fr. Exteriors free year-round (rue des Grandes Chambrettes). Central position in the heart of Bonneville, 10 minutes' walk from the SNCF station (Léman Express), A40 Bonneville exit 5 minutes. NOTE: an English-language Tourist Office mentions 'temporary closure' for restoration, but bonneville.fr and ccfg.fr confirm scheduled visits in 2025–2026 — check the exact calendar before travelling. Combinable with the Château de Beauregard and the Château de Châtillon-sur-Cluses.

src.what_is:
The Château de Sonnaz is one of Thonon-les-Bains' emblematic monuments, set at the heart of the upper town facing Lake Geneva, with sweeping views of the Swiss shore and the Jura range. It was built in 1666 by the Gerbaix de Sonnaz, one of the oldest Savoyard noble families, on the ruins of the medieval castle of Thonon built by Marie of Burgundy (wife of Amadeus VIII) and dismantled in the 16th century. The sovereigns of the House of Savoy regularly stayed here during their visits to the Chablais.

The building, sober and harmonious in its classical Grand Siècle style, is today a cultural hub for the Chablais. It houses both the Chablais Museum and the Thonon Tourist Office — a rare combination that makes it a must-visit. The Chablais Museum offers a permanent exhibition on the region's history, daily life and traditional crafts.

Open Tue–Sun (closed Mon and public holidays). Adult admission around €4; free under 18 and on the first Sunday of the month.

src.q: Can you visit inside the Château de Thénières?
src.a: No, the Château de Thénières at Ballaison today houses the Bas-Chablais Community of Communes (CCBC) and is not open for regular tourist visits. Free external view from the road.

src.q: What is the history of the Château de Thénières?
src.a: The Château de Thénières was built in 1863 by the architect Bruger at the request of General Count Ernest de Boigne, on the ruins of a medieval fortress. Sold in 1893 to a Lyon banker, later acquired by the SIVOM of Bas-Chablais.

src.q: Where is the Château de Thénières?
src.a: The Château de Thénières lies on the Boissy hillside at Ballaison (74140), at 733 m altitude, in the Bas-Chablais. GPS: 46.303° N · 6.325° E. About 15 km from Thonon-les-Bains.

src.q: How much does it cost to visit the Château de Thénières?
src.a: The external view of the Château de Thénières is free. There is no regular tourist interior visit (CCBC headquarters). Verified on 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening times of the Château de Thénières?
src.a: The external view of the Château de Thénières is free year-round. The CCBC offices inside the château are open on weekdays, but without tourist visits. Verified on 10 June 2026.

src.q: Why does the Château de Thénières look like a medieval castle?
src.a: The Château de Thénières is a pseudo-medieval 19th-century construction (1863) deliberately imitating a medieval castle — the work of the architect Bruger for Ernest de Boigne, in the Romantic taste of the time.

src.q: Where can you park to see the Château de Thénières?
src.a: Parking on the CCBC car park inside the Château de Thénières grounds (use outside office hours for the external view). Access from Douvaine or Thonon-les-Bains via secondary roads.

src.q: What is there to see around the Château de Thénières at Ballaison?
src.a: Around the Château de Thénières, you can discover the other Bas-Chablais châteaux (Avully, Buffavent), Yvoire and its Garden of the Five Senses, Douvaine, or head down to Thonon-les-Bains and the Château de Ripaille.

src.lead: Two rival medieval fortresses facing each other at the summit of a hilltop overlooking Thonon and Lake Léman, with a listed Romanesque chapel and frescoes from the 11th century.

segments.src: Two rival medieval fortresses facing each other at the summit of a hilltop overlooking Thonon and Lake Léman, with a listed Romanesque chapel and frescoes from the 11th century.
segments.mt: Dwie rywalizujące średniowieczne twierdze zwrócone ku sobie na szczycie wzgórza z widokiem na Thonon i jezioro Léman, z wymienioną romańską kaplicą i freskami z XI wieku.

src.what_is:
The Châteaux des Allinges are two medieval fortresses in ruins — Château-Neuf and Château-Vieux — located facing each other at the summit of a hilltop 712 metres high, 4 km south-west of Thonon-les-Bains. Built in the 10th century and separated by only 150 metres, they respectively belonged to the House of Savoy and the Lords of Faucigny and clashed for nearly three centuries until the Treaty of Paris in 1355.

The site comprises the remains of two castle enclosures, an 11th-century Romanesque chapel listed as a Historical Monument housing Savoy's oldest fresco, and a viewpoint at 717 metres offering an exceptional panorama over Lake Léman, the Jura and the Prealps. Château-Neuf served as a residence for Saint Francis of Sales from 1594 to 1598 during his evangelization mission to the Chablais following the Savoyard reconquest against the Protestant Bernese. The site remains a place of Salesian pilgrimage.

Dismantled in 1703 on the orders of the Duke of Savoy Victor Amadeus II facing the French threat, Château-Neuf nevertheless retains its chapel restored in 1836 by the Bishop of Annecy. Château-Vieux, abandoned as early as the 14th century, displays older remains including an imposing square keep of 25 metres high. The site has been listed as a Historical Monument since 2010 and is part of the UNESCO Chablais Global Geopark.

src.title: Explore the ruins of both castles
src.description: Walk through the remains of Château-Neuf and Château-Vieux, discover the ramparts, foundations of the square keep 14 m on each side, castle enclosures and traces of the fortified town abandoned in the 14th century.

src.title: Visit the Romanesque chapel and its frescoes
src.description: Discover the 11th-century castle chapel listed as a Historical Monument, admire Savoy's oldest fresco in the barrel-vaulted apse, and reflect in this place of Salesian pilgrimage.

src.title: Enjoy the panorama over Lake Léman
src.description: From the viewpoint at 717 m, admire one of the finest panoramas of Chablais: dominant view over Thonon-les-Bains, Lake Léman, the Swiss and French Jura, and the Dent d'Oche (2,222 m).

src.title: Hike from Allinges or Mâcheron
src.description: Access the site via three hiking trails: from Allinges church (village centre), from Mâcheron hamlet (south), or from Le Villard hamlet in Perrignier. Marked trails with picnic tables.

src.title: Attend cultural events
src.description: The site hosts throughout the year historical reenactments, live performances and temporary exhibitions organized by the municipality and local heritage associations.

src.title: Discover the Chablais UNESCO Geopark
src.description: The site is one of 23 geosites in the Chablais UNESCO Global Geopark. Interpretation panels on the geological history of the hilltop, from the molasse sea to alpine glaciers.

src.q: What is Châtel Accrobranche 1600?
src.a: Châtel Accrobranche 1600 (Châtel Aventures) is a tree-top adventure park at 1,600 m altitude in Châtel, in the Portes du Soleil. 7 courses from age 2, Quick Jump (free-fall), access by the Super-Châtel gondola.

src.q: What is the minimum age at Châtel Accrobranche 1600?
src.a: Châtel Accrobranche 1600 welcomes children from age 2 (up to 1.05 m tall, accompanied by an adult) on the P'tit Kid Park. More committed courses are available from 1.05 m and 1.35 m.

src.q: What are the prices at Châtel Accrobranche 1600?
src.a: 2025 individual prices at Châtel Accrobranche 1600 (to be confirmed for 2026): P'tit Kid Park €14.90, Kid Park €22.50, Super Park €31.20. Gondola, gear and activity are included. Verified on 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening times of Châtel Accrobranche 1600?
src.a: Châtel Accrobranche 1600 is open every day from 4 July to 30 August 2026. Last admission at 3 pm for 2 h, at 4 pm for 1 h. Verified on chatel.com on 10 June 2026.

src.q: What is the Quick Jump at Châtel Accrobranche 1600?
src.a: The Quick Jump at Châtel Accrobranche 1600 is a controlled free-fall experience from over 10 m — a strong thrill, extra to the tree-top ticket.

src.q: How do you get to Châtel Accrobranche 1600?
src.a: Access to Châtel Accrobranche 1600 is by the Super-Châtel gondola in Châtel village (74390). The few-minute ride is included in the tree-top ticket.

src.q: Do you need to book at Châtel Accrobranche 1600?
src.a: Booking is advised at Châtel Accrobranche 1600, especially in peak season (July–August). Bookings on chatel.com or chatelreservation.com.

src.q: What gear do you need for Châtel Accrobranche 1600?
src.a: For Châtel Accrobranche 1600, plan closed shoes (trainers), sportswear, long hair tied back. Helmets, harnesses and lanyards are supplied by the park.

segments.mt: Dla Châtel Accrobranche 1600, plan zamkniętych butów (trenerów), odzieży sportowej, długie włosy związane z powrotem. Kaski, uprzęże i smycze są dostarczane przez park.

src.what_is:
The Path of the Alpage is a marked discovery trail inviting walkers to explore the Vallée Verte in the footsteps of Guy Chatiliez, author of the 1977 book Alpage de mon enfance. From a Savoyard family at Habère-Lullin, Chatiliez recounted his childhood memories in the alpine pastures his mother took him to from age six. The trail revisits these places and invites walkers, panel by panel, to relive the atmospheres and gestures of alpine pastoral life described in the book.

The route measures 6.5 km with 330 m of cumulative climb, about 2 h 30 of walking at an easy pace. The start is behind Habère-Lullin church, a 10-minute walk from the village centre, on a gently descending path with dry toilets along the way. The trail features 8 themed stations, each with a panel covering an aspect of alpine pasture life: cheese-making, herd, gestures, equipment, seasons.

QR codes on each panel link to audio extracts from the book. Free year-round, best from May to October.

The trail revisits these places and invites walkers, panel by panel, to relive the atmospheres and gestures of alpine pastoral life described in the book.
segments.mt: . Z rodziny Savoyard w Habère-Lullin, Chatiliez opowiedział swoje wspomnienia z dzieciństwa na alpejskich pastwiskach jego matka zabrała go do wieku od 6 lat. Ścieżka ponownie odwiedza te miejsca i zaprasza wędrowców, panel po panelu, do przeżywania atmosfery i gestów życia duszpasterskiego alpejskiego opisanego w książce.

src.q: What is the alpine-pasture trail at Habère-Lullin?
src.a: The alpine-pasture trail is a 6.5 km discovery loop at Habère-Lullin (Vallée Verte), inspired by Guy Chatiliez's book 'Alpage de mon enfance'. 8 stations with panels and QR codes on alpine-pasture life.

src.q: How long is the alpine-pasture trail?
src.a: The alpine-pasture trail at Habère-Lullin is about 6.5 km as a loop, with 330 m of climb, i.e. 2 h 30 to 3 h of walking at a relaxed pace.

src.q: Where does the alpine-pasture trail at Habère-Lullin start?
src.a: The alpine-pasture trail starts behind the church at Habère-Lullin (74420). A 10-minute walk from the village centre along a gently descending lane.

src.q: How much does the alpine-pasture trail cost?
src.a: The alpine-pasture trail itself is entirely free. Guy Chatiliez's book 'Alpage de mon enfance' (sold at the town hall and TOs) is extra. Verified on 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening times of the alpine-pasture trail?
src.a: The alpine-pasture trail at Habère-Lullin is freely accessible from May to October depending on snow cover. Mid-mountain trail — avoid in snow or thunderstorms. Verified on 10 June 2026.

src.q: Who is Guy Chatiliez, the author behind the alpine-pasture trail?
src.a: Guy Chatiliez is the author of 'Alpage de mon enfance' (1977), a memoir of the Habère-Lullin alpine pastures where his mother took him from the age of six. The alpine-pasture trail retraces his steps through the Vallée Verte.

src.q: Is the alpine-pasture trail suitable for children?
src.a: Yes, the alpine-pasture trail is suitable for families with children from age 7–8 who are confident walkers. 6.5 km and 330 m of climb, with no technical difficulty. Water and walking shoes recommended.

src.q: Where can you buy the book 'Alpage de mon enfance'?
src.a: The book 'Alpage de mon enfance' by Guy Chatiliez is on sale at the Habère-Lullin town hall and the Habère-Poche and Bellevaux Tourist Offices — useful to prepare or extend the alpine-pasture trail.

src.title: Discovery taster
src.description: Short ride (~30 min) pulled by the team, ideal for families with young children or quick visits. First contact with the dogs and mushing.

src.title: Half-day ride
src.description: ~2h on the high-altitude nordic trails. The musher drives the team and explains his trade. Around €50-70.

src.title: Full musher day
src.description: Learn to drive the team, long-distance run with lunch at a refuge or picnic. From age 14-16. Around €90-100.

src.title: Multi-day raid
src.description: 2-5 day expeditions with bivouac/refuge, in-depth driving training. For enthusiasts.

src.title: Kennel visit
src.description: Meet the Nordic dogs, learn about the musher's work, see the puppies if in season. Possible before or after the ride.

src.title: Photos and videos
src.description: The musher often photographs the ride; souvenirs are shared at the end (depending on package). Capture the moment.

src.title: Birthdays and groups
src.description: Kids' birthday packages, group rides (friends, corporate seminars, stag/hen). Quote on request.

src.title: Professional musher guidance
src.description: All rides are led by professional mushers who know their dogs and ensure safety (pace, terrain, weather).

fields.src: Col de la Colombière · Le Reposoir (Haute-Savoie) | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Col de la Colombière · Le Reposoir (Haute-Savoie) | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Col de la Colombière · Le Reposoir (Haute-Savoie)

src.what_is:
The Col de la Colombière (1,613 m) links Le Reposoir to Le Grand-Bornand via the D4, forming one of the iconic stages of the Route des Grandes Alpes. A must for cyclists, the pass has hosted the Tour de France more than 20 times since 1960 and ranks among the legendary climbs of the Northern Alps. On the Reposoir side (north), the climb is brutal, with gradients of up to 11% in the final kilometres; on the Grand-Bornand side (south), the ascent is more gradual but just as spectacular.

At the top, the panorama sweeps across the Aravis and Bargy ranges, with the Pointe Percée (2,750 m) as a backdrop. A mountain restaurant, a car park and picnic areas let walkers and cyclists make the most of the spot. Several hiking trails leave from the col: the Tête des Annes (1,869 m), the Doran refuge (1,495 m, 1h30 walk) and the Lac de Peyre (2,080 m). Ibex regularly graze the surrounding pastures, and the col is a prime spot for observing the bearded vulture.

The road is closed in winter, usually from mid-November to early May depending on snow cover. In 2026 the col reopened on 6 May at 2pm. Access is free year-round during the open season, and a free shuttle bus runs to the col from Le Grand-Bornand from 11 July to 21 August.

segments.mt: od 1960 roku i jest wśród legendarnych wspinaczy Alp Północnych. Po stronie Reposoir (na północy) wspinaczka jest brutalna, z gradientami do 11% w ostatnich kilometrach; po stronie Grand- Bornand (na południu), wzniesienie jest bardziej stopniowe, ale równie spektakularne.

, with the Pointe Percée (2,750 m) as a backdrop. A mountain restaurant, a car park and picnic areas let walkers and cyclists make the most of the spot. Several hiking trails leave from the col: the

segments.src: The road is closed in winter, usually from mid-November to early May depending on snow cover. In 2026 the col reopened on 6 May at 2pm. Access is free year-round during the open season, and a free shuttle bus runs to the col from Le Grand-Bornand from 11 July to 21 August.
segments.mt: Droga jest zamknięta w zimie, zwykle od połowy listopada do początku maja w zależności od pokrywy śniegowej. W 2026 roku Col został ponownie otwarty 6 maja o godz. 14.00. Dostęp jest bezpłatny rok-runda w sezonie otwartym, a bezpłatny autobus do Col z Le Grand-Bornand od 11 lipca do 21 sierpnia.

src.question: When is the Col de la Colombière open?
src.answer: The col is generally open from early May to mid-November. In 2026, it opened on 6 May at 2 pm and will close around 18 November depending on snow conditions. Check inforoute74 for the current road state.

src.question: How hard is the climb by bike?
src.answer: Reposoir side (north): 16.5 km, 1,108 m climb, 6.8% average gradient, HC-classified with a final kilometre at 11%. Grand-Bornand side (south): 11.7 km, 690 m climb, 6% average gradient, more regular but demanding final kilometres.

src.question: Can you hike from the col?
src.answer: Yes, several trails leave from the col: Tête des Annes (2 h round trip, 560 m climb), Refuge de Doran (3 h round trip, 780 m climb), Lac de Peyre (3 h 30 round trip). Yellow waymarking, intermediate to confirmed level. IGN map 3430ET recommended.

src.question: Is there a restaurant at the col?
src.answer: Yes, the Restaurant La Colombière sits at the top of the col. Open June to September, it serves Savoyard specialities with a panoramic terrace. A coffee stop or a full meal are both possible.

src.question: Is the col suitable for children?
src.answer: Yes, the top of the col offers a safe flat area with parking, picnic tables and an easy trail to the Tête des Annes (30 min one way). Note: narrow winding road on the way up — bring travel-sickness aids if needed.

src.question: Can you watch wildlife at the col?
src.answer: Yes, ibex and chamois frequent the surrounding pastures, especially early in the morning. The col is also a viewing spot for the bearded vulture. Guided watching days are organised in summer by the Nature Park.

src.question: Is there a shuttle to avoid driving?
src.answer: Yes, a free shuttle links Le Grand-Bornand (village and Chinaillon) to the Col de la Colombière from 11 July to 21 August 2026. Regular departures all day. Convenient for hikers and cyclists doing one-way trips.

src.question: Do you need to book the restaurant at the col?
src.answer: Booking is recommended in July–August and on sunny weekends. The restaurant welcomes cyclists, motorcyclists and walkers without a booking depending on availability, but it can be very busy at lunchtime.

fields.src: Col de la Croix-Fry · Manigod (Haute-Savoie) | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Col de la Croix-Fry · Manigod (Haute-Savoie) | Loisirs 74

src.title: Cycling: legendary Tour de France climb
src.body: The pass has been climbed six times by the Tour de France. Two routes to choose from: via Manigod (13 km, 6.5% average, sections at 10-11%) or via La Clusaz (18 km, 4.6% average, more regular). Splendid views of the Aravis range.

src.title: Hiking on the Beauregard plateau
src.body: Starting point for multiple trails to the Croix de Colomban, Pointe de Beauregard or Tête de Cabeau. Exceptional panoramas of Mont-Blanc, l'Étale, Mont Charvin and la Tournette. Family-friendly loops and challenging routes.

src.title: Alpine skiing and night skiing in Manigod
src.body: Family resort with 25 km of slopes connected to La Clusaz. The pass offers the largest night skiing area in Savoie Mont-Blanc (8 lit slopes, 4 lifts, every evening during school holidays, Fridays and Saturdays off-season).

src.title: Cross-country skiing on the Beauregard plateau
src.body: Nordic area of 25 km (skating and classic) with breathtaking views of Mont-Blanc and the Aravis. Suitable for beginners and advanced. Night skiing on Saturdays from December to March. Welcome chalet, equipment rental.

src.title: Visit to Ferme de Lorette
src.body: 45-minute walk from the pass, discovery of a traditional alpine farm with 70 dairy cows. Free or guided tour daily at 2:30 PM in summer. Tasting of farm Reblochon cheese and local products.

src.title: Mountain biking and e-biking on massif trails
src.body: Marked routes for mountain bikes and e-bikes starting from the pass, through alpine pastures and forests. Access to Tour de l'Andran, Pré à l'Ours or towards Merdassier. E-bike rental on site.

fields.src: Col de la Forclaz — 1,147 m belvedere & paragliding hub over Lake Annecy | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Col de la Forclaz — 1,147 m belvedere & paragliding hub over Lake Annecy | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Col de la Forclaz - 1,147 m belvedere & piast paralotniarski nad jeziorem Annecy

src.what_is:
The Col de la Forclaz, at 1,147 m altitude in the Talloires-Montmin commune, is one of the most iconic belvederes over Lake Annecy. The pass links the village of Talloires, on the lake's eastern shore, with the Montmin valley descending towards Vesonne. It offers a panoramic view of the lake, the Bauges peaks and La Tournette (2,351 m).

Above all, it is a world-class paragliding site: thanks to its aspect and near-daily thermals, the take-off area — set up by the commune and the FFVL — can host up to 200 flights per day in peak season, making it one of the most frequented spots in Europe. Several local schools (Aerodream, Flyeo and others) offer tandem baptism flights from around €100 depending on the package and flight duration.

The col is also a starting point for the area's best-known hikes: the La Tournette ascent (5-6 h, +927 m elevation gain, exceptional panorama over the lake), the Seven Fountains loop (≈ 1 h, family-friendly), and loops towards Mont Lachat. The road is also popular with cyclists climbing from Talloires (≈ 8 km, 7% average gradient) or from Vesonne on the Montmin side.

Free access by road from Talloires or Montmin. Several car parks at the col (can be saturated in July-August). A panoramic restaurant with a lake-facing terrace rounds out the stop. Best season is May to October: the road may close in winter depending on snow.

src.q: Is there an entry fee to the col?
src.a: No, road access to the col is entirely free. Free parking. Paragliding flights, the restaurant and some shuttles are paid.

src.q: How do I get up to the col?
src.a: By car on the D42 from Talloires (8 km, 7% gradient). By bike, it's a very popular climb for Annecy cyclists.

src.q: Can I do a tandem paragliding flight?
src.a: Yes, several schools (Aerodream, Flyeo and others) offer tandem flights from around €100 depending on the package and flight duration. Booking advised in peak season.

src.q: How many flights leave the col each day?
src.a: Up to 200 flights per day in peak season, making it one of Europe's most frequented paragliding sites.

src.q: What hikes start from the col?
src.a: The La Tournette ascent (5-6 h round trip, +927 m), the Seven Fountains loop (1 h, family), and several loops to Mont Lachat and the Montmin valley.

src.q: When is the best time to visit?
src.a: May to October. The road may close in winter depending on snow. In summer, arrive early for parking and to enjoy the morning cool.

src.q: Is there a restaurant at the col?
src.a: Yes, a panoramic restaurant with a terrace facing Lake Annecy is at the col. Ideal for lunch after a hike or flight.

src.q: Is the col wheelchair-accessible?
src.a: The col itself is accessible by car without difficulty. The panoramic terrace and restaurant are accessible. The hiking trails are not adapted for wheelchairs.

src.what_is:
The Col des Aravis is a road pass at 1,486 m that separates the commune of La Clusaz, in Haute-Savoie, from La Giettaz, in Savoie. It is the lowest point through the Aravis range, a chain extending 25 km from north to south, from Bonneville to Ugine, culminating at Pointe Percée (2,752 m).

The pass is crossed by the D909 departmental road, opened in 1902, today a stage of the Route des Grandes Alpes® linking Thonon-les-Bains to Nice. On site: the Sainte-Anne chapel, restaurants, souvenir shops, alpine farms selling Reblochon cheese directly, parking areas, and hiking trailheads. On a clear day, open view of the Mont-Blanc massif. The pass also welcomes cyclists and bikers who tackle this legendary ascent.

The history of the pass is closely linked to the tourist development of La Clusaz: the opening of the road in 1902 marked the beginning of the resort's expansion. Today, the Col des Aravis is a must for mountain lovers, hikers and touring cyclists. The alpine depression where the chapel stands is framed by the cornices of the Etale and the spectacular opening of the Porte des Aravis to the north.

segments.src: is a road pass at 1,486 m that separates the commune of La Clusaz, in Haute-Savoie, from La Giettaz, in Savoie. It is the lowest point through the Aravis range, a chain extending 25 km from north to south, from Bonneville to Ugine, culminating at Pointe Percée (2,752 m).
segments.mt: jest przejazdem drogowym o długości 1486 m, który oddziela gminę La Clusaz od La Giettaz w Savoie. Jest to najniższy punkt przez pasmo Aravis, łańcuch rozciągający się 25 km od północy do południa, od Bonneville do Ugine, kulminacyjny w Pointe Percée (2,752 m).

segments.src: The pass is crossed by the D909 departmental road, opened in 1902, today a stage of the Route des Grandes Alpes® linking Thonon-les-Bains to Nice. On site: the Sainte-Anne chapel, restaurants, souvenir shops, alpine farms selling Reblochon cheese directly, parking areas, and hiking trailheads. On a clear day, open view of the Mont-Blanc massif. The pass also welcomes cyclists and bikers who tackle this legendary ascent.
segments.mt: Przełęcz jest skrzyżowana przez D909 departmental road, otwarty w 1902 roku, dziś etap Route des Grandes Alpes ® łączący Thonon-les-Bains do Nicei. Na miejscu: Kaplica Sainte- Anne, restauracje, sklepy z pamiątkami, farmy alpejskie sprzedające ser Reblochon bezpośrednio, parking i wędrówki. W jasny dzień, otwarty widok na masyw Mont-Blanc. Przełęcz wita również rowerzystów i motocyklistów, którzy radzą sobie z tym legendarnym wzniesieniem.

segments.src: The history of the pass is closely linked to the tourist development of La Clusaz: the opening of the road in 1902 marked the beginning of the resort's expansion. Today, the Col des Aravis is a must for mountain lovers, hikers and touring cyclists. The alpine depression where the chapel stands is framed by the cornices of the Etale and the spectacular opening of the Porte des Aravis to the north.
segments.mt: Historia przełęczy jest ściśle związana z rozwojem turystycznym La Clusaz: otwarcie drogi w 1902 r. było początkiem ekspansji ośrodka. Dziś Col des Aravis jest koniecznością dla miłośników gór, turystów i rowerzystów turystycznych. Alpejska depresja, na której stoi kaplica, jest oprawiana przez zakręty Etale i spektakularne otwarcie Porte des Aravis na północ.

fields.src: Tree-top adventure park at 1,450 m in Megève: 70 stations including 90 m zip-line, tree surfing, children's courses from age 5. Permanent safety via magnetic carabiners.

segments.src: Tree-top adventure park at 1,450 m in Megève: 70 stations including 90 m zip-line, tree surfing, children's courses from age 5. Permanent safety via magnetic carabiners.
segments.mt: Park przygodowy na wysokości 450 m w Megève: 70 stacji, w tym 90 m linii zip-line, tree surfing, kursy dla dzieci w wieku od 5 lat. Stałe bezpieczeństwo za pomocą karabińczyków magnetycznych.

src.what_is:
Côte 2000 Aventure is a tree-climbing adventure park located at 1,450 m altitude above Megève's airfield, at the foot of the Aiguilles Croches, offering an aerial adventure in an exceptional alpine setting. The park features a main course with 70 stations spread over 3 loops of increasing difficulty (blue, black, double black), ranging between 6 and 11 meters high, and a children's course with 30 stations at 2 meters height. This configuration welcomes both beginners and experienced climbers in a preserved forest environment.

The park distinguishes itself through its permanent safety system with magnetic carabiners, ensuring visitors remain attached from start to finish. This cutting-edge technology reassures families and allows children from age 5 to practice tree-climbing in complete safety. The diversity of stations is remarkable: zip-lines (including one of 90 meters), tree surfing, tightropes, trapezes, nets, walkways, and Tarzan jumps. Throughout the course, visitors never descend to the ground, offering a true aerial adventure through the trees.

What makes Côte 2000 Aventure unique in Haute-Savoie is its altitude and panoramic views of the Mont-Blanc massif. Unlike other valley-level tree-climbing parks, here the experience combines the adrenaline of aerial stations with the majesty of alpine landscapes. The park is particularly appreciated for its family atmosphere, warm welcome, and quality supervision. School groups and summer camps find it an ideal adventure terrain for building team spirit and developing self-confidence.

W parku znajduje się danie główne z 70 stacjami rozłożonymi na 3 pętle o rosnących trudnościach (niebieski, czarny, podwójny czarny), o wysokości od 6 do 11 metrów oraz kurs dla dzieci z 30 stacjami na wysokości 2 metrów. Ta konfiguracja z zadowoleniem przyjmuje zarówno początkujących, jak i doświadczonych wspinaczy w zachowanym środowisku leśnym.

fields.src: Children playing in the net workshops at Criq'Parc, a forest playground in Saint-Jorioz, on the shore of Lake Annecy

segments.src: Children playing in the net workshops at Criq'Parc, a forest playground in Saint-Jorioz, on the shore of Lake Annecy
segments.mt: Dzieci grające w warsztatach sieciowych na placu zabaw w Criq'Parc nad jeziorem Annecy

src.what_is:
Criq'Parc is a tree playground for children aged 3 to 12, located in the forest at La Crique site, on the shore of Lake Annecy in Saint-Jorioz. All workshops are accessible independently, without harnesses, as the installations are less than one metre from the ground or surrounded by safety nets. Children move freely under the supervision of an accompanying adult.

The playground spans one hectare of forest and features around twenty fun workshops: tree courses, zip-lines, net maze, giant trampoline, tree cabins, net sea, serpent swing, and a new aerial course over 1.20 m high (reserved for older children). A Mini-Kids area welcomes 2–3 year-olds with age-appropriate games. An organic eco-snack (crêpes, savoury galettes, ice cream, local drinks) completes the offer on site.

Founded in 2017 by Etienne Andréys with an eco-responsible approach, Criq'Parc is part of the multi-activity La Crique site, which also includes a cove-shaped beach (free access), water sports rentals (paddle, kayak, pedalo), and Criq'Bloc (bouldering wall). The park organises seasonal events (treasure hunts, autumn festival, Easter and Halloween activities). Seasonal opening from late March to early November, Wednesdays, weekends and daily during school holidays, from 10am to 6pm.

segments.src: is a tree playground for children aged 3 to 12, located in the forest at La Crique site, on the shore of Lake Annecy in Saint-Jorioz. Children move freely under the supervision of an accompanying adult.
segments.mt: Jest to plac zabaw dla dzieci w wieku od 3 do 12 lat, położony w lesie w miejscowości La Crique, na brzegu jeziora Annecy w Saint-Jorioz. Wszystkie warsztaty są dostępne niezależnie, bez uprzęży, ponieważ instalacje znajdują się mniej niż metr od podłoża lub są otoczone sieciami bezpieczeństwa. Dzieci przemieszczają się swobodnie pod nadzorem osoby dorosłej towarzyszącej.

Mini- Dziecięcy obszar wita 2-3-latków z odpowiednim wiekiem gry. Organiczne eko-przekąski (crêpes, pikantne galty, lody, lokalne napoje) uzupełniają ofertę na miejscu.

segments.src: Founded in 2017 by Etienne Andréys with an eco-responsible approach, Criq'Parc is part of the multi-activity La Crique site, which also includes a cove-shaped beach (free access), water sports rentals (paddle, kayak, pedalo), and Criq'Bloc (bouldering wall). The park organises seasonal events (treasure hunts, autumn festival, Easter and Halloween activities). Park organizuje imprezy sezonowe (polowania skarbów, jesienny festiwal, Wielkanoc i Halloween). Sezonowe otwarcie od końca marca do początku listopada, środy, weekendy i codziennie w czasie wakacji szkolnych, od 10.00 do 18.00.

src.bike: Green way around Lake Annecy accessible from Annecy (protected cycle path along the west shore). Accueil Vélo labelled site with bike parking on site.

src.q: How do I book Criq'Parc?
src.a: Book online at parc.annecy-lacrique.com or purchase on the day at Criq'Parc. Online booking is optional, except for special events (Easter and Halloween) where it becomes mandatory.

src.q: What are Criq'Parc's rates?
src.a: Child rate (3–12 years): €15 for 1 child, €14/child for 2 children, €13/child from 3 children. Adult participant: €8 (sliding scale). Accompanying adult: free. Season pass: €87/child. Verified 4 June 2026.

src.q: What are Criq'Parc's opening hours?
src.a: Criq'Parc is open from 29 March to 2 November, Wednesdays, weekends and public holidays from 10am to 6pm, and daily during school holidays. Verified on the official website 4 June 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

src.q: Is Criq'Parc a ropes course?
src.a: No. Criq'Parc is a tree playground without harnesses: all workshops are less than one metre from the ground or surrounded by safety nets, accessible independently from age 3.

src.q: Do adults have to pay to accompany their children to Criq'Parc?
src.a: No. The accompanying adult (who supervises from the ground without climbing) enters Criq'Parc free of charge. The participating adult (who climbs with children) pays €8 (sliding scale from 2 adults).

src.q: Is there a play area for under 3s at Criq'Parc?
src.a: Yes. Criq'Parc's Mini-Kids area welcomes children from age 2 with age-appropriate games: platform, wooden mud kitchen, low-level workshops. Rate: €6 (free if another paying child).

segments.src: Book online at parc.annecy-lacrique.com or purchase on the day at Criq'Parc. Online booking is optional, except for special events (Easter and Halloween) where it becomes mandatory.

src.what_is:
The Compagnie des Bateaux du Lac d'Annecy is the main passenger cruise operator on Lake Annecy, with a fleet of five boats: Allobroge, Belle Étoile, Cygne, La Savoie and Le Libellule (restaurant boat). At the end of 2024 the company reached its 100% electric or hybrid propulsion target — a year ahead of schedule and a first for a French cruise operator.

Boarding is at place aux Bois in Annecy. Commented cruises last 1 hour or 1 h 30 with views of the lake's mountains (La Tournette, Bauges, Bornes). Daily departures are timed to the season: in high season every hour from 10 am to 6 pm; in low season the timetable is reduced — check before travelling.

2026 fares: adult €19 (1 h) / €22 (1 h 30), child 4–12 €13.50 / €15, 0–3 €2. Open year-round. Partial accessibility for wheelchair users (the Cygne is the accessible boat).

src.what_is:
The CGN Évian stop serves the year-round N1 Lausanne–Évian line on Lake Geneva, operated by the Compagnie Générale de Navigation sur le Léman, the historic operator founded in 1873. The crossing takes about 35 minutes. Boarding is at Quai Albert Besson in the port of Évian-les-Bains.

The CGN runs the most prestigious Belle Époque fleet in the world: five historic late-19th to early-20th-century steamers (Italie, Vevey, La Suisse, Simplon, Savoie), classified as Swiss historic monuments and still in service, complemented by modern wheelchair-accessible boats.

2026 fares: adult from €16.80 (single); free under 5, reduced fare under 16. Year-round service; high-season frequencies, reduced in winter — check cgn.ch. Modern boats are wheelchair-accessible.

src.what_is:
The CGN Thonon stop runs the N2 Lausanne-Ouchy ↔ Thonon-les-Bains line year-round, with a 55-minute crossing of Lake Geneva. Boarding is at Port de Rives, in the historic heart of Thonon. Most departures are operated by the Naviexpress service, a fleet of modern fast catamarans.

In high season the stop also welcomes some of the CGN's five historic Belle Époque steamers (Italie, Vevey, La Suisse, Simplon, Savoie — all classified as Swiss historic monuments). These boats run gourmet cruises and lake tours, giving passengers a chance to sail aboard a turn-of-the-century steamer.

2026 fares: adult from €16.80; free under 5, reduced fare under 16. Year-round service; frequencies vary by season — check cgn.ch. Modern boats are wheelchair-accessible.

src.what_is:
The CGN Yvoire stop serves the medieval village of Yvoire, listed among the Plus Beaux Villages de France. With its 14th-century ramparts, château, Jardin des 5 Sens and cobbled lanes, Yvoire is one of the most popular destinations on the French shore of Lake Geneva.

The seasonal N3 line connects Yvoire to Nyon, in Switzerland's canton of Vaud, in just 20 minutes. The crossings are run aboard the CGN's prestigious Belle Époque fleet, giving passengers a short but memorable lake trip between Switzerland and France.

2026 fares: adult from €16.80; free under 5, reduced fare under 16. Season: weekends 4 April–29 May; daily 30 May–20 September; weekends 21 September–1 November. Wheelchair-accessible.

fields.src: Dévalkart de Manigod — Summer mountain coaster at La Croix-Fry | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Dévalkart de Manigod — Summer mountain coaster at La Croix-Fry | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Dévalkart de Manigod - Summer Mountain Coaster at La Croix- Fry British 124; Loisirs 74

src.q: Where is the Dévalkart de Manigod?
src.a: The Dévalkart is in the heart of the Manigod / La Croix-Fry resort, in the Aravis massif (74230). Car access from Annecy via Thônes (~40 min) or La Clusaz (~15 min).

src.q: When is it open?
src.a: The Dévalkart is mainly open from June to September, depending on weather and resort opening. Check daily opening at manigod.com or the Tourist Office.

src.q: From what age can you ride?
src.a: Generally, children can ride as passengers from age 3 accompanied by an adult, and drive from age 8 subject to their size. Precise conditions are shown on site.

src.q: How much does a descent cost?
src.a: A descent generally costs €5 to €8, with 5/10/day passes at lower per-descent rates. Exact 2026 prices to confirm at the box office.

src.q: How long is a descent?
src.a: A descent lasts about 3 to 5 minutes depending on pace. Return to the start is usually by chairlift (~10 min ride up).

src.q: Do I need to book?
src.a: No, generally no booking required: tickets on site. In high season (summer holidays, fine weekends), prefer morning or late afternoon to avoid queues.

src.q: Is it open in winter?
src.a: The Dévalkart is mainly a summer activity (June-September). In winter, the resort is open for alpine skiing and traditional snow sledging takes over.

src.what_is:
Domaine de Guidou is a protected natural site of 85 hectares located in the municipality of Sciez, on the shores of Lake Geneva. Classified as Natura 2000, it is owned by the Conservatoire du Littoral et des Rivages Lacustres and is managed collectively by the municipality of Sciez, LPO Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes, the ONF, and the Guidou Estate Management Association. The site comprises a mosaic of habitats: wooded areas, cereal crop fields, wet and dry meadows, and reed beds.

One of the most remarkable features of Domaine de Guidou is the presence of fossil sand dunes at Bois du Vernay, a formation found almost nowhere else in Europe. Until the 18th century, wind action on exposed lake sandbars created dunes similar to those found on seaside beaches. Although regulation of the lake level since the 20th century has slowed this process, the dunes remain visible in the landscape, now covered with vegetation.

The estate is home to 520 recorded plant species, including rare arable weeds (linked to cereal cultivation) now vulnerable due to pesticide use. The shallow depth of the lake in the Coudrée bay also makes it the most important site on French shores for wintering waterfowl: tufted ducks and pochards gather by the thousands each year, accompanied by goldeneyes. In spring, numerous waders (sandpipers, plovers) and herons (egrets, herons) make stops here during migration.

The site comprises a mosaic of habitats: wooded areas, cereal crop fields, wet and dry meadows, and reed beds.

src.title: Observe birds from the observatory
src.description: The birdwatching observatory at Grande Corne meadow allows observation of nesting and migratory birds (tufted ducks, herons, egrets, sandpipers) while ensuring their tranquility.

src.title: Discover the fossil dunes of Bois du Vernay
src.description: The fossil sand dunes, created by wind on the lake shores until the 18th century, constitute a rare geological curiosity. Marked trail through the woods.

src.title: Walk or cycle along marked trails
src.description: Network of developed trails (including a boardwalk over floodable meadow) allowing you to explore the site's diverse habitats without disturbing sensitive areas. Dogs on leash accepted.

src.title: Participate in LPO eco-volunteer work parties
src.description: The LPO regularly organizes maintenance work parties (mowing orchid dunes, removing invasive plants, maintaining edges and ponds) open to volunteers. Contact: haute-savoie[a]lpo.fr.

src.title: Discover rare arable weeds flora
src.description: Several agricultural plots in the estate are specifically managed to preserve arable weeds (species linked to cereal cultivation), now vulnerable across Europe.

src.title: Explore the historical heritage linked to Coudrée Castle
src.description: Formerly, Domaine de Guidou was part of a vast 250-hectare property belonging to the Allinges-Coudrée family, builders of Coudrée Castle in the 10th century.

segments.mt: Spacer lub cykl wzdłuż oznakowanych szlaków

segments.src: Network of developed trails (including a boardwalk over floodable meadow) allowing you to explore the site's diverse habitats without disturbing sensitive areas. Dogs on leash accepted.
segments.mt: Sieć rozbudowanych szlaków (w tym promenady nad łąką zatapialną), która pozwala na zwiedzanie różnych siedlisk terenu bez niepokojących wrażliwych obszarów. Psy na smyczy przyjęte.

segments.src: Participate in LPO eco-volunteer work parties
segments.mt: Uczestniczyć w pracach organizacji wolontariackich LPO

segments.src: The LPO regularly organizes maintenance work parties (mowing orchid dunes, removing invasive plants, maintaining edges and ponds) open to volunteers. Contact: haute-savoie[a]lpo.fr.
segments.mt: LPO regularnie organizuje prace konserwacyjne (koszenie wydm orchidei, usuwanie inwazyjnych roślin, utrzymywanie krawędzi i stawów) otwarte dla wolontariuszy. Kontakt: haute- savoie [a] lpo.fr.

segments.src: Several agricultural plots in the estate are specifically managed to preserve arable weeds (species linked to cereal cultivation), now vulnerable across Europe.
segments.mt: Kilka działek rolnych na osiedlu jest specjalnie zarządzanych w celu ochrony chwastów ornych (gatunki związane z uprawą zbóż), obecnie podatnych na zagrożenia w całej Europie.

segments.src: Explore the historical heritage linked to Coudrée Castle
segments.mt: Poznaj dziedzictwo historyczne związane z zamkiem Coudrée

segments.src: Formerly, Domaine de Guidou was part of a vast 250-hectare property belonging to the Allinges-Coudrée family, builders of Coudrée Castle in the 10th century.
segments.mt: Wcześniej Domaine de Guidou był częścią ogromnej 250- hektarowej nieruchomości należącej do rodziny Allinges- Coudrée, budowniczych zamku Coudrée w X wieku.

src.what_is:
The Domaine de Rovorée-La Châtaignière is a 24-hectare sensitive natural area situated on the shores of Lake Léman, between Yvoire and Excenevex. Owned by the Haute-Savoie Department and the Conservatoire du Littoral, it is one of the largest publicly accessible natural sites on the French shore of Lake Léman. The domain includes an ancient forest with centuries-old chestnut trees, meadows, lakeside shores bordering the lake for 1.2 km, tufa grottos and remnants of the medieval stronghold of Rovorée.

The site stands out for its exceptional biodiversity: it is home to 350 plant species, 87 bird species, 29 butterfly species and 13 bat species, including the pygmy pipistrelle (unique colony in Haute-Savoie). The domain is not an urban park but a preserved natural space, shaped over centuries by human practices. The Rovorée manor and La Châtaignière residence, two 19th-century manor houses, now host temporary exhibitions and artist residencies.

segments.src: is a 24-hectare sensitive natural area situated on the shores of Lake Léman, between Yvoire and Excenevex. Owned by the Haute-Savoie Department and the Conservatoire du Littoral, it is one of the largest publicly accessible natural sites on the French shore of Lake Léman. The domain includes an ancient forest with centuries-old chestnut trees, meadows, lakeside shores bordering the lake for 1.2 km, tufa grottos and remnants of the medieval stronghold of Rovorée.
segments.mt: jest 24-hektarowym wrażliwym obszarem naturalnym położonym na brzegu jeziora Léman, pomiędzy Yvoire i Excenevex. Jest własnością departamentu Haute-Savoie i Conservatoire du Littoral, jest jednym z największych publicznie dostępnych miejsc na francuskim brzegu jeziora Léman. Domena obejmuje starożytny las z stuletnimi kasztanowcami, łąkami, brzegami jezior graniczącymi z jeziorem przez 1,2 km, tufowymi grotami i pozostałościami średniowiecznej twierdzy Rovorée.

segments.src: The site stands out for its exceptional biodiversity: it is home to 350 plant species, 87 bird species, 29 butterfly species and 13 bat species, including the pygmy pipistrelle (unique colony in Haute-Savoie). The domain is not an urban park but a preserved natural space, shaped over centuries by human practices. The Rovorée manor and La Châtaignière residence, two 19th-century manor houses, now host temporary exhibitions and artist residencies.
segments.mt: Miejsce to wyróżnia się wyjątkową różnorodnością biologiczną: jest domem dla 350 gatunków roślin, 87 gatunków ptaków, 29 gatunków motyli i 13 gatunków nietoperzy, w tym pigmy pistrelle (unikalna kolonia w Haute-Savoie). Domena ta nie jest parkiem miejskim, ale zachowaną przestrzenią naturalną, ukształtowaną na przestrzeni wieków przez praktyki człowieka. Posiadłość Rovorée i rezydencja La Châtaignière 'a, dwa rezydencje w XIX wieku, obecnie odbywają się wystawy tymczasowe i rezydencje artystów.

src.q: Is entry to Domaine de Rovorée-La Châtaignière free?
src.a: Yes. Entry to Domaine de Rovorée-La Châtaignière is free and unrestricted year-round. On-site parking is also free (blue zone 4 hours).

src.q: How do I reach Domaine de Rovorée-La Châtaignière from Yvoire?
src.a: Domaine de Rovorée-La Châtaignière is located 1.5 km east of Yvoire. Accessible on foot or by bike via the green lane (20 minutes' walk). By car, follow the D25 towards Excenevex. A solar boat operates from Yvoire harbour in summer.

src.q: What is the best season to visit Domaine de Rovorée-La Châtaignière?
src.a: Spring and summer (April–September) are ideal for discovering the biodiversity of Domaine de Rovorée-La Châtaignière: meadow flowering, bird song, and free guided tours in July–August. Autumn offers beautiful colours.

src.q: Are dogs allowed at Domaine de Rovorée-La Châtaignière?
src.a: Yes. Dogs are allowed at Domaine de Rovorée-La Châtaignière, but must be kept on a leash. The site is a Sensitive Natural Area with remarkable wildlife.

src.q: Is Domaine de Rovorée-La Châtaignière accessible with a stroller or wheelchair?
src.a: Yes, partially. Domaine de Rovorée-La Châtaignière has trails partly accessible to people with reduced mobility. Vehicle access is possible to the La Châtaignière domain. Some lakeside trails are natural and not passable by vehicle.

src.q: Are there guided tours at Domaine de Rovorée-La Châtaignière?
src.a: Yes. Domaine de Rovorée-La Châtaignière offers free guided tours daily from Saturday 6 July (July–August), at 3 pm in front of the manor. Duration 1 hour. Themes: natural, historical and architectural heritage.

src.what_is:
The Écomusée du Bois et de la Forêt is housed in the old hydraulic sawmill of Les Étouvières in Thônes, in the heart of the Aravis. The sawmill, built in the 19th century, still operates today thanks to its water wheel fed by the Nom stream, driving belts and gears in the basement — a rare and living testimony to pre-electric water-powered sawing.

Visits begin with a live sawing demonstration: the guide explains the mechanism, starts the system and saws a log before your eyes. It is the highlight of the site and a memorable moment for all ages, especially children, captivated by the rushing water and the moving gears.

Beyond the sawmill, the ecomuseum presents a display on mountain forest life: the trades of woodcutter and sawyer, local species (spruce, fir, beech), traditional forestry in the Pays de Thônes. A forest interpretation trail with information panels lets you extend your visit outdoors along the stream.

For families, woodworking workshops are available for children from age 6 on Wednesday mornings during school holidays. 2026 prices: €7 adult, €5 child 6-18, free under 6. Seasonal opening from April to October. Address: 1324 Route de Montremont, 74230 Thônes — phone +33 4 50 32 18 10.

src.q: What are the 2026 prices?
src.a: Adult €7, child 6-18 €5, free under 6. Group and school rates on request.

src.q: What are the opening hours?
src.a: Open April to October. Self-guided visit 2:30-5:30 PM in April-June and September-October, extended hours in July-August. Closed in winter.

src.q: Does the sawmill really work?
src.a: Yes, the 19th-century hydraulic sawmill still operates: the water wheel drives belts and gears, and a log is sawn during the demonstration.

src.q: Is it suitable for kids?
src.a: Very much so. Families are welcome from age 3, the sawing demo fascinates children, and woodworking workshops are offered from age 6 during school holidays.

src.q: How long does it take?
src.a: Allow 1 to 1.5 h for the guided visit and exhibition. Add 30 min for the outdoor interpretation trail if weather allows.

src.q: Do I need to book?
src.a: Booking is advised in peak season and for kids' workshops. For individual visits, drop-in is possible during opening hours.

src.q: Is there parking?
src.a: Yes, free parking outside the ecomuseum. Access is via route de Montremont from Thônes (2 km from the centre).

src.q: Is it wheelchair accessible?
src.a: Access to the main building is possible but the sawmill has several levels and stairs. Contact the team to adapt the visit.

src.what_is:
The Lake Annecy Ecomuseum is one of the pivot museums of Lake Annecy: located at Sevrier (74320), on the western shore famous for its Paccard bell foundry, it has offered for several decades an immersive encounter with 19th-century Savoyards. The route explores daily life, crafts, traditions and landscapes of the Annecy basin at a pivotal time — just before the rise of thermal tourism and industrialisation.

The museum has heavily modernised its offer in recent years with a strong family focus: playful age-graded trails (2–3, 3–6 years), puzzle-games (6–10, 10+), and even an open-air riddle circuit available at the ticket office. The idea: let visitors live Savoyard daily sensations — handle, observe, guess — rather than simply look.

Adult €7, child €5, family pass €22. Open year-round (closed Tuesday and certain holidays). Parking and refreshment area on site.

src.q: What is the Lake Annecy Ecomuseum?
src.a: The Lake Annecy Ecomuseum at Sevrier is an immersive museum devoted to 19th-century Savoyard life on the lakeshore. Indoor trails with puzzle-games and an outdoor puzzle trail, for families and school groups.

src.q: How much does the Lake Annecy Ecomuseum cost?
src.a: Lake Annecy Ecomuseum prices: adult €7, child €5, family (2 adults + 2 children) €22. Adult group rates €6.50 to €9.50, children's groups €6.50 (from 20 people). Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening times of the Lake Annecy Ecomuseum?
src.a: Opening times of the Lake Annecy Ecomuseum at Sevrier vary by season (mainly April to September). 22-30 April: Tue-Thu 9:30-12:00/14:00-17:00. 7 July - 31 Aug: Mon-Fri 9:30-12:00/14:00-18:00. Check ecomusee-lacannecy.com. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: Is the Lake Annecy Ecomuseum suitable for children?
src.a: Yes, the Lake Annecy Ecomuseum is very family-oriented: playful trails for ages 2-3 and 3-6, puzzle-games for ages 6-10 and 10+, outdoor puzzle trail. Attractive family ticket at €22.

src.q: How long should I allow for the Lake Annecy Ecomuseum?
src.a: Allow 1 hr to 1 hr 30 for the full Lake Annecy Ecomuseum visit with indoor puzzle-game. Longer with the outdoor puzzle trail — a good half-day in all.

src.q: Where is the Lake Annecy Ecomuseum?
src.a: The Lake Annecy Ecomuseum is on Route de l'Église in Sevrier (74320), about 8 km south of Annecy on the western shore of the lake. GPS: 45.864° N · 6.139° E. Telephone: +33 (0)4 50 52 41 05.

src.q: Is the Lake Annecy Ecomuseum open all year round?
src.a: No, the Lake Annecy Ecomuseum runs mainly from April to September following a detailed seasonal calendar. Off-season, it opens by reservation for groups. Verified on ecomusee-lacannecy.com on 10 June 2026.

src.q: Do I need to book for the Lake Annecy Ecomuseum?
src.a: No booking required for individual visits to the Lake Annecy Ecomuseum. Groups (≥ 20 people) and school groups book in advance by telephone on +33 (0)4 50 52 41 05.

src.what_is:
The Écomusée Paysalp is the peasant heritage ecomuseum of the Faucigny, located at 628 avenue de Savoie in Viuz-en-Sallaz. Run by the Paysalp non-profit (founded in the 1980s), it was awarded the national ecomuseum label in 2003 and now welcomes around 40,000 visitors a year.

The main Peasant Museum offers 500 m² of immersive exhibition on mountain life from 1850 to 1950: reconstructed farmhouse interiors, agricultural tools, traditional costumes and old trades. Two highlights: a fully restored 1924 classroom and 'Le Kube', an aerial film projected inside a panoramic cube.

Paysalp manages a total of eight Faucigny heritage sites, including the Prieuré de Peillonnex, the Château de Faucigny and the Verger conservatoire de Sevraz (80 heritage apple and pear varieties). Fares: adult €7, children 5–15 €5, free under 5. Note: open Monday–Friday, closed weekends and public holidays.

fields.src: Écomusée de la pêche et du lac Thonon · Lake Geneva aquariums · 2026

segments.src: Écomusée de la pêche et du lac Thonon · Lake Geneva aquariums · 2026
segments.mt: Écomusée de la pêche et du lac Thonon · Jezioro Genewskie

src.what_is:
The Écomusée de la pêche et du lac is the only museum with public aquariums in Haute-Savoie. Run by the town of Thonon-les-Bains since 1987, it occupies three traditional fishermen's huts at the historic Port de Rives, a fishing district dating back to the 12th century.

Inside, two Lake Geneva aquariums show local species (perch, féra, Arctic char, burbot, trout, carp, roach, signal crayfish), alongside a naturalised ornithology collection, traditional fishing gear (boats, traps, nets, period and modern engines), the preserved Paul Perroud hut kept as a mid-20th-century fisherman's workshop, and a documentary on today's professional fishing on Lake Geneva.

2026 season: open from 30 May to 30 September. Very affordable fares: adult €3.50, reduced €2.60, free under 18, free for everyone every Sunday. A combined ticket with the Musée du Chablais costs €6/€4 for both municipal museums.

src.what_is:
E.réel Annecy is a 300 m² virtual-reality centre located at Grand Épagny, in the commune of Sillingy (the name 'Annecy' refers to the wider Annecy basin — the venue is not in Annecy itself). The catalogue lists more than 80 VR experiences on individual 9 m² stations: action games, VR escape rooms, art experiences, sports simulators, educational and cultural content.

The centre is open to children from age 8 with an adult, and from 16 alone, with no upper age limit. It hosts families, groups of friends, children's and teen birthdays, hen and stag parties, corporate seminars and team-building events. Companions wait in a free, comfortable lounge area during sessions.

Sessions are sold in 30-minute or 1-hour slots, from €14 per person. Off-peak discounts apply outside Wednesdays, French school holidays and public holidays. The Pass culture is accepted. Open year-round — ideal for rainy days and evenings in the Annecy basin.

src.q: Comment réserver chez E.réel Annecy ?
src.a: Réservation à E.réel Annecy en ligne sur ereel.fr (système de réservation rapide) ou par téléphone au +33 (0)4 50 22 08 60. Conseillée le week-end et en soirée. Anniversaires, EVG/EVJF, team building : devis sur demande.

src.q: Quel est le tarif chez E.réel Annecy ?
src.a: E.réel Annecy : tarif à partir de 14 € par personne. Grille complète non publiée en clair — le prix final dépend de la session (30 min ou 1 h), du nombre de joueurs et de l'heure (creuse/pleine). Pass culture accepté. Devis pour groupes au 04 50 22 08 60. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.

src.q: Quelles sont les horaires d'E.réel Annecy ?
src.a: E.réel Annecy ouvre 7j/7 toute l'année. Lun 14h-19h, Mar 14h-20h, Mer 10h-12h30 et 14h-21h, Jeu 14h-22h15, Ven 14h-23h30, Sam 10h-12h30 et 14h-23h30, Dim 10h-12h30 et 14h-19h. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

src.q: Où se trouve E.réel Annecy exactement ?
src.a: E.réel Annecy est situé à Sillingy (Le Grand Épagny), 486 Route des Prés Rollier, ZAC de Bromines, 74330. Le nom commercial 'Annecy' renvoie au bassin annécien ; le centre est à 15 min en voiture du centre d'Annecy, accessible par la ligne 7 Sibra arrêt 'Roseaux'.

src.q: À partir de quel âge chez E.réel Annecy ?
src.a: E.réel Annecy accepte les enfants dès 8 ans accompagnés et 16 ans seul, sans âge maximum (jusqu'à 78 ans cités). Les accompagnateurs disposent d'un espace lounge confortable gratuit pendant la session.

src.q: Combien d'expériences VR à E.réel Annecy ?
src.a: E.réel Annecy propose plus de 80 expériences VR au catalogue, régulièrement renouvelé : jeux d'action, escape games VR, simulateurs sportifs et de pilotage, expériences artistiques et culturelles. Sur stations individuelles de 9 m².

src.q: E.réel Annecy accepte-t-il le Pass culture ?
src.a: Oui. E.réel Annecy accepte le Pass culture (15-18 ans) pour le règlement des sessions, dans la limite du solde disponible. Bon plan pour les jeunes du bassin annécien.

src.q: Comment venir à E.réel Annecy depuis Annecy centre ?
src.a: E.réel Annecy est à 15 min d'Annecy centre par avenue de Genève (D1508) jusqu'à Épagny Metz-Tessy, sortie Centre commercial Grand Épagny. En bus : ligne 7 Sibra arrêt 'Roseaux' depuis la gare SNCF. Parking privatif gratuit sur place.

fields.src: Ablok Argonay — Annecy bouldering gym | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Ablok Argonay — Annecy bouldering gym | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Ablok Argonay - Annecy siłownia bouldering 124; Loisirs 74

src.what_is:
Ablok Annecy, located in Argonay in the activity zone north of the Annecy area, is an indoor bouldering gym known for its quality route-setting and friendly atmosphere. The concept is simple: only bouldering, ropeless climbing on 4-5 m walls with thick mat landings. 3D volumes, colour-coded circuits and distinct sectors allow everyone to progress from beginner grades (yellow/green) to expert circuits (black/purple).

Who is it for? Ablok welcomes all levels, from the curious newcomer to the experienced climber chasing demanding circuits. Group classes for kids/teens/adults, holiday courses, discovery sessions and birthday packages are on offer. No need to bring gear: shoes can be rented at the entrance and chalk is sold on site. The gym also has a training board (Moonboard or similar) to work on strength and movement.

Prices and access. Adult entry is around €13-15 at full rate, with monthly/yearly memberships and 10-entry cards. The gym is open daily, with extended evening and weekend hours. A bar/snack lets you refuel before or after your session. Easy access from Annecy (Argonay exit on the expressway) or Geneva via the A41.

src.q: What's the difference between Ablok and Climb Up?
src.a: Ablok is a 100% bouldering gym (ropeless climbing, 4-5 m). Climb Up in Annecy offers both bouldering and 18 m lead/top-rope routes. The two complement each other.

src.q: From what age?
src.a: Bouldering is family-friendly from age 4-6 depending on the child; classes usually start at age 6-7. No upper age limit.

src.q: How much is a session?
src.a: Adult entry is around €13-15, with monthly memberships and 10-entry cards. Exact 2026 prices on ablok.fr.

src.q: Do I need my own gear?
src.a: No. Climbing shoes can be rented at the entrance (~€3-5) and chalk is sold on site. Comfortable flexible clothing recommended.

src.q: Can I host a birthday party?
src.a: Yes, birthday package from age 6 with an instructor, gym access and possible private snack area. Book in advance.

src.q: Are there classes for adult beginners?
src.a: Yes, 1h-1h30 discovery sessions and weekly adult classes. Ideal to learn the basics of movement reading and falling.

src.q: Is there a bar or food?
src.a: Yes, Ablok has a bar with drinks and snacks to relax before or after climbing.

fields.src: Climb Up Annecy — Indoor climbing gym, lead & bouldering | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Climb Up Annecy — Indoor climbing gym, lead & bouldering | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Climb Up Annecy - Wewnętrzna siłownia wspinaczkowa, ołów & bouldering 124; Loisirs 74

src.q: Do I need a climbing passport to use the rope routes?
src.a: Yes, to climb the rope routes independently (lead or top-rope with a partner) a climbing passport or in-house validation is required. Otherwise, take a discovery session first.

src.q: From what age can you come?
src.a: Bouldering is family-friendly from age 4-6 (depending on the child). Children's classes usually start around age 6-8. Independent rope climbing is reserved for ages 14+ with a passport.

src.q: How much is a session at Climb Up Annecy?
src.a: Adult full price is around €13-16. Discounts for kids, students, members, and 10-entry cards. Exact 2026 prices on climbup.fr.

src.q: Do I need to book?
src.a: No booking needed for open climbing. However booking is recommended for classes, discovery sessions, birthdays and groups. Online on climbup.fr.

src.q: Can I rent gear on site?
src.a: Yes, full rental: harness, shoes, chalk bag. Ideal to try without buying gear, or for visitors.

src.q: Can I host a birthday party?
src.a: Yes, kids' birthday package with a qualified instructor, gym access and possible privatisation of an area for cake. Book several weeks ahead.

src.q: Which time slots are quietest?
src.a: Weekday mornings (10am-noon) and early afternoons (2-5pm) are usually quietest. Evenings (6-9pm) and weekends are busy.

segments.src: Do I need a climbing passport to use the rope routes?
segments.mt: Potrzebuję paszportu wspinaczkowego, żeby używać tras linowych?

segments.src: Yes, to climb the rope routes independently (lead or top-rope with a partner) a climbing passport or in-house validation is required. Otherwise, take a discovery session first.
segments.mt: Tak, aby wspinać się na linie niezależnie (ołów lub top- liny z partnerem) wymagany jest paszport wspinaczkowy lub walidacja wewnątrz domu. W przeciwnym razie najpierw zajmijcie się odkryciem.

segments.src: From what age can you come?
Bouldering is family-friendly from age 4-6 (depending on the child). Children's classes usually start around age 6-8. Independent rope climbing is reserved for ages 14+ with a passport.
segments.mt: Bouldering jest przyjazny rodzinie od lat 4- 6 (w zależności od dziecka). Zajęcia dla dzieci zazwyczaj zaczynają się w wieku 6-8 lat. Niezależna wspinaczka linowa jest zarezerwowana na 14 lat + z paszportem.

segments.src: How much is a session at Climb Up Annecy?
segments.mt: Ile kosztuje sesja w Climb Up Annecy?

segments.src: Adult full price is around €13-16. Discounts for kids, students, members, and 10-entry cards. Exact 2026 prices on climbup.fr.
segments.mt: Pełna cena dla dorosłych wynosi około 13- 16 EUR. Zniżki dla dzieci, studentów, członków i 10-kartek wejściowych. Dokładne ceny 2026 na climbup.fr.

segments.src: No booking needed for open climbing. However booking is recommended for classes, discovery sessions, birthdays and groups. Online on climbup.fr.
segments.mt: Nie trzeba rezerwować dla otwartej wspinaczki. Jednakże rezerwacja jest zalecana dla klas, sesji odkrywczych, urodzin i grup. Online on climbup.fr.

segments.src: Can I rent gear on site?
Yes, full rental: harness, shoes, chalk bag. Ideal to try without buying gear, or for visitors.
segments.mt: Tak, pełny wynajem: uprząż, buty, worek kredowy. Idealny do próby bez zakupu sprzętu, lub dla odwiedzających.

segments.src: Can I host a birthday party?
Yes, kids' birthday package with a qualified instructor, gym access and possible privatisation of an area for cake. Book several weeks ahead.
segments.mt: Tak, urodzinowa paczka dla dzieci z wykwalifikowanym instruktorem, dostęp do siłowni i możliwa prywatyzacja miejsca na ciasto. Zarezerwuj kilka tygodni wcześniej.

segments.src: Weekday mornings (10am-noon) and early afternoons (2-5pm) are usually quietest. Evenings (6-9pm) and weekends are busy.
segments.mt: Tygodniowe poranki (10- południe) i wczesne popołudnia (2- 5pm) są zazwyczaj najcichsze. Wieczory (6- 9pm) i weekendy są zajęte.

fields.src: Cortigrimpe — Climbing gym & bistro in Epagny Metz-Tessy | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Cortigrimpe — Climbing gym & bistro in Epagny Metz-Tessy | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Cortigrimpe - siłownia wspinaczkowa i bistro w Epagny Metz-Tessy

src.what_is:
Cortigrimpe (officially Cortigrimpe 74) is one of the main climbing gyms in the Annecy area, set up at the Parc Activité des Longeray in Epagny Metz-Tessy, ten minutes by car from central Annecy and right next to the airport. The site combines 1,000 m² of bouldering, roped walls and an on-site bistro-bar.

The bouldering wall covers all profiles — slabs, vertical, overhangs — with a hold variety suited to every level, from beginners discovering the sport to advanced climbers working on movement and reading. The two indoor rope areas, 8 and 10 metres high, are equipped with six autobelays so you can climb solo safely, with routes graded 4B to 7B. Two 10-metre outdoor walls round out the offering in summer for sun-soaked climbing in the Parc des Longeray courtyard.

The venue stands apart from other gyms thanks to its integrated restaurant offer: a gourmet bistro and bar that can be visited with or without a gym ticket — perfect for a coffee after a session or a group meal. A dedicated 4-7 area lets children climb under parental supervision — no formal lessons, just a tailored environment.

Rates run from €9 per session for children up to €16.50 for an adult drop-in, with youth/student (€10.50) and reduced (€14.50) tiers. An unlimited monthly membership at €52 is offered for regulars. No gear is mandatory for bouldering; harness and shoes are available for rent for the roped walls.

src.what_is:
Space Bloc is a bouldering gym opened in Sillingy, 10 minutes north-west of Annecy. With more than 750 m² of climbing surface entirely dedicated to bouldering (rope-free climbing on 4-4.5 m walls with crash mats), it is one of the largest bouldering spaces in the Annecy area.

The gym offers regularly refreshed boulders covering all levels, from beginner to expert (yellow to black grades). Routes are reset every month by the in-house setting team — there is always something new to climb. A dedicated kids' area welcomes children from age 4 with suitable blocks in a secure environment.

Beyond drop-in climbing, Space Bloc offers weekly classes all year for kids (4-5, 6-8, 8-10, 10-12), teens and adults, as well as holiday camps (week-long, from €135). First-timers are welcomed: no gear needed for a first session, just sportswear. Climbing shoes are available to rent.

On site, a bar/snack with social area lets you extend the session. Adult day pass €14 (2024-2026 indicative). Passes and packages on the website. Open year-round 7 days a week, perfect on rainy days or to complement a day outdoors. Address: 87 Route des Marais de Culas, 74330 Sillingy — phone +33 4 50 19 49 84.

src.q: How much does an escape-game session cost?
src.a: Per-person pricing scales with group size: €38 as a duo, €32 for 3, €28 for 4-5, €24 for a full team of 6 (2026 rates).

src.q: Is booking required?
src.a: Yes, book online at break-out-game.fr/annecy/reserver/ or call +33 4 50 05 49 63. Weekends and school holidays fill up fast.

src.q: What's the minimum age?
src.a: Escape rooms are suitable from age 8-10 depending on the room, with adult supervision. The board-game bar and quiz room are open to all ages.

src.q: How long should I plan for?
src.a: 60 minutes in-room, plus 15-20 min for briefing/photo/debrief — total around 1 h 30 on site.

src.q: Which room should beginners pick?
src.a: All three are designed to be beginner-friendly. The game master tailors hints to your pace — just mention your experience level when booking.

src.q: Can I combine escape game with axe throwing?
src.a: Yes, multiple activities can be booked in the same session. It's a popular combo for hen/stag parties and birthdays.

src.q: Is the venue wheelchair-accessible?
src.a: The board-game bar and quiz room are accessible. The escape rooms include a few steps — call +33 4 50 05 49 63 to check your specific case.

src.q: Can I rent the venue for a private event?
src.a: Yes — partial or full hire for birthdays, seminars, hen/stag and corporate parties. Quote on request.

src.what_is:
Mystères du Lac is one of Annecy's most complete escape games: seven themed rooms open since 2017, in central Annecy at 23 rue Sommeiller (corner of rue Carnot) — 5 minutes from the train station and 3 minutes from Bonlieu.

The rooms cover varied worlds: The Alchemist (mystical lab), The Black Lily Investigation (historical mystery), Submarine K74 (chase under Lake Annecy), The Secret of the Allobroges (Gallo-Roman puzzle), The Grand Tour, The Gourmet (culinary world) and Welcome to Trolldom (fantasy). Each room lasts 60 minutes for 2 to 6 players.

Prices depend on the room and headcount: €22-38 per person, with The Alchemist at €27-35/player. Online booking strongly recommended at mysteresdulac.com/reservation. The venue also offers outdoor city games in Annecy. Great for friends, families (from 10-12 yo), birthdays, bachelor parties or team-building.

src.what_is:
The Morzine Espace Aquatique is the resort's aquatic centre at 608 B route du Palais des Sports. It combines an indoor area with a 25 m pool, a 12.5 m pool, a paddling pool, 3 saunas, 2 hammams and a restaurant, with an outdoor area featuring two pools including an Olympic 50 m pool, a paddling pool, a slide and a solarium.

2026 prices: €6 adult (pool only), €12 adult pool + wellness (saunas/hammams), €4 child 5-17, €4 student (M'Ra card). One of the few mountain centres with an outdoor 50 m pool. Tel. 04 50 79 01 69.

Open year-round with seasonal hours: longer in ski season and summer. Check the official site before visiting.
