src.what_is:
The Espace Tairraz is a twin museum in the heart of Chamonix, on the Saint-Michel car-park forecourt: it combines the Crystal Museum (renovated in 2021) and the Mountaineering Centre. Run by the Communauté de Communes de la Vallée de Chamonix-Mont-Blanc (CCVCMB), it is part of the same network as the future Musée du Mont-Blanc and the Maison de la Mémoire Janny Couttet.

The Crystal Museum displays nearly 1,900 mineralogy pieces from the collections of the Musée Alpin and the Chamonix–Mont-Blanc and Northern Alps Mineralogy Club. The collection is internationally renowned, with an exceptional set of pink-red fluorites, smoky quartz, axinites, siderites and epidotes, most of them from the Mont-Blanc massif. The temporary exhibition 'Trésor minéral: the Raymond Thibault collection' runs until December 2027.

Fares: adult €8, reduced €5.50, free under 18. Guided tours on Thursdays at 2.15 pm and 4.15 pm during French school holidays, led by a crystal-hunter from the Mineralogy Club. The CCVCMB Museum Pass at €25/year covers six valley sites. Easy wheelchair access via a direct lift from the Saint-Michel car park.

src.title: Guided tour by the Guides du Patrimoine
src.description: The Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm can only be visited on a guided tour led by the Guides du Patrimoine Savoie Mont Blanc — expert interpretation of Savoyard peasant life.

src.title: Traditional kitchen (cuzna)
src.description: Discover the cuzna, the kitchen of the Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm in Samoëns: hearth, utensils, period crockery, a faithful staging of early-19th-century life.

src.title: Pèle, the heated common room
src.description: The pèle of the Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm is the living room heated by the stove — the heart of the traditional Savoyard house, where the family would gather.

src.title: Cowshed and hayloft
src.description: At the Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm, discover the cowshed where cows wintered and the hayloft for storing hay — the practical layout of a 1815 Savoyard farm.

src.title: Collection of 2,000 utensils
src.description: The Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm holds a catalogued collection of more than 2,000 period utensils: tools, cookware, clogs, sledges, looms.

src.title: Bread oven demonstration (summer)
src.description: In summer the Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm offers events around the bread oven: a demonstration of how it works and, on some days, a tasting of freshly baked bread.

src.title: Discover the sunny slopes of Samoëns
src.description: The Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm stands on the sunny adrets of Samoëns — slopes traditionally inhabited where the farm produced its own food.

src.title: Combine with a Grand Massif day
src.description: The Clos Parchet Ecomuseum Farm combines with a visit to Samoëns village (Jaysinia garden, Place du Gros Tilleul) and a Grand Massif day out.

src.what_is:
Filenvol is a unique Haute-Savoie adventure park at the foot of the Petit Salève, in Monnetier-Mornex — 30 minutes from Annecy and 15 minutes from Annemasse. Its centrepiece is the Parcabout: a sculpture of ropes, fishing and catamaran nets suspended in the trees between 3 and 15 metres above the ground. Visitors walk, run, bounce and slide on the nets with no safety harness at all.

Three entry/exit points feed the Parcabout, with no fixed direction of travel. Children under 5 have their own safe space, the 'Toupe-Toupe'. Time on the nets is capped at three hours (the landscaped park stays open). The site is open 365 days a year, summer and winter alike.

Fares are clear: free 0–4, seniors 65+ €16, ages 5–65 €25, with group discounts (€24 from 4 people, €20 from 10) and a 10-entry pass at €200 (transferable, no expiry). Birthday packages from €115 (3 h) or €205 (3 h + snack). The Parcadrone, the site's second activity, is temporarily closed.

fields.src: Filenvol est l'un des rares parcs de Haute-Savoie ouvert 365 jours par an. Été (1er avril – 31 octobre) 9h–18h, hiver 10h–17h. Les formules Matinale (dès 9h/10h) et Tardive (dès 16h/15h30) à 19 € sont idéales pour profiter des heures les plus calmes. Le Parcabout reste agréable même par temps gris ou frais grâce à la canopée des arbres — par grande pluie, les filets restent praticables mais glissants (prévoir tenue technique). En hiver et au printemps, l'altitude (≈700 m) garantit une fraîcheur agréable. Le Parcadrone est temporairement fermé au 21 mai 2026 — appeler avant de venir si c'était votre activité principale.

src.q: Comment réserver à Filenvol ?
src.a: Filenvol fonctionne en accès libre pour les visites individuelles, sans réservation préalable. Réservation obligatoire pour groupes, anniversaires, 'nuit dans les filets', séminaires et privatisations via filenvol.com/contact/ ou au +33 (0)4 50 43 08 56.

src.q: Quel est le tarif de Filenvol ?
src.a: Filenvol : entrée unique 3 h 25 € (5-65 ans), gratuit 0-4 ans, sénior +65 ans 16 €. Forfaits dégressifs (24 €/pers à 4, 20 €/pers à 10), carnet 10 entrées 200 € sans validité. Formule Matinale/Tardive 19 €. Anniversaires 115-730 €. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.

src.q: Quelles sont les horaires de Filenvol ?
src.a: Filenvol est ouvert 365 jours par an sans aucune exception. Été (1er avril – 31 octobre) tous les jours 9h–18h. Hiver (1er novembre – 31 mars) tous les jours 10h–17h. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

src.q: Y a-t-il un harnais à Filenvol ?
src.a: Non. À Filenvol, vous déambulez sur les filets sans aucun équipement de sécurité et en toute liberté. Le Parcabout est une sculpture de filets de catamaran et cordes de bateaux suspendus entre 3 et 15 m du sol — pas un parcours accrobranche classique.

src.q: À partir de quel âge à Filenvol ?
src.a: Filenvol accueille les enfants dès la naissance (gratuit 0-4 ans). Les moins de 5 ans disposent du filet sécurisé Toupe-Toupe dédié. À partir de 5 ans, accès aux filets principaux du Parcabout sous responsabilité parentale.

src.q: Le Parcadrone de Filenvol est-il ouvert ?
src.a: Non. Au 21 mai 2026, le Parcadrone de Filenvol est temporairement fermé. Seul le Parcabout et le parc paysager sont accessibles. Appeler le +33 (0)4 50 43 08 56 pour connaître la date de réouverture.

src.q: Filenvol accepte-t-il les animaux ?
src.a: Non. Filenvol interdit explicitement les animaux dans le parc, y compris en laisse. Cette politique est confirmée par Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes Tourisme (mise à jour 21/05/2026).

src.q: Comment venir à Filenvol depuis Annemasse ?
src.a: Filenvol est à 15 min d'Annemasse par D1206 puis D2 jusqu'à Monnetier-Mornex, 300 m après la mairie. En bus : ligne 4 TAC arrêt 'Pont d'Étrembières' puis ligne 170 Proximiti arrêt 'Mornex Grusses' à 13 min à pied. Parking gratuit sur place.

fields.src: Bronze and bells in the Fonderie Paccard workshop at Sevrier on the shore of Lake Annecy

segments.src: Bronze and bells in the Fonderie Paccard workshop at Sevrier on the shore of Lake Annecy
segments.mt: Brąz i dzwony w warsztacie Fonderie Paccard na brzegu jeziora Annecy

src.what_is:
The Fonderie Paccard is one of the last three working bell foundries of its size in France, founded in Sevrier in 1796 by Antoine Paccard. On the western shore of Lake Annecy, the site combines a Bell Museum that traces 230 years of history (museum founded in 1984) and the working foundry, where the seventh generation of bell founders still casts bells shipped around the world.

The foundry has cast more than 120,000 bells since 1796, including some world-famous masterpieces: the Savoyarde (1891, 18,835 kg — the largest bell in France, at Sacré-Cœur in Montmartre), the 57 replicas of the American Liberty Bell (1950) and the World Peace Bell (1998, 33 tonnes — the world's largest swinging bell, Newport, Kentucky).

Several visit formats are offered: self-guided museum visit (€9.50), guided museum + bell tower (€13, daily), guided museum + foundry (€14, weekends only) and the Thursday Discovery Tour (€20) with video, guided museum visit, live bell-casting demonstration and Ars Sonora concert — except in August (no casting due to summer heat). Free under 10.

Toute l'année : Lundi à Samedi 10h-12h30 et 14h-18h. Dimanche et jours fériés : APRÈS-MIDI UNIQUEMENT 14h-18h. Jeudi sur réservation obligatoire (places limitées). Premières 3 semaines d'août : visite musée + fonderie tous les jours 10h-12h30 et 14h-18h. Fermeture annuelle 25 décembre et 1er janvier. Dernière entrée 2 h avant fermeture. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

Visite libre musée : adulte 9,50 €, enfant 10-18 ans 7 €, réduit 8 €. Gratuit -10 ans. Musée + Fonderie (week-end) 14 €/10 €. Musée + Clocher (tous les jours) 13 €/9 €. Visite Découverte le jeudi (vidéo + coulée + concert Ars Sonora) 20 €/15 €/17,50 € - SAUF AOÛT. Passeport annuel 29 €. Groupes ≥10 pers. dès 8 €/pers., 1 gratuit pour 20 payants. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.

Gratuit sur place au 215 route de Piron. Accès direct depuis la D1508 (route du lac).

src.q: Comment réserver à la Fonderie Paccard ?
src.a: Réservation à la Fonderie Paccard par e-mail musee@paccard.fr, formulaire de contact sur musee-paccard.com, ou téléphone 04 50 52 47 11. Réservation OBLIGATOIRE pour la Visite Découverte du jeudi (places limitées) et les visites guidées week-end.

src.q: Quel est le tarif de la Fonderie Paccard ?
src.a: Fonderie Paccard : visite libre musée 9,50 € adulte, 7 € enfant 10-18 ans, -10 ans gratuit. Musée + Fonderie (week-end) 14 €/10 €. Musée + Clocher 13 €/9 €. Visite Découverte du jeudi (avec coulée + concert) 20 €. Passeport annuel 29 €. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.

src.q: Quelles sont les horaires de la Fonderie Paccard ?
src.a: Fonderie Paccard : Lun-Sam 10h-12h30 et 14h-18h. Dim/jours fériés : 14h-18h uniquement. Premières 3 semaines d'août : tous les jours. Fermé 25 déc et 1er janv. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

src.q: Quand voit-on une coulée à la Fonderie Paccard ?
src.a: La coulée des cloches à la Fonderie Paccard a lieu LE JEUDI UNIQUEMENT dans le cadre de la Visite Découverte (20 €, sur réservation), SAUF EN AOÛT (températures trop élevées, pas de coulée). Toute l'année, le musée et la fonderie restent visitables.

src.q: À partir de quel âge à la Fonderie Paccard ?
src.a: La Fonderie Paccard est gratuite pour les moins de 10 ans, tarif enfant 7 € pour 10-18 ans (visite libre musée). La Visite Découverte du jeudi avec coulée et concert est adaptée dès 8-10 ans, déconseillée aux très jeunes enfants (chaleur, bruit).

src.q: Quelles cloches célèbres sont sorties de la Fonderie Paccard ?
src.a: La Fonderie Paccard a coulé plus de 120 000 cloches depuis 1796 dont : la Savoyarde (1891, 18 835 kg, Sacré-Cœur de Montmartre), 57 répliques de la Liberty Bell (1950), la World Peace Bell (1998, 33 t, plus grande oscillante du monde, Newport KY).

src.q: La Fonderie Paccard est-elle accessible aux PMR ?
src.a: Partiellement. La Fonderie Paccard a un musée accessible aux fauteuils roulants. Le clocher et certains ateliers de la fonderie comportent des escaliers. Contacter à l'avance au 04 50 52 47 11 pour adapter la visite à vos besoins.

src.q: Comment venir à la Fonderie Paccard depuis Annecy ?
src.a: La Fonderie Paccard est à 10 min d'Annecy par la D1508 route du lac, fléchage 'Musée Paccard' à Sevrier (215 route de Piron). En bus Sibra ligne 51 arrêt 'Sevrier Piron'. La voie verte du lac d'Annecy passe immédiatement devant — accès vélo idéal.

segments.src: La coulée des cloches à la Fonderie Paccard a lieu LE JEUDI UNIQUEMENT dans le cadre de la Visite Découverte (20 €, sur réservation), SAUF EN AOÛT (températures trop élevées, pas de coulée). Toute l'année, le musée et la fonderie restent visitables.

segments.src: Quelles cloches célèbres sont sorties de la Fonderie Paccard ?
segments.mt: Quelles bloches célèbres sont sorties de la Fonderie Paccard?

segments.src: La Fonderie Paccard a coulé plus de 120 000 cloches depuis 1796 dont : la Savoyarde (1891, 18 835 kg, Sacré-Cœur de Montmartre), 57 répliques de la Liberty Bell (1950), la World Peace Bell (1998, 33 t, plus grande oscillante du monde, Newport KY).

src.what_is:
Golf du Belvédère is a golf school and 9-hole course at Saint-Martin-Bellevue (Fillière municipality), 15 minutes from Annecy. The site combines a modern tiered practice range with covered and open bays, a putting green, a practice bunker and a chipping area — accessible to beginners as well as seasoned players.

The practice range can be used year-round thanks to its covered bays, with a bucket system: €3 per bucket, €30 for 11, €60 for 23, €90 for 36. The 9-hole course offers attractive green fees: €28 weekday, €34 weekend (€32/38 for the day). Ideal to discover golf or keep your level without the budget of a championship course.

The club has a family-friendly atmosphere and offers group and private lessons with a PGA pro, beginner clinics and schools & kids packages. Open every day except Tuesday, 8.30 am - 6.30 pm. Free parking, on-site clubhouse and restaurant. Tel. 04 50 60 31 78.

fields.src: Classified natural site in Servoz (Chamonix valley): 1.6 km wooden footbridge above the gorges and their dramatic waterfalls, including the Cascade de l'Aigle.

segments.src: Classified natural site in Servoz (Chamonix valley): 1.6 km wooden footbridge above the gorges and their dramatic waterfalls, including the Cascade de l'Aigle.
segments.mt: Utajnione miejsce przyrodnicze w dolinie Chamonix: 1,6 km drewniany most nad gorgami i ich dramatyczne wodospady, w tym Cascade de l 'Aigle.

src.what_is:
The Gorges de la Diosaz, in Servoz, are one of the most spectacular natural sites in the Chamonix-Mont-Blanc valley. Listed since 1911, they were fitted out in the late 19th century by the Servoz commune, which still manages them. A 1.6 km wooden and metal walkway winds above the torrent, sometimes dozens of metres above the bottom of the gorges.

The walkway runs from the ticket office downstream up to the Cascade de l'Aigle, the highlight of the visit, passing a sequence of waterfalls and rocky pools: the Cascade Soufflet, the Cascade des Danses, the Cascade du Pont Naturel and several giant's kettles. The water flows from the Mont-Buet and the Aiguilles Rouges range — which is why the flow remains powerful even in midsummer.

Entry is paid during the open season (typically May to mid-October); an information panel at Le Vieux-Servoz covers geology, flora and fauna. The round trip takes 1h30 to 2h on foot. The site is hugely popular with families and pairs well with a visit to Chamonix, the Plateau d'Assy or the Mont-Blanc.

src.q: Is access to the Gorges du Fier paid?
src.a: Yes for the footbridges: adult €5.50 to €6, child 7-15 €3, free under 7. The Chemin des Châteaux around the Gorges du Fier is however free to access.

src.q: When are the Gorges du Fier open?
src.a: The Gorges du Fier are open daily from 15 March to 15 October, from 9:30 am to 6:15 pm in low season and until 7:15 pm in summer (15 June – 10 September). The ticket office closes 1 hour before the site. The site is closed from 16 October to 14 March.

src.q: Can I bring my dog to the Gorges du Fier?
src.a: Not on the footbridges: dogs are not allowed on the trail of the Gorges du Fier. They are however allowed on the free Chemin des Châteaux loop around the site.

src.q: Are the Gorges du Fier accessible to strollers?
src.a: No. Strollers are not allowed on the footbridges of the Gorges du Fier (insufficient width). A baby carrier is mandatory for children who cannot walk independently; a loan is available on site against ID, subject to stock.

src.q: How long does a visit to the Gorges du Fier take?
src.a: A visit to the Gorges du Fier takes about 1 hour round trip, on a route slightly over 1 km long. The path is flat, with benches along the footbridges.

src.q: Is there parking at the Gorges du Fier?
src.a: Yes. Two free car parks serve the Gorges du Fier: the main car park on route des Gorges in Lovagny (close to the site), and a second on the Chavanod side (smaller, accessed via a stepped path).

src.q: Can Gorges du Fier and Château de Montrottier be combined?
src.a: Yes. The Gorges du Fier and the Château de Montrottier are linked by the Chemin des Châteaux, a free 2 km walking loop with 13 interpretation panels. The commune of Lovagny has developed this route to make combining the two sites easy.

src.q: Are the Gorges du Fier accessible to people with reduced mobility?
src.a: No. The footbridges of the Gorges du Fier are not wheelchair accessible due to the insufficient width of the passage. The Chemin des Châteaux around also has uneven terrain.

src.what_is: The Gorges du Pont du Diable form a deep canyon carved by the Dranse river at the hamlet of « Le Jotty » in the commune of La Vernaz, between Thonon-les-Bains and the Vallée d'Aulps. Developed for visitors since the late 19th century, the site is explored via a network of metal walkways anchored to the rock face, close to the water and the glacial potholes. The route climbs about 110 metres and includes many stairs.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Gorges du Pont du Diable?
src.a: Open daily from 4 April to 30 September 2026, including public holidays and rainy days. 10:30am–4:30pm in April–June and September, 9am–6pm in July–August. Last ticket 30 minutes before closing.

src.q: How much is admission?
src.a: €19 for adults (16+), €14 for youths aged 4–15, free up to age 3. Large-family rate (5+ people): €16 per adult and €12.50 per child.

src.q: Can you visit in the rain?
src.a: Yes, the gorge can be visited even in the rain. Sturdy shoes are strongly advised.

src.q: Is the site stroller-accessible?
src.a: No. The walk has a 110 m elevation gain and many stairs. A baby carrier is required for children who cannot walk on their own. Dogs are not allowed.

src.q: What are Annemasse Grand Casino's opening times?
src.a: Annemasse Grand Casino is open Monday to Thursday and Sunday from 10:00 to 02:00, Friday and Saturday from 10:00 to 04:00. Checked on casino-annemasse.partouche.com on 10 June 2026.

src.q: What is the minimum age at Annemasse Grand Casino?
src.a: Access to Annemasse Grand Casino is restricted to adults aged 18 and over, on presentation of ID. Minors are not admitted.

src.q: How much does it cost to enter Annemasse Grand Casino?
src.a: Entry to Annemasse Grand Casino is free. Only games and dining are charged. ID required to enter. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: How many slot machines at Annemasse Grand Casino?
src.a: Annemasse Grand Casino offers 190 slot machines (per the casino; some listings show 169-175): video poker, classic reels and video reels.

src.q: What table games are available at Annemasse Grand Casino?
src.a: Annemasse Grand Casino offers English Roulette, Black Jack, Ultimate Poker, Craps, electronic games and a dedicated poker room.

src.q: Is there a restaurant at Annemasse Grand Casino?
src.a: Yes, Annemasse Grand Casino houses the Le Trèfle restaurant, with a varied menu to extend your evening. Booking recommended at weekends.

src.q: How do I get to Annemasse Grand Casino?
src.a: Annemasse Grand Casino is at 2 avenue de l'Europe in Annemasse, accessible by the cross-border Annemasse-Geneva tramway, Annemasse Léman Express station, or by car via the A40. On-site car park.

src.q: Do I need to book at Annemasse Grand Casino?
src.a: Entry to Annemasse Grand Casino requires no booking, just ID (18+). Booking recommended at Le Trèfle, especially in the evening and at weekends.

Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

Été Grand Parc : adulte 15 € 'Malin' (J-5) / 18 € 'Liberté' ; enfant 3–11 ans 12 € / 14 €. Pack 2 parcs adulte 25 € / 21 € ; enfant 17 € / 14 €. Abonnement été 45 € adulte / 30 € enfant. Grand Parc de Noël : adulte 20 €, enfant 15 €. Médiévales : adulte 20 €, enfant 13 €. Hameau Pâques : adulte 15 €, enfant 10 €. Moins de 3 ans gratuit. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.
Sur grandparc-andilly.com/billeterie — tarif 'Malin' à -3 € adulte si achat 5 jours avant la visite. Achat aux caisses le jour même au tarif 'Liberté'. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.
Gratuit sur place, grande capacité en forêt. Saturation possible week-ends d'été et soirs de Médiévales — arriver avant 10h30.

src.q: How do I book Grotte et Cascade de Seythenex?
src.a: Reservation is not required to visit Grotte et Cascade de Seythenex. Tickets are purchased on-site at the visitor center. For groups of 20 people or more, it is recommended to reserve by phone at 04 50 44 55 97.

src.q: What is the admission price for Grotte et Cascade de Seythenex?
src.a: Adult admission to Grotte et Cascade de Seythenex is €10 and child admission (4–12 years) is €8. Free for under 4 years. Group rate for adults: €9 (from 20 people). Group rate for children: €7. Verified May 14, 2026.

src.q: What are the hours of Grotte et Cascade de Seythenex?
src.a: Grotte et Cascade de Seythenex is open April 18 – September 20. April, May, June, and September: daily 10am–5pm. July and August: daily 9am–6pm. Verified on official site May 14, 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

src.q: Is Grotte et Cascade de Seythenex accessible with small children?
src.a: Strollers cannot access Grotte et Cascade de Seythenex as the underground circuit features stairs, walkways, and narrow passages. Visits are possible by carrying young children. The exterior waterfall circuit is partially accessible to strollers.

src.q: Can I zip-line at Grotte et Cascade de Seythenex?
src.a: Yes, two 150-meter zip-lines soar above the waterfall at 40 meters height at Grotte et Cascade de Seythenex. Minimum weight required: 30 kg. Zip-lines cost extra beyond the cave tour admission (inquire on-site).

src.q: How long does a visit to Grotte et Cascade de Seythenex take?
src.a: The guided tour of Grotte et Cascade de Seythenex lasts approximately 40 minutes. The exterior waterfall circuit takes 20 to 30 minutes. With the mill exhibition and time on-site, plan 1.5 to 2 hours for the entire site.

src.what_is:
The GR® de Pays Tour du Lac d'Annecy is the official FFRandonnée long-distance walking loop that circles Lake Annecy from the hillsides above. According to the federation, 'its 80 km route with 5,140 m of climb runs through alpine pastures, villages, hamlets, limestone summits and natural parks, taking in some legendary passes including La Tournette'. It is the walking counterpart to the lakeside Voie Verte du Lac d'Annecy greenway.

The classic start is at Le Pâquier in Annecy. Typical stages: Annecy → Le Semnoz (1,699 m) → Roc des Bœufs (1,774 m) → Doussard → refuge de Pré Vérel → La Tournette (2,351 m) → Col de Bluffy → back to Annecy. The trail uses GRP yellow-red waymarking and crosses alpine pastures, villages, limestone summits (the Pre-Alpine ridges) and the Bauges and Bornes natural parks.

Free year-round access. Best season: May to October (passes are often snow-covered outside this window). Mountain refuges €50–70/night half-board, advance booking essential in summer. For experienced hikers — stages of 5–8 hours, 800–1,300 m climb per day. Not wheelchair- or stroller-accessible.

src.what_is:
The GR® de Pays Tour du Pays du Mont-Blanc is the French reference walking loop around the Mont-Blanc massif, designed as a 100% Haute-Savoie alternative to the international Tour du Mont-Blanc. It stays on the French side, taking in the Pays du Mont-Blanc and the foothills of the massif.

The trail crosses the main communes of the Pays du Mont-Blanc: Sallanches (classic start), Passy (with the Désert de Platé in view), Servoz, Les Houches, Chamonix (Aiguilles Rouges nature reserve), Saint-Gervais, Les Contamines, Megève and Combloux — a near-360° tour of Mont Blanc as seen from France.

Free year-round access. Difficulty moderate to difficult. Length ~165 km · Pays du Mont-Blanc loop, full trail 6–10 days. Best season: mid-June to mid-September. Not wheelchair-accessible (alpine trail).

src.q: Comment réserver à l'Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux ?
src.a: L'entrée libre à l'Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux ne nécessite pas de réservation — il suffit de venir aux horaires d'ouverture. Pour les anniversaires (8 à 20 enfants), réservation obligatoire sur vetraz.parctortuga.fr.

src.q: Quel est le tarif de l'Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux ?
src.a: Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux : enfant +95 cm jusqu'à 14 ans 14,90 € la journée, enfant -95 cm 9,90 €, moins de 12 mois gratuit. Un adulte gratuit par enfant payant ; adulte supplémentaire 2 €. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.

src.q: Quelles sont les horaires de l'Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux ?
src.a: L'Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux est ouverte 7 jours sur 7 de 9h30 à 20h00 toute l'année. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

src.q: À partir de quel âge l'Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux est-elle adaptée ?
src.a: L'Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux accueille les enfants dès la naissance jusqu'à 12 ans. Un espace dédié aux 0-3 ans avec piscines à balles et jeux mousse est séparé de la zone 3-12 ans avec structures multi-niveaux.

src.q: Les chaussettes sont-elles obligatoires à l'Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux ?
src.a: Oui. Les chaussettes sont obligatoires à l'Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux pour accéder aux structures de jeux, pour des raisons d'hygiène et de sécurité. Apporter ses propres chaussettes ou les acheter sur place à l'accueil.

src.q: Peut-on pique-niquer à l'Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux ?
src.a: Non. Le pique-nique est interdit à l'Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux. La restauration se fait sur place à la Taverne de la Tortue (burgers, poke bowls maison) avec Wifi gratuit et un espace pour 450 personnes assises.

src.q: Quel est le système de jetons à l'Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux ?
src.a: Le karting électrique et les jeux d'arcade de l'Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux fonctionnent avec des jetons en supplément du billet : 1 jeton 2 €, pack 6 jetons 10 €, pack 13 jetons 20 €, pack 35 jetons 50 €.

src.q: Comment venir à l'Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux depuis Genève ?
src.a: L'Île de Tortuga Vétraz-Monthoux est à 15 min de Genève par A40/E25 sortie Annemasse puis D903. Parking gratuit grande capacité. En transports : Léman Express + tram T1 jusqu'à Moillesulaz puis bus TAC, ou taxi depuis Annemasse SNCF (4 km).

src.title: Family walk Châtel → Abondance
src.description: 16 km linear route along the Dranse, flat and stabilised. Refreshment stops possible along the way. Ideal for a half-day or with a return shuttle.

src.title: Mountain bike and e-bike
src.description: Several signed variants: 15 km descent Châtel→Abondance, 20.6 km loop, 13 km climb. Suitable for mountain, hybrid or electric bikes. Hire available in the resorts.

src.title: Discover AOP Abondance cheese
src.description: The path runs through the Abondance valley, home of the AOP Abondance cheese. Dairy farms and the Maison du Fromage can be visited.

src.title: Visit Abondance Abbey
src.description: On arrival in Abondance, the Notre-Dame d'Abondance abbey (12th century) and its painted cloister deserve a stop. A remarkable Historic Monument.

src.title: Watch the river and its wildlife
src.description: The Dranse de Sous-le-Pas is a designated mountain stream: brown trout, dipper, dragonflies. Very photogenic wild stretches.

src.title: Paddle or dip your feet in the water
src.description: Several access points to the river let you dip your feet in summer. Cool water, perfect after a sunny walk.

src.title: Stroller- or senior-friendly outing
src.description: Fully flat and stabilised ground: a rare mountain path accessible with an all-terrain stroller or with reduced mobility (on sections).

segments.src: 16 km linear route along the Dranse, flat and stabilised. Refreshment stops possible along the way. Ideal for a half-day or with a return shuttle.

segments.src: Several signed variants: 15 km descent Châtel→Abondance, 20.6 km loop, 13 km climb. Suitable for mountain, hybrid or electric bikes. Hire available in the resorts.

segments.src: The Dranse de Sous-le-Pas is a designated mountain stream: brown trout, dipper, dragonflies. Very photogenic wild stretches.

src.what_is:
The Jardin des Cimes is a contemporary landscape alpine garden at the Plateau d'Assy in Passy (74190), Haute-Savoie. It is laid out as five themed gardens: a kitchen garden, a sensory garden, an exotic garden, an alpine garden and a rockery — a planted journey through the world's mountain flora.

The setting is the garden's signature feature: an uninterrupted view of the Mont-Blanc massif (4,810 m), the Aiguilles de Chamonix and the Dômes de Miage. Few French gardens can offer this kind of high-mountain backdrop, which the contemporary landscape design plays with throughout the visit.

2026 fares: adult €7.50, free under 12. Season: 8 May to 27 September 2026, daily 10 am–6 pm. Partial wheelchair access (the garden is on a slope).

src.q: How do I book the Jardin des Cinq Sens?
src.a: Online booking is available at jardin5sens.net (ticket office). It is recommended during peak season (July–August). You can also purchase your ticket directly at the Jardin des Cinq Sens entrance desk on the day of your visit.

src.q: What is the price of the Jardin des Cinq Sens?
src.a: The adult price for the Jardin des Cinq Sens is €15. Children 6–18 years: €8.50. Free for children under 6. Family Pass (2 adults + 2 children): €38.50. Student (under 26): €11. Season pass (unlimited entry April–October): €25. Verified 6 June 2026.

src.q: What are the hours for the Jardin des Cinq Sens?
src.a: The Jardin des Cinq Sens is open daily 10 April to 7 September 2026 from 10:00 AM to 6:30 PM (ticket office closes at 5:30 PM). 8 September to 4 October 2026: 10:00 AM to 5:30 PM (ticket office closes at 4:30 PM). Annual closure early October through mid-April. Verified on official website 6 June 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

src.q: Are dogs allowed at the Jardin des Cinq Sens?
src.a: No. Dogs and other pets are not allowed at the Jardin des Cinq Sens, including those on leash, in a bag, or in your arms. Only guide dogs are authorized to accompany their owner.

src.q: Is the Jardin des Cinq Sens accessible by wheelchair?
src.a: Yes. The Jardin des Cinq Sens is wheelchair accessible for seated users, but accompaniment is highly recommended: paths are narrow and graveled, and two passages have slopes exceeding accessibility guidelines. The sensory nature of the visit allows everyone to experience the garden.

src.q: How long does a visit to the Jardin des Cinq Sens take?
src.a: It takes approximately 1 hour to fully enjoy the Jardin des Cinq Sens: to touch textures, smell fragrances, and admire the spaces. Some visitors spend more than 2 hours observing all varieties. You can stay as long as you wish. It is possible to leave for lunch and return later in the day.

src.q: Is there a quiz for children at the Jardin des Cinq Sens?
src.a: Yes. The Jardin des Cinq Sens offers a free discovery quiz for children to learn while having fun. Children under 6 visit the garden free of charge, and a special family rate (2 adults + 2 children: €38.50) is available.

src.q: Where can I park to visit the Jardin des Cinq Sens?
src.a: The village of Yvoire is pedestrian-only. Paid parking is available approximately 300 meters from the Jardin des Cinq Sens. During peak season (July–August), it is recommended to arrive early in the morning to find parking easily.

src.q: Can I visit Yvoire and the Jardin des Cinq Sens on the same day?
src.a: Yes, it's ideal! The Jardin des Cinq Sens is located in the heart of the medieval village of Yvoire, ranked among France's Most Beautiful Villages. Allow 1 to 2 hours for the garden, then stroll through the medieval alleyways, visit the ramparts, and enjoy the shores of Lake Geneva. A full day is perfect to discover everything.

src.what_is:
The Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia in Samoëns (74340) holds the French 'Jardin Remarquable' label and has been under the scientific direction of the Muséum National d'Histoire Naturelle (MNHN) since 1936 — a rare distinction for a municipal botanical garden in France.

The garden displays more than 2,500 alpine species from mountains worldwide: the Alps (France, Switzerland, Italy, Austria), the Pyrenees, the Atlas, the Caucasus, the Himalayas, the Rockies and the Andes. All species are systematically labelled, making it both a stroll and a real outdoor science exhibition.

Free entry year-round when open. Season: 1 May–15 September (10 am–7 pm), then 16 September–31 October (10 am–5 pm). Closed 15 December–15 April and in case of snow, frost, high wind or thunderstorms. Partial wheelchair access — the garden is on a slope.

src.q: Comment réserver le Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia ?
src.a: Pour réserver le Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia, rendez-vous sur le site officiel samoens.com ou téléphonez au +33 (0)4 50 34 49 86. Réservation en ligne conseillée en haute saison juillet-août.

src.q: Quel est le tarif du Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia en 2026 ?
src.a: Tarifs 2026 du Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia : Adulte Gratuit (entrée libre), Enfant Gratuit, Gratuit. Vérifié le 24 mai 2026 sur samoens.com - sous réserve de modifications.

src.q: Quels sont les horaires du Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia ?
src.a: Horaires du Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia : Mai-septembre : tous les jours 10h-19h. Septembre-octobre : 10h-17h. Fermé 15 décembre-15 . Saison : 1er mai - 15 septembre (10h-19h), 16 s. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 24 mai 2026 - sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

src.q: Le Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia est-il accessible aux PMR ?
src.a: PMR au Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia : Partielle (jardin en pente). Vérifier les conditions précises sur place. Pour les activités physiques (accrobranche, karting, sports nautiques), la PMR n'est généralement pas applicable.

src.q: Les animaux sont-ils acceptés au Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia ?
src.a: Animaux au Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia : Acceptés tenus en laisse. Vérifier les conditions précises sur place avant la visite. Précautions particulières pour activités sportives ou sites animaliers selon les espèces.

src.q: Combien de temps pour visiter le Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia ?
src.a: Durée moyenne au Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia : 1-2 h. Selon le profil de visiteur et l'activité choisie. Prévoir plus de temps en haute saison juillet-août en raison de l'affluence touristique.

src.q: Comment se rendre au Jardin botanique alpin La Jaÿsinia ?

src.what_is:
Les Jardins Secrets is a remarkable garden in Vaulx, about fifteen minutes north of Annecy. Classified 'Remarkable Garden' by the French Ministry of Culture, the site unfolds across 7000 m² with around thirty garden spaces inspired by Middle-Eastern patios: inner courtyards, fountains, carved wooden kiosks, hedged walks, mosaics. It is the work of a passionate family who built this unique place over decades.

The visit follows your own rhythm along a suggested route linking the 33 spaces: Mediterranean patios, water garden, intimate alcoves, trompe-l'œil views. Plan 1h30 to 2h30 depending on pace. A tea room offers a tasty break, and a shop sells crafts and plants. The site also hosts weddings and private events, and offers guided visits on request.

The garden is open mid-April to mid-October, daily in high season. Adult ~€12-14, child ~€6-8, free for very young (2026 prices to confirm). Free car park on site. Access from Annecy via the D1508. Ideal for a family half-day or romantic getaway near Lake Annecy.

src.what_is:
The Parc des Jardins de Haute-Savoie is a landscaped botanical garden located at La Balme-de-Sillingy, about 15 minutes north-west of Annecy. The site brings together around fifty themed plots and is home to the French national collection of Asian flowering cherries (Prunus genus), considered one of the most complete in France. Paths wind between the collections, alternating ornamental beds, sensory garden, shade structure, ponds and remarkable tree areas.

Visits are self-guided along marked paths. Atmospheres change with the seasons: spectacular cherry blossom in April-May, perennial beds and rose garden in June-July, hydrangeas and grasses in summer, autumn colours in September-October. The park suits botany enthusiasts (labelled collections, identification programme) as much as families looking for a calm, accessible nature outing.

The site also includes a shade structure sheltering shade-loving plants, a sensory garden with a seasonal barefoot trail, and several shaded rest areas. Opening is seasonal (typically April to October) — check the official website for exact times. Occasional events (themed guided tours, gardening workshops, plant sales) follow the annual calendar.

src.q: From what age can you drive at Urban Kart-In?
src.a: Adapted karts are accessible from around age 10, subject to size. Leisure and more powerful karts are accessible to teens and adults. Check conditions on urbankart-in.com.

src.q: Are the karts electric?
src.a: Yes, Urban Kart-In uses 100% electric karts: no noise, no emissions, instant start-up and constant torque. A modern and eco-friendly experience without fuel smell.

src.q: How much does a session cost?
src.a: Pricing is generally €20-30 per session depending on duration and kart type. Multi-session packs, birthday packages and group rates available. Exact 2026 prices to confirm.

src.q: How long is a session?
src.a: A single session represents around 10 minutes of track time. A full grand prix (practice + qualifying + final + podium) lasts around 1h30. Allow 30 extra minutes for briefing and kit-up.

src.q: Do I need to book?
src.a: Booking is recommended, especially on weekends, school holidays and for groups/stag/hen/birthdays/seminars. Book online on urbankart-in.com or by phone.

src.q: Is the activity open year-round?
src.a: Yes, Urban Kart-In is indoor and air-conditioned, so the activity is available all year. An excellent alternative in rain, summer heat or winter.

src.q: Can I organise a birthday or seminar?
src.a: Yes, Urban Kart-In offers birthday packages (2 sessions + private room for cake/drinks, souvenir podium) and tailored corporate grand prix (meeting room, catering, official ranking). Contact the venue for a quote.

src.what_is:
The MK Circuit, at Scientrier, is an outdoor karting track approved FFSA category 1 — competition level — and home to a French karting school. The fleet includes adult karts, children's karts and 2-stroke karts, with session rentals and professional supervision suitable for families from age 7.

According to the official mk-circuit.com, the track hosts the French karting school accredited by the FFSA: introductory courses for young drivers and stages from beginner to advanced.

2026 fares: adult €28, child €19 (from age 7). Season: March to November. Opening hours: Wednesdays, weekends and school holidays 2 pm–6 pm; daily 10 am–7 pm in summer; weather-dependent. Partial accessibility (paddock access).

src.what_is:
Karting de Rumilly (Karting Evasion) is the largest outdoor karting complex in Savoie, Haute-Savoie and Isère, located at the heart of the Base de Loisirs des Pérouses in Rumilly (74150). With its two FFSA-approved circuits — a main 1150-metre track for experienced drivers and a 550-metre track ideal for beginners — this circuit stands out as a major motorsport reference in the Alps, halfway between Annecy, Aix-les-Bains and Chambéry.

What sets Karting de Rumilly apart from other Haute-Savoie circuits is its fleet of 100 karts suited to all ages: baby karts from age 3, two-seater karts from age 4, children's karts from age 7, up to high-performance adult karts. The site also hosts the French Karting School (EFK), formerly labeled Charles Germain, three times voted best driving school in France and affiliated with FFSA. Whether beginner or experienced driver, you benefit from qualified instructors' supervision to progress safely.

After your racing sessions, head to Pub Le Melbourne, a friendly space inspired by the F1 spirit and street art, to enjoy house specialties. The complex also organizes group packages (Sprint Race, Mini Grand Prix, Endurance), corporate seminars, birthday parties and competitive events year-round.

2026 Prices: Adult €30, Child €25, Teen €27 (10-min session). Sprint Race group package from €69 per person. Open 7 days/week 9am-6pm (winter) or 9am-7pm (summer), except 25 December and 1 January. Wheelchair access: partial (paddock).

src.what_is:
The Team Bouvier 74, at Pringy (commune of Annecy), is a non-profit club dedicated to karting and motocross. It operates on a traditional club model: access is reserved to members and supervised courses, with no walk-in commercial hire — unlike the karting tracks fully open to the public.

The club covers both disciplines: karting on its own circuit and motocross on dedicated land. It welcomes members for training and stages, and is a niche option in the Annecy area for those who want a club-based motorsport experience rather than a one-off paying activity.

2026 membership: adult €50/year, child €30/year. Season: year-round (weather-dependent). Hours: by booking only — non-profit club with limited access. Not wheelchair-accessible (motorsport activity).

src.what_is:
The Thônes karting venue is a karting complex located in Thônes, in the Aravis valley, Haute-Savoie. On site, a technical track with tight bends, straights and chicanes, accessible to adults (powerful karts) and to children (junior karts with limited speed). Full equipment is provided (full-face helmet, hygiene hood, suit on request) and a safety briefing precedes each session.

Formats and pricing. Sessions are sold by duration: 10 min (introduction, ~€18-22), 20 min in two sessions (ideal to learn the track, ~€30-40), 30 min and more (~€50+). Group rates available. Birthday, hen/stag, team-building and seminar packages: Grand-Prix races, team endurance, rankings, podium and cup. Booking required for groups; advised for individuals at weekends and during school holidays.

Audience and conditions. Junior karts available from age 7-8 (min height ~1.25 m); adult karts from age 14 (min height ~1.45 m); some twin-seat options let a younger child ride with an adult. Cover clothing advised (trousers, long sleeves, closed shoes). Indoor venues open year-round — outdoor tracks follow the season. Ideal all-weather activity and rainy-day or après-ski alternative in the valley.

src.what_is:
La Forêt Enchantée is a family theme park in Sillingy, about twenty minutes north of Annecy. Set in a shaded woodland, it is entirely dedicated to children aged 2 to 12 and their families. Around forty attractions are spread around the park: rides, giant slides, mini-quads, trampolines, inflatables, water games in summer, and a little train to tour the facilities.

The spirit is firmly family-oriented: single all-in ticket, short queues, attentive supervision. Children can freely move from one attraction to another, accompanied by an adult or not depending on their age. Picnic areas are arranged under the trees, a snack restaurant offers simple meals and ice cream, and shaded areas allow breaks between activities.

The season runs April to October, with extended hours on weekends, public holidays and school holidays. La Forêt Enchantée is very popular for birthdays (dedicated packages with private room and snack) and welcomes groups (leisure centres, schools). Booking is recommended for groups; walk-in for individual visits.

src.a: €5.50 adult, €3 under 18 and reduced, free under 6. Group €60.

src.a: No for individuals. Required for group visits (Tue-Fri 9 or 10.30 am).

src.q: Is it good for kids?
src.a: Yes, hands-on exhibits for all ages. Workshops during holidays. Free under 6.

src.q: Is there a cinema?
src.a: Not in the CCSTI itself, but the La Turbine hub has a cinema nearby.

src.q: Is the centre accessible?
src.a: Yes, fully accessible to reduced-mobility visitors.

src.q: How do I get there?
src.a: Cran-Gevrier (Annecy), at La Turbine cultural hub. By Sibra bus from central Annecy or car (on-site parking).

src.q: Can you swim in the Lac Bénit?
src.a: No. Swimming is prohibited in the Lac Bénit by a permanent municipal order from the communes of Mont-Saxonnex and Marnaz, along with all water activities (boating, diving, ice skating). The site is listed under Natura 2000.

src.q: Is the Lac Bénit accessible right now?
src.a: The Lac Bénit site is still reachable on foot, but since 6 January 2026 a municipal order has closed the Bété trail. Only the climb from the Morsulaz car park is usable. Check the commune website before setting out.

src.q: Can I bring my dog to the Lac Bénit?
src.a: Yes, dogs are allowed at the Lac Bénit on a leash. Dog swimming is prohibited, as for humans, to protect the lake's fauna.

src.q: Is there parking at the Lac Bénit?
src.a: Yes. Two free car parks allow walking access to the Lac Bénit: the Bété car park and the Morsulaz car park, both in Mont-Saxonnex. Motor traffic beyond these points is prohibited.

src.q: Is the hike to the Lac Bénit suitable for children?
src.a: The climb to the Lac Bénit from Morsulaz is short but steep (significant average gradient). The route remains accessible to families used to mountain walking. The gentler Bété trail is currently closed by municipal order.

src.q: Is there a refreshment bar at the Lac Bénit?
src.a: Yes. An alpine refreshment bar operates by the Lac Bénit, open from Pentecost to late September according to the Tourist Office. Precise hours are not published on an official source.

src.q: Is bivouac allowed at the Lac Bénit?
src.a: Bivouac is allowed only between 7 pm and 9 am, with no ground fire (stove permitted). Bivouac around the Lac Bénit itself is prohibited, under the municipal order.

src.q: Are drones allowed at the Lac Bénit?
src.a: No. Drone overflight of the Lac Bénit site is prohibited by municipal order.

src.q: How big is the Lac Bénit?
src.a: The Lac Bénit covers 4.1 hectares with a maximum depth of 8.7 metres, at 1,452 m elevation. Its catchment area is 90.6 hectares.

src.what_is:
Lac Blanc is a glacial lake located at 2,352 metres elevation in the Aiguilles Rouges massif, in Chamonix-Mont-Blanc municipality. The lake comprises two bodies of water linked by a small watercourse and sits within the Aiguilles Rouges national nature reserve, a protected area created to preserve alpine fauna and flora. From its shores, Lac Blanc offers exceptional panoramic views of Mont-Blanc massif, Aiguille Verte, Grandes Jorasses, Mer de Glace, and Argentière glacier.

Several hiking routes provide access to the lake. The most common and family-friendly route departs from the Flégère cable car (1,877 m) at Les Praz de Chamonix, followed by approximately 2 hours' walking to reach the lake. Experienced hikers can take the route from Col des Montets or Tré-le-Champ (approximately 3 hours' walking), featuring technical passages and metal ladders. Lac Blanc lies on the Tour du Mont-Blanc (TMB) route, a classic stage for long-distance hikers.

A mountain refuge, Refuge du Lac Blanc, is located immediately next to the lake, offering food and lodging. The current refuge was rebuilt after an avalanche destroyed the original structure in 1986. Below the lake, Lacs des Chéserys offer complementary views and are a popular descent stop. Camping is permitted only at Lacs des Chéserys (between 7 pm and 9 am), not at Lac Blanc itself. Swimming is prohibited in Lac Blanc and Lacs des Chéserys under a prefectural decree of 22 May 2025.

segments.src: is a glacial lake located at 2,352 metres elevation in the Aiguilles Rouges massif, in Chamonix-Mont-Blanc municipality. The lake comprises two bodies of water linked by a small watercourse and sits within the Aiguilles Rouges national nature reserve, a protected area created to preserve alpine fauna and flora. From its shores, Lac Blanc offers exceptional panoramic views of Mont-Blanc massif, Aiguille Verte, Grandes Jorasses, Mer de Glace, and Argentière glacier.
segments.mt: jest jeziorem lodowym położonym na wysokości 2,352 metrów w masywie Aiguilles Rouges, w gminie Chamonix-Mont-Blanc. Jezioro składa się z dwóch części wód połączonych małym ciekiem wodnym i znajduje się w Narodowym Rezerwacie Przyrody Aiguilles Rouges, chronionym obszarze stworzonym w celu zachowania alpejskiej fauny i flory. Z jego brzegów Lac Blanc oferuje wyjątkowe panoramiczne widoki na masyw Mont-Blanc, Aiguille Verte, Grandes Jorasses, Mer de Glace i Argentière lodowiec.

segments.src: Several hiking routes provide access to the lake. The most common and family-friendly route departs from the Flégère cable car (1,877 m) at Les Praz de Chamonix, followed by approximately 2 hours' walking to reach the lake. Experienced hikers can take the route from Col des Montets or Tré-le-Champ (approximately 3 hours' walking), featuring technical passages and metal ladders. Lac Blanc lies on the Tour du Mont-Blanc (TMB) route, a classic stage for long-distance hikers.
segments.mt: Kilka szlaków turystycznych zapewnia dostęp do jeziora. Najczęstsza i najbardziej rodzinna trasa odjeżdża z kolejki linowej Flégère (1877 m) w Les Praz de Chamonix, a następnie około 2 godzin spacerem do jeziora. Doświadczeni turyści mogą wybrać trasę z Col des Montets lub Tré- le- Champ (około 3 godzin spaceru), wyposażony w przejścia techniczne i drabiny metalowe. Lac Blanc leży na trasie Tour du Mont-Blanc (TMB), klasyczny etap dla turystów długodystansowych.

segments.src: A mountain refuge, Refuge du Lac Blanc, is located immediately next to the lake, offering food and lodging. The current refuge was rebuilt after an avalanche destroyed the original structure in 1986. Below the lake, Lacs des Chéserys offer complementary views and are a popular descent stop. Camping is permitted only at Lacs des Chéserys (between 7 pm and 9 am), not at Lac Blanc itself. Swimming is prohibited in Lac Blanc and Lacs des Chéserys under a prefectural decree of 22 May 2025.
segments.mt: Schronisko górskie, Refuge du Lac Blanc, znajduje się bezpośrednio obok jeziora, oferując jedzenie i zakwaterowanie. Obecne schronienie zostało odbudowane po tym, jak lawina zniszczyła pierwotną strukturę w 1986 roku. Pod jeziorem Lacs des Chéseris oferuje dodatkowe widoki i są popularnym przystankiem schodzenia. Kemping jest dozwolony tylko w Lacs des Chéseris (między 19: 00 a 9: 00), a nie w samym Lac Blanc. Pływanie jest zabronione w Lac Blanc i Lacs des Chéseris na mocy dekretu prefekturalnego z dnia 22 maja 2025 r.

src.q: Is access to Lac Blanc chargeable?
src.a: Access to Lac Blanc itself is free. However, the Flégère cable car (the most common access option) is payable: approximately €26 return for adults, €24.70 for children 5–14 years (indicative rates). Lac Blanc is also accessible free of charge on foot from Col des Montets.

src.q: How do I reach Lac Blanc?
src.a: Two main routes: (1) From Les Praz de Chamonix, Flégère cable car to 1,877 m, then 2 hours' walking to Lac Blanc. (2) From Tré-le-Champ car park at Col des Montets, 3 hours' hiking with technical passages (ladders). The Flégère option is more accessible.

src.q: What is the best season to visit Lac Blanc?
src.a: June to October. September is often preferred: lower crowds, exceptional light, stable conditions. July–August are very busy (up to 1,500 visitors daily). In June, the upper lake may remain partially frozen. Starting early morning allows you to avoid crowds and observe wildlife.

src.q: Are dogs allowed at Lac Blanc?
src.a: No. Dogs are prohibited at Lac Blanc as it sits within the Aiguilles Rouges national nature reserve. This restriction protects wild fauna (ibex, marmots, chamois) and fragile ecosystems.

src.q: Can you swim in Lac Blanc?
src.a: No. Swimming is prohibited in Lac Blanc and Lacs des Chéserys under a prefectural decree of 22 May 2025. This measure protects the fragile alpine lake ecosystem and limits visitor impact.

src.q: Is Lac Blanc accessible as a family with children?
src.a: Yes, for children aged 8–10 and up who are accustomed to mountain hiking. The Flégère route is marked and secure but includes technical passages (metal ladders) and 570 m elevation gain. Bring hiking boots, water, and snacks.

src.q: Can you camp at Lac Blanc?
src.a: No. Camping is prohibited at Lac Blanc itself. However, it is permitted at Lacs des Chéserys (below) between 7 pm and 9 am only. In high season (notably 14 July), up to one hundred tents may be set up at Chéserys.

src.q: Is there a refuge at Lac Blanc?
src.a: Yes. Refuge du Lac Blanc, located immediately next to the lake at 2,352 m elevation, offers food and lodging June to September. Cash payment only. The current refuge was rebuilt after the 1986 avalanche that destroyed the original building.

src.what_is:
Lac Cornu is a high mountain lake located at 2,275 meters altitude in the Aiguilles Rouges massif, in the commune of Chamonix-Mont-Blanc. It lies at the heart of the Aiguilles Rouges National Nature Reserve, in a mineral and wild setting dominated by Aiguille de Charlanon. The lake owes its name to its distinctive crescent shape, visible from Cornu Pass (2,414 m) which overlooks it.

With an area of 5.4 hectares, Lac Cornu is one of the emblematic high-altitude lakes of the Aiguilles Rouges massif, with a maximum depth of 22 meters. The lake is frozen or snow-covered for approximately nine months per year; the summer access period is short, typically from early July to mid-September. Fishing is permitted (Arctic char and lake trout), subject to a license, and the lake offers a peaceful setting facing the Mont-Blanc massif.

segments.src: is a high mountain lake located at 2,275 meters altitude in the Aiguilles Rouges massif, in the commune of Chamonix-Mont-Blanc. It lies at the heart of the Aiguilles Rouges National Nature Reserve, in a mineral and wild setting dominated by Aiguille de Charlanon. The lake owes its name to its distinctive crescent shape, visible from Cornu Pass (2,414 m) which overlooks it.
segments.mt: jest wysokim górskim jeziorem położonym na wysokości 275 metrów w masywie Aiguilles Rouges, w gminie Chamonix-Mont-Blanc. Znajduje się w sercu Narodowego Rezerwatu Przyrody Aiguilles Rouges, w środowisku mineralnym i dzikim zdominowanym przez Aiguille de Charlanon. Jezioro zawdzięcza swoją nazwę charakterystycznym kształtem półksiężyca, widocznym z Cornu Pass (2,414 m), który go nie widzi.

segments.src: is one of the emblematic high-altitude lakes of the Aiguilles Rouges massif, with a maximum depth of 22 meters. The lake is frozen or snow-covered for approximately nine months per year; the summer access period is short, typically from early July to mid-September. Fishing is permitted (Arctic char and lake trout), subject to a license, and the lake offers a peaceful setting facing the Mont-Blanc massif.
segments.mt: jest jednym z symbolicznych jezior wysokościowych masywu Aiguilles Rouges, o maksymalnej głębokości 22 metrów. Jezioro jest zamarznięte lub pokryte śniegiem przez około dziewięć miesięcy rocznie; letni okres dostępu jest krótki, zazwyczaj od początku lipca do połowy września. Wędkowanie jest dozwolone (arktyczny czar i pstrąg jeziorowy), z zastrzeżeniem licencji, a jezioro oferuje spokojne położenie przed masywem Mont-Blanc.

src.title: Hike to the lake from Planpraz
src.description: Mountain trail from the Planpraz cable car arrival point (2,000 m), with scree traversal and ascent to Cornu Pass before descending to the lake. Allow 3h30 round trip.

src.title: Observe alpine wildlife
src.description: Ibex, chamois and marmots frequent the slopes around the lake. The nature reserve provides a pristine setting for observing alpine fauna.

src.title: Fish in the lake
src.description: Fishing is permitted in Lac Cornu (Arctic char, lake trout) subject to a valid fishing license. Wild and peaceful setting at 2,275 m altitude.

src.title: Continue to the Lacs Noirs
src.description: From Cornu Pass, you can continue to Lacs Noirs (2,535 m) by following the yellow trail markers. Allow approximately 1 hour additional round trip from the pass.

src.title: Admire the Mont-Blanc panorama
src.description: Clear view of the Mont-Blanc massif from Cornu Pass and the lake surroundings. Exceptional viewpoint overlooking the glaciers and Chamonix Needles.

src.title: Photograph the lake and its reflections
src.description: The lake offers remarkable reflections of surrounding peaks, notably Aiguille de Charlanon. Morning light is particularly beautiful in July-August.

segments.src: Hike to the lake from Planpraz
Mountain trail from the Planpraz cable car arrival point (2,000 m), with scree traversal and ascent to Cornu Pass before descending to the lake. Allow 3h30 round trip.
segments.mt: Szlak górski od punktu przybycia kolejki linowej Planpraz (2000 m), z krzywą trawersacją i wzniesieniem się do Cornu Pass przed zejściem do jeziora. Pozwól 3h30 w obie strony.

segments.src: Ibex, chamois and marmots frequent the slopes around the lake. The nature reserve provides a pristine setting for observing alpine fauna.
segments.mt: Ibex, chamois i marmots często na zboczach wokół jeziora. Rezerwat przyrody zapewnia nieskazitelne warunki do obserwacji alpejskiej fauny.

segments.src: Fishing is permitted in Lac Cornu (Arctic char, lake trout) subject to a valid fishing license. Wild and peaceful setting at 2,275 m altitude.
segments.mt: Wędkowanie jest dozwolone w Lac Cornu (arktyczny znak, pstrąg jeziorny) z zastrzeżeniem ważnej licencji połowowej. Dzikie i spokojne położenie na wysokości 275 m.

segments.src: From Cornu Pass, you can continue to Lacs Noirs (2,535 m) by following the yellow trail markers. Allow approximately 1 hour additional round trip from the pass.
segments.mt: Z Cornu Pass, można kontynuować Lacs Noirs (2535 m), podążając za żółtymi markery szlaków. Należy odstawić na 1 godzinę dodatkową podróż w obie strony.

segments.src: Clear view of the Mont-Blanc massif from Cornu Pass and the lake surroundings. Exceptional viewpoint overlooking the glaciers and Chamonix Needles.
segments.mt: Przejrzysty widok na masyw Mont-Blanc od Cornu Pass i okolice jeziora. Wyjątkowy punkt widzenia z widokiem na lodowce i igły Chamonix.

segments.src: Photograph the lake and its reflections
The lake offers remarkable reflections of surrounding peaks, notably Aiguille de Charlanon. Morning light is particularly beautiful in July-August.
segments.mt: Jezioro oferuje niezwykłe odbicia otaczających szczytów, zwłaszcza Aiguille de Charlanon. Poranne światło jest szczególnie piękne w lipcu-Sierpień.

fields.src: Lac Cornu is accessible only during the summer season, typically from early July to mid-September. The lake is frozen or snow-covered for the rest of the year (approximately 9 months). The best period is between mid-July and late August, when residual snow has melted and the trail is well clear. In early season (June-early July), snow patches may remain on the trail, making progress more difficult. Morning light (departing early from Planpraz) offers spectacular reflections on the lake. Note: the Planpraz cable car operates on a summer schedule (typically late June to late September) — verify hours before departing and plan to descend before closing (last departure often around 5–6 p.m.).

Note: the Planpraz cable car operates on a summer schedule (typically late June to late September) — verify hours before departing and plan to descend before closing (last departure often around 5–6 p.m.).
segments.mt: Lac Cornu jest dostępny tylko w sezonie letnim, zazwyczaj od początku lipca do połowy września. Jezioro jest zamarznięte lub pokryte śniegiem przez resztę roku (około 9 miesięcy). Najlepszym okresem jest od połowy lipca do końca sierpnia, kiedy pozostałości śniegu stopił i szlak jest dobrze jasne. Na początku sezonu (czerwiec - początek lipca), łaty śnieżne mogą pozostać na szlaku, co utrudnia postęp. Poranne światło (wychodzące wcześnie z Planpraz) oferuje spektakularne odbicia na jeziorze. Uwaga: kolejka kolejowa Planpraz działa na letnim harmonogramie (zazwyczaj pod koniec czerwca do końca września) - weryfikuje godziny przed odlotem i planuje zejście przed zamknięciem (ostatni wyjazd często około godz. 17.00-18.00).

src.what_is:
The Lac de Passy, also called the Îles Leisure Base, is an artificial lake over one kilometre long covering approximately 50 hectares, located at 547 metres elevation in the Arve valley. Created in the 1970s following excavation of materials needed for construction of the A40 motorway (the White Road), it is today the largest developed lake in the Mont-Blanc region.

This remarkable site offers a developed sandy beach with supervised swimming zone in July and August, grassy creeks, wooden jetties, and numerous water and land-based activities: stand-up paddle, pedal boats, electric boats, fishing (second-category waters with night fishing permitted), mini-golf, go-kart racing, fitness trail, mountain-bike path, and orienteering course. A 2 km trail circles the lake in approximately 30 minutes, accessible by stroller, with children's play areas.

The lake offers exceptional views of the Mont-Blanc massif and the Fiz range. Accessible year-round, it provides a relaxing setting in summer and winter alike (walks along the snow-covered lake). A 3-star campsite and snack restaurant are on-site. The site also hosts aquabike courses during the summer season.

Stworzony w latach 70. po wykopaniu materiałów potrzebnych do budowy autostrady A40 (White Road), jest obecnie największym rozwiniętym jeziorem w regionie Mont-Blanc.

Na miejscu znajduje się trzygwiazdkowy kemping i restauracja z przekąskami. Strona prowadzi również kursy aquabike w sezonie letnim.

src.q: Can you swim in the Dronières Lake?
src.a: No. Swimming is not allowed in the Dronières Lake. Swimming takes place in the adjacent Espace Bernard Pellarin aquatic centre, a separate, paid and seasonal facility run by the Communauté de communes du Pays de Cruseilles.

src.q: Is access to the Dronières Lake paid?
src.a: No. Access to the Dronières Lake and Parc des Dronières is free and open year-round. Only a few activities are paid: fishing (permit required), the TactiQ tree-top course, and the adjacent pool.

src.q: Can I bring my dog to the Dronières Lake?
src.a: Yes. Animals are allowed at the Parc des Dronières and on the Dronières Lake loop. Follow standard rules: leash recommended, clean up after your dog, and keep clear of the pool area in season.

src.q: Is there parking at the Dronières Lake?
src.a: Yes, the Dronières Lake has two free car parks: the tennis car park (start of the lake loop) and the pool car park. Coach parking is also available.

src.q: Do I need a permit to fish at the Dronières Lake?
src.a: Yes. The Dronières Lake is classified as a closed water and managed by the association Les Eaux Closes. A specific permit is required (€13/day, €80/year in 2025). The French federal FNPF / AAPPMA card is not valid here. Permits are sold at La Fleuristerie and Gamm Vert in Cruseilles, or online via HelloAsso.

src.q: What fish species can be caught at the Dronières Lake?
src.a: At the Dronières Lake you can catch carp, roach, rudd, tench, zander, pike, perch and trout. Les Eaux Closes regularly stocks the lake with trout.

src.q: How long is the Dronières Lake loop?
src.a: The Dronières Lake loop is 2 km long, about 30 minutes on foot. The path is stabilised, accessible to strollers, and starts from the tennis car park.

src.q: Is the Dronières Lake accessible for people with reduced mobility?
src.a: The Dronières Lake loop is accessible to strollers thanks to its stabilised path. The adjacent aquatic centre is equipped for people with reduced mobility.

src.q: What is there for children at the Dronières Lake?
src.a: The Parc des Dronières surrounding the Dronières Lake offers an animal park with fallow deer, red deer and mouflons (feeding not allowed), the TactiQ tree-top adventure course, a 2.5 km fitness trail, a permanent orienteering course, and new playgrounds delivered in 2026.

src.q: How do I get to the Dronières Lake by bus from Annecy or Geneva?
src.a: Line 272 Alpbus / tpg / Cars Région serves Cruseilles from Annecy and Geneva Airport, with 21 return trips per day. The closest stop to the Dronières Lake is Cruseilles – Chef-Lieu, followed by about 1 km on foot along the Route des Dronières.

segments.src: L'Hachez-Vous Annecy — Axe throwing & games bar | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: L'Hachez-Vous Annecy - Rzucanie siekierą i gry bar 124; Loisirs 74

src.what_is:
L'Hachez-Vous Annecy is the first axe-throwing centre in the Annecy area, located in Annecy, Haute-Savoie. The venue offers 7 individual lanes, allowing up to 21 people to play simultaneously, in the warm atmosphere of a games bar. Each session starts with a safety briefing led by a qualified coach who teaches the proper technique (grip, stance, rotational or no-spin throw), before moving on to various games and challenges to make the experience accessible for beginners and seasoned throwers alike.

Who is it for? The activity is open from age 14 and is a hit with groups of friends, stag/hen parties, teen and adult birthdays, corporate team-building events or couples looking for an original night out. The centre offers coached sessions of about 1h-1h30, lane privatisation and full birthday packages with cake and drinks. The venue also features a full games bar — billiards, foosball, electronic darts, board games — and a selection of local craft beers to extend the evening after your session.

Booking and access. Booking is strongly recommended, especially weekends and evenings: it can be done online on lancerdehache-annecy.fr or by phone. Prices vary by package (single session, birthday, stag/hen, privatisation) and generally run around €20-30 per person for a session. The activity is indoor, open year-round and ideal in rainy weather or for evenings. Comfortable clothes and closed shoes recommended; the coaches provide all equipment and ensure safety at all times.

src.q: From what age can you throw an axe in Annecy?
src.a: The activity is open from age 14 at L'Hachez-Vous Annecy. Minors must be accompanied or have parental authorisation per the centre's conditions.

src.q: How much does a session cost?
src.a: A coached session usually costs €20-30 per person depending on the package (single session, birthday, stag/hen, privatisation). Exact 2026 prices to confirm on lancerdehache-annecy.fr.

src.q: How long is a session?
src.a: A standard session lasts about 1h to 1h30, including safety briefing and learning. Plan a little more for group packages with tournament.

src.q: Do I need to book?
src.a: Yes, booking is strongly recommended, especially Friday/Saturday evenings, Sundays and school holidays. Book online or by phone.

src.q: Is the activity dangerous?
src.a: No, as long as the coach's instructions are followed. The centre is designed with netting, regulation targets and systematic briefings. The coach is present throughout.

src.q: Can the venue be privatised?
src.a: Yes, full or partial privatisation packages exist for stag/hen parties, birthdays and businesses. Request a quote directly from the centre.

src.q: What to do after the session?
src.a: The games bar (pool, foosball, darts, board games) and the craft-beer selection make it easy to extend the evening on site.

fields.src: Laser Game Evolution Annemasse — Laser arena in Ville-la-Grand | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Laser Game Evolution Annemasse — Laser arena in Ville-la-Grand | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Laser Game Evolution Annemasse - Arena laserowa w Ville-la-Grand XQ124; Loisirs 74

src.q: Where is Laser Game Evolution Annemasse?
src.a: The centre is in Ville-la-Grand (74100), right next to Annemasse, 15 minutes from Geneva via the Mon-Idée or Moillesulaz border crossings, and 45 minutes from Annecy on the A41.

src.q: From what age can you play?
src.a: Laser Game Evolution Annemasse welcomes players from around age 8, provided they are comfortable wearing the vest. For younger players, some centres adapt the equipment. Check with the venue.

src.q: How much does a game cost?
src.a: A game generally costs €9-12. Bundles of 2 or 3 games at lower per-game rates are available, along with group rates, birthday packages and stag/hen rates. Exact 2026 prices to confirm.

src.q: How long is a game?
src.a: Each game lasts 15-20 minutes, plus around 10 minutes of briefing before and debriefing after. With a 2-game pack, plan around 1h on site.

src.q: Can I pay in Swiss francs?
src.a: Main payment is in euros. For CHF or Swiss bank card payment, check directly with the centre. Most international cards are accepted.

src.q: Can I organise a birthday party?
src.a: Yes, the laser game offers dedicated birthday packages: 2 games + private room (subject to availability) for cake and drinks, personalised invitations and souvenir photo. Book several weeks ahead.

src.q: Is there parking on site?
src.a: Parking is available on site. Public transport from Annemasse (TAC) or Geneva (TPG via Annemasse) is also an option.

segments.src: From what age can you play?
Laser Game Evolution Annemasse welcomes players from around age 8, provided they are comfortable wearing the vest. For younger players, some centres adapt the equipment. Check with the venue.
segments.mt: Laser Game Evolution Annemasse wita graczy w wieku około 8 lat, pod warunkiem, że są wygodne w kamizelce. Dla młodszych graczy niektóre ośrodki dostosowują sprzęt. Sprawdź w miejscu.

segments.src: How much does a game cost?
A game generally costs €9-12. Bundles of 2 or 3 games at lower per-game rates are available, along with group rates, birthday packages and stag/hen rates. Exact 2026 prices to confirm.
segments.mt: Gra zazwyczaj kosztuje 9- 12 euro. Dostępne są zestawy 2 lub 3 gier o niższych wskaźnikach przegrywania, wraz z stawkami grupowymi, pakietami urodzinowymi i stawkami jelenia / kury. Dokładne ceny 2026 do potwierdzenia.

segments.src: Each game lasts 15-20 minutes, plus around 10 minutes of briefing before and debriefing after. With a 2-game pack, plan around 1h on site.
segments.mt: Każda gra trwa 15- 20 minut, plus około 10 minut odprawa przed i odprawa po. Z 2- pakiet gier, zaplanować około 1h na miejscu.

segments.src: Can I pay in Swiss francs?
Main payment is in euros. For CHF or Swiss bank card payment, check directly with the centre. Most international cards are accepted.
segments.mt: Główną płatność jest w euro. W przypadku płatności kartą bankową CHF lub szwajcarską sprawdź bezpośrednio w centrum. Większość międzynarodowych kart jest akceptowana.

segments.src: Can I organise a birthday party?
Yes, the laser game offers dedicated birthday packages: 2 games + private room (subject to availability) for cake and drinks, personalised invitations and souvenir photo. Book several weeks ahead.
segments.mt: Tak, gra laserowa oferuje dedykowane pakiety urodzinowe: 2 gry + prywatny pokój (w zależności od dostępności) na ciasto i napoje, spersonalizowane zaproszenia i zdjęcie pamiątkowe. Zarezerwuj kilka tygodni wcześniej.

segments.src: Parking is available on site. Public transport from Annemasse (TAC) or Geneva (TPG via Annemasse) is also an option.
segments.mt: Parking jest dostępny na miejscu. Opcją jest również transport publiczny z Annemasse (TAC) lub Genewy (TPG poprzez Annemasse).

src.q: Where is Lasermaxx Sillingy?
src.a: Lasermaxx Sillingy is in Sillingy (74330), around 12 km north of Annecy, in a business park. Quick access from Annecy on the D1508 (~15 min) or from Geneva via the A41 exit Annecy-Nord (~35 min).

src.what_is:
Le Semnoz is the northernmost mountain of the Bauges massif. At 15 km long, the plateau overlooks the western shore of Lake Annecy from the Col de Leschaux in the south to the city of Annecy in the north. Its highest point, the Crêt de Châtillon, reaches 1699 m and features an orientation table offering a 360° view over Mont Blanc, La Tournette, the Aravis, the Bauges, Lake Annecy and, in clear weather, Lake Bourget.

Le Semnoz sits entirely within the Bauges Massif Regional Natural Park. The Semnoz state forest and the Annecy communal forest cover more than 820 hectares of beech and fir, managed by the ONF. The ski resort was created in the late 1960s and has been managed by the Grand Annecy agglomeration since 2019. The site is freely accessible year-round.

A true natural balcony above Annecy, Le Semnoz appeals through its immediate proximity to the city (20 minutes by road) and the diversity of its activities: hiking and mountain biking in summer, alpine and Nordic skiing in winter, educational animal park, summer toboggan on rails, and numerous interpretation trails. It is also a privileged site for paragliding and alpine sunrises. The alpine pastures, where cows and goats graze between June and September, produce traditional mountain cheeses sold in the mountain chalets.

The plateau offers a preserved natural setting, both wild and welcoming, where forest meets pastureland and where deer, hinds and mouflons are regularly spotted in the Grande Jeanne park. Le Semnoz regularly features in regional sporting competitions, including the Annecy Maxi Race, and welcomes thousands of walkers, athletes and families each year seeking fresh air and exceptional panoramas.

Its highest point, the Crêt de Châtillon, reaches 1699 m and features an orientation table offering a 360° view over Mont Blanc, La Tournette, the Aravis, the Bauges, Lake Annecy and, in clear weather, Lake Bourget.
segments.mt: jest najbardziej wysuniętą na północ górą masywu Bauges. Na długości 15 km płaskowyż nad zachodnim brzegiem jeziora Annecy od Col de Leschaux na południu do miasta Annecy na północy. Jego najwyższy punkt, Crêt de Châtillon, osiąga 1699 m i posiada stół orientacyjny oferuje 360 ° widok na Mont Blanc, La Tournette, Aravis, Bauges, Jezioro Annecy i, w czystej pogodzie, Jezioro Bourget.

segments.mt: . Lasy stanowe Semnoz i Annecy obejmują ponad 820 hektarów buków i jodłów, zarządzanych przez ONF. Ośrodek narciarski powstał pod koniec lat 60. XX wieku i jest zarządzany przez aglomerację Grand Annecy od 2019 roku. Strona jest dostępna przez cały rok.

Jest to również uprzywilejowana strona dla paralotniarstwa i alpejskich wschodów słońca. Na pastwiskach alpejskich, gdzie krowy i kozy paszą się od czerwca do września, produkowane są tradycyjne górskie sery sprzedawane w górskich schroniskach.

src.q: Comment réserver à Léman Forest ?
src.a: Réservation à Léman Forest en ligne sur leman-forest.com/en/book-a-rope-course-session ou par téléphone au +33 (0)4 50 76 74 74. Réservation conseillée en haute saison et obligatoire pour les groupes de 20 personnes et plus.

src.q: Quel est le tarif de Léman Forest ?
src.a: Les tarifs des parcours accrobranche et du laser game de Léman Forest ne sont pas publiés en clair sur le site officiel à la date de vérification. Grille tarifaire à jour disponible au +33 (0)4 50 76 74 74 ou par e-mail. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.

src.q: Quelles sont les horaires de Léman Forest ?
src.a: Léman Forest ouvre de Pâques à la Toussaint. Juillet-août tous les jours 10h–19h, avril-juin et septembre-octobre week-ends + vacances scolaires zone A. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières et météo.

src.q: À partir de quel âge à Léman Forest ?
src.a: Léman Forest accueille les enfants dès 2 ans sur les parcours Bambino (max 1,5 m du sol), dès 5 ans en Lutin, dès 8 ans (>1,40 m) sur les grands parcours, et 16 ans + sur le parcours noir réservé aux sportifs confirmés.

src.q: Que voit-on de la tyrolienne franco-suisse de Léman Forest ?
src.a: La tyrolienne des contrebandiers de Léman Forest traverse la frontière France-Suisse au-dessus du torrent de la Morge. Unique en Europe : survol panoramique du Léman et des montagnes, l'attraction signature du parc, incluse dans les grands parcours.

src.q: Léman Forest est-il ouvert par temps de pluie ?
src.a: Non. Léman Forest ferme par vent fort, pluie soutenue ou orage, sans préavis. Consulter la météo suisse (plus fiable selon l'exploitant) avant de venir, et la page Facebook officielle pour les fermetures du jour.

src.q: Léman Forest accepte-t-il les groupes ?
src.a: Oui. Léman Forest accueille groupes (≥20 personnes, tarifs sur demande), anniversaires (formules dès 6 entrées avec boissons et gourmandises), EVJF/EVG avec entrée libre pour le marié/la mariée dès 10 entrées.

src.q: Comment venir à Léman Forest depuis Évian ?
src.a: Léman Forest est à 20 min d'Évian-les-Bains par la N5 puis D1005 le long du Léman direction Saint-Gingolph. Gare SNCF Saint-Gingolph Suisse à 5 min à pied du parc, ou bateau CGN en saison depuis Évian.

src.what_is:
Léman Kid is an indoor play park for children aged 0 to 12, on Impasse de la Poudrière in Thonon-les-Bains. The space is organised in three age zones (0–2, 3–5, 4–12), heated and air-conditioned, with electric mini-karting, multi-level structures, motor-skills courses and private birthday rooms.

Pricing is clear and unlimited-time: free under 1 (companion €1), €7 ages 1–3, €10 ages 3–12. The Pass Family (10 entries valid 2 years — €60 under 3, €90 for 3+) includes 2 free adult companions and €1 karting tokens. Birthday packages from €18/child (2 h, 4–8 children, 2 free adults).

A savoury snack menu is served 11 am–1 pm (orders by 12.45 pm). Outside food and drinks are not allowed, except baby food, bottles (microwave available), water and fruit. Socks are required for parents and children (on sale at reception). Note: closed 23–24 May 2026 for the Whitsun weekend.
