src.q: Comment réserver à Léman Kid ?
src.a: Léman Kid fonctionne en accès libre aux horaires d'ouverture, sans réservation. Réservation obligatoire pour les anniversaires (créneaux matin / 14h / 14h30) en ligne sur lemankid.com ou par téléphone au +33 7 44 40 87 44.

src.q: Quel est le tarif de Léman Kid ?
src.a: Léman Kid : moins de 1 an gratuit (accompagnant +1 €), 1-3 ans 7 €, 3-12 ans 10 €, accompagnant supplémentaire +1 €, +12 ans gratuit. Pass Family 10 entrées 60 € (<3 ans) ou 90 € (≥3 ans). Anniversaire 18 €/enfant. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.

src.q: Quelles sont les horaires de Léman Kid ?
src.a: Léman Kid : hors vacances Mercredi, Samedi, Dimanche 10h–18h. Vacances zone A et jours fériés Mardi-Dimanche 10h–18h. Snack salé 11h–13h. FERMÉ 23-24 mai 2026 (Pentecôte). Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

src.q: À partir de quel âge à Léman Kid ?
src.a: Léman Kid accueille les enfants dès la naissance jusqu'à 12 ans dans 3 zones d'âge dédiées : 0-2 ans (bébés), 3-5 ans (maternelle), 4-12 ans (grands). Gratuit moins de 1 an et plus de 12 ans (accompagnant). Locaux chauffés et climatisés.

src.q: Les chaussettes sont-elles obligatoires à Léman Kid ?
src.a: Oui. Chaussettes OBLIGATOIRES à Léman Kid pour parents et enfants (en vente à l'accueil si oubli). Pieds nus et chaussures interdits sur les tapis et structures de jeux, pour des raisons d'hygiène et de sécurité.

src.q: Peut-on manger à Léman Kid ?
src.a: Léman Kid propose un snack salé sur place entre 11h et 13h (prise de commande jusqu'à 12h45). Les aliments et boissons extérieurs sont interdits, sauf aliments spécifiques bébé, biberons (micro-ondes à disposition), eau et fruits.

src.q: Quel est le tarif anniversaire à Léman Kid ?
src.a: Anniversaire à Léman Kid : 18 €/enfant pour 2 h, créneaux matin / 14h / 14h30, 4 à 8 enfants par formule, 2 adultes gratuits inclus. Salle privative, animation, gâteau et goûter en option. Réservation en ligne sur lemankid.com.

src.q: Comment venir à Léman Kid depuis Évian ?
src.a: Léman Kid est à 15 min d'Évian-les-Bains par N5 et contournement de Thonon, sortie n°2 Armoy/Bellevaux puis direction Thonon-les-Bains. En transports : Léman Express L2 jusqu'à gare SNCF Thonon (3,5 km) puis bus BUT! Parking gratuit sur place.

src.what_is:
Les Aigles du Léman is an 8-hectare wildlife park located in the Domaine du Guidou in Sciez, on the shores of Lake Léman, entirely dedicated to the knowledge and protection of birds of prey. The park is home to nearly 350 birds belonging to more than 80 different species — eagles, sea eagles, condors, buzzards, owls, and hawks — distributed across more than one hundred aviaries designed for their welfare and breeding.

The park's main attraction is La Terre des Aigles, the world's largest bird of prey aviary with an area of 18,000 m², where approximately sixty birds roam in complete freedom around visitors in a unique immersive experience. The park also offers four different daily shows in its Eagle Stadium with 1,000 seats (400 covered), allowing visitors to observe raptors in flight just metres above their heads.

Since its creation, Les Aigles du Léman has conducted an important conservation and reintroduction programme for the white-tailed eagle in the Lake Léman region, visible in the dedicated eagle house. The park also operates a winter park located at 1,500 m altitude at Pointe de Nyon (Morzine), open December to March during ski season.

segments.src: is an 8-hectare wildlife park located in the Domaine du Guidou in Sciez, on the shores of Lake Léman, entirely dedicated to the knowledge and protection of birds of prey. The park is home to nearly 350 birds belonging to more than 80 different species — eagles, sea eagles, condors, buzzards, owls, and hawks — distributed across more than one hundred aviaries designed for their welfare and breeding.
segments.mt: 8-hektarowy park przyrody położony w Domaine du Guidou w Sciez, nad brzegiem jeziora Léman, całkowicie poświęcony wiedzy i ochronie ptaków drapieżnych. W parku znajduje się blisko 350 ptaków należących do ponad 80 różnych gatunków - orłów, orłów morskich, kondorów, myszołów, sowy i jastrzębi - rozmieszczonych w ponad stu wioskach przeznaczonych dla ich dobra i hodowli.

The park also offers four different daily shows in its Eagle Stadium with 1,000 seats (400 covered), allowing visitors to observe raptors in flight just metres above their heads.
segments.mt: , największy na świecie ptak myśliwski o powierzchni 18 000 m ², gdzie około 60 ptaków wędruje w całkowitej wolności wokół zwiedzających w unikalnym, zanurzonym doświadczeniu. Park oferuje również cztery różne codzienne pokazy na Eagle Stadium z 1000 miejsc (400 pokryte), co pozwala odwiedzającym obserwować raptory w locie zaledwie metrów nad ich głowami.

segments.src: Since its creation, Les Aigles du Léman has conducted an important conservation and reintroduction programme for the white-tailed eagle in the Lake Léman region, visible in the dedicated eagle house. The park also operates a winter park located at 1,500 m altitude at Pointe de Nyon (Morzine), open December to March during ski season.
segments.mt: Od czasu powstania Les Aigles du Léman prowadził ważny program konserwacji i ponownego wprowadzenia dla orła białoogonowego w regionie jeziora Léman, widoczny w dedykowanym domu orła. Park posiada również park zimowy położony na wysokości 1500 m nad Pointe de Nyon (Morzine), otwarty od grudnia do marca w sezonie narciarskim.

src.title: Attend the free-flight shows
src.description: Four different daily shows in the Eagle Stadium: Lords of the Sky (11:00 and 15:30) featuring 80 birds in free flight, and Birds of the Lake (14:00 and 17:00) dedicated to aquatic raptors with fishing and diving demonstrations.

src.title: Explore La Terre des Aigles
src.description: Walk through the world's largest bird of prey aviary (18,000 m²) where 60 eagles, condors and vultures roam free just metres from visitors. Guided tours available for an additional fee (€2 per person upon registration).

src.title: Discover the reintroduction programme
src.description: Visit the eagle house and white-tailed eagle reintroduction centre, a species that disappeared from the Lake Léman region which the park is working to reintroduce. Educational exhibition on the role of raptors in the ecosystem.

src.title: Educational activities for children
src.description: The Little Feathers House (13:30): activities about this season's baby raptors. The Rabbit-odrome (17:00): dedicated space for young children with direct contact with rabbits. Forest playground.

src.title: Track the eagles via the PYGAPP app
src.description: Mobile application allowing real-time tracking of the park's eagles through cameras installed in nests and GPS tags. Monitor births, movements and bird behaviour.

src.title: Picnic and dine on-site
src.description: Shaded picnic areas by the river. La Fauconnerie bar-restaurant (self-service with flower-decked terrace). Balbu'bar snack located in the giant La Lagune aviary with views of the osprey fishing basin.

segments.src: Four different daily shows in the Eagle Stadium: Lords of the Sky (11:00 and 15:30) featuring 80 birds in free flight, and Birds of the Lake (14:00 and 17:00) dedicated to aquatic raptors with fishing and diving demonstrations.

Free. Large car park along national road D1005 in the Domaine du Guidou. Access to park entrance via a short path between the Fire Museum and the Prehistory Museum.

src.q: How do I book Les Aigles du Léman?
src.a: Book online at the official website lesaiglesduleman.com (Ticketing section). Tickets can also be purchased directly at the park's ticket offices at the entrance to Domaine du Guidou. Booking recommended during peak season (July–August).

src.q: What are the prices for Les Aigles du Léman?
src.a: Adult €15. Child 4–14 years €12. Reduced rate (disabled) €10. Free for under 4 years. Groups from 20 people: adult €13, child €10. Guided tour of La Terre des Aigles additional €2 per person. Verified 14 May 2026.

src.q: What are the opening hours of Les Aigles du Léman?
src.a: 4–19 April: daily 12:00–17:30. 20 April–31 May: weekends and public holidays 12:00–17:30. 1 June–3 July: 10:00–16:00 weekdays, 12:00–17:30 Wednesdays and weekends. 4 July–30 August: daily 10:00–18:00. Verified on official website 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

src.q: Are dogs allowed at Les Aigles du Léman?
src.a: No. Dogs are not permitted within Les Aigles du Léman for safety reasons: protection of visitors, park birds, and the dogs themselves. This rule applies to all pets.

src.q: How much time should I allow for a visit to Les Aigles du Léman?
src.a: Allow 3 to 5 hours for a complete visit to Les Aigles du Léman. This time allows you to visit the aviaries (including La Terre des Aigles), attend the 4 daily shows included with your ticket, participate in educational activities and enjoy the playgrounds and dining facilities.

src.q: What shows are offered at Les Aigles du Léman?
src.a: Les Aigles du Léman offers 4 daily shows in the Eagle Stadium (1,000 seats with 400 covered). Lords of the Sky at 11:00 and 15:30: grand show with 80 raptors in free flight. Birds of the Lake at 14:00 and 17:00: fishing and diving demonstrations of aquatic raptors. All shows are included in the admission price.

src.q: Is Les Aigles du Léman accessible for wheelchair users?
src.a: Yes. Les Aigles du Léman is accessible to people with reduced mobility. Reduced rate for disabled visitors €10 (instead of €15). Shaded and covered areas available at show areas. Free parking in immediate proximity to the entrance.

src.q: Can I eat on-site at Les Aigles du Léman?
src.a: Yes. Les Aigles du Léman has two dining options: La Fauconnerie bar-restaurant (self-service with shaded terrace) and Balbu'bar snack located in the La Lagune aviary. Shaded picnic areas by the river for visitors bringing their own food.

src.what_is:
The Maison de Barberine is a Vallorcine museum housed in a traditional alpine home built in 1705 by Nicolas Ancey, shown on the 1730 Sardinian survey map and listed Monument Historique on 5 January 2023. Located in the hamlet of Barberine at Vallorcine, about 5 km from the Swiss border, it is a remarkable surviving example of pre-modern alpine housing, with its dwelling, stable and barn under one roof.

It has been run since 1987 by the Maison de Barberine Association — Musée de Vallorcine, a non-profit founded by sixteen volunteers led by Bernard and Michel Ancey, descendants of the Ancey 'des Colas' family. The museum is part of the CCVCMB Museum Pass network at €25/year, valid across six valley sites. The official framing is traditional 18th-century Vallorcine architecture and valley ethnography, not a Walser identity.

Closed at present, the museum reopens for the summer season from 1 July to 31 August 2026: Monday–Friday 2.30 pm–5.30 pm, Sunday 3 pm–5.30 pm, closed Saturday. Adult €5, reduced €3.50, free under 18. 2026 highlight: relocation of the adjacent 'regât du Crôt' (raccard) after a 2025 3D scan.

src.what_is:
The Maison de la Mémoire et du Patrimoine Janny Couttet is the heritage centre of Chamonix, at 90 rue des Moulins. It occupies the former Sisters' House (or Maison Baud), built in 1828 and almost entirely rebuilt after the tragic rue des Moulins fire in 2006 (only the granite outer walls survived). Acquired by the commune in 2002, opened as the Maison de la Mémoire on 1 March 2011 and later renamed 'Janny Couttet' in tribute to the Chamonix mountain guide.

Run by the Communauté de Communes de la Vallée de Chamonix-Mont-Blanc (CCVCMB), part of the same network as the future Musée du Mont-Blanc and the Espace Tairraz, it acts as a documentary resource centre open to researchers and showcases the photographic, oral and sound archives collected by the town's Mémoires service on Chamonix history and culture.

Closed between exhibitions from 9 March to 30 June 2026. Reopens in summer 2026 with the temporary exhibition 'Chamonix 1924: inventing the Winter Olympics', marking the centenary of the first Winter Games and running until November 2026. Fares: adult €5, reduced €4, free under 18. CCVCMB Museum Pass at €25/year covers six valley sites.

fields.src: Paysalp's alpine heritage media library and conservation centre at Viuz-en-Sallaz. Mémoire Alpine database, free consultation. Mon–Fri 9 am–5 pm. 800 av. de Savoie.

segments.src: Paysalp's alpine heritage media library and conservation centre at Viuz-en-Sallaz. Mémoire Alpine database, free consultation. Mon–Fri 9 am–5 pm. 800 av. de Savoie.
segments.mt: Alpejska biblioteka i centrum konserwatorskie w Viuz-en-Sallaz. Baza danych Mémoire Alpine, bezpłatne konsultacje. Mon- Fri 9 - 17 pm. 800 av. de Savoie.

src.what_is:
The Maison de la Mémoire Paysalp is a separate site from the main Paysalp Peasant Museum, located at the Bâtiment des 4 rivières, 800 avenue de Savoie in Viuz-en-Sallaz. It is the alpine heritage media library and conservation centre run by the Paysalp non-profit, aimed at researchers, genealogists, ethnologists and lovers of Savoyard culture.

Its documentary collection is exceptional: the 'Mémoire Alpine' database (memoire-alpine.com) gathers photographic, audiovisual and textual archives on Savoyard culture, agropastoralism and alpine ethnology. The Watt & Bike installation lets visitors pedal to listen to oral testimonies from farmers, craftsmen and elders. Bespoke heritage programmes can be booked through Paysalp.

Free entry for media-library use. Paid bespoke activities. Public consultation hours: Monday–Friday 9 am–12.30 pm and 1.30 pm–5 pm, Wednesday afternoon 1.30 pm–5 pm, and the first Saturday of the month 9.30 am–5 pm. Access via the rear of the MJC. A natural complement to the main Paysalp Peasant Museum 200 m away at 628 avenue de Savoie.

fields.src: Façade of the former Mikerne farmhouse housing the Maison du Salève at Présilly at the foot of the massif

segments.src: Façade of the former Mikerne farmhouse housing the Maison du Salève at Présilly at the foot of the massif
segments.mt: Fasada dawnej farmy Mikerne w Maison du Salève u podnóża masywu

src.what_is:
The Maison du Salève is the interpretation centre dedicated to the Salève massif, the emblematic mountain of the French Genevois that peaks at 1,379 m and dominates the Lake Geneva basin. It sits at the foot of the massif in Présilly, in an 18th-century farmhouse historically attached to the Chartreuse de Pomier, and is run by the Syndicat Mixte du Salève.

The permanent exhibition 'Le Salève, une montagne et des hommes' retraces the geology of the massif (limestone shaped by karst erosion), early human presence (Magdalenian remains from the Paleolithic), the historic transport links including the famous Salève cable-car, the rise of mountain sports (climbing from the 19th century, hiking, paragliding, mountain biking) and the fauna and flora of the range. Panoramic films, interactive displays and discovery trails round off the visit.

Fares are accessible: adult €6, youth 6–18 €3, accessibility €3, family €15 (2 adults + up to 4 youths), free under 6. The Geneva Pass, Pass Empreintes and Pass Léman all give free entry. Unique loyalty bonus: visit once and come back free for a year with your ticket, accompanying a paying visitor.

src.q: Comment réserver à la Maison du Salève ?
src.a: Réservation à la Maison du Salève par téléphone au +33 (0)4 50 95 92 16 ou par e-mail info@maisondusaleve.com. Système de billetterie en ligne en cours de mise en place. Réservation obligatoire pour les groupes, les anniversaires et les animations.

src.q: Quel est le tarif de la Maison du Salève ?
src.a: Maison du Salève : adulte 6 €, réduit 5 €, jeune 6-18 ans 3 €, handicap 3 €, -6 ans gratuit, entrée famille (2 ad + 4 jeunes max) 15 €. GRATUIT pour Geneva Pass, Pass Empreintes, Pass Léman. Animations enfant 6-8 €. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.

src.q: Quelles sont les horaires de la Maison du Salève ?
src.a: Maison du Salève : fév-juin et sept-déc Mer/Sam/Dim 14h-18h. Vacances zone A Mar-Dim. Juillet-août Mar-Dim 10h-18h. Fermeture annuelle 5 janv - 1er fév. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

src.q: La Maison du Salève accepte-t-elle le Geneva Pass ?
src.a: Oui. La Maison du Salève accepte le Geneva Pass, le Pass Empreintes, le Pass Léman et UDOTSI 74 — entrée gratuite sur présentation. Atout transfrontalier pour les visiteurs suisses du bassin lémanique.

src.q: Que voit-on à la Maison du Salève ?
src.a: La Maison du Salève présente la géologie du massif, les premiers humains (magdaléniens), le téléphérique du Salève, les sports de montagne et la faune-flore. Films panoramiques, dispositifs interactifs et sentiers de découverte vers la Chartreuse de Pomier.

src.q: Y a-t-il une offre fidélité à la Maison du Salève ?
src.a: Oui. La Maison du Salève propose 'Visitez une fois, revenez gratuitement pendant 1 an' sur présentation de votre billet d'entrée et accompagné d'un payant. Idéal pour découvrir les expositions temporaires successives.

src.q: La Maison du Salève est-elle accessible aux PMR ?
src.a: Oui. La Maison du Salève est accessible aux fauteuils roulants, et le personnel est formé à l'accueil des personnes en situation de handicap mental ou psychique. Tarif 3 € sur justificatif. Places PMR devant le bâtiment.

src.q: Comment venir à la Maison du Salève depuis Genève ?
src.a: La Maison du Salève est à 25 min de Genève par A41 sortie 13 Saint-Julien Sud puis D 1201 vers Annecy. En bus : ligne 272 LIHSA arrêt 'Chartreuse de Pomier' à 1 km à pied. Parking gratuit pour voitures et bus à 100 m avant.

segments.mt: La Maison du Salève présente la géologie du massif, les premier humains (magdaléniens), le téléphérique du Salève, les sports de montagne et la faune- flore. Filmy panoramy, dispositifs interactifs et sentiers de découverte vers la Chartreuse de Pomier.

segments.src: La Maison du Salève est-elle accessible aux PMR ?
segments.mt: La Maison du Salève est- elle access aux PMR?

segments.src: Oui. La Maison du Salève est accessible aux fauteuils roulants, et le personnel est formé à l'accueil des personnes en situation de handicap mental ou psychique. Tarif 3 € sur justificatif. Places PMR devant le bâtiment.

segments.src: Comment venir à la Maison du Salève depuis Genève ?
segments.mt: Komentarz venir à la Maison du Salève depuis Genève?

segments.src: La Maison du Salève est à 25 min de Genève par A41 sortie 13 Saint-Julien Sud puis D 1201 vers Annecy. En bus : ligne 272 LIHSA arrêt 'Chartreuse de Pomier' à 1 km à pied. Parking gratuit pour voitures et bus à 100 m avant.

src.what_is:
The Maison Forte de Hautetour in Saint-Gervais-les-Bains (74170) is one of the most accessible heritage sites in the Mont-Blanc valley. A 13th-century fortified house, it was the last seat of the Montjoie châtellenie (the valley running up to Les Contamines). Fallen into disuse in the 19th century, it underwent a major restoration in 2012 by the commune and reopened to the public in 2015 as a municipal cultural centre, in the heart of the village 10 minutes' walk from the Mont-Blanc Express station.

The new permanent display 'Mont-Blanc, côté Val Montjoie' (inaugurated 2025) covers the history of the Saint-Gervais guides' company, local alpinism since the 19th century, the geology of the massif and life at altitude. The winter 2025–2026 exhibition 'History of Mountain Rescue' (16 December 2025–30 April 2026) covers rescue techniques, equipment evolution and the PGHM and CRS Montagne services. The site also hosts the Pôle Archipel Art Contemporain, a municipal art centre with temporary shows, workshops and artist residencies.

Very affordable fares: adult €5, reduced €4, free under 5. Open year-round: Wed–Sun 2 pm–6 pm outside school holidays, daily 2 pm–7 pm in high season (July–August, Christmas and February holidays). Closed 25 December and 1 January. Partial wheelchair access. Free municipal car park 2 minutes away.

src.q: How much does the Maison du Fromage Abondance cost?
src.a: Entry to the Maison du Fromage Abondance is €5.50 full price and €4.50 reduced (students, jobseekers, families). The DUO Abbey + Maison du Fromage ticket is €9. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Maison du Fromage Abondance?
src.a: The Maison du Fromage Abondance is open Tue-Sat 9.30 am-12 pm and 2 pm-5.30 pm out of season, and daily 9.30 am-12 pm and 2 pm-6 pm in July-August (except Sunday morning). Annual closure 1 November-15 December. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: Does the visit to the Maison du Fromage Abondance include a tasting?
src.a: Yes. Every visit to the Maison du Fromage Abondance ends with a tasting of Abondance cheese paired with a glass of Savoie wine or local apple juice, included in the entry price.

src.q: How long should I plan to visit the Maison du Fromage Abondance?
src.a: Allow 1 h to 1 h 30 for a self-guided visit to the Maison du Fromage Abondance, tasting included. The guided tour (€3 supplement) may take a little longer.

src.q: Is the Maison du Fromage Abondance family-friendly?
src.a: Yes. The Maison du Fromage Abondance offers a sensory route suited to children (hands-on stations, sounds, images) and a family rate. Children take part in the tasting with apple juice.

src.q: Where can I park to visit the Maison du Fromage Abondance?
src.a: Parking is free in the heart of Abondance village, immediately next to the Maison du Fromage and the abbey. Easy access from Thonon (D902) or Châtel (D22).

src.q: Can I combine the Maison du Fromage Abondance with the Abbey?
src.a: Yes, and it's highly recommended: the DUO ticket at €9 covers both the Maison du Fromage Abondance AND the Abbaye Notre-Dame d'Abondance (14th-century painted cloister, a major listed monument).

src.q: Is the Maison du Fromage Abondance accessible to visitors with reduced mobility?
src.a: Yes. The Maison du Fromage Abondance building is accessible to people with reduced mobility. Contact reception on 04 50 73 06 34 to prepare your visit.

src.what_is:
Mont Baron is a summit of the Bornes massif reaching 1,299 metres altitude, situated above the eastern shore of Lake Annecy. Connected to Mont Veyrier (1,291 m) by a spectacular ridge trail, Mont Baron constitutes one of the most accessible and popular viewpoints in the Annecy region.

Since July 2024, vehicle access to Col des Contrebandiers and Pré Vernet has been prohibited by municipal decree. Hikers now start from the small harbour of Annecy-le-Vieux (447 m), adding approximately 600 metres of extra elevation. The trail first crosses beech and spruce forest before emerging onto the panoramic ridge. Halfway up, the concrete platform of the former Veyrier-du-Lac cable car (1,254 m) remains, equipped with an orientation table — the aerial station was dismantled in 2001 following a tragic accident in 1998.

The actual summit is a ten-minute walk north of this platform, on a limestone ridge that is exposed but secured by cables and barriers at exposed passages. From there, the view spans 180°: Lake Annecy below, Semnoz to the southwest, summits of the Bauges massif (Tournette, Dents de Lanfon, Arcalod) to the southeast, the Aravis range and Mont Blanc to the east (visible via Col des Aravis), and the Jura to the north. The ridge continues towards Mont Veyrier for those wishing to extend the hike.

segments.mt: to szczyt masywu Bornesa na wysokości 1,299 m n.p.m., położony nad wschodnim brzegiem jeziora Annecy. Mont Baron jest jednym z najbardziej dostępnych i popularnych punktów widokowych w regionie Annecy.

segments.src: Since July 2024, vehicle access to Col des Contrebandiers and Pré Vernet has been prohibited by municipal decree. Hikers now start from the small harbour of Annecy-le-Vieux (447 m), adding approximately 600 metres of extra elevation. Halfway up, the concrete platform of the former Veyrier-du-Lac cable car (1,254 m) remains, equipped with an orientation table — the aerial station was dismantled in 2001 following a tragic accident in 1998.
segments.mt: Od lipca 2024 r. Turyści zaczynają teraz od małego portu Annecy- le- Vieux (447 m), dodając około 600 metrów dodatkowej elewacji. Szlak najpierw przecina buk i las świerkowy, zanim pojawi się na wzgórzu panoramicznym. W połowie drogi do góry pozostaje betonowa platforma dawnej kolejki linowej Veyrier-du-Lac (1,254 m), wyposażona w stół orientacyjny - stacja lotnicza została zdemontowana w 2001 r. po tragicznym wypadku w 1998 r.

segments.src: The actual summit is a ten-minute walk north of this platform, on a limestone ridge that is exposed but secured by cables and barriers at exposed passages. From there, the view spans 180°: Lake Annecy below, Semnoz to the southwest, summits of the Bauges massif (Tournette, Dents de Lanfon, Arcalod) to the southeast, the Aravis range and Mont Blanc to the east (visible via Col des Aravis), and the Jura to the north. The ridge continues towards Mont Veyrier for those wishing to extend the hike.
segments.mt: Rzeczywisty szczyt znajduje się 10 minut spacerem na północ od tej platformy, na grzbiecie wapiennym, który jest narażony, ale zabezpieczone przez kable i bariery na odsłoniętych przejść. Stamtąd widok rozciąga się o 180 °: Jezioro Annecy poniżej, Semnoz na południowy zachód, szczyty masywu Bauges (Tournette, Dents de Lanfon, Arcalod) na południowy wschód, zasięg Aravis i Mont Blanc na wschód (widoczne przez Col des Aravis) i Jura na północ. W kierunku Mont Veyrier wznosi się grzbiet dla tych, którzy chcą przedłużyć wędrówkę.

src.q: How much does the cable car cost?
src.a: 2026 prices: adult return €13, child €5.80, family pass €49, 10-trip carnet €75. A Unireso combined ticket at CHF 21 (TPG zone 10 day pass + cable car return) is available.

src.q: What are the cable car timetables?
src.a: 2026 season: daily from 1 April to 1 November, 9 am-6.45 pm. Evening openings on Thursday, Friday and Saturday until 10.45 pm.

src.q: How can I reach the Salève from Geneva without a car?
src.a: TPG tram from Cornavin to the Veyrier Douane stop, then a 9-minute walk to the cable car. The Unireso combined ticket at CHF 21 covers both legs.

src.q: Is there a fee to use the trails?
src.a: No, access to the massif's trails is free and open year-round. Only the cable car and the summit restaurant are paid.

src.q: Can I paraglide at the Salève?
src.a: Yes, it's one of the region's flagship paragliding sites. Salève Airlines and other schools offer tandem flights from the summit — booking recommended.

src.q: How many trails are there?
src.a: About 250 km of way-marked trails cover the whole massif — hiking, mountain biking, trail running, from family routes to technical lines.

src.q: Is the Salève accessible in winter?
src.a: The cable car is closed from 1 November to 31 March. The trails remain open on foot or with snowshoes depending on snow cover — beware of ice on the rocks.

src.q: What is there to see from the summit?
src.a: A 360° view over Geneva and Lake Geneva, the Jura to the west, the Annecy basin to the south-east and, on a clear day, the Mont Blanc range.

segments.src: How much does the cable car cost?
2026 prices: adult return €13, child €5.80, family pass €49, 10-trip carnet €75. A Unireso combined ticket at CHF 21 (TPG zone 10 day pass + cable car return) is available.
segments.mt: 2026 ceny: zwrot dla dorosłych 13 €, dziecko 5,80 €, przepustka rodzinna 49 €, karnet 10- podróży 75 €. Dostępny jest łączny bilet Unireso w CHF 21 (strefa TPG 10 dni przejazdu + powrót kolejki linowej).

segments.src: What are the cable car timetables?
segments.mt: Jakie są rozkłady jazdy kolejki linowej?

segments.src: 2026 season: daily from 1 April to 1 November, 9 am-6.45 pm. Evening openings on Thursday, Friday and Saturday until 10.45 pm.
segments.mt: Sezon 2026: codziennie od 1 kwietnia do 1 listopada, 9 rano - 18.45. Wieczorne otwarcie w czwartek, piątek i sobotę do 22.45.

segments.src: How can I reach the Salève from Geneva without a car?
segments.mt: Jak mogę dotrzeć do Salève z Genewy bez samochodu?

segments.src: TPG tram from Cornavin to the Veyrier Douane stop, then a 9-minute walk to the cable car. The Unireso combined ticket at CHF 21 covers both legs.
segments.mt: Tramwaj TPG z Cornavin do przystanku Veyrier Douane, a następnie 9 minut spacerem do kolejki linowej. Połączenie biletu Unireso w CHF 21 obejmuje obie nogi.

segments.src: Is there a fee to use the trails?
segments.mt: Jest opłata za korzystanie z szlaków?

segments.src: No, access to the massif's trails is free and open year-round. Only the cable car and the summit restaurant are paid.
segments.mt: Nie, dostęp do szlaków masywu jest wolny i otwarty przez cały rok. Tylko kolejka linowa i restauracja na szczycie są opłacane.

segments.src: Can I paraglide at the Salève?
Yes, it's one of the region's flagship paragliding sites. Salève Airlines and other schools offer tandem flights from the summit — booking recommended.
segments.mt: Tak, to jedna z flagowych stron paralotniarskich regionu. Salève Airlines i inne szkoły oferują tandemowe loty ze szczytu - zalecana rezerwacja.

segments.src: About 250 km of way-marked trails cover the whole massif — hiking, mountain biking, trail running, from family routes to technical lines.
segments.mt: Około 250 km szlaków oznakowanych drogami obejmuje cały masyw - turystyka piesza, kolarstwo górskie, bieganie szlakiem, od szlaków rodzinnych po linie techniczne.

segments.src: Is the Salève accessible in winter?
segments.mt: Czy Salève jest dostępne w zimie?

segments.src: The cable car is closed from 1 November to 31 March. The trails remain open on foot or with snowshoes depending on snow cover — beware of ice on the rocks.
segments.mt: Kolejka linowa jest zamknięta od 1 listopada do 31 marca. Ścieżki pozostają otwarte pieszo lub ze śnieżnymi butami w zależności od pokrywy śnieżnej - strzeż się lodu na skałach.

segments.src: What is there to see from the summit?
A 360° view over Geneva and Lake Geneva, the Jura to the west, the Annecy basin to the south-east and, on a clear day, the Mont Blanc range.
segments.mt: Widok 360 ° nad Genewą i jeziorem Genewa, Jura na zachód, dorzecze Annecy na południowy wschód i, w jasny dzień, zasięg Mont Blanc.

src.title: Panoramic Lake Geneva + Mont Blanc flight
src.description: About 1 hour flight at dawn. Exceptional views over Lake Geneva, the Chablais, the Valais Alps and, depending on the route, the Mont Blanc massif.

src.title: Briefing and inflation
src.description: Complete safety briefing by the pilot before take-off, then envelope inflation live (45 min, a show in itself).

src.title: Celebration toast and certificate
src.description: Ballooning tradition: on landing, champagne (or juice for minors) and a flight certificate signed by the pilot.

src.title: Private flight for two
src.description: Romantic format for two people alone in the basket, ideal for anniversary, marriage proposal or exceptional gift. Pricing on quote.

src.title: Shared group flight
src.description: Classic format with 4 to 10 passengers depending on the basket. Sociable, accessible price. Great for friends or family.

src.title: Event flights and bespoke services
src.description: Birthdays, weddings, corporate seminars, incentives, film shoots. Detailed quotes on demand.

src.title: Photo flight and aerial reconnaissance
src.description: Photo/video kit on board for exceptional aerial views of Lake Geneva, the Chablais and Mont Blanc. To be confirmed.

src.title: Gift voucher valid several months
src.description: Digital or printed gift box, valid several months, for birthday, Christmas, Valentine's.

src.q: How much does the Vintage Aircraft Museum at Excenevex cost?
src.a: Entry to the Vintage Aircraft Museum at Excenevex is free. Donations to the Ailes Anciennes Haute-Savoie association are welcome to fund aircraft restoration. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Vintage Aircraft Museum at Excenevex?
src.a: The Vintage Aircraft Museum at Excenevex is open year-round on Thursdays and Saturdays, 2 pm-6 pm. Guided tours on Saturdays at 10.30 am and 2.30 pm. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: What is there to see at the Vintage Aircraft Museum at Excenevex?
src.a: At the Vintage Aircraft Museum at Excenevex you discover military aircraft (Mirage IIIE), helicopters, gliders and aeronautical pieces undergoing restoration by enthusiast volunteers since 1992.

src.q: Does the Vintage Aircraft Museum have a flight simulator?
src.a: Yes. The Vintage Aircraft Museum at Excenevex offers a flight simulator available to visitors: take the controls of the aircraft of your choice, an immersive experience suited to young and old.

src.q: Are there guided tours at the Vintage Aircraft Museum?
src.a: Yes. The Vintage Aircraft Museum at Excenevex offers guided tours by the volunteers (retired pilots, mechanics, engineers) on Saturdays at 10.30 am and 2.30 pm. Conversations with the restorers possible.

src.q: Where is the Vintage Aircraft Museum?
src.a: The Vintage Aircraft Museum is at Excenevex, on the Sciez-Excenevex aerodrome site, on the northern shore of Lake Geneva, 10 min from Thonon-les-Bains.

src.q: Is the Vintage Aircraft Museum suitable for children?
src.a: Yes. The Vintage Aircraft Museum at Excenevex is very popular with children: flight simulator, large aircraft seen up close, open restoration workshops. A recommended family outing.

src.q: How long should I plan for the Vintage Aircraft Museum?
src.a: Allow 1 h to 2 h to visit the Vintage Aircraft Museum at Excenevex, depending on use of the simulator, conversations with the volunteers and your interest in the restoration work.

src.what_is:
The Musée Archéologique de Viuz-Faverges is an independent site museum at 855 route de Viuz in Faverges-Seythenex, next to the church of Saint-Jean-Baptiste de Viuz. Run since its foundation in May 1972 by the Association des Amis de Viuz-Faverges (the local history and archaeology society), it is a long-standing association-run museum with serious collections from local excavations.

It tells the story of Casuaria, the Gallo-Roman vicus of about 25 hectares and 2,500 inhabitants, comparable in size to ancient Annecy. The three rooms are dominated by the Gallo-Roman period, drawn from excavations of the sanctuary, the vicus and the Thovey villa. Highlight pieces: the flat copper axe of Englannaz (2300–2200 BC), a 189-bead amber necklace (450–400 BC), a red-clay anthropomorphic statuette (1st–2nd c. AD), a 3rd-century bronze cauldron, and votive offerings from the Gallo-Roman fana (over 6,500 m² excavated in 1980).

The adjacent church of Saint-Jean-Baptiste de Viuz is part of the visit, with its archaeological crypt: 1st-century Roman building, 6th-, 8th–9th- and c. 1000 churches, 12th-century Romanesque chancel and 1696 baroque stalls. Fares: adult €4, child 5–14 €2.50, free under 5. The '3 sites, 3 stories' pass at €7 covers the museum plus the Butterfly and Insect Museum and the Lords of Faverges Donjon.

src.what_is:
The Museum of Sacred Art is at Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce, a high-altitude hamlet (≈1,170 m) of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains in the Mont Blanc area. It occupies the 18th-c. former presbytery, next to the remarkable baroque Saint-Nicolas church (Historic Monument). Opened in December 2010, it highlights the baroque sacred-art treasure of the parish, one of the richest in the diocese of Annecy.

The full collection counts over 600 objects, mostly 17th-18th c.; about fifty are permanently shown: polychrome wood sculptures (saints, Virgins, angels), silverware (chalices, ciboria), and liturgical ornaments. Reduced price; opening hours vary with the season.

segments.src: is at Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce, a high-altitude hamlet (≈1,170 m) of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains in the Mont Blanc area. It occupies the 18th-c. former presbytery, next to the remarkable baroque Saint-Nicolas church (Historic Monument). Opened in December 2010, it highlights the baroque sacred-art treasure of the parish, one of the richest in the diocese of Annecy.
segments.mt: znajduje się w miejscowości Saint- Nicolas-de- Véroce, w miejscowości Saint-Gervais-les-Bains w obszarze Mont Blanc. Zajmuje 18th-c prezbiterium, obok niezwykłego barokowego kościoła Saint- Nicolas (zabytkowy pomnik). Otwarty w grudniu 2010 roku, uwydatnia barokowy święty skarb parafii, jednej z najbogatszych w diecezji Annecy.

segments.src: The full collection counts over 600 objects, mostly 17th-18th c.; about fifty are permanently shown: polychrome wood sculptures (saints, Virgins, angels), silverware (chalices, ciboria), and liturgical ornaments. Reduced price; opening hours vary with the season.
segments.mt: Pełna kolekcja liczy ponad 600 obiektów, głównie 17th-18th c.; około pięćdziesięciu są trwale pokazane: polichromatyczne rzeźby z drewna (świętych, Dziewic, aniołów), sztućce (kielichy, cyboria) i ozdoby liturgiczne. Cena obniżona; godziny otwarcia różnią się w zależności od sezonu.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains?
src.a: The Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains (at Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce) mainly offers guided tours on Friday at 3 pm during school holidays. Outside holidays, opening by appointment. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: Where is the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains?
src.a: The Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains is in the former 18th-century presbytery of the Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce hamlet (approx. 1,170 m), 8 km from central Saint-Gervais, next to the Baroque church.

src.q: How much does the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains cost?
src.a: The price of the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains is not published on official sources. Contact the Tourist Office on +33 (0)4 50 47 76 08 for the current price.

src.q: What is there to see at the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains?
src.a: The Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains exhibits around fifty objects (out of 600+ kept): 17th-18th-century Baroque polychrome sculptures, liturgical gold and silverware, paintings and embroidered priestly vestments.

src.q: Do I need to book for the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains?
src.a: Yes. Outside school holidays, the visit to the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains is by appointment via the Tourist Office. During holidays, public visits on Friday at 3 pm.

src.q: Can I combine the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais with the Baroque church?
src.a: Yes, and it's highly recommended: the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais adjoins the Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce church, a Baroque jewel listed as a Historic Monument (1726-1733). A complete cultural outing.

src.q: How long should I plan for the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains?
src.a: Allow about 1 hour for the guided tour of the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains alone, and 1 h 30 to 2 h combined with the Baroque Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce church.

src.q: How do I get to the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains?
src.a: The Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains is reached from central Saint-Gervais via the D43 (8 km, road cleared of snow year-round). Parking on the Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce hamlet square.

segments.src: What are the opening hours of the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains?
segments.mt: Jakie są godziny otwarcia Muzeum Sztuki Świętej Saint-Gervais-les-Bains?

segments.src: The Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains (at Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce) mainly offers guided tours on Friday at 3 pm during school holidays. Outside holidays, opening by appointment. Verified 10 June 2026.
segments.mt: Muzeum Sztuki Świętej Saint-Gervais-les-Bains (w Saint- Nicolas - de- Véroce) oferuje głównie wycieczki z przewodnikiem w piątek o 15: 00 w czasie wakacji szkolnych. Poza wakacjami, otwarcie po spotkaniu. Zweryfikowany 10 czerwca 2026 r.

segments.src: Where is the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains?
segments.mt: Gdzie jest Muzeum Sztuki Świętej Saint-Gervais-les-Bains?

segments.src: The Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains is in the former 18th-century presbytery of the Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce hamlet (approx. 1,170 m), 8 km from central Saint-Gervais, next to the Baroque church.
segments.mt: Muzeum Sztuki Świętej Saint-Gervais-les-Bains znajduje się w dawnej XIX-wiecznej prezbiterium saint- Nicolas-de- Véroce (ok. 1,170 m), 8 km od centrum Saint- Gervais, obok barokowego kościoła.

segments.src: How much does the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains cost?
segments.mt: Ile kosztuje Muzeum Sztuki Świętej Saint-Gervais-les-Bains?

segments.src: The price of the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains is not published on official sources. Contact the Tourist Office on +33 (0)4 50 47 76 08 for the current price.
segments.mt: Cena Muzeum Sztuki Świętej Saint-Gervais-les-Bains nie jest publikowana na oficjalnych źródłach. Skontaktuj się z Biurem Turystycznym pod numerem + 33 (0) 4 50 47 76 08.

segments.src: What is there to see at the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains?
segments.mt: Co można zobaczyć w Muzeum Sztuki Świętej Saint-Gervais-les-Bains?

segments.src: The Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains exhibits around fifty objects (out of 600+ kept): 17th-18th-century Baroque polychrome sculptures, liturgical gold and silverware, paintings and embroidered priestly vestments.
segments.mt: Muzeum Sztuki Świętej Saint-Gervais-les-Bains eksponuje około pięćdziesięciu przedmiotów (z 600 + zachowanych): 17.-18. wieczne barokowe polichromatyczne rzeźby, złoto liturgiczne i sztućce, obrazy i haftowane szaty kapłańskie.

segments.src: Do I need to book for the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains?
segments.mt: Czy muszę zarezerwować dla Muzeum Sztuki Świętej Saint-Gervais-les-Bains?

segments.src: Yes. Outside school holidays, the visit to the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais-les-Bains is by appointment via the Tourist Office. During holidays, public visits on Friday at 3 pm.
segments.mt: Tak. Poza wakacjami szkolnymi wizyta w Muzeum Sztuki Świętej w Saint-Gervais-les-Bains odbywa się przez biuro turystyczne. Podczas wakacji, wizyty publiczne w piątek o 15: 00.

segments.src: Can I combine the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais with the Baroque church?
segments.mt: Czy mogę połączyć Święte Muzeum Sztuki Świętej Gervais z barokowym kościołem?

segments.src: Yes, and it's highly recommended: the Sacred Art Museum of Saint-Gervais adjoins the Saint-Nicolas-de-Véroce church, a Baroque jewel listed as a Historic Monument (1726-1733). A complete cultural outing.
segments.mt: Tak, i jest to wysoce zalecane: Święte Muzeum Sztuki Świętej-Gervais dołącza do kościoła Świętego Mikołaja-de- Véroce, barokowy klejnot wymieniony jako zabytek (1726- 1733). Kompletna wyprawa kulturalna.

src.what_is:
The Château d'Annecy is the medieval and Renaissance fortress that has overlooked Annecy's old town since the 12th century. Home to the Counts of Geneva (12th-14th c.) and then the Dukes of Genevois-Nemours (15th-17th c.), it was listed as a Historic Monument in 1959 and has belonged to the Ville d'Annecy since 1953. The site combines four historic lodgings and the iconic Tour de la Reine, a 30-metre keep with walls over 4 m thick.

The castle now houses the Musée-Château d'Annecy, a regional art and history museum with 19th-century Savoyard paintings, furniture and a remarkable lake archaeology section on the prehistoric pile-dwelling sites of Lake Annecy (UNESCO World Heritage since 2011). The Regional Alpine Lakes Observatory occupies the Logis Perrière, and the Tour de la Reine offers a sweeping panorama of the lake, the old town and the Aravis range.

2026 prices: adult 6.50 EUR (Sept-June) / 7 EUR in July-August, reduced 3.50 EUR, free under 12 and on the 1st Sunday of the month from October to May. Joint ticket with the Palais de l'Île 8-9 EUR. Summer hours: daily 10.30am-6pm; winter 10am-12pm and 2pm-5pm, closed Tuesdays. Partial step-free access (Tour de la Reine not accessible).

src.what_is:
The Animated Film Museum is Annecy's new museum dedicated to the art of animation, opening to the public on 19 June 2026 at the heart of the Cité internationale du cinéma d'animation, in the rehabilitated buildings of the Haras d'Annecy (listed Historic Monument, 2007). The opening is timed two days before the 50th Annecy International Animation Film Festival (21-27 June 2026).

Operated by CITIA (which also runs the Festival and the MIFA market), the museum draws on the scientific curatorship of the Musée-Château d'Annecy, holder of the 'Musées de France' collection of more than 8,000 works (including the Émile Cohl room). The 450 sqm permanent exhibition traces the history and techniques of animated film, from the pioneers to today's studios.

The museum also features a 650 sqm annual exhibition in the former riding hall (first edition 'Ankama, 25 years of creations', 19 June 2026-31 January 2027), a 160 sqm gallery for temporary shows ('Wildwood, LAIKA', 21 June-27 September 2026), a 332-seat cinema owned by the Haute-Savoie department, and the Grenier à Images education space. Architecture by Devaux & Devaux (DDA), scenography by Scénorama.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in Douvaine?
src.a: The Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in Douvaine is open every Tuesday in July and August, 2.30 pm-5.30 pm. Outside the summer season, opening by appointment on +33 (0)4 50 94 11 79. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: How much does the Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in Douvaine cost?
src.a: The price of the Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in Douvaine is not published on the official sources consulted. Contact Philippe Piccot directly on +33 (0)4 50 94 11 79 for the current price.

src.q: What is there to see at the Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in Douvaine?
src.a: The Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in Douvaine displays every Buisse-Bottazzi film-projector model (1946-2007), complemented by period posters tracing the history of popular cinema.

src.q: Who was Philippe Piccot of the Douvaine Cinema Museum?
src.a: Philippe Piccot, founder of the Douvaine Cinema Museum, is the former manager of the Cinéma du Chablais. A film enthusiast, he safeguarded the entire production of the Buisse-Bottazzi projectors.

src.q: How long should I plan for the Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in Douvaine?
src.a: Allow 1 h to 1 h 30 for a visit to the Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in Douvaine, depending on projector demonstrations and Philippe Piccot's own stories.

src.q: Where is the Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in Douvaine?
src.a: The Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum is at 43 bis rue du Bourg Neuf, in the centre of Douvaine, 20 min from Geneva and 15 min from Thonon-les-Bains.

src.q: Do I need to book at the Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in Douvaine?
src.a: Outside the summer season, a visit to the Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in Douvaine is by appointment only on +33 (0)4 50 94 11 79. In July-August, open access on Tuesdays 2.30 pm-5.30 pm.

src.q: Is the Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum suitable for children?
src.a: Yes. The Philippe Piccot Cinema Museum in Douvaine is an unusual outing for children: they discover how films were projected before digital with a real film reel.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Cobbler's Museum at Alby-sur-Chéran?
src.a: The Cobbler's Museum at Alby-sur-Chéran is open mainly from June to August: June and low season Tue-Sat 10 am-12 pm and 1.30 pm-5.30 pm; July-August extended hours 10 am-12 pm and 3 pm-7/8 pm. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: How much does the Cobbler's Museum at Alby-sur-Chéran cost?
src.a: Entry to the Cobbler's Museum at Alby-sur-Chéran is free for self-guided visits. Group guided tours are paid, on request from the town hall. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: What is there to see at the Cobbler's Museum at Alby-sur-Chéran?
src.a: The Cobbler's Museum at Alby-sur-Chéran displays the tools, sewing machines, manuals and Savoyard footwear of the village cobblers (300 craftsmen in 1880), under the arcades of the Trophée.

src.q: Where is the Cobbler's Museum at Alby-sur-Chéran?
src.a: The Cobbler's Museum is at 9 place du Trophée, in the heart of the medieval town of Alby-sur-Chéran (74540), 20 min from Annecy via the A41 (Alby exit).

src.q: How long should I plan for the Cobbler's Museum at Alby-sur-Chéran?
src.a: Allow 30 min to 1 h to visit the Cobbler's Museum at Alby-sur-Chéran, and about 2 h combined with a stroll through the medieval town (arcades, castle, old bridge).

src.q: Do I need to book for the Cobbler's Museum at Alby-sur-Chéran?
src.a: No, not for individual self-guided visits. Booking is required for group guided tours, by request to the Alby-sur-Chéran Town Hall on +33 (0)4 50 68 11 09.

src.q: Is the Cobbler's Museum at Alby-sur-Chéran suitable for children?
src.a: Yes. The Cobbler's Museum at Alby-sur-Chéran is a short, hands-on visit for families: tools, machines, old shoes. To combine with the medieval town.

src.q: What is the history of the Cobbler's Museum at Alby-sur-Chéran?
src.a: The Cobbler's Museum of Alby-sur-Chéran was founded in 1980 and installed in the medieval town in 1999 to preserve the memory of the village's 300 cobblers who made Savoyard footwear in the 19th century.

src.what_is:
The Musée du Bâtiment in Ville-la-Grand is a community museum that is entirely free, devoted to the historic trades and tools of the building industry. Run by the Cercle des Compagnons du Bâtiment association, it was founded in 2012 by Jean-Pierre Maulini, a former masonry contractor, and Raymond Bardet, then mayor of Ville-la-Grand. The building is owned by the town, securing its long-term future.

The visit unfolds over three floors of 180 sqm and presents historic tools from six building trades: stone cutting, masonry, blacksmithing, joinery, carpentry and woodwork. The volunteer companions give demonstrations and explanations, and some of the tools can be handled, making the museum a hands-on and educational stop for children and craft enthusiasts.

Admission is entirely free; donations (66% tax-deductible) are welcome. Hours: Wednesday to Friday 2.30-5pm and Sunday 9.30am-12.30pm. Groups and schools by appointment via the online form or musee.batiment74@gmail.com. Full step-free access (ramp, lift, accessible WC). Free parking at Place du Porte-Bonheur (140 spaces) nearby.

src.what_is:
The Musée du Mont-Blanc, formerly the Chamonix Alpine Museum, reopens to the public on 11 July 2026 after 5 years of renovation and 8.6 million EUR of investment. The museum occupies the historic Chamonix Palace (1913-1914), the first of the three Belle Époque palaces of Chamonix, at 89 avenue Michel Croz.

Managed by the Chamonix-Mont-Blanc Valley Community of Communes (CCVCMB) since 2010 and a 'Musée de France' since 2003, the museum holds 17,000 catalogued items across 1,800 sqm of exhibition space, making it the largest mountain-themed museum in France. The new scenography is built around three axes: Adaptations, Exploit and Décrire.

The inaugural exhibition 'Métamorphoses du regard, les Alpes magnifiées' runs from 11 July 2026 to 30 April 2027. Prices: adult 8 EUR, reduced 5.50 EUR, free under 18. The CCVCMB Museums Pass at 25 EUR/year gives unlimited access to the 6 sites of the network.

src.q: How much does the Wildlife Museum at Bellevaux cost?
src.a: The Wildlife Museum at Bellevaux costs €4 adult, €3 child 4-16, €3.20 student, free under 4. Combined two-museum ticket: €7 adult / €5 child. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Wildlife Museum at Bellevaux?
src.a: The Wildlife Museum at Bellevaux opens mainly during school holidays: April Monday-Wednesday-Friday, and July-August Monday to Friday 10 am-12 pm and 2 pm-4 pm (Thursday 2 pm-6 pm). Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: What is there to see at the Wildlife Museum at Bellevaux?
src.a: The Wildlife Museum at Bellevaux displays more than 200 naturalised animals of the Haute-Savoie mountains: chamois, ibex, marmots, lynx, black grouse, golden eagles, bearded vultures, with sound terminals.

src.q: Is the Wildlife Museum at Bellevaux suitable for children?
src.a: Yes, perfectly. The Wildlife Museum at Bellevaux is suited to children aged 4-12: interactive sound terminals, tracks to identify, animals at child height. Child rate €3, free under 4.

src.q: Where is the Wildlife Museum at Bellevaux?
src.a: The Wildlife Museum is beneath the Bellevaux town hall, on the village square (74470), at the heart of the Brevon valley in Haut-Chablais. 30 min from Thonon.

src.q: Is the Wildlife Museum at Bellevaux accessible to visitors with reduced mobility?
src.a: Yes. The Wildlife Museum at Bellevaux is accessible to people with reduced mobility (located on the ground floor beneath the town hall).

src.q: Can I combine the Wildlife Museum with another Bellevaux museum?
src.a: Yes. The combined ticket at €7 adult / €5 child covers the Wildlife Museum AND Bellevaux's other museum (Brevon Valley History Museum). Excellent value.

src.q: How long should I plan for the Wildlife Museum at Bellevaux?
src.a: Allow 30 min to 1 h for the Wildlife Museum at Bellevaux alone, and 1 h 30 to 2 h to visit both Bellevaux museums with the combined ticket.

segments.src: Yes. The combined ticket at €7 adult / €5 child covers the Wildlife Museum AND Bellevaux's other museum (Brevon Valley History Museum). Excellent value.

segments.src: How long should I plan for the Wildlife Museum at Bellevaux?
segments.mt: Jak długo mam planować muzeum dzikiej przyrody w Bellevaux?

segments.src: Allow 30 min to 1 h for the Wildlife Museum at Bellevaux alone, and 1 h 30 to 2 h to visit both Bellevaux museums with the combined ticket.
segments.mt: Pozwolić 30 min do 1 h na Muzeum Dzikiej Przyrody w samym Bellevaux i 1 h 30 do 2 h odwiedzić oba muzea Bellevaux razem z biletem.

src.q: How much does the Granges de Servette Museum at Chens-sur-Léman cost?
src.a: Entry to the Granges de Servette Museum at Chens-sur-Léman is free. No ticket or booking required to visit this contemporary-art collection. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Granges de Servette Museum?
src.a: The Granges de Servette Museum is open from 29 June to 31 August, every day 3 pm to 7 pm. Occasional openings during the Heritage Days in September. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: What is there to see at the Granges de Servette Museum at Chens-sur-Léman?
src.a: The Granges de Servette Museum presents a permanent contemporary-art collection (the Lyon school since 1958) and more than 1,000 antique tools of traditional crafts.

src.q: Where is the Granges de Servette Museum?
src.a: The Granges de Servette Museum is at the hamlet of Collongette, 224 Chemin des Granges de Servette, 74140 Chens-sur-Léman, 25 min from Thonon and 20 min from Geneva.

src.q: Is the Granges de Servette Museum at Chens-sur-Léman family-friendly?
src.a: Yes. The Granges de Servette Museum at Chens-sur-Léman offers a varied tour (contemporary art and antique tools) that interests young and old. Free entry for all.

src.q: Does the Granges de Servette Museum host temporary exhibitions?
src.a: Yes. The Granges de Servette Museum hosts temporary exhibitions every summer dedicated to new contemporary artists: painting, sculpture, photography.

src.q: How long should I plan for the Granges de Servette Museum?
src.a: Allow 1 h to 1 h 30 for a visit to the Granges de Servette Museum at Chens-sur-Léman, depending on your interest in contemporary art and the antique-tool collection.

src.q: Can I combine the Granges de Servette Museum with Yvoire?
src.a: Yes. The Granges de Servette Museum at Chens-sur-Léman is 10 min from the medieval village of Yvoire (Plus Beaux Villages de France), ideal for a culture-and-heritage day on the shores of Lake Geneva.

src.lead: At the Espace Carpano & Pons in Cluses, this guardian of time of the Arve valley retraces the saga of the peasant clockmakers from the 18th century to the bar-turning revolution. The world's largest collection of escapements.

segments.src: At the Espace Carpano & Pons in Cluses, this guardian of time of the Arve valley retraces the saga of the peasant clockmakers from the 18th century to the bar-turning revolution. The world's largest collection of escapements.
segments.mt: W Espace Carpano & Pons w Cluses, ten strażnik czasu doliny Arve odtwarza sagę chłopskich zegarmistrzów od XVIII wieku do rewolucji barowej. Największa na świecie kolekcja ucieczek.

src.what_is:
The Musée de l'Horlogerie et du Décolletage at Cluses is the timekeeper of the Arve valley, world capital of precision bar-turning. Housed in the Espace Carpano & Pons at 100 place du 11 Novembre, the museum tells the extraordinary industrial and social saga of the valley: the evolution of the know-how of the peasant watchmakers of the 18th century into the emergence of decolletage during the industrial revolution, and on to today's precision industry.

The collection traces the evolution of time-measuring instruments from astronomy to precision watchmaking: sundials and astronomical rings, nocturlabes, candle and oil clocks, table and edifice clocks, cartels and watches whose complexity is unveiled by enlarged scale models of mechanisms. The museum holds the world's largest collection of escapements — the watchmaking system that regulates the gear train.

The second half is devoted to bar-turning, the industry that transformed the Arve valley: historic turning machines, micro-components, applications in aerospace, automotive, Swiss watchmaking and medical sectors. Prices: full price €5, youth €3, free under 12. Free the first Sunday of the month, September to June. Open Tuesday-Saturday 2 pm-6 pm September-June, and daily 10 am-12 pm and 2 pm-6 pm in July-August. Annual closure 11-30 November.

src.q: How much does the Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at Cluses cost?
src.a: The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at Cluses costs €5 full price, €3 for young people, and is free for under-12s. Free entry on the first Sunday of the month (September-June). Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at Cluses?
src.a: The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at Cluses is open Tue-Sat 2 pm-6 pm from September to June, and every day 10 am-12 pm and 2 pm-6 pm in July-August. Annual closure 11-30 November. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: What is there to see at the Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at Cluses?
src.a: The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at Cluses presents the world's largest collection of horological escapements, the history of the peasant clockmakers of the Arve and the industrial revolution of bar-turning.

src.q: Where is the Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at Cluses?
src.a: The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at Cluses is at the Espace Carpano & Pons, 100 place du 11 Novembre, in the centre of Cluses (74300). Train station 500 m away.

src.q: Is the Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at Cluses suitable for children?
src.a: Yes. The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at Cluses features enlarged mechanical models, workshops during school holidays, and free entry for under-12s.

src.q: How long should I plan for the Clock and Bar-Turning Museum of Cluses?
src.a: Allow 1 h to 1 h 30 to visit the Clock and Bar-Turning Museum of Cluses, depending on your interest in precision engineering and the Arve's industrial revolution.

src.q: Is the Clock and Bar-Turning Museum accessible to visitors with reduced mobility?
src.a: Yes. The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at Cluses is accessible to people with reduced mobility, in the renovated Espace Carpano & Pons.

src.q: When is the Clock and Bar-Turning Museum free?
src.a: The Clock and Bar-Turning Museum at Cluses is free on the first Sunday of every month (September to June), and year-round for under-12s.

src.what_is:
The Musée de la Nature at Gruffy is a privileged place to discover the biodiversity of Haute-Savoie, nestled between the southern shore of Lake Annecy, the Bauges range and the Semnoz. Located on route de Beauregard, it is organised into four complementary spaces that make it a very complete family outing, fully accessible to people with reduced mobility.

The "Wildlife" room takes visitors into the secret life of wild and domestic animals through hundreds of taxidermied species staged in their reconstructed habitats (forests, meadows, lakes). The bee room reveals the work of more than 30,000 worker and forager bees through a glass hive where you can watch honey being made in real time, alongside equipment illustrating the history of Savoyard beekeeping.

The outdoor educational garden brings together aromatic and medicinal plants and a forgotten-vegetable plot. The conservation orchard shelters apple, pear and plum trees of local heritage varieties, plus 5 active hives to understand the interaction between bees and nature. The museum is open to the general public during school holidays and by reservation for groups and schools all year. Single fee: €6 (free under 2). Contact: +33 (0)4 50 77 58 60, resa.musee.gruffy@gmail.com.

src.q: How much does the Nature Museum at Gruffy cost?
src.a: The single price for the Nature Museum at Gruffy is €6 per person, free for under-2s. Groups €12/person with activity. Family: 3rd person onwards €10. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Nature Museum at Gruffy?
src.a: The Nature Museum at Gruffy is open to the general public during school holidays, and year-round by reservation for groups and schools. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: What is there to see at the Nature Museum at Gruffy?
src.a: The Nature Museum at Gruffy is made up of 4 spaces: naturalised mountain wildlife, a glass-fronted hive with 30,000 bees, a teaching garden of medicinal plants, and a conservation orchard.

src.q: Is the Nature Museum at Gruffy accessible to visitors with reduced mobility?
src.a: Yes. The Nature Museum at Gruffy is accessible to people with reduced mobility, as is, in part, the outdoor teaching garden and conservation orchard.

src.q: Is the Nature Museum at Gruffy suitable for children?
src.a: Yes, perfectly. The Nature Museum at Gruffy offers nature workshops during school holidays, a fascinating glass-fronted hive and naturalised animals at child height. Free under 2.

src.q: Where is the Nature Museum at Gruffy?
src.a: The Nature Museum is at 335 Route de Beauregard, locality Les Choseaux, 74540 Gruffy, south of Lake Annecy. 25 min from Annecy via A41 Alby exit.

src.q: Do I need to book for the Nature Museum at Gruffy?
src.a: Booking is recommended for individual visits to the Nature Museum at Gruffy, and required for groups and schools: resa.musee.gruffy@gmail.com or 04 50 77 58 60.

src.q: How long should I plan for the Nature Museum at Gruffy?
src.a: Allow 1 h 30 to 2 h to explore the 4 spaces of the Nature Museum at Gruffy (wildlife, bees, teaching garden, conservation orchard).

src.what_is:
The Musée Patrimonial du Pays de Thônes, founded in 1937 by François Cochat and extended by his son Lucien, is one of the richest municipal museums in Haute-Savoie. Awarded the "Musée de France" label, it offers a full journey into the history and heritage of the Pays de Thônes (Reposoir, Aravis, Manigod and Bouchet valleys) at the heart of the Savoyard Pre-Alps.

The route covers all aspects of local life: geology (Aravis limestone, fossils), prehistory and early human occupation, traditional industry and crafts (cloth of Thônes, wood turning, saws, cheesemaking trades), paintings and Savoyard baroque religious art, wild flora and fauna of the Aravis, and the famous fireman's costume of Thônes built around the celebrated Fête des Sapeurs (annual May parade), a military tradition unique in France and dating back to the 19th century.

The personalised welcome and the museum's offer of self-guided or guided visits include fun, interactive activities for the youngest. The ticket also includes the seasonal visit to the Manoir de la Tour, an emblematic medieval residence in Thônes. The museum is free for children under 12. Hours: Monday 2.30 pm-5.30 pm, Tuesday 9 am-12 pm, Wednesday 9 am-12 pm and 1.30 pm-5.30 pm, Thursday-Friday 9 am-12 pm and 2.30 pm-5.30 pm, Saturday 9.30 am-12 pm and 2.30 pm-6 pm. Contact: +33 (0)4 50 02 97 76.

src.q: How much does the Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum cost?
src.a: The Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum is free for children under 12. The adult price is to be confirmed with the Thônes Town Hall on +33 (0)4 50 02 97 76. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum?
src.a: The Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum is open Monday to Saturday with varying hours: Monday 2.30 pm-5.30 pm, Tuesday 9 am-12 pm, Wednesday 9 am-12 pm and 1.30 pm-5.30 pm, Thu-Fri 9 am-12 pm and 2.30 pm-5.30 pm, Saturday 9.30 am-12 pm and 2.30 pm-6 pm. Verified 10 June 2026.

src.q: What is there to see at the Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum?
src.a: The Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum presents geology, prehistory, crafts, Baroque religious art, fauna and flora of the Aravis, and the firefighters' costume of the famous Fête des Sapeurs.

src.q: Is the Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum a Musée de France?
src.a: Yes. The Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum is awarded the 'Musée de France' label, recognising the scientific quality of its collections and their inclusion in the national heritage.

src.q: Who founded the Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum?
src.a: The Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum was founded in 1937 by François Cochat, who donated his rich collection. His son Lucien Cochat continued his work. The museum bears their names.

src.q: Are there activities for children at the Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum?
src.a: Yes. The Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum regularly offers playful and interactive activities for younger visitors. Personalised welcome. Free for children under 12.

src.q: Does the Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum ticket include the Manoir de la Tour?
src.a: Yes, in season. Entry to the Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum includes a visit to the Manoir de la Tour (open in summer), an emblematic medieval residence in the historic centre of Thônes.

src.q: Where is the Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum?
src.a: The Pays de Thônes Heritage Museum is in central Thônes (74230), 30 min from Annecy via the D909 and 15 min from La Clusaz.

src.what_is:
The Musée Paysan at Viuz-en-Sallaz is one of the flagship sites of PAYSALP Culture & Patrimoine, a recognised association running a fully fledged multi-site ecomuseum in the Haut-Faucigny. The museum offers an immersive 500 m² exhibition that retraces mountain life between 1850 and 1950, a pivotal period between ancestral traditions and early modernisation.

The route brings to life the craftsmen, peasants and villagers of the time: carpenter, blacksmith, farmers, schoolteacher. Each tells their daily story through careful reconstructions, authentic objects and audio or tactile devices. A 1924 schoolroom is built into the visit: visitors can sit on the wooden benches and try a few questions from the old Certificat d'Études Primaires — a moment always enjoyed by families and school groups alike.

The museum also offers two paid bookable activities: the "Absurd Game" (life-size board game, €10 adult / €7 child) and the "Explor Games" (immersive investigation game, €10/person). Museum prices: €7 adult, €5 child 5-15, free under 5. Hours: Monday-Friday 10 am-12.30 pm and 1.30 pm-5 pm (until 6 pm in summer from 10 July to 1 September). Group activities year-round by reservation. Address: 628 avenue de Savoie, 74250 Viuz-en-Sallaz.

src.q: What is there to see at the PAYSALP Peasant Museum at Viuz-en-Sallaz?
src.a: The PAYSALP Peasant Museum at Viuz-en-Sallaz offers a 500 m² immersive exhibition on mountain life 1850-1950, with a 1924 classroom where you can sit a certificat d'études.

src.q: Is the PAYSALP Peasant Museum suitable for children?
src.a: Yes, perfectly. The PAYSALP Peasant Museum at Viuz-en-Sallaz features an immersive scenography that children love, an interactive 1924 classroom and Absurd Game / Explor Games activities. Free under 5.

src.a: The PAYSALP Peasant Museum is at 628 avenue de Savoie, 74250 Viuz-en-Sallaz, 25 min from Annemasse (D907) and 25 min from Cluses (D26). Free on-site parking.

src.q: Is the PAYSALP Peasant Museum accessible to visitors with reduced mobility?
src.a: Yes. The building of the PAYSALP Peasant Museum at Viuz-en-Sallaz is accessible to people with reduced mobility.

src.q: What is the PAYSALP Peasant Museum's Absurd Game?
src.a: The PAYSALP Peasant Museum's Absurd Game is a life-size board game played inside the museum (€10 adult, €7 child, by reservation). A fun activity for families and groups.

src.a: Allow 1 h 30 to 2 h to visit the PAYSALP Peasant Museum at Viuz-en-Sallaz, depending on engagement with the 1924 classroom and possible participation in the Absurd Game or Explor Games.

src.what_is:
The Musée de la Poterie Savoyarde is tucked away at Évires, a hamlet of the new commune of Fillière, on the Bornes Plateau. The museum is housed under the vast timber frame of an 1842 farmhouse, a heritage building typical of the region. It was founded in 1981 by Jean-Christophe Hermann, whose family has worked clay in Haute-Savoie for several generations at the nearby Poterie d'Évires.

The collection holds more than 2,000 pieces representing every type of pottery used in Savoy, from the 12th century to the 1930s. Cookware (toupins, pots, gratin dishes), preservation vessels (jars for fat, butter, honey), decorative ceramics, popular faience, baking dishes. The museum also displays hundreds of potter's tools and plaster or clay moulds from the 18th and 19th centuries, a rare record of artisanal technique.

The visit is guided and individual only, by appointment, which makes it a personal and high-quality experience. The museum is open from Easter to All Saints. Contact: +33 (0)4 50 62 01 90. It is one of Europe's most complete sites for Savoyard ceramics, ideally combined with a visit to the contemporary Poterie d'Évires (Hermann family workshop), which still produces work in the same tradition today.

fields.src: Museum of Prehistory & Geology in Sciez — Lake Geneva shore | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Museum of Prehistory & Geology in Sciez — Lake Geneva shore | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Muzeum Prehistorii i Geologii w Sciez - Jezioro Genewskie nad brzegiem 124; Loisirs 74

src.what_is:
The Museum of Prehistory and Geology of Sciez is a small local museum in Sciez (74140), on the shore of Lake Geneva, in Haute-Savoie. The museum presents varied collections on the geological and prehistoric history of the Lake Geneva area and the Northern Alps: fossils, minerals, flint and polished stone tools, remains of prehistoric civilisations (Palaeolithic, Neolithic, Bronze and Iron Ages) and Roman fragments occasionally found in the area. It's a volunteer-run museum, with guided tours and collection care provided by passionate volunteers.

Who is it for? The museum is aimed at families with children from age 6-7 (curious about history and natural sciences), school groups (by booking), local archaeology enthusiasts and visitors staying in Sciez or around the lake. The visit is relatively short (45 min-1h30 depending on interest), ideal alongside a lakeside walk, a meal in a Sciez restaurant or a visit to the Sciez château and the Maison des oiseaux.

Practical info and access. Opening is generally seasonal (June-September) and by appointment the rest of the year. Entry is modest (often €3-5) or even free for events or children. The museum is accessible by car from Thonon-les-Bains (~15 min) or Geneva (~40 min) via the D1005 along Lake Geneva. Parking nearby. Combines well with a family day at the lake: beach, walk, Sciez château (historic Aérium gallery), Maison des oiseaux.

To muzeum z przewodnikiem i opieką kolekcyjną zapewnioną przez żarliwych wolontariuszy.

Wizyta jest stosunkowo krótka (45 min- 1h30 w zależności od zainteresowania), idealny obok spaceru nad jeziorem, posiłek w restauracji Sciez lub wizyta w Sciez château i Maison des oiseaux.

The museum is accessible by car from Thonon-les-Bains (~15 min) or Geneva (~40 min) via the D1005 along Lake Geneva. Parking nearby. Combines well with a family day at the lake: beach, walk, Sciez château (historic Aérium gallery), Maison des oiseaux.

Muzeum jest dostępne samochodem z Thonon-les-Bains (~ 15 min) lub Genewy (~ 40 min) przez D1005 wzdłuż jeziora Genewa. Parking niedaleko. Dobrze łączy się z rodzinnym dniem nad jeziorem: plaża, spacer, Sciez château (historyczna galeria Aérium), Maison des oiseaux.

src.what_is:
The Musée du ski ancien at La Chapelle-d'Abondance is a private museum set in a vintage Savoyard farmhouse from 1783 rich with history. The building itself is part of the visit: visitors discover the reconstructed interior of an alpine farmhouse, from the stable to the kitchen, where many everyday objects of peasant life in the Abondance Valley are on show.

The centrepiece is a collection of skis retracing the history of skiing from 1900 onwards, the era of alpine skiing pioneers in the Chablais. You can follow the evolution of gear and technique: leather bindings, solid wood skis, the first profiled boards, sealskins for climbing, traditional sleds and gut-laced snowshoes. Every piece of kit was redesigned to go faster and more safely, and the museum shows that progression in a vivid, moving way.

The museum is open year-round, on demand, which makes for a tailor-made experience. Reservation by SMS is required, specifying "Musée du Ski" to +33 (0)7 62 21 88 68, at most 2 days before the visit. Single fee €4. Ideal as a side trip during a ski or hiking stay at La Chapelle-d'Abondance, a family resort of the Portes du Soleil domain, or as a rainy-day backup.

src.title: Collection 5000+ papillons et insectes 5 continents
src.description: Plus de 5 000 papillons et insectes des 5 continents. Seul muséum de ce type en Savoie et Haute-Savoie, l'un des 3 seulement en France. Collection scientifique majeure de la région.

src.title: Salle Europe-Sibérie
src.description: Faune entomologique locale et nordique : papillons des Alpes, coléoptères de montagne, espèces sibériennes. Compréhension de la biodiversité européenne et arctique.

src.title: Salle Amazonie - papillons morpho
src.description: Papillons morpho bleu géants, insectes tropicaux d'Amérique du Sud, scarabées exotiques. Salle phare pour les amateurs de couleurs spectaculaires et les enfants.

src.title: Salle Afrique-Asie
src.description: Papillons de nuit géants (atlas, hercule), scarabées goliath, mantes religieuses exotiques d'Afrique et d'Asie. Espèces les plus grandes et impressionnantes de la collection.

src.title: Vivarium de phasmes vivants
src.description: Vivarium présentant des phasmes (insectes-bâtons) vivants. Observation en direct du camouflage et du mode de vie de ces insectes mimétiques. Apprécié des enfants.

src.title: Pass '3 sites 3 histoires' 8 € adulte
src.description: Pass combinant le Muséum des Papillons et Insectes + le Musée Archéologique de Viuz-Faverges + le Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges. Adulte 8 € au lieu de 9 €, enfant 5,50 €. À acheter dans un des 3 sites.

src.title: Visites guidées sur réservation
src.description: Visite guidée du Muséum par un membre de l'association Les Amis du Muséum sur réservation au 07 78 41 33 51. Durée environ 1h30, 15 € forfait. Idéal pour les groupes et passionnés d'entomologie.

src.title: Expositions temporaires saisonnières
src.description: Expositions thématiques renouvelées chaque saison (Madagascar lors de la dernière saison documentée). Programmation annoncée sur museumfaverges.wordpress.com et faverges-seythenex.fr.

src.q: Comment réserver au Muséum des Papillons et Insectes ?
src.a: Le Muséum des Papillons et Insectes est en accès libre aux horaires d'ouverture (juin-août Lun-Ven 14h-18h30, Sam 9h30-12h30). Hors saison sur RDV mercredi au +33 (0)7 78 41 33 51. Visites guidées et groupes au même numéro.

src.q: Quel est le tarif du Muséum des Papillons et Insectes ?
src.a: Muséum des Papillons et Insectes : adulte 4 €, enfant 5-14 ans 3 €, gratuit -5 ans. Groupe adultes 3,50 €/enfant 2,50 €. Visite guidée 15 € forfait. Pass '3 sites 3 histoires' adulte 8 €/enfant 5,50 €. Vérifié le 23 mai 2026.

src.q: Quelles sont les horaires du Muséum des Papillons et Insectes ?
src.a: Muséum des Papillons et Insectes : jusqu'au 15 juin Mer 14h-18h sur RDV. 17 juin-31 août Lun-Ven 14h-18h30, Sam 9h30-12h30, fermé Dim. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 23 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

src.q: Que voit-on au Muséum des Papillons et Insectes ?
src.a: Le Muséum des Papillons et Insectes présente 5000+ papillons et insectes des 5 continents en 3 salles : Europe-Sibérie, Amazonie, Afrique-Asie. Vivarium de phasmes vivants. Seul muséum du genre en Savoie et Haute-Savoie.

src.q: Qu'est-ce que le Pass '3 sites 3 histoires' au Muséum des Papillons et Insectes ?
src.a: Le Pass '3 sites 3 histoires' couple le Muséum des Papillons et Insectes + Musée Archéologique de Viuz-Faverges + Donjon des Seigneurs de Faverges. Adulte 8 € (au lieu de 9 €), enfant 5-14 ans 5,50 €. À acheter dans l'un des 3 sites.

src.q: Qui gère le Muséum des Papillons et Insectes ?
src.a: Le Muséum des Papillons et Insectes est géré par l'association Les Amis du Muséum (président Ulrich Gagneron), hébergé dans le Château de Faverges propriété de la commune de Faverges-Seythenex. Association loi 1901 bénévole.

src.q: Le Muséum des Papillons et Insectes est-il accessible aux PMR ?
src.a: Oui complètement. Toutes les salles du Muséum des Papillons et Insectes sont accessibles aux fauteuils roulants y compris les sanitaires PMR. Tarif identique pour tous (4 € adulte, 3 € enfant), gratuit moins de 5 ans.

src.q: Comment venir au Muséum des Papillons et Insectes depuis Annecy ?
src.a: Le Muséum des Papillons et Insectes est à 25 min d'Annecy par N508 puis D1508 jusqu'à Faverges-Seythenex. Cars Région ligne 51 Annecy-Albertville arrêt Faverges centre, 1 km à pied par la route du col de Tamié.

src.what_is:
The municipal museum "Notre Histoire" at Rumilly is one of the most contemporary museums in Haute-Savoie in terms of curation. Housed at 5 place de la Manufacture in the former tobacco manufactory (an emblematic industrial building of the town), it takes a distinctive, contemporary look at the history of the town of Rumilly and its surrounding region (the Albanais), between Lake Annecy and Lake Bourget.

The collection route presents paintings, prints, sculptures and craft and industry objects that help to understand Rumilly and how it has evolved: historic capital of the Albanais, industrial town (tobacco manufactory, Tefal), a town caught between urban and rural worlds. The modern scenography, the absence of any "dusty" curation and the engagement of the mediators make it a lively museum, particularly well-suited to younger audiences.

Very accessible prices: full price €3.70, reduced rate €3 (over 65, jobseekers, RSA, AAH, ASPA benefits), free for everyone on Wednesdays, free all year for under-22s and students. Hours: Wednesday-Saturday 2.30 pm-5.30 pm, closed Sunday-Tuesday and public holidays. Annual closures: 8-26 August and 23-26 December 2026. Temporary exhibition 2026: "Rumilly, town or countryside?" running from 11 February 2026 to 2 January 2027. Contact: +33 (0)4 50 64 64 18.

The modern scenography, the absence of any "dusty" curation and the engagement of the mediators make it a lively museum, particularly well-suited to younger audiences.
