Nowoczesna scenografia, brak jakichkolwiek "zakurzonych" kuracji i zaangażowanie mediatorów sprawiają, że jest to żywe muzeum, szczególnie dobrze przystosowane do młodszych odbiorców.

src.what_is:
The Palais de l'Île is one of the most photographed monuments in France: a medieval building set on a natural rocky islet in the middle of the Thiou canal, in the heart of Annecy's old town. Built from the late 12th-early 13th century, it is mentioned as early as 1325 as the prison of the Counts of Geneva, before becoming a mint (1356-late 14th c.), a seigneurial residence and finally a courthouse at the end of the 16th century.

The Palais served as a prison until 1864 and still holds cells, a torture room and oubliettes. It was listed as a Historic Monument on 16 February 1900, one of the very first in Haute-Savoie. Since 2002 it has housed the CIAP, the City of Annecy's Heritage and Architecture Interpretation Centre, in this 'Ville d'art et d'histoire'.

The visit offers a permanent trail on the urban and architectural history of Annecy from the Middle Ages to today, with regular temporary exhibitions. The Palais belongs to the Musées d'Annecy network. A joint ticket with the Château at 8 EUR (9 EUR July-August) makes the perfect heritage combo.

src.q: What exhibition is currently at Palais Lumière?
src.a: From April 19, 2025 to January 4, 2026, Palais Lumière presents 'Paris-Brussels, 1880-1914', a selection of over three hundred works from a private collection: paintings, drawings, watercolors, prints, illustrated books. Guided visits daily at 14:30.

src.q: Is Palais Lumière wheelchair accessible?
src.a: Yes. Palais Lumière has a lift (80 x 130 cm), accessible toilets with grab bars, and wheelchairs available at reception on request. The building received the S3A label in 2026 guaranteeing accessibility for persons with intellectual disabilities. Hearing loop available at reception.

src.q: How much time should I allow to visit Palais Lumière?
src.a: Allow 1 to 2 hours to visit the temporary exhibition at Palais Lumière, depending on your pace. Guided tours last approximately 1 hour (+ €4 in addition to admission). Family visits for children aged 6–12 last 40 minutes.

src.q: Where can I park to visit Palais Lumière?
src.a: Évian Port parking (paid) is located immediately next to Palais Lumière, on quai Charles-Albert Besson. Adapted disabled parking spaces available next to the building. Palais Lumière is also accessible on foot from the CGN landing stage (200 m) and SNCF station (900 m, 10 min).

src.q: Are dogs allowed at Palais Lumière?
src.a: Only assistance dogs are accepted in the exhibition rooms of Palais Lumière. Other animals are not allowed.

segments.src: How do I buy tickets for Palais Lumière?
segments.mt: Jak kupić bilety na Palais Lumière?

segments.src: Tickets for Palais Lumière are purchased on-site only, at the reception desk of Palais Lumière, quai Charles-Albert Besson. There is no online ticketing. Full price €9, reduced €7, free for under 16.
segments.mt: Bilety na Palais Lumière są kupowane tylko na miejscu, w recepcji Palais Lumière, quai Charles- Albert Besson. Nie ma biletów online. Pełna cena 9 €, obniżona o 7 €, za darmo poniżej 16.

src.lead: On the heights of Taninges, in the Giffre valley, the Parc du Château occupies the former 18th-century domain built by Joachim de la Grange, Count of Taninges. The Clos du Chalet (1857) has overlooked the town since 1995, now a freely accessible public park.

src.what_is:
The Parc du Château overlooks the town of Taninges, historic capital of the Faucigny, at the heart of the Giffre valley. The site occupies the heights where, in the 18th century, Joachim de la Grange, Count of Taninges and Flérier, had a vast château built — a witness to the Savoyard aristocracy of the Ancien Régime that shaped the identity of the quarter.

A century later, in 1857, Laurent-Marie Humbert, a jacquemard (watchmaker-industrialist) who had made his fortune in Paris, returned to his ancestral lands and built a new residence with outbuildings on 1,400 m: the Clos du Chalet. The estate dominated Taninges until 1995, when the property became public.

Today the Parc du Château is a free public space, open all year, every day. It offers remarkable views over Taninges, the Pointe de Marcelly and the Aravis range. Lawns, mature trees and stabilised paths make it a family walking spot, a picnic ground and the setting of summer events such as the FestiGrat's festival. Info: Praz de Lys Sommand Tourist Office, +33 (0)4 50 34 25 05.

src.q: Is entry to the Parc du Château in Taninges paid?
src.a: No. The Parc du Château in Taninges is a freely accessible public space, open year-round, 24/7. No ticket or booking required. Verified on 10 June 2026.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Parc du Château in Taninges?
src.a: The Parc du Château in Taninges is open continuously, 24/7, every day all year. The park is unfenced and does not close. Verified on 10 June 2026.

src.q: Where is the Parc du Château in Taninges?
src.a: The Parc du Château sits on the heights of Taninges (74440), at the quai du Bras de Fer, overlooking the village centre. About 15 min from Cluses via the D902.

src.q: What is the history of the Parc du Château in Taninges?
src.a: The Parc du Château in Taninges occupies the former 18th-century domain built by Joachim de la Grange, Count of Taninges, then the Clos du Chalet (1857) built by Laurent-Marie Humbert, a watchmaker who made his fortune in Paris.

src.q: What can you do at the Parc du Château in Taninges?
src.a: At the Parc du Château in Taninges you can stroll the paths, picnic, enjoy the view of the town and the Giffre valley, and attend the FestiGrat's festival in summer.

src.q: Is the Parc du Château in Taninges stroller-accessible?
src.a: Yes. The main paths of the Parc du Château in Taninges are stabilised and stroller-accessible. Moderate gradient on the access path from the village.

src.q: Are dogs allowed at the Parc du Château in Taninges?
src.a: Yes. Dogs are allowed at the Parc du Château in Taninges provided they are kept on a lead, in line with public-park rules.

src.q: Is there a château to see at the Parc du Château in Taninges?
src.a: The Parc du Château in Taninges sits on the site of the former châteaux of Count Joachim de la Grange (18th c.) and the Clos du Chalet (1857), demolished or transformed. The park keeps their memory and their views.

src.v: 2495 Chemin de Merlet, 74310 Les Houches

May, June, September (except Mondays): daily 10am–6pm (open public holidays). July–August: daily 9:30am–7:30pm. Closed throughout 2027 for pasture rehabilitation. Verified on official website 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

Adult (from 16 years): €9. Child (4–15 years): €6. Free under 4 years. Reduced rate: groups (15+ people on reservation), persons with disabilities, hikers who walked or cycled from the valley. 15% of ticket price funds 2027 operations (closure for rehabilitation). Verified 14 May 2026.
Required for all days of the season. Free online reservation on parcdemerlet.com, open 10 days in advance until the same day (subject to availability). Payment at reception on day of visit.

Free. 4 tiered parking areas (150 spaces total): P1 (300 m from entrance) and P2 (800 m) reserved for persons with disabilities, families with young children, elderly persons and disabled persons. P3 (1 km) and P4 (1.5 km) for all. Allow 10–30 minutes walking uphill (50–100 m elevation gain). Free shuttle from Les Houches SNCF station in peak season (12 round trips/day).
Not allowed. Dogs not permitted in Parc de Merlet (even on leash) to preserve the tranquility of freely roaming animals. Secure kennel with water available at entrance.

Not wheelchair accessible for the entire route. Some facilities are accessible (restaurant, restrooms, reception area). Strollers: prefer a baby carrier. Easy route suitable for all-terrain strollers with large wheels.
2–3 hours on site (easy route: 1 hour; fitness route: 2–3 hours with 200 m elevation gain). Allow 10–30 minutes walking from parking areas.
Tel. +33 7 67 22 82 69 · parcdemerlet.com

segments.mt: Niedostępny dla wózków inwalidzkich na całej trasie. Niektóre udogodnienia są dostępne (restauracja, toalety, recepcja). Wózki: preferować nośnik dla dzieci. Łatwa trasa dla wózków terenowych z dużymi kołami.

segments.src: 2–3 hours on site (easy route: 1 hour; fitness route: 2–3 hours with 200 m elevation gain). Allow 10–30 minutes walking from parking areas.
segments.mt: 2-3 godziny na miejscu (łatwa droga: 1 godzina; droga fitness: 2-3 godziny z 200 m przyrostu wysokości). Pozwól 10- 30 minut spacerem od parkingu.

fields.src: Parc de l'Imperial · Annecy (74) | Loisirs 74
Male peacock displaying its feathers at Parc de l'Impérial in Annecy, a lakeside public park

segments.src: Male peacock displaying its feathers at Parc de l'Impérial in Annecy, a lakeside public park
segments.mt: Paw prezentujący swoje pióra w parku publicznym Parc de l'Impérial

src.what_is:
The Parc de l'Imperial, officially Parc Charles Bosson, is a five-hectare public garden set on the northern shore of Lake Annecy, right next to the Imperial Palace, the Casino and the Imperial beach. Laid out on a former Belle Epoque lakeside estate, it extends the Pâquier along the lakefront and forms one of the largest green walking spaces in central Annecy.

Visitors enjoy a collection of roses, a perennial border, flower beds renewed each season by the city gardeners, shaded lawns and an aviary that delights families. Flat, accessible paths dotted with benches and orientation tables open onto the Tournette and the Semnoz before leading out to the Imperial beach (paid in summer), the harbour and the casino.

The site is part of Annecy's Ville d'art et d'histoire label, with interpretive panels and a direct link to the Jardins de l'Europe. It is a natural stop between the old town, the lake and the avenue d'Albigny — and just a short walk from the Pont des Amours and the canals district.

Położony na dawnej posiadłości nad jeziorem Belle Epoque, rozciąga Pâquier wzdłuż nabrzeża jeziora i tworzy jedną z największych zielonych przestrzeni spacerowych w centrum Annecy.

Andilly Heritage Trail · Charly, Jussy & Saint-Symphorien | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Andilly Heritage Trail · Charly, Jussy & Saint-Symphorien | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Andilly Heritage Trail · Charly, Jussy & Saint- Symphorien

src.what_is:
The Andilly Heritage Trail is a free self-guided interpretive walk crossing the commune of Andilly, in Haute-Savoie, on the south side of the Col du Mont Sion, 6 km north-west of Cruseilles. It comprises 36 illustrated panels spread across the commune's three villages — Charly, Jussy and Saint-Symphorien — and draws on Dominique Bouverat's book "Andilly – Charly, Jussy et Saint-Symphorien – Pages d'histoire".

Designed by the commune and the local association, the trail tells the rural and religious story of the Genevois: 15th-century chapels (the Saint-Jacques chapel of Charly, whose bell tower has been listed as a Monument historique since 1975), fountains, bread ovens, granite houses and traces of village life.

A waymarked variant called "Sur les traces des graniteurs du Mont Sion" extends the walk towards the col, evoking the Piedmontese and Lombard stonecutters who worked the granite in the 19th century. The route can be followed as a single loop or village by village — an ideal cultural stop between Cruseilles, the Saleve and the Geneva border.

src.q: Is the Andilly Heritage Trail free?
src.a: Yes. The Andilly Heritage Trail is an interpretation trail with free access and no charge year-round. The 36 illustrated panels are installed outdoors and can be visited without reservation.

src.q: Where does the Andilly Heritage Trail start?
src.a: The usual starting point of the Heritage Trail is at the Charly chapel (Saint-Jacques chapel, 15th century), in the heart of the village of Charly in Andilly. You can also join it from Jussy or Saint-Symphorien.

src.q: How long does it take to walk the Andilly Heritage Trail?
src.a: Allow 45 min to 1 hour per village for the Andilly Heritage Trail, so 2 to 3 hours to walk Charly, Jussy and Saint-Symphorien in one go. You can also split it across several outings.

src.q: Is the Andilly Heritage Trail suitable for children?
src.a: Yes. The Andilly Heritage Trail lends itself well to a family outing: the 36 illustrated panels tell local history in an accessible way, the villages allow for short stops and the neighbouring Grand Parc d'Andilly can round off the day.

src.q: What is there to see on the Andilly Heritage Trail?
src.a: The Heritage Trail showcases small built heritage (fountains, ovens, washhouses, wayside crosses), village chapels including the Saint-Jacques chapel in Charly (bell tower listed as a Historic Monument in 1975) and the history of the Mont Sion granite cutters.

src.q: Can the Andilly Heritage Trail be extended?
src.a: Yes. A complementary trail 'In the footsteps of the Mont Sion granite cutters' extends the Andilly trail with a loop of about 10 km towards Charly, Presilly and the Mont Sion pass, linked to the history of the 19th-century Piedmontese and Lombard stonecutters.

fields.src: Multi-activity adventure park facing Mont-Blanc: 10 treetop courses, 1.2 km of zip-lines, airbag, human catapult, outdoor escape games. From €6.

segments.src: Multi-activity adventure park facing Mont-Blanc: 10 treetop courses, 1.2 km of zip-lines, airbag, human catapult, outdoor escape games. From €6.
segments.mt: Wielofunkcyjny park przygodowy z widokiem na Mont-Blanc: 10 kursów na drzewach drzew, 1,2 km linii zygzakowatych, poduszka powietrzna, ludzka katapulta, na zewnątrz gry ucieczki. Od 6 euro.

fields.src: High season extends from late June to late August, with daily opening 10am to 7pm and peak crowds in July-August: prefer mornings or book online to avoid queues. The park also opens weekends in June (11am–6pm) and Wednesdays, Saturdays and Sundays afternoons in spring and autumn, quieter periods ideal for enjoying the site peacefully. The park is closed in April and May 2026 (maintenance). School holiday weeks (All Saints, February) see afternoon opening daily. Summer weather allows combining treetop courses and lake swimming; bring comfortable clothes (trousers, trainers) and sun protection.

Park otwiera również weekendy w czerwcu (11am- 6pm) i środy, soboty i niedziele popołudnia wiosną i jesienią, spokojniejsze okresy idealne do korzystania z witryny spokojnie. Park jest zamknięty w kwietniu i maju 2026 (konserwacja). Tygodniami wakacji szkolnych (All Saints, Luty) są codzienne otwarcie popołudniowe. Letnia pogoda pozwala na połączenie kursów treetop i pływanie nad jeziorem; przynieść wygodne ubrania (spodnie, trenerzy) i ochrony przeciwsłonecznej.

src.q: How do I book Passy Accro Lac?
src.a: Book online at passyaccrolac.com (secure payment) or by phone 06 02 66 09 88. Booking is strongly recommended in high season (July-August) and weekends. On-site purchase possible subject to availability. Passy Accro Lac also offers group and birthday packages on prior booking.

src.q: What is the price of Passy Accro Lac?
src.a: Passy Accro Lac treetop courses range from €6 (Pitchounes, 10 games) to €26 (Everest, 96 games). Intermediate prices: Little hikers €11, Big hikers €14, Climbers €17, Alpinists €23. Options: airbag €2/jump, catapult €10/2 jumps, escape game €68 (4 people). Verified 14 May 2026.

src.q: What are the hours of Passy Accro Lac?
src.a: Passy Accro Lac opens in June on weekends 11am to 6pm, in July-August daily 10am to 7pm, in spring and autumn Wednesdays, Saturdays and Sundays afternoon, and daily afternoons during school holidays. The park is closed in April and May 2026. Verified on official website 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

src.q: From what age can children visit Passy Accro Lac?
src.a: Passy Accro Lac welcomes children from age 2 on the Pitchounes course (without harness). The zip-line course for ages 3-6 (40 m) is accessible from age 3. Adult courses are accessible depending on height and independence. Mandatory supervision of a capable adult for children.

src.q: Are dogs allowed at Passy Accro Lac?
src.a: Yes, dogs are accepted at Passy Accro Lac but leashed only on high paths along the lake. They are prohibited on the recreation base itself (treetop courses, beach, play areas). Arrange secure tying if you wish to do the courses.

src.q: Is there parking at Passy Accro Lac?
src.a: Yes. Passy Accro Lac parking is free and located directly at the entrance to Passy lake recreation base (beach parking). Spaces available for cars and coaches. Access from lake road, signposted from D1205.

src.q: Can you swim in the lake after Passy Accro Lac?
src.a: Yes. The Passy lake recreation base where Passy Accro Lac is located has a supervised beach in summer (July-August). Swimming is free, grassy coves and equipped beach. Picnic tables, water fountain and giant board games available on site.

src.q: What is the difference between Passy Accro Lac courses?
src.a: The 10 courses at Passy Accro Lac differ in number of games, height and difficulty. Pitchounes (10 games, €6) for ages 2-3, Little hikers (27 games, €11) and Big hikers (37 games, €14) for children, Climbers (54 games, €17) and Alpinists (77 games, €23) for teens/adults, Everest (96 games, €26) for most athletic (14 m high). All with continuous safety line.

src.what_is:
The Patinoire Jean-Régis is the public indoor ice rink of the city of Annecy, measuring 58 x 28 metres. Located rue du Stade, near the Parc des Sports, it is a flagship municipal facility. The schedule combines public sessions, the Friday-night disco and coached classes in figure skating and hockey.

The rink is a key sporting facility of the city, hosting individual sessions, school groups and clubs throughout the year. The Friday disco evening from 8pm to 11pm is a highlight for teenagers and visiting tourists, with music, lighting and animation.

2026 prices: adult 4.60 EUR + skate hire 3.40 EUR, child 3 EUR. Season: year-round. Hours: Wed, Sat and Sun 2-6pm; Friday evening 8-11pm (disco); daily during school holidays. Step-free access available.

src.what_is:
The La Clusaz ice rink is a covered 1,000 m² facility on the Place du Pré de Foire, in the heart of the village. Run by the town hall, it welcomes the public every winter for public skating, night sessions and Monday-night Eisstock (Austrian-style curling) tournaments.

The venue offers skate rental (€4-5) and helmet rental (€2), background music and a family vibe. The rink is covered but open on the sides, preserving ice quality while keeping the alpine atmosphere. Ideal to round off a ski day or for a family outing off the slopes.

The 2025-2026 season runs from 20 December 2025 to 30 April 2026. 2026 prices: €6.50 adult, €5 child (5-16), 6-entry card €30 (€55 with rentals). Peak-season hours: Fri & Sun 10-12 / 2-7 pm; Wed & Sat 2-7 pm; Mon & Thu 8-10 pm and 10-12 / 2-7 pm; Tue 10-12 / 2-6 pm. Shoulder season (31 Mar to 30 Apr): Tue-Sat & public holidays 2-6 pm; Sun 10-12 / 2-6 pm. Monday Eisstock tournaments 8-10 pm — booking recommended.

src.what_is:
The Piscine L'Île Bleue is the municipal aquatic centre of Seynod, a borough of Annecy. It is an indoor pool with a retractable roof: the glass canopy opens in summer to bring in the sun. It belongs to the UP2A network (Annecy agglomeration pool users) alongside four other pools.

The facility has two pools: a 25 m sports pool with six lanes for lap swimming, lessons and club training, and a 17 m leisure pool with shallower zones for families. A heated paddling pool caters to toddlers. A jacuzzi within the pool area completes the bathing experience.

For relaxation, the wellness area features a sauna, a hammam and a salt cave — ideal to use after a swim. A built-in fitness room lets you combine swimming with cardio and strength training. A snack bar is available on site.

2026 prices: €4.80 adult, €3.60 reduced (under 18, over 65, students, disability), free under 3. 12-entry passes: €48 (adult) / €36 (reduced). Hours vary between school terms and holidays — open 7 days a week with lunch/evening slots on weekdays and wider weekend hours. Phone: +33 4 50 69 17 03.

src.what_is:
The Piscine Jean Régis (also known as Piscine des Fins) is one of Annecy's municipal pools, in the northern districts at 90 chemin des Fins nord. Run by the agglomeration (UP2A), it welcomes the public, schools and clubs every day.

Three pools: a 25 m sports pool (4 lanes), a 15 m learning pool and a paddling pool for toddlers. 2026 prices are among the cheapest in Annecy: €4.60 adult, €3.50 (under 18, over 65, students, disabled persons and carer), free under 5. 12-entry passes: €46 adult / €35 reduced.

School-term hours: Mon 6.45-8 am and 11.15-1.30 pm; Tue & Fri 11.15-1.30 and 8-9.30 pm; Wed 11.15-5 and 6-8.30 pm; Thu 11.15-1.30 and 6-8.30 pm; Sat 10-1 and 2.30-7 pm; Sun 9-1 and 2-6 pm. Summer (4 July - 29 August): Mon-Fri 11.15 am-8 pm, Sat-Sun 10 am-6 pm. Tel. 04 50 57 56 02.

src.what_is:
Albigny Beach is the largest free public beach on Lake Annecy. Located in the Albigny district (former municipality of Annecy-le-Vieux), it stretches over 400 meters of grassy and sandy shoreline facing the mountains. It is the only swimming area in this sector and one of only two free beaches in the city, along with Marquisats Beach.

A grassy beach bordered by trees and flowering hedges, it offers a spacious and sunny setting throughout the day. The lake bed is sandy with a gentle slope facilitating water access for young children. Three sanitary blocks, free outdoor showers, accessible water entry with free flotation chairs. Lifeguard supervision from noon to 7 p.m. in July–August.

Very crowded in summer — its main drawback. Difficult to find spots from noon onward during peak season. Water is clear in the morning, becomes murky in the afternoon with the crowds. Located between Impérial Palace Park to the west and Petit Port marina to the east, accessible on foot from Pâquier (30 minutes of brisk walking), by bike (cycle path along the beach), or by bus (Sibra lines, stops nearby).

segments.src: is the largest free public beach on Lake Annecy. It is the only swimming area in this sector and one of only two free beaches in the city, along with Marquisats Beach.
segments.mt: jest największą bezpłatną plażą publiczną nad jeziorem Annecy. Położony w dzielnicy Albigny (była gmina Annecy- le- Vieux), rozciąga się ponad 400 metrów trawiastej i piaszczystej linii brzegowej z widokiem na góry. Jest to jedyny obszar pływacki w tym sektorze i jeden z tylko dwóch wolnych plaż w mieście, wraz z Marquisats Beach.

segments.src: A grassy beach bordered by trees and flowering hedges, it offers a spacious and sunny setting throughout the day. Lifeguard supervision from noon to 7 p.m. in July–August.
segments.mt: Trawiasta plaża graniczona przez drzewa i kwitnące żywopłoty, oferuje przestronne i słoneczne otoczenie przez cały dzień. Łóżko nad jeziorem jest piaszczyste z łagodnym stokiem ułatwiającym dostęp do wody dla małych dzieci. Trzy bloki sanitarne, prysznic na świeżym powietrzu, dostęp do wody z bezpłatnymi krzesłami flotacyjnymi. Nadzór nad strażnikami od południa do 19: 00 w lipcu-sierpniu.

Located between Impérial Palace Park to the west and Petit Port marina to the east, accessible on foot from Pâquier (30 minutes of brisk walking), by bike (cycle path along the beach), or by bus (Sibra lines, stops nearby).
segments.mt: Bardzo zatłoczony latem - jego główna wada. Trudno znaleźć miejsca od południa dalej w sezonie szczytowym. Woda jest czysta rano, staje się mglista po południu z tłumem. Położony między Impérial Palace Park na zachodzie i Petit Port marina na wschodzie, dostępne na piechotę od Pâquier (30 minut mokrych spacerów), rowerem (ścieżka rowerowa wzdłuż plaży), lub autobusem (linie Sibra, przystanki w pobliżu).

src.q: Is access to Albigny Beach paid?
src.a: No. Access to Albigny Beach is free year-round. It is one of two free public beaches in Annecy, along with Marquisats Beach.

src.q: Is Albigny Beach lifeguard-supervised?
src.a: Yes. Albigny Beach is supervised by lifeguards in July and August, daily from 11 a.m. to 7 p.m. Outside this period, swimming is unsupervised.

src.q: Is there free parking near Albigny Beach?
src.a: No. Parking Albigny and Parking Petit Port near Albigny Beach are paid. They are very full in summer — it is recommended to arrive by bike (cycle path) or bus (Sibra lines 2 and 5, free in July–August).

src.q: Are dogs allowed at Albigny Beach?
src.a: No. Dogs are not allowed at Albigny Beach.

src.q: Is Albigny Beach wheelchair accessible?
src.a: Yes. Albigny Beach has an accessible water entry ramp and free flotation chairs in July–August.

src.q: What is the best time to visit Albigny Beach in summer?
src.a: Arrive at Albigny Beach before 11 a.m. From noon onward in July–August, shaded and sunny spots are taken and it becomes difficult to find space on the lawn. Water is also clearer in the morning.

src.q: Are there showers and restrooms at Albigny Beach?
src.a: Yes. Albigny Beach has three free sanitary blocks (one at each end, one on the other side of the road) and two blocks of four free outdoor showers.

src.q: Can you rent kayaks or paddleboards at Albigny Beach?
src.a: Yes. A kayak, paddleboard, and roller rental operator is located immediately next to Albigny Beach on the Annecy side. Also a mini-golf between the beach and Impérial Palace.

src.what_is:
The Plage Municipale d'Amphion is a 15,000 sqm lawn and pebble beach with the Blue Flag label, on the shores of Lake Geneva, in Publier, Haute-Savoie. The beach combines a tree-shaded lawn and a pebble shore with playgrounds, beach-volley, ping-pong, pétanque and two lakeside guinguettes plus a snack bar in summer.

The Plage d'Amphion holds the Blue Flag 2026 label (Teragir ranking, 18 May 2026), as one of the four kilometres of Publier's lakeshore, the longest French stretch of Lake Geneva. It also features a distinctive Coin Nordic: a Finnish sauna with direct immersion in the lake, a rare nordic experience in France.

Lifeguards on duty July-August 2026. 2026 summer prices: adult free, child free. Blue Flag 2026: yes. Step-free access: partial (main paths accessible).

src.what_is:
The Plage d'Angon is a vast, shaded lawn beach on Lake Annecy at Talloires-Montmin. Set at the hamlet of Angon, 2 km south of the centre of Talloires, it offers a confidential, cool atmosphere away from the busier central Talloires beach, with mature trees, beach-volley and pétanque courts.

The beach faces an exceptional panorama: the Château de Duingt on its islet (11th century) and the western shore of Lake Annecy. Its south-east orientation means afternoon sun and a golden evening light, perfect for a relaxed swim or a picnic at the end of the day.

Lifeguards on duty July-August 2026 (12.30-6.30pm). 2026 summer prices: adult free (paying car park nearby), child free. Blue Flag 2026: no. Step-free access: partial (lawn access, no Tiralo).

src.what_is:
The Plage de La Brune is a 7,000 sqm lawn and sandy-bank beach on Lake Annecy at Veyrier-du-Lac. Equipped with a paddling pool, a T-shaped pontoon, a diving board and a snack-restaurant, it is one of the lake's flagship Blue Flag beaches and a favourite of families on the eastern shore.

The beach holds the Blue Flag label since 2009 and confirmed its renewal in the 2026 Teragir ranking, for a 17th consecutive year, a strong reference for water quality and environmental management. A Tiralo is available for swimmers with reduced mobility, and beach-volley and children's games are nearby.

Lifeguards 1 July-31 August 2026 (11am-7pm). 2026 summer prices: adult free, child free (paying car park 1 May-15 September). Blue Flag 2026: yes. Step-free access: yes (Tiralo).

fields.src: The beach is accessible year-round and free. The best period for swimming is June to September, with water reaching 20–24°C in July–August. During high season (27 June–31 August), the beach is paid and supervised from 10am to 7pm, with heavy crowds in sunny afternoons — prefer early morning or evening. Off-season (April–May, September–October), access is free, the atmosphere more peaceful, but water cooler (15–18°C). In winter and spring, the beach remains open for lakeside walks, but without supervision or swimming possible.

segments.src: The beach is accessible year-round and free. The best period for swimming is June to September, with water reaching 20–24°C in July–August. During high season (27 June–31 August), the beach is paid and supervised from 10am to 7pm, with heavy crowds in sunny afternoons — prefer early morning or evening. Off-season (April–May, September–October), access is free, the atmosphere more peaceful, but water cooler (15–18°C). In winter and spring, the beach remains open for lakeside walks, but without supervision or swimming possible.
segments.mt: Plaża jest dostępna przez cały rok i za darmo. Najlepszym okresem pływania jest czerwiec do września, z wodą do 20- 24 ° C w lipcu - sierpień. W sezonie (27 czerwca - 31 sierpnia) plaża jest płatna i nadzorowana od 10 rano do 19: 00, z ciężkimi tłumami w popołudniach słonecznych - wolą wcześnie rano lub wieczorem. Poza sezonem (April- maj, wrzesień - październik), dostęp jest bezpłatny, atmosfera bardziej spokojne, ale chłodziarka wody (15- 18 ° C). Zimą i wiosną plaża pozostaje otwarta na spacery nad jeziorem, ale bez nadzoru lub pływania możliwe.

src.q: Is Menthon-Saint-Bernard Beach paid?
src.a: Menthon-Saint-Bernard Beach is paid only in July and August (27 June–31 August 2026, 10am–7pm). The rest of the year, access is free.

src.q: What is the rate at Menthon-Saint-Bernard Beach?
src.a: During high season, the adult rate is €4.60, the rate for children under 16 is €2.50. Residents of the municipality pay €2.50 (adults) and resident children enter free. 10-entry pass adult: €42. Verified 14 May 2026.

src.q: Are dogs allowed at Menthon-Saint-Bernard Beach?
src.a: No. Dogs are not allowed at Menthon-Saint-Bernard Beach.

src.q: How do I get to Menthon-Saint-Bernard Beach?
src.a: Menthon-Saint-Bernard Beach is accessible from Annecy (12 km) via the D909a heading towards Talloires. In summer, prefer bike via the Lake Circuit cycle path (12 km, flat) or the Sibra bus (free July–August).

src.q: Where can I park at Menthon-Saint-Bernard Beach?
src.a: Free parking on Port Road (500 m away) is recommended in summer. Two small parking areas immediately near Menthon-Saint-Bernard Beach are often full.

src.q: Is Menthon-Saint-Bernard Beach supervised?
src.a: Yes. Menthon-Saint-Bernard Beach is supervised by lifeguards in July and August, from 10am to 7pm. Off-season, no supervision is provided.

src.q: How do I book Plage de Saint-Jorioz?
src.a: Plage de Saint-Jorioz does not require advance booking. You purchase your entry ticket on site at the kiosks (July–August), or online via the booking system at saint-jorioz.fr (smart card, package or single ticket). Outside July–August, access is free and open.

src.q: What is the price for Plage de Saint-Jorioz?
src.a: Adult rate (18+): €2.60. Child 10–17 years: €1. Under 10: free. Entry after 16:30: €1 (all ages). Season pass (unlimited access Jul–Aug): €22. Plage de Saint-Jorioz is free September to June. Verified 14 May 2026.

src.q: What are the hours for Plage de Saint-Jorioz?
src.a: Plage de Saint-Jorioz is open year-round. Free access 1 January to 30 June and 1 September to 31 December. Paid access with lifeguard supervision 1 July to 31 August from 9:30 to 17:30. Closed 7:00 to 9:30 for cleaning in July–August. Verified on official website 14 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

src.q: Are dogs allowed at Plage de Saint-Jorioz?
src.a: No. Dogs are prohibited at Plage de Saint-Jorioz year-round, even on leash, in accordance with Blue Flag certification.

src.q: Is there free parking at Plage de Saint-Jorioz?
src.a: Yes. Plage de Saint-Jorioz has a large free shaded car park in front of the entrance. It often fills in summer: arrive before 11:00 or use overflow parking (marina or sports complex).

src.q: Is Plage de Saint-Jorioz accessible to people with reduced mobility?
src.a: Yes, Plage de Saint-Jorioz is Tourism and Disability certified. A water access ramp and two amphibious wheelchairs (Tiralo and Hippocampe) are provided free in July–August. Contact the lifeguards on duty for details.

src.what_is:
The Plage de Sévrier is a recently renovated lawn and fine-gravel beach on the western shore of Lake Annecy at Sévrier, Haute-Savoie. Stretching for about 200 m along the RD 1508, it offers an open view of the Château de Duingt and the eastern shore of the lake, with a gentle entry into the water that is ideal for families.

The recent renovation added 94 bike spaces, the largest covered and secured bike park of any beach on Lake Annecy. With direct access to the Voie verte du Lac d'Annecy, the beach is perfect for cycling families combining the cycle path with a swim, with no need to move the car.

Lifeguards 27 June-31 August 2026. 2026 summer prices: adult free (paying parking, ticket machine 9am-7pm), child free. Blue Flag 2026: no. Step-free access: partial (ramp access).

src.what_is:
The Plage de Talloires is a 250x200 m sand and lawn beach on Lake Annecy at Talloires-Montmin, with a paddling pool, diving boards, a slide and an integrated water-sports centre. With 440 parking spaces, it is one of the most equipped beaches on the lake.

The lakefront was redesigned during winter 2025-2026 (municipal order no. 46/2026), with new lawn, redrawn paths and modernised facilities, jointly funded by the village and the Grand Annecy authority. Reopening is scheduled for May 2026 for the summer season.

The beach has been free since 2025 (previously 2.50 EUR per visit), a notable shift compared to the Plage de l'Impérial in Annecy, which becomes paying in 2026. Lifeguards 20 June-30 August 2026 (10am-7pm). 2026 summer prices: adult free, child free. Blue Flag 2026: no. Step-free access: yes (ramp, showers).

src.what_is:
Plage des Marquisats is a free grassy beach located on the west shore of Lake Annecy, 500 meters from Annecy town center. Approximately 200 meters long and 20 meters wide, it is bordered by a sand bank that facilitates water access. The lake floor is covered with pebbles and the water deepens rapidly a few meters from the shore.

Together with Albigny Beach, Plage des Marquisats is one of only two entirely free beaches in Annecy. It benefits from a prime location near the water sports center, tennis courts, the Brise Glace concert hall, and the Marquisats site, which houses the superior art and design school (ESAAA). To the north lies Annecy's water sports base where canoes, kayaks, sailboats, and divers operate — the launch zone is off-limits for swimming.

The beach is shaded by numerous trees, making it particularly popular with families with young children in summer. It is supervised by lifeguards from the start of summer school holidays until August 31, from 11 a.m. to 7 p.m. Outside the season, the beach remains open for walks, picnics, sports, or simply enjoying the view of the lake and surrounding mountains.

Około 200 metrów długości i 20 metrów szerokości, graniczy z piaskiem, który ułatwia dostęp do wody. Podłoga jeziora pokryta jest kamieniami, a woda szybko pogłębia się kilka metrów od brzegu.

Na północy znajduje się baza sportów wodnych Annecy, gdzie działają kajaki, kajaki, żaglowce i nurkowie - strefa startowa jest poza granicami pływania.

fields.src: Plage des Marquisats is accessible year-round. In summer (July–August), it is very busy on fine days — visit early morning for clear water and calm, or after 5 p.m. when crowds subside. Summer lifeguard supervision runs from the start of school holidays to August 31, from 11 a.m. to 7 p.m. Off-season (September to June), the atmosphere is quieter: perfect for picnicking, walking, exercising, or enjoying the lake view. The beach remains shaded by trees even in full summer.

segments.mt: Plage des Marquisats jest dostępny przez cały rok. Latem (lipiec - sierpień), to jest bardzo zajęty w piękne dni - odwiedź wczesnym rankiem dla czystej wody i spokoju, lub po 17.00, gdy tłumy ustąpią. Letni nadzór nad ratownikami trwa od rozpoczęcia wakacji szkolnych do 31 sierpnia, od godz. 11.00 do 19.00 w sezonie (wrzesień do czerwca), atmosfera jest cichsza: idealna do pikników, spacerów, ćwiczeń lub podziwiania widoku jeziora. Plaża pozostaje zacieniona przez drzewa nawet w pełnym lecie.

src.q: Is admission to Plage des Marquisats paid?
src.a: No. Plage des Marquisats is completely free year-round. It is one of only two free beaches in Annecy, along with Albigny Beach.

src.q: How do I get to Plage des Marquisats from Annecy town center?
src.a: Plage des Marquisats is 10 minutes walk from the old town along the lake. By bus, take line 6, 'Marquisats' stop directly across from the beach. By bike, use the cycle path alongside the beach.

src.q: Is Plage des Marquisats supervised?
src.a: Yes. Plage des Marquisats is supervised by lifeguards from the start of summer school holidays to August 31, from 11 a.m. to 7 p.m. daily.

src.q: Are dogs allowed at Plage des Marquisats?
src.a: No. Dogs are not permitted on Plage des Marquisats. Off-season, you may walk your dog in the tree-lined area on the south side (toward Sévrier), but not on the beach lawn itself.

src.q: Is Plage des Marquisats accessible to people with reduced mobility?
src.a: Yes. Plage des Marquisats is an inclusive beach with Hippocampe and Tiralo floating wheelchairs, wheelchair lift, secure pathways, audio beacons for visually impaired visitors, and accessible changing facilities. Inclusion ambassadors present July 5 to August 31.

src.q: Is there free parking at Plage des Marquisats?
src.a: Yes, several free parking lots (Marquisats, Colmyr, Tillier) serve Plage des Marquisats, but they fill up quickly in summer. Alternative: Hôtel de Ville underground parking (paid), 5–10 minutes walk.

src.q: What is the best time to visit Plage des Marquisats?
src.a: The beach is pleasant year-round. In summer, visit early morning for clear water and quiet, or after 5 p.m. when crowds decrease. Off-season, Plage des Marquisats is ideal for picnicking, walking, or sports by the lake.

src.q: Are water sports activities available from Plage des Marquisats?
src.a: Yes. Plage des Marquisats is adjacent to sailing, canoe-kayak, rowing, and diving clubs. Kayak, paddleboard, and pedal boat rentals available nearby. Perfect for exploring the lake from this beach.

src.what_is:
The Plage de l'Impérial is Annecy's iconic beach, stretching 17,000 sqm of imported sand and lawn on the shore of Lake Annecy between the Plage d'Albigny and the Pâquier. Bordered directly by the Voie verte du Lac d'Annecy, it is easily reached on foot, by bike or jogging from the city centre in about 5 minutes.

Major change in 2026: the beach becomes paying in the summer season, according to the city of Annecy. Deputy mayor Marie Bosson stated to France 3 Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes (April 2026) that the beach will be 'secured, fenced and therefore paying' in response to crowd-control issues, with announced 'very modest' fees in the first year (final rates to be confirmed by municipal order).

Lifeguards on duty July-August 2026 (11am-7pm). 2026 summer prices: adult paying from 2026 - rate to be confirmed ('very modest' first-year fees), child rate to be confirmed. Blue Flag 2026: no. Step-free access: yes (Tiralo available).

src.title: Changement gestion 2026 - PAYANTE
src.description: La Plage de l'Impérial à Annecy devient PAYANTE dès l'été 2026 selon décision de la municipalité d'Annecy. La maire déléguée Marie Bosson a déclaré à France 3 Auvergne-Rhône-Alpes (avril 2026) que la plage sera 'sécurisée, clôturée et par conséquent payante' pour répondre aux 'débordements' constatés. Tarifs annoncés 'très modiques' la première année, à confirmer par arrêté municipal.

src.title: 17 000 m² entre Albigny et Pâquier
src.description: La Plage de l'Impérial s'étend sur 17 000 m² de pelouse + plage de sable entre la Plage d'Albigny et le Pâquier au centre d'Annecy. Bordée directement par la Voie verte du Lac d'Annecy - accès vélo, marche, course possible depuis le centre-ville d'Annecy en 5 min.

src.title: Plongeoirs et toboggan fermés depuis 2022
src.description: La Plage de l'Impérial dispose historiquement de plongeoirs et d'un toboggan nautique, fermés pour raisons de sécurité depuis 2022. Rénovation des équipements de baignade prévue pour 2027 selon planning municipal annoncé par la commune d'Annecy.

src.what_is:
The Plage Municipale de Thonon-les-Bains is a 3-hectare beach on the shore of Lake Geneva at Thonon-les-Bains, along the Quai de Ripaille. The largest fully equipped municipal beach on the French shore of the lake, it features a heated 50 m Olympic pool, a heated leisure pool, a paddling pool and an integrated water-sports centre run by the town.

The site offers a rare combination of facilities: diving boards at 1.50 m, 3 m, 5 m and 10 m, a 100 m water slide and a 3,000 sqm marked lake-swimming area. It is one of the few public sites in France to pair a full swimming-pool complex with a supervised lake beach. It holds the Blue Flag 2026 label.

Lifeguards 2 May-13 September 2026. 2026 summer prices: adult 5.70 EUR (rate from 25 May 2026, verified on ville-thonon.fr), child 4 EUR (5-15), free under 5. Blue Flag 2026: yes. Step-free access: yes (ramps, accessible showers).

src.lead: Port des Clerges water sports base, in Thonon-les-Bains: historic starting point for rowing and canoe-kayak on Lake Geneva. Built in the 1980s on chemin de Montjoux, it hosts resident clubs and school groups year-round.

src.what_is:
The Port des Clerges watersports centre is the historic facility at Thonon-les-Bains for paddling and rowing on Lake Geneva. Set on the chemin de Montjoux, east of Thonon's main harbour, it was built in the early 1980s and is home to resident clubs for rowing (Chablais Aviron Thonon) and canoe-kayak, as well as school groups and associations from across the Chablais.

Since April 2018 the centre has been managed by Thonon Agglomeration, which has launched a restructuring programme estimated at around 7 million euros (of which roughly 3 million for the dyke) to secure the facility and develop water sports on the lake.

Through its clubs, the centre offers rowing introductions on Lake Geneva, coastal rowing, leisure outings, canoe-kayak and seasonal courses. It is a quiet launch point to explore Lake Geneva away from the main harbour and the Rives bay promenade.

segments.mt: Port des Clerges centrum sportów wodnych

src.title: Family discovery rafting
src.description: Classic Dranse descent in a 6-8-person raft with guide. Family-friendly routes, class II-III sections. From age 8 depending on flow.

src.title: Sporty rafting
src.description: Most engaged Dranse sections (class III-IV), with technical rapids and sustained whitewater. From age 12-14, good fitness.

src.title: Hydrospeed
src.description: Descent on an individual float with fins and helmet, partially in the whitewater. Extreme thrills, from age 12-14 depending on route.

src.title: Canoraft / inflatable kayak
src.description: Inflatable kayak in duo or solo, to discover river autonomy. Great for improving river reading under guide supervision.

src.title: Half-day
src.description: Standard format: ~1h30-2h on the water + briefing. Ideal alongside other Chablais activities. Around €45-55.

src.title: Full day
src.description: Two descents in a row, or rafting + hydrospeed with picnic on site. Around €75-95 depending on format.

src.title: Stag/hen, groups & business
src.description: Group packages (from 6-8 people), stag/hen, corporate seminars. Privatisation possible, quote on request.

src.title: State-qualified guides
src.description: All guides hold a DEJEPS or BEES Whitewater qualification, per French regulations. Safety and river reading guaranteed.

segments.src: Classic Dranse descent in a 6-8-person raft with guide. Family-friendly routes, class II-III sections. From age 8 depending on flow.
segments.mt: Klasyczne zejście Dranse 'a na tratwie z przewodnikiem. Trasy przyjazne rodzinie, sekcje III klasy. Od 8 roku życia w zależności od przepływu.

segments.src: Most engaged Dranse sections (class III-IV), with technical rapids and sustained whitewater. From age 12-14, good fitness.
segments.mt: Najbardziej zaangażowane sekcje Dranse (klasa III- IV), z rapidami technicznymi i trwałą Whitewater. Od 12- 14 roku życia, dobra kondycja.

segments.mt: Dwa zjazdy z rzędu, lub rafting + hydrospeed z piknikiem na miejscu. Około 75- 95 €w zależności od formatu.

segments.src: Group packages (from 6-8 people), stag/hen, corporate seminars. Privatisation possible, quote on request.
segments.mt: Pakiety grupowe (od 6- 8 osób), jelenia / kury, seminaria firmowe. Możliwość prywatyzacji, cytuję na życzenie.

segments.src: All guides hold a DEJEPS or BEES Whitewater qualification, per French regulations. Safety and river reading guaranteed.
segments.mt: Wszystkie przewodniki posiadają uprawnienia DEJEPS lub BEES Whitewater, zgodnie z przepisami francuskimi. Bezpieczeństwo i czytanie rzek gwarantowane.

src.q: Do I need to swim for rafting on the Dranse?
src.a: Yes, swimming is mandatory for safe rafting or hydrospeed. The required level is the ability to float and move in whitewater without panicking. Good general fitness is also needed.

src.q: What is the minimum age?
src.a: Family discovery rafting is open from age 8 (depending on Dranse flow). Sporty rafting and hydrospeed require at least 12-14 years. Frogs Rafting adapts the route to age and level.

src.q: What does a descent cost?
src.a: Around €45-55 for discovery rafting and €55-65 for hydrospeed or sporty rafting. Group and full-day packages on request. Exact 2026 prices to confirm on frogsrafting.com.

src.q: What does Frogs Rafting provide?
src.a: Frogs Rafting provides all technical equipment: wetsuit, neoprene socks, helmet, lifejacket, paddle (rafting/canoraft) or fins (hydrospeed). Bring: swimwear, towel, worn-out trainers or river shoes, water and snack.

src.q: Are trips cancelled if it rains?
src.a: Rain does not always cancel the trip — it can even raise the flow. However, heavy rainfall can make the Dranse too dangerous (flood). The decision is made the day before by the guide, with rescheduling or refund.

src.q: What's the difference between rafting, hydrospeed and canoraft?
src.a: Rafting is in a team on a 6-8-person raft with guide. Hydrospeed is solo, on a float with fins, more extreme thrills. Canoraft is an inflatable kayak, duo or solo, more focused on learning and river reading.

src.q: Does Frogs Rafting carry insurance?
src.a: Yes, like all professional operators in France, Frogs Rafting carries professional liability insurance. Guides are state-qualified. For personal cover (cancellation, accident), your home/health insurance is usually enough.

src.lead: Remains of the Gothic castle of Habère-Lullin, built in the 15th century by the marquises of Lullin and burnt down by the SS on Christmas night 1943. A few wall sections and a memorial monument recall the tragedy of the Vallée Verte.

segments.src: Remains of the Gothic castle of Habère-Lullin, built in the 15th century by the marquises of Lullin and burnt down by the SS on Christmas night 1943. A few wall sections and a memorial monument recall the tragedy of the Vallée Verte.
segments.mt: Pozostałości gotyckiego zamku Habère-Lullin, zbudowany w XV wieku przez markizy Lullin i spalony przez SS w noc Bożego Narodzenia 1943. Kilka sekcji ściennych i pomnik pamiętają tragedię Vallée Verte.

src.what_is:
The Ruins of the Château d'Habère-Lullin overlook the village of Habère-Lullin in the Vallée Verte, 25 km south-east of Thonon-les-Bains. The château's origins go back to the 15th century, when the marquis of Lullin built a first Gothic-style castle; further wings were added until the 17th century, while preserving the medieval silhouette. The seigneury passed in the early 14th century to the nobles of Fernex, returned in 1429 to Thomas de Genève-Lullin, and was bought in 1696 by the Gerbais de Sonnaz family.

The castle was burnt down on the night of 25–26 December 1943 by the SS of the 28th Todt police regiment: a dance organised for young men evading the STO and for resistance fighters was betrayed and encircled. Several people were shot on the spot, others deported — 32 victims in total, one of the most painful episodes of the Resistance in Haute-Savoie.

Today only a few sections of wall survive, along with a memorial monument raised to the young victims. The site is freely accessible all year round and forms a place of remembrance at the heart of the Vallée Verte.

Początki zamku sięgają XV wieku, kiedy markiz Lullin zbudował pierwszy zamek w stylu Gothic; do XVII wieku dodano kolejne skrzydła, zachowując jednocześnie średniowieczną sylwetkę. Seigneury przeszedł na początku XIV wieku do szlachty Fernex, wrócił w 1429 do Thomas de Genève- Lullin, i został kupiony w 1696 roku przez rodzinę Gerbais de Sonnaz.

Kilka osób zostało zastrzelonych na miejscu, inni deportowani - w sumie 32 ofiary, jeden z najbardziej bolesnych epizodów ruchu oporu w Haute-Savoie.

src.q: Can you visit the Ruins of the castle of Habère-Lullin freely?
src.a: Yes. The Ruins of the castle of Habère-Lullin are open with free access and no charge year-round. Guided commentary tours can be organised on reservation by the Alpes du Léman Tourist Office.

src.q: What is the history of the Ruins of the castle of Habère-Lullin?
src.a: The castle of Habère-Lullin was built in the 15th century by the marquises of Lullin in Gothic style, enlarged until the 17th century then acquired by the Gerbais de Sonnaz family in 1696. It was burnt down by the SS on the night of 25 to 26 December 1943, during the Christmas 1943 massacre that cost the lives of 32 people.

src.q: What remains of the castle of Habère-Lullin today?
src.a: Today only a few wall sections of the Ruins of the castle of Habère-Lullin remain, along with a memorial monument erected in memory of the young victims of the Christmas 1943 massacre.

src.q: Where are the Ruins of the castle of Habère-Lullin located?
src.a: The Ruins of the castle of Habère-Lullin are in the centre of the village of Habère-Lullin (74420), in the Vallée Verte, about 25 km south-east of Thonon-les-Bains.

src.q: How long does it take to visit the Ruins of the castle of Habère-Lullin?
src.a: Allow 20 to 30 min on site to discover the Ruins of the castle of Habère-Lullin and read the memorial panels. The stop can be extended with a walk in the village and the Vallée Verte.

src.q: Are the Ruins of the castle of Habère-Lullin family-friendly?
src.a: Yes, the Ruins of the castle of Habère-Lullin can be visited with the family. The ground can be uneven around the remains: bring closed shoes and the usual care for young children near the walls.

segments.src: Can you visit the Ruins of the castle of Habère-Lullin freely?
segments.mt: Czy możesz odwiedzić Ruiny zamku Habère-Lullin swobodnie?

segments.src: Yes. The Ruins of the castle of Habère-Lullin are open with free access and no charge year-round. Guided commentary tours can be organised on reservation by the Alpes du Léman Tourist Office.
segments.mt: Tak. Ruiny zamku Habère-Lullin są otwarte z bezpłatnym dostępem i bez opłat przez cały rok. Wycieczki z przewodnikiem mogą być organizowane przez Biuro Turystyczne Alpes du Léman.

Został spalony przez SS w nocy od 25 do 26 grudnia 1943 roku, podczas masakry Bożego Narodzenia 1943, która kosztowała życie 32 osób.

src.what_is:
The Sentier des Espagnols / Pas du Roc is a historical trail on the Plateau des Glières with a remarkable double memory. The cliff steps along the route were cut in 1830 by Spanish republican refugees who had fled to Haute-Savoie after the collapse of the Spanish liberal revolution of 1820-1823, a moving chapter of 19th-century European political migration.

The trail leads to the Pas du Roc at 1,800 m altitude, a strategic passage cut into the cliff face of the plateau. The same path then became a key crossing point for the Maquis des Glières in 1944, used by Resistance fighters to access and resupply the plateau. The route is therefore both a memorial to political refuge and to the French Resistance.

Free entry all year. Difficulty: moderate. Distance: 8 km loop, 600 m elevation gain, 3-4 h walking time. Best season: May to October. Free parking at the Plateau des Glières car park. Not suitable for wheelchairs (mountain trail).

src.what_is:
The 'Senses and Discovery of Farm Animals' Walking Trail is one of the two flagship routes of the Ferme de Chosal, an educational farm at Copponex, in the French Genevois area, 15 min from Cruseilles. The trail invites visitors to observe and approach the farm animals in their enclosures, set in beautifully landscaped grounds.

Along the way visitors meet donkeys, sheep, goats, billy goats, pigs, hens, pheasants, guinea fowl, rabbits and guinea pigs. Panels describe the life, diet and behaviour of each species. The trail complements the barefoot 'Nature et Sens' sensory loop on the same site. The Ferme de Chosal is run by a workforce integration organisation that trains and employs people with disabilities: visits therefore also have a real social and inclusive dimension. The farm is open from 5 April to 3 November, 10 am to 6 pm (last entry 4.30 pm), with admission at €9.80 for adults and €7 for children; under-2s are free and reduced rates are available for groups, schools and visitors with disabilities.

src.what_is:
Vitam Wellness & Spa is a true relaxation sanctuary spanning 1,100 m² in Neydens, just 15 minutes from Geneva and 30 minutes from Annecy. Set within the UCPA Vitam leisure centre, this space offers a complete experience of relaxation and rejuvenation where water in all its forms becomes your wellness ally.

The Roman-Irish circuit forms the heart of the experience: alternate between hot and cold air baths, humid and dry vapours, pools at varying temperatures to stimulate circulation and invigorate the body. Finnish saunas, Oriental hammams, a 33°C heated outdoor balneotherapy pool equipped with bubble beds, hydrojets, swan-neck jets and jacuzzis: each space is designed for deep relaxation.

What sets Vitam apart from other Haute-Savoie spas is the included access to the aquatic area: 25m pool, slides, current river and outdoor lagoon pool (seasonal). You enjoy two complementary worlds in a single entry. The facility also features a cosy rest room with herbal tea bar, a spa bar offering hand/foot/face treatments, and a menu of treatments inspired by traditions from around the world (Algotherm, Tradition d'Orient, Abhyanga). The on-site Vitam'Café lets you extend your relaxation with light refreshments.

Open 7 days a week, Vitam adapts to all schedules with extended hours (until 9pm weekdays) and flexible time-based pricing. Free parking, wheelchair access, equipped changing rooms: everything is designed for your comfort.

src.q: Do I need to book in advance?
src.a: Booking is not required for general spa access, but strongly recommended for treatments (72h advance). During high season and weekends, arrive early to avoid crowds.

src.q: What's included in the entry price?
src.a: Spa entry includes access to the Roman-Irish circuit, saunas, hammams, outdoor balneotherapy pool, herbal tea bar and all wellness facilities, PLUS full access to the aquatic area (pool, slides, lagoon pool). Treatments and massages are extra.

src.q: What should I bring?
src.a: Swimming costume required (must be worn throughout), large bath towel and flip-flops. Secure lockers and changing rooms provided. Towels available for rent on-site.

src.q: Is the spa suitable for first-time visitors?
src.a: Yes, the circuit is self-guided and intuitive. An explanatory booklet for the Roman-Irish circuit is available at reception. Staff are available to guide you through the different areas.

src.q: Can I come with children?
src.a: The wellness & spa area is accessible from age 16. Those aged 16-18 must be accompanied by an adult. The aquatic area (included) welcomes all ages with a paddling pool for little ones.

src.q: Is there a restaurant on-site?
src.a: Yes, Vitam'Café in the aquatic area offers hot dishes, salads, snacks and drinks. Direct access from the spa without needing to get dressed. Vending machines and free herbal tea bar in the rest area.

src.q: When is the best time to avoid crowds?
src.a: Tuesdays, Wednesdays and Thursdays outside school holidays are quietest, especially in the morning (10am-12pm). Weekends and Wednesday afternoons are the busiest times.

src.q: Is the spa wheelchair accessible?
src.a: Yes, the area is wheelchair accessible with lift, adapted changing rooms, accessible toilets and assistance available. The balneotherapy pool has easier access. Free companion entry upon presentation of disability card.

fields.src: Grotte de Balme in Magland — Caving in Haute-Savoie | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Grotte de Balme in Magland — Caving in Haute-Savoie | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Grotte de Balme in Magland - Caving in Haute-Savoie

src.what_is:
The Grotte de Balme in Magland is one of the emblematic natural caves of the Arve valley, in Haute-Savoie. Set in the limestone massif that lines the valley between Cluses and Sallanches, it has long attracted amateur and confirmed cavers for its network of galleries, halls and mineral formations (stalactites, draperies, rimstone pools). It is an unsited natural site: access is by a mountain path, and progression inside requires equipment, lighting and experience — or better, the supervision of a qualified instructor.

Why come (and with whom). The Grotte de Balme is well-suited to a first taste of caving for anyone wanting to discover this discipline that combines exploration, geological observation and physical commitment. Several guide offices in the Arve and Faucigny valleys offer half-day or full-day guided outings, with gear provided (oversuit, helmet with headlamp, harness depending on the route). Outings are usually open to participants from age 10-12, in good physical condition and not claustrophobic; some sections may involve narrow passages or short abseils.

Caution and good to know. As the cave is not equipped for the public, independent visiting is not recommended without proven experience and proper gear — the risks of getting lost, falling or sudden flooding are real. The best season runs from spring to autumn, avoiding heavy rain events. For a safe discovery, contact the caving instructors of the Arve valley (CDS 74, guide unions) or the Cluses-Magland Tourist Office. Guided outings cost around €40-50 (half-day intro) up to €80 (more committing day) depending on the package; 2026 prices to confirm.

It is an unsited natural site: access is by a mountain path, and progression inside requires equipment, lighting and experience — or better, the supervision of a qualified instructor.
segments.mt: jest jedną z symbolicznych jaskiń naturalnych doliny Arve, w Haute-Savoie. Umieszczony w wapiennym masywie, który łączy dolinę między Cluses i Sallanches, od dawna przyciągał amatorów i potwierdzone jaskółki dla swojej sieci galerii, hali i formacji mineralnych (stalaktyty, draperie, baseny rimstone). Jest to niespotykane miejsce naturalne: dostęp odbywa się drogą górską, a postęp wewnątrz wymaga sprzętu, oświetlenia i doświadczenia - lub lepiej, nadzoru wykwalifikowanego instruktora.

segments.src: The Grotte de Balme is well-suited to a first taste of caving for anyone wanting to discover this discipline that combines exploration, geological observation and physical commitment. Several guide offices in the Arve and Faucigny valleys offer half-day or full-day guided outings, with gear provided (oversuit, helmet with headlamp, harness depending on the route). Outings are usually open to participants from age 10-12, in good physical condition and not claustrophobic; some sections may involve narrow passages or short abseils.
segments.mt: Grotte de Balme jest dobrze przystosowany do pierwszego smaku ugniatania dla każdego, kto chce odkryć tę dyscyplinę, która łączy eksplorację, geologiczną obserwację i zaangażowanie fizyczne. Kilka biur przewodnich w dolinach Arve i Faucigny oferuje półdniowe lub całodniowe wyjścia z przewodnikiem, z zapewnionym sprzętem (kombinezon, hełm z reflektorem, uprząż w zależności od trasy). Wyjścia są zazwyczaj otwarte dla uczestników od 10- 12 roku życia, w dobrym stanie fizycznym, a nie klaustrofobii; niektóre sekcje mogą obejmować wąskie przejścia lub krótkie otwory.

segments.src: As the cave is not equipped for the public, independent visiting is
segments.mt: Ponieważ jaskinia nie jest wyposażona dla publiczności, niezależna wizyta jest

segments.src: recommended without proven experience and proper gear — the risks of getting lost, falling or sudden flooding are real. The best season runs from spring to autumn, avoiding heavy rain events. For a safe discovery, contact the caving instructors of the Arve valley (CDS 74, guide unions) or the Cluses-Magland Tourist Office. Guided outings cost around €40-50 (half-day intro) up to €80 (more committing day) depending on the package; 2026 prices to confirm.
segments.mt: zaleca się bez udowodnionego doświadczenia i odpowiedniego sprzętu - ryzyko utraty, upadku lub nagłej powodzi są realne. Najlepszy sezon trwa od wiosny do jesieni, unikając ciężkich opadów deszczu. Aby uzyskać bezpieczne odkrycie, skontaktuj się z instruktorami krycia doliny Arve (CDS 74, związki przewodników) lub z biurem turystycznym Cluses- Magland. Wyjazdy z przewodnikiem kosztują około 40- 50 EUR (wstęp w połowie dnia) do 80 EUR (więcej dni zobowiązania) w zależności od pakietu; 2026 ceny do potwierdzenia.

src.q: Can the Grotte de Balme be visited independently?
src.a: As the cave is not equipped for the public, independent visiting is only realistic for confirmed, self-sufficient and equipped cavers. For a safe discovery, going with a qualified instructor is very strongly recommended.

src.q: What gear is needed?
src.a: For a guided outing, specific gear (oversuit, helmet with headlamp, harness) is provided. Bring high hiking or mountain shoes, gloves, warm clothes (the cave is ~8-10°C).

src.q: From what age?
src.a: Guided intro outings are usually accessible from age 10-12 depending on the instructor, in good physical condition and not claustrophobic.

src.q: How much does a guided outing cost?
src.a: Plan around €40-50 for a half-day intro and €70-80 for a more committing day. 2026 prices to confirm with local caving instructors.

src.q: When is the best season?
src.a: May to October, avoiding heavy rain or snowmelt (flood risk). Inside the cave stays around 8-10°C year-round.

src.q: Is there any risk?
src.a: Yes: getting lost, falling, sudden flooding after rain. Independent practice without experience or gear is not recommended. Going with a qualified instructor is the best safety guarantee.

src.q: Where to find an instructor?
src.a: Contact the Cluses-Montagnes Tourist Office or the Haute-Savoie Departmental Caving Committee (CDS 74) for a list of instructors and guide offices active in the area.

src.what_is:
The Stelsia Casino Megève is one of Haute-Savoie's two casinos on the Mont-Blanc side and the only casino in this Savoyard village. Located at 192 rue Charles Feige, right in the centre, it belongs to the Le Stelsia Casinos group, which runs several venues across France.

The complex offers 65 latest-generation slot machines (stakes from €0.01 to €5.00), electronic roulette, traditional games (English roulette, blackjack, seasonal poker), a VIP room, plus the atmospheric Gallery Bar and the Le Rendez-vous nightclub to round off the evening. The casino also hosts occasional exhibitions and themed events.

Access to the gaming room is free of charge but requires a valid ID and appropriate dress; entry is forbidden to under-18s, people on the gaming-exclusion register and pets. Opening hours vary by season (extended to around 4 a.m. in the high winter season, reduced in shoulder season) - check the official website for the day's schedule.

src.q: From what age can you do Tactiq Aventure in Cruseilles?
src.a: Tactiq Aventure is open from 90 cm in height (Discovery course, around 4-5 years old). The Junior course starts at 1.15 m and the Vertige course at 1.40 m, which corresponds to teenagers and adults.

src.q: How much do Tactiq Aventure courses cost in Cruseilles?
src.a: 2026 prices at Tactiq Aventure: €16 for the children's Discovery course, €20 for the Junior course, €25 for the adult Vertige course (€22 for under-16s) and €10 for the Tactiq Game (escape forest).

src.q: When is Tactiq Aventure open?
src.a: Tactiq Aventure is open from April to November 2026 according to a seasonal calendar: 1.30 p.m.-6.30 p.m. in April, Wednesdays, weekends and bank holidays until early July, then daily 10 a.m.-7 p.m. from 4 July to 30 August. Check tactiqaventure.com for the exact schedule.

src.q: Do you need to book at Tactiq Aventure?
src.a: Booking is strongly advised at Tactiq Aventure, especially in high season (July-August) and at weekends, on tactiqaventure.com or by phone on 04 50 32 91 85.

src.q: Where is Tactiq Aventure?
src.a: Tactiq Aventure is set in the heart of Parc des Dronières, route des Dronières, 74350 Cruseilles, halfway between Annecy and Saint-Julien-en-Genevois (~20 km from each).

src.q: Is Tactiq Aventure accessible to groups?
src.a: Yes. Tactiq Aventure offers dedicated rates and packages for groups of 10 or more (school groups, birthdays, corporate events) on booking.

src.q: What is the Tactiq Game?
src.a: The Tactiq Game is an outdoor adventure game in the style of an "escape forest" at the heart of the Dronières forest, blending riddles and observation. It costs €10 per person, in addition to or independently of the tree-top courses.

src.what_is:
The Jaillet gondola links the village of Megève (~1,113 m) to the Jaillet plateau at 1,585 m altitude in just a few minutes. Operated by SEM du Jaillet / Les Portes du Mont-Blanc, it is the gateway in winter to the Portes du Mont-Blanc ski area (Megève, Combloux, Cordon, La Giettaz). The lower station is at 821 route du Jaillet, with free 7-hour parking.

In summer, the top of the lift opens onto a free panoramic play area facing Mont Blanc, with trampolines, climbing structures and swings. Family trails leave directly from the top station: the Lac du Jaillet loop (20 minutes), the Col du Jaillet with its Roman granite milestone discovered in 1964, and a route through the woods to Combloux. Mountain-bike trails and the Giant Swing round out the summer offer.

2026 prices: adult return around EUR 18-22, child EUR 12-15. Season: mid-December to mid-April (winter) and mid-June to early September (summer). Hours: 9 a.m. to 5 p.m. daily in season. Accessible for visitors with reduced mobility (top station and summit play area).

src.q: How do I book the Mont-Chéry gondola at Les Gets?
src.a: The Mont-Chéry gondola: Online at pass.lesgets.com, operated directly by SOLEGETS (no reseller). Pedestrian, MTB and multi-ride cards available. Also sold at the lower station ticket office.

src.q: What are the prices for the Mont-Chéry gondola?
src.a: The Mont-Chéry gondola: Single ride adult: €7.20, child: €5.40. Summit round-trip adult: €10, child: €7. Family pass (min. 4 people): adult €9, child €6.30. 6-ride card: €27.50. Free under 5. Verified 28 April 2026.

src.q: What are the opening hours of the Mont-Chéry gondola?
src.a: The Mont-Chéry gondola: Summer peak season (July–Aug): daily 9:00 – 17:30. Winter: approx. 9:00 – 16:45. Exact dates on lesgets.com. Verified 28 April 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

src.q: Can you ride the gondola back down from Mont-Chéry?
src.a: Yes — the gondola carries passengers both up and down. This is useful after a hike to avoid the steep descent on foot. Downhill tickets are available at the ticket office.

src.q: What is the view like from the top of Mont-Chéry?
src.a: The Mont-Chéry gondola: A 360° panorama: Mont Blanc massif, the Aravis range, Lac Léman, the Swiss Alps and the Dents du Midi. One of the widest accessible mountain views in the area.

src.q: Does the Mont-Chéry gondola operate during the MTB World Cup?
src.a: Yes, but MTB access is suspended from 24 August 17:30 until end of season. Pedestrian trips remain available. Spectators can ride the gondola to watch the racing.

src.what_is:
The Panoramic Mont-Blanc gondola is a cross-border cable car between France and Italy, linking the summit of the Aiguille du Midi cable car (3,777 m) to the Pointe Helbronner (3,466 m, Italy) in about 30 minutes. Operated by the Compagnie du Mont-Blanc on the French side and connected to the Skyway Monte Bianco on the Italian side, it spans 5 km above the Géant Glacier.

The journey is made in pulsed 4-seat cabins, with no contact with other passengers. The view takes in the Géant Glacier, the Mont Blanc du Tacul, the Grandes Jorasses and the Dent du Géant - one of the most spectacular glacier panoramas in Europe accessible without crampons.

At Pointe Helbronner on the Italian side, a panoramic terrace projects out at 3,466 m, with exhibitions on the minerals of the massif and a connection to the Skyway Monte Bianco down to Courmayeur in the Aosta Valley. Summer season: 4 July to 27 September 2026, weather permitting. Booking strongly recommended; pass priced as a supplement to the Aiguille du Midi ticket.

src.what_is:
The Pléney Gondola departs from the heart of Morzine village (1,000 m) and climbs to the Pléney summit, opening up a wide view over the Chablais, Lake Geneva and the Mont Blanc range. It is the most central lift in Morzine, located on Route du Téléphérique steps from the village shops.

In summer (July – August), the gondola serves Morzine's renowned bikepark, a summer sledge run and marked hiking trails. On Tuesday and Thursday evenings from 8 July to 28 August, it runs until 7:00 pm for sunset hikes and dinners in altitude. In winter, it is the main access from the village to the Morzine – Les Gets ski domain.

Wtorkowe i czwartkowe wieczory od 8 lipca do 28 sierpnia odbywają się do 19: 00 na wycieczki i kolacje na wysokości. W zimie jest to główny dostęp z wioski do domeny narciarskiej Morzine - Les Gets.

src.q: How do I book the Super-Châtel Gondola?
src.a: Book the Super-Châtel Gondola online at skipass-chatel.com or buy at the ticket desk on Chemin de Sous le Crêt in Châtel village.

src.q: How much does the Super-Châtel Gondola cost in summer?
src.a: Super-Châtel Gondola adult return: €8.90–9.80. One-way: €5.60–6.20. Child 5–15 yrs return: €6.60–7.30. Free under 5. Portes du Soleil Multi Pass: free access. Verified on the official site on 9 May 2026.

src.q: What are the Super-Châtel Gondola summer 2026 hours?
src.a: The Super-Châtel Gondola runs 26 June–31 August 2026 daily. Exact times to confirm (2025: 9:00 am–5:15 pm). Verified on the official site on 9 May 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

src.q: Is the Super-Châtel Gondola alpine lake accessible in summer?
src.a: Yes, the Super-Châtel Gondola gives access to the 2-hectare alpine lake in July and August only. It is also reachable on foot from Châtel village in about 50 minutes.

src.q: Is the Portes du Soleil Multi Pass valid on the Super-Châtel Gondola?
src.a: Yes, a valid Portes du Soleil Multi Pass gives free unlimited pedestrian access to the Super-Châtel Gondola and all other operational Châtel lifts in summer.

src.q: Can I walk up to the Super-Châtel Gondola top from Châtel village?
src.a: Yes, a hiking path reaches the Super-Châtel Gondola plateau from Châtel village in about 50 minutes. This is the only option outside the July–August gondola season.

src.what_is:
The Aiguille du Midi cable car links Chamonix-Mont-Blanc (1,035 m) to the summit of the Aiguille du Midi at 3,842 m in two sections and roughly 20 minutes. Operated by the Compagnie du Mont-Blanc, it is one of the highest cable cars in Europe and the quickest panoramic vantage point facing the Mont Blanc range.

At the top, visitors can step into the famous Step into the Void glass box, suspended 1,035 m above the valley; ride the summit lift to the 360-degree panoramic terrace; explore the Espace Vertical mountaineering museum at 3,777 m; watch the Mont-Blanc 4D film at the lower station; and cross to the Italian side aboard the Panoramic Mont-Blanc gondola to Pointe Helbronner (summer only).

2026 prices: adult return EUR 60.20 - 83, child rate available. Season: year-round (subject to weather and maintenance windows). Hours: from 7:10 a.m. to 6 p.m., variable depending on season. Partially accessible for visitors with reduced mobility (top section by lift). The Aiguille du Midi is also the launch point for the Vallée Blanche, the legendary 22 km off-piste glacier descent.

src.q: Comment réserver le Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi ?
src.a: Réservation du Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi en ligne sur aiguilledumidi.montblancnaturalresort.com avec créneau horaire obligatoire en juillet-août. Achat également aux caisses de la gare de départ à Chamonix sous réserve de places.

src.q: Quel est le tarif du Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi ?
src.a: Le tarif adulte aller-retour du Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi est de 60,20 € à 83 € selon la date (dynamique). Jeune/senior 51,20–70,60 €. Tribu 186,70–257,40 €. Combo Helbronner Italie 125,50 €. Vérifié le 21 mai 2026.

src.q: Quelles sont les horaires du Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi ?
src.a: Le Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi ouvre en été du 1 juillet au 23 août tous les jours 6h10–18h00, du 13 juin au 30 juin 7h10–17h30. Hiver 8h10–16h30. Vérifié sur le site officiel le 21 mai 2026 — sous réserve de modifications saisonnières.

src.q: Le Pas dans le Vide du Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi est-il inclus ?
src.a: Oui. Le billet du Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi inclut le Pas dans le Vide, la terrasse panoramique 360°, l'ascenseur du sommet et l'Espace Vertical. Seul le cinéma 4D Mont-Blanc se règle en supplément aux caisses, par CB.

src.q: Peut-on aller en Italie depuis le Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi ?
src.a: Oui en été. Depuis le sommet du Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi, la télécabine Panoramic Mont-Blanc relie la pointe Helbronner (3 466 m, Italie) en 30 min. Ouverture 4 juillet – 27 septembre 2026. Passeport ou CNI obligatoire.

src.q: À partir de quel âge prendre le Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi ?
src.a: Le Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi est interdit aux moins de 3 ans et fortement déconseillé aux moins de 5 ans à cause de l'altitude (3 842 m). Pathologies cardio-respiratoires : consulter un médecin avant la visite.

src.q: Où se garer pour le Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi ?
src.a: Deux parkings payants à 5 min à pied du Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi : le Grépon (couvert, centre-ville) et le parking Aiguille du Midi (extérieur, bornes électriques). Arriver avant 8 h en haute saison.

src.q: Le Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi est-il accessible aux PMR ?
src.a: Oui, le sommet du Téléphérique de l'Aiguille du Midi est accessible aux fauteuils roulants avec accompagnement : terrasse 3 842 m, Pas dans le Vide, belvédère Rebuffat. Toilettes adaptées à la gare de départ. Tarif réduit 50 % personne + accompagnant.

src.q: Is the Grands Montets cable car open in 2026?
src.a: The ski area is accessible in winter (6 Dec 2025–3 May 2026) via the Plan Joran gondola (skiers only). It is closed from 4 May to 30 November 2026 for the new 3S cable car works. Full reopening is planned for December 2026.

src.q: What does skiing at the Grands Montets cost in 2026?
src.a: The Grands Montets is included in the Chamonix Le Pass: adult day pass 59.20–74 € depending on the period. An AlpiSki AGM ski touring ticket is also available at 36.80 € for adults (desk purchase only). Verified 28 April 2026.

src.q: What are the Grands Montets opening hours?
src.a: Winter 2025-2026 (6 Dec–3 May): opens daily at 8:30. Closed May–November 2026 for construction. Reopening planned December 2026. Verified 28 April 2026 — subject to changes.

src.q: Why is the Grands Montets cable car closed?
src.a: The original cable cars (Lognan I and II) were destroyed by fire in August 2018. A new 3S cable car designed by Renzo Piano has been under construction since 2024, linking Argentière to the 3,275 m summit. The Plan Joran gondola provides interim winter access.

src.q: When will the new Grands Montets cable car open?
src.a: Compagnie du Mont-Blanc targets December 2026 for the commissioning of the new Renzo Piano 3S cable car, linking Argentière to Lognan and the 3,275 m summit. Follow progress at montblancnaturalresort.com.

src.q: Can I visit the Grands Montets on foot or in summer?
src.a: Not in summer 2026: the Plan Joran gondola is closed from 4 May to 30 November 2026 for construction. In winter, pedestrian access is not permitted on Plan Joran (skiers only). Full public reopening is planned for December 2026.

src.q: Is there a restaurant at the Grands Montets?
src.a: A restaurant operates at the Lognan level (2,134 m) during the winter ski season. Facilities at the summit will be part of the new station opening in December 2026. Check the official site for current service availability.

src.what_is:
The Salève cable car is a high-capacity panoramic lift linking the French commune of Étrembières (Pas-de-l'Échelle, 432 m) to the summit of the Salève (1,097 m), known as the 'Balcony of Geneva', in just 5 minutes. The two 60-seat cabins make it one of the highest-capacity lines in the region.

Co-operated by RATP Dev, TPG (Transports Publics Genevois) and COMAG (Poma), the cable car is integrated into the Geneva public transport network: TPG line 8 reaches Veyrier-Douane (a short walk to the lift), with a direct shuttle to the base on summer Sundays. A reduced fare of EUR 12 return applies on presentation of an Oùra travel card, a SwissPass GA, a Swiss half-fare card or a Geneva City Pass.

At the top, the free Salève Secret exhibition (over 100 m2), a climbing wall, the panoramic Restaurant 360, waymarked hiking trails, paragliding launches and views over Geneva, Lake Geneva, the Jura and Mont Blanc complete the offer. 2026 prices: adult return EUR 18. Hours: 9 a.m. to 10:45 p.m. Thursday to Saturday in summer; reduced opening the rest of the week.

src.title: Undergo a conventional thermal cure
src.description: 18-day cure for rheumatology, digestive conditions or urinary conditions, with medical prescription and partial National Health Insurance coverage. Mandatory reservation.

src.title: Book a wellness treatment with evian® water
src.description: Signature treatments with natural mineral water: hydromassage bath, rain shower massage, jet pool, clay poultices. Sauna/hammam access included before or after treatment.

src.description: Pay-per-visit access to the mixed sauna, two single-sex hammams and rest areas with views of Lake Léman. Entry from €12.

src.description: Group classes in the gym (pilates, yoga, cardio) and in thermal pool at 33-34°C (aquabike, aquarun, aquapilates, baby swimming). Mandatory reservation for aquabike and aquarun.

src.title: Get a massage or facial treatment
src.description: Personalized relaxation massages (20 to 75 min), reflexology, Derm Acte or Pure Altitude facials. Performed by qualified practitioners in naturally lit cabins.
