src.description: Cryotherapy session down to -160°C for 3 minutes to boost cellular oxygenation, activate drainage and produce pain-relieving and anti-inflammatory effects.

fields.src: Thermes d'Évian is open year-round, with a two-week annual closure during year-end holidays. The Wellness area and thermal cures operate Monday to Saturday, with extended weekday hours (until 7pm) allowing visits after work. The Aqua and Fitness areas remain accessible until 9:15pm on weekdays. January through March is the season for conventional cures (cure participants welcome from January 12). Free cures and wellness treatments are available year-round by reservation. Summer offers the advantage of enjoying the Thermes' private garden and outdoor relaxation areas with lake views. Book several days in advance for wellness treatments and aquabike/aquarun classes (limited to 15 participants).

segments.mt: Thermes d'Évian jest otwarty przez cały rok, z dwutygodniowym corocznym zamknięciem podczas wakacji końca roku. Od poniedziałku do soboty działa strefa wellness i uzdrawianie termiczne z wydłużoną godziną powszednią (do 19: 00), pozwalającą na wizyty po pracy. Obszary Aqua i Fitness pozostają dostępne do 21: 15 w dni robocze. Od stycznia do marca jest sezon na konwencjonalne leki (uczestnicy leczenia mile widziane od 12 stycznia). Bezpłatne leczenie i zabiegi wellness są dostępne przez cały rok rezerwacji. Lato oferuje zaletę cieszenia się prywatnym ogrodem i odkrytymi obszarami relaksu z widokiem na jezioro. Zarezerwuj kilka dni wcześniej dla zabiegów wellness i klas aquabike / aquarun (ograniczona do 15 uczestników).

src.q: How do I book Thermes d'Évian?
src.a: Book online at lesthermesevian.com or by phone +33 4 50 75 02 30 (Monday-Saturday 9am-6pm). For conventional cures, a medical prescription is required. Wellness treatments and aquabike/aquarun classes must be reserved in advance at Thermes d'Évian.

src.q: What are the rates at Thermes d'Évian?
src.a: Sauna/hammam access €12. Fitness access €15. Pool + sauna/hammam access €20. Full-day Total Fitness entry €30. Wellness treatments: reflexology €85 (45 min), hydrating facial €110 (60 min). 18-day conventional cures: rates on request, partial National Health Insurance coverage based on eligibility. Verified May 14, 2026.

src.q: What are the hours of Thermes d'Évian?
src.a: Wellness area: Monday-Friday 8am-7pm, Saturday 8am-6pm. Aqua and Fitness areas: until 9:15pm on weekdays. Two-week annual closure during year-end holidays. Detailed hours at lesthermesevian.com. Verified on official website May 14, 2026 — subject to seasonal changes.

src.q: Is Thermes d'Évian wheelchair accessible?
src.a: Yes, Thermes d'Évian is wheelchair accessible with assistance. The establishment has an elevator (80×130 cm), doors ≥77 cm, adapted WC with grab bars and staff trained in disability accommodation. Drop-off available at the entrance.

src.q: Is there free parking at Thermes d'Évian?
src.a: Yes, free private parking is available at Thermes d'Évian, subject to availability. Parking is reserved for light vehicles (no motorhomes, caravans or vans). Fitness members receive an access badge (€10 deposit).

src.q: Do I need a medical certificate for a cure at Thermes d'Évian?
src.a: For 18-day conventional cures (rheumatology, digestive conditions, urinary conditions), a medical prescription is mandatory. For free cures, wellness treatments and fitness access, no medical certificate is required, but Thermes d'Évian recommends checking your physical condition with a doctor before any sports activity.

src.q: What is the best time to visit the Thermal Baths?
src.a: The Thermal Baths are open year-round. To avoid crowds, choose weekdays outside school holidays. Friday and Saturday evenings (until 9pm) offer a nighttime atmosphere under the stars in the outdoor pools.

src.q: Can we come with children?
src.a: Yes, from age 13 accompanied by an adult for general access. Family slots (3-12 years) exist on Sunday mornings outside holidays and certain days during school holidays. Baby sessions (6 months–2 years) with coach are offered on weekend mornings.

src.q: What should we bring?
src.a: A swimsuit (long shorts and burkinis not allowed), clean plastic sandals (or purchase on-site €6) and a coin for lockers. Bathrobes and towels are provided. Bring your own towel for changing rooms if desired.

src.q: What is the recommended visit duration?
src.a: The classic package is 3 hours, ideal to enjoy the full circuit: indoor and outdoor pools, saunas, hammams, herbal tea room and sensory experiences. Allow time to relax fully.

src.q: Do we need to book in advance?
src.a: Yes, booking is strongly recommended, especially in high season (school holidays, weekends). You can book online at thermes-saint-gervais.com or by phone at +33 4 50 47 54 57.

src.q: Is there parking?
src.a: Yes, free parking is available in the immediate vicinity of the Thermal Baths. Access is also very easy on foot from Le Fayet SNCF station (5 min) or via the free elevator from Saint-Gervais center.

src.q: Are the Thermal Baths accessible to people with reduced mobility?
src.a: Yes, the establishment renovated in 2018 has adapted facilities. It is recommended to contact the Thermal Baths in advance for any specific assistance request.

src.q: Can we buy products on-site?
src.a: Yes, a boutique offers the complete range of Saint-Gervais Mont-Blanc dermo-thermal cosmetics based on thermal water, as well as gift sets. Purchase possible on-site or online.

segments.mt: Klasyczny pakiet jest 3 godziny, idealny do korzystania z całego obwodu: kryte i odkryte baseny, sauny, młotki, ziołowa herbaciarnia i doświadczenia sensoryczne. Daj czas na pełne odprężenie.

segments.src: Do we need to book in advance?
Yes, booking is strongly recommended, especially in high season (school holidays, weekends). You can book online at thermes-saint-gervais.com or by phone at +33 4 50 47 54 57.
segments.mt: Tak, rezerwacja jest zdecydowanie zalecane, zwłaszcza w sezonie (wakacje szkolne, weekendy). Możesz zarezerwować online na termes- saint- gervais.com lub telefonicznie na + 33 4 50 47 54 57.

segments.src: Yes, free parking is available in the immediate vicinity of the Thermal Baths. Access is also very easy on foot from Le Fayet SNCF station (5 min) or via the free elevator from Saint-Gervais center.
segments.mt: Tak, bezpłatny parking dostępny jest w bezpośrednim sąsiedztwie łaźni termalnej. Z stacji Le Fayet SNCF (5 min) lub bezpłatną windą z centrum Saint- Gervais można łatwo dojechać pieszo.

segments.src: Are the Thermal Baths accessible to people with reduced mobility?
segments.mt: Czy kąpiele termalne są dostępne dla osób o ograniczonej sprawności ruchowej?

segments.src: Yes, the establishment renovated in 2018 has adapted facilities. It is recommended to contact the Thermal Baths in advance for any specific assistance request.
segments.mt: Tak, zakład odnowiony w 2018 r. dostosował urządzenia. Zaleca się wcześniejsze skontaktowanie się z Kąpielami Termalnymi w celu uzyskania jakiejkolwiek konkretnej pomocy.
Czy możemy kupić produkty na miejscu?

segments.src: Yes, a boutique offers the complete range of Saint-Gervais Mont-Blanc dermo-thermal cosmetics based on thermal water, as well as gift sets. Purchase possible on-site or online.
segments.mt: Tak, butik oferuje pełną gamę kosmetyków dermotermalnych Saint- Gervais Mont-Blanc opartych na wodzie termalnej, jak również zestawy prezentów. Zakup możliwy na miejscu lub online.

src.lead: The Thiou, natural outflow of Lake Annecy, runs for 3.5 km through the old town to the Fier. Pont des Amours, canal du Vassé, Palais de l'Île, Sadi Carnot floodgates: Annecy's heritage backbone is best discovered on foot.

segments.src: The Thiou, natural outflow of Lake Annecy, runs for 3.5 km through the old town to the Fier. Pont des Amours, canal du Vassé, Palais de l'Île, Sadi Carnot floodgates: Annecy's heritage backbone is best discovered on foot.
segments.mt: Thiou, naturalny wypływ jeziora Annecy, biegnie 3,5 km przez stare miasto do Fier. Pont des Amours, canal du Vassé, Palais de l 'île, Sadi Carnot jest najlepiej odkryte na piechotę.

src.what_is:
The Thiou is a short 3.5 km river and the natural outflow of Lake Annecy, joining the Fier at Meythet (which in turn flows into the Rhône). As it leaves the lake, it splits into two main arms - Le Port and the Vassé canal - which cross the historic heart of Annecy before rejoining downstream of the Palais de l'Île. With an average flow of 8 m³/s, it is both the city's scenic axis and its historic backbone.

The Thiou is the spine of the old quarter, designated Ville d'Art et d'Histoire: its quaysides, arcades, Pont des Amours, flower-lined footbridges and the emblematic Palais de l'Île (12th century) have become Annecy's postcard image. The river also has an industrial past: the Aussedat paper mill, the Cran forges, tanneries and printing works ran on its waterpower in the 19th century.

After Savoy's union with France (1860), engineer Sadi Carnot redesigned the canals and in 1874 installed a clever system of 62 floodgates over 6 km, which still regulate the lake's level today. A heritage walk mapped out by the City of Annecy can be followed on foot, free year-round.

src.what_is:
The Tour de Bellecombe at Reignier-Ésery (74930) is one of the most impressive medieval remnants of the Savoyard Genevois. A square 12th-13th-century keep, it is the last surviving element of a now-vanished medieval castle - only the imposing keep withstood destruction and the passage of time.

The castle had a remarkable historical function: it controlled a toll on the Arve river, where merchants crossing between the Duchy of Savoy and the Republic of Geneva had to pay a duty levied from the fortress. That financial role justified the construction of such a powerful tower, today 25 m high (originally around 30 m) and clearly visible from a great distance.

Free access all year, no ticketing. A 5-minute footpath leads from the free car park to the foot of the keep. The site is unguarded, with no interior visit (the tower is closed to the public) but a remarkable exterior viewpoint over the Arve valley. Dogs on leads tolerated; not wheelchair-accessible (uneven path).

fields.src: 14th-century medieval tower known as Tour des Langues at the Port de Rives in Thonon-les-Bains on the shore of Lake Geneva, Haute-Savoie

segments.src: 14th-century medieval tower known as Tour des Langues at the Port de Rives in Thonon-les-Bains on the shore of Lake Geneva, Haute-Savoie
segments.mt: Średniowieczna wieża Tour des Langues w Port de Rives w Thonon-les-Bains nad jeziorem Genewa, Haute-Savoie

src.what_is:
The Tour des Langues at Thonon-les-Bains (74200) is one of the last medieval remnants of the Port de Rives, the historic harbour of Thonon on Lake Geneva. A 14th-century tower, it is all that survives of a fortified house belonging to the mestralie of Thonon - the fiscal and territorial administration established by Philip I of Savoy in 1283 to collect the feudal dues of the Chablais.

The origin of the name 'des Langues' (of the tongues) is one of the most picturesque toponymic anecdotes in Haute-Savoie. The butchers of Thonon were required to deliver to the mestralie every year the tongues of every ox they slaughtered in town. This feudal due in kind remained in force until it was abolished in 1773.

Free access all year, no ticketing. The tower is visible from the lakeshore promenade and from the Port de Rives, a 5-minute walk from the city centre. Information panel on site. Not open to the public inside; viewing from outside only. Free harbour parking; the surrounding promenade is fully wheelchair-accessible.

src.q: Quelle est la longueur du Tour du Mont-Blanc ?
src.a: Selon FFRandonnée et mongr.fr, l'itinéraire classique du Tour du Mont-Blanc fait 170 km en 11 étapes anti-horaire. La boucle GR® TMB complète avec toutes variantes (Aiguilles Rouges, col de Tricot, val Ferret) atteint 215 km.

src.q: L'accès au Tour du Mont-Blanc est-il payant ?
src.a: Non. Le sentier du Tour du Mont-Blanc est libre et gratuit toute l'année. Seuls les refuges et gîtes (~40 sur le tour) sont payants : 60-90 €/nuit demi-pension. Réservation obligatoire dès janvier-février.

src.q: Quelles sont les étapes haute-savoyardes du Tour du Mont-Blanc ?
src.a: Le Tour du Mont-Blanc compte 4 étapes en Haute-Savoie : Les Houches → Les Contamines (col Voza), Les Contamines → Les Chapieux, Tré-le-Champ → La Flégère (Aiguilles Rouges), La Flégère → Les Houches retour.

src.q: Combien de jours pour faire le Tour du Mont-Blanc ?
src.a: Le Tour du Mont-Blanc se parcourt en 10 à 12 jours pour l'itinéraire intégral 170 km. Étapes 15-20 km / 5-8h marche / 600-1 200 m D+ par jour. Variantes courtes 5-7 jours possibles pour randonneurs sportifs.

src.q: Quand peut-on faire le Tour du Mont-Blanc ?
src.a: Le Tour du Mont-Blanc se parcourt exclusivement de mi-juin à mi-septembre. Hors saison : cols à 2 000-2 600 m enneigés, refuges fermés. Saison optimale : début juillet à début septembre selon enneigement annuel.

src.q: Le Tour du Mont-Blanc est-il accessible aux PMR ?
src.a: Non. Le Tour du Mont-Blanc n'est pas accessible PMR ni poussettes : sentier de montagne avec passages techniques (échelles et chaînes aux Aiguilles Rouges), exposition altitude, terrain rocheux. Public randonneurs confirmés uniquement.

src.q: Faut-il réserver les refuges du Tour du Mont-Blanc ?
src.a: Oui IMPÉRATIVEMENT. Les ~40 refuges du Tour du Mont-Blanc sont saturés dès mars-avril pour les nuits week-end juillet-août. RÉSERVATION JANVIER-FÉVRIER OBLIGATOIRE sur sites individuels des refuges ou plateformes Espace Mont-Blanc.

src.q: Quel est le sens classique du Tour du Mont-Blanc ?
src.a: Le sens classique du Tour du Mont-Blanc est ANTI-HORAIRE au départ des Houches (Haute-Savoie). Itinéraire : Les Houches → Les Contamines → Les Chapieux → Courmayeur (Italie) → Champex (Suisse) → Tré-le-Champ → La Flégère → retour Les Houches.

fields.src: Tour of Abondance Village - Vallée d'Abondance | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Tour of Abondance Village - Vallée d'Abondance | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Tour of Abondance Village - Vallée d 'Abondance

src.what_is:
The Tour of Abondance Village is a very easy family walk that takes about 1 h 30 to discover the historic town of Abondance, its heritage and the surrounding nature. The loop starts from the village centre and first follows the path along the Dranse. It then climbs gently through woodland on the slopes above Abondance to a viewpoint with an open panorama over the village, its mountains and the bell tower.

The route passes close to the Abbaye d'Abondance (12th century), a Cistercian site open daily in summer, and the Maison du Fromage Abondance, where you can stop to discover the making of Abondance PDO pressed-cooked cheese and the diversity of Savoyard cheeses. From there, a forest track along the banks of the Dranse leads back to the village centre.

The route is waymarked in yellow (PDIPR); a map is available at the tourist information office. Dogs must be kept on a lead because of the grazing herds.

src.q: How long does the Tour of Abondance Village take?
src.a: The Tour of Abondance Village is a very easy family walk of about 1 h 30 starting from the centre of the village.

src.q: Is the Tour of Abondance Village free?
src.a: Yes. The Tour of Abondance Village has free access year-round. Only the Abbey and the Maison du Fromage, both on the route, charge for visits.

src.q: Where can I find the Tour of Abondance Village map?
src.a: The Tour of Abondance Village map is available free at the Pays d'Évian Vallée d'Abondance tourist information office in Abondance itself.

src.q: What can you see on the Tour of Abondance Village?
src.a: The Tour of Abondance Village takes in the village centre, the Abbaye d'Abondance (12th century), the Maison du Fromage Abondance, the banks of the Dranse and a viewpoint over the bell tower and the peaks of the Vallée d'Abondance.

src.q: Is the Tour of Abondance Village suitable for children?
src.a: Yes. The Tour of Abondance Village is rated "very easy" and designed for families: short route, no technical difficulty, alternating riverside, woodland and heritage.

src.q: Can you do the Tour of Abondance Village with a dog?
src.a: Yes, provided the dog is on a lead, especially near the alpine pastures and grazing herds that line part of the Tour of Abondance Village.

segments.src: How long does the Tour of Abondance Village take?
segments.mt: Jak długo trwa zwiedzanie wioski Abondance?

segments.src: The Tour of Abondance Village is a very easy family walk of about 1 h 30 starting from the centre of the village.
segments.mt: Wycieczka Abondance Village jest bardzo łatwy rodzinny spacer około 1 h 30 począwszy od centrum wsi.

segments.src: Is the Tour of Abondance Village free?
segments.mt: Czy zwiedzanie wioski Abondance jest bezpłatne?

segments.src: Yes. The Tour of Abondance Village has free access year-round. Only the Abbey and the Maison du Fromage, both on the route, charge for visits.
segments.mt: Tak. Tour z Abondance Village ma bezpłatny dostęp rok-runda. Tylko Abbey i Maison du Fromage, na trasie, pobierają opłaty za wizyty.

segments.src: Where can I find the Tour of Abondance Village map?
segments.mt: Gdzie mogę znaleźć mapę zwiedzania wioski Abondance?

segments.src: The Tour of Abondance Village map is available free at the Pays d'Évian Vallée d'Abondance tourist information office in Abondance itself.
segments.mt: Mapa wsi Abondance jest dostępna bezpłatnie w Pays d 'Évian Vallée d' Abondance biura informacji turystycznej w samym Abondance.

segments.src: What can you see on the Tour of Abondance Village?
segments.mt: Co można zobaczyć na trasie Abondance Village?

segments.src: The Tour of Abondance Village takes in the village centre, the Abbaye d'Abondance (12th century), the Maison du Fromage Abondance, the banks of the Dranse and a viewpoint over the bell tower and the peaks of the Vallée d'Abondance.
segments.mt: Wycieczka Abondance Village odbywa się w centrum wsi, Abbaye d 'Abondance (XII wiek), Maison du Fromage Abondance, brzegi Dranse i punkt widzenia nad dzwonnicą i szczyty Vallée d' Abondance.

segments.src: Is the Tour of Abondance Village suitable for children?
segments.mt: Czy zwiedzanie wioski Abondance jest odpowiednie dla dzieci?

segments.src: Yes. The Tour of Abondance Village is rated "very easy" and designed for families: short route, no technical difficulty, alternating riverside, woodland and heritage.
segments.mt: Tak. Wycieczka Abondance Village jest oceniana jako "bardzo łatwa" i przeznaczona dla rodzin: krótka trasa, brak trudności technicznych, naprzemienne brzegi rzeki, lasy i dziedzictwo.

segments.src: Can you do the Tour of Abondance Village with a dog?
segments.mt: Możesz zrobić wycieczkę po wiosce Abondance z psem?

segments.src: Yes, provided the dog is on a lead, especially near the alpine pastures and grazing herds that line part of the Tour of Abondance Village.
segments.mt: Tak, pod warunkiem, że pies jest na prowadzeniu, zwłaszcza w pobliżu alpejskich pastwisk i stad wypasu, które linii część Tour Abondance Village.

src.what_is:
The Montenvers train is a rack railway brought into service in 1909, linking Chamonix-Mont-Blanc station (1,042 m) to the Montenvers site (1,913 m) in about 20 minutes. Operated by the Compagnie du Mont-Blanc, it delivers visitors to the iconic belvedere facing the Mer de Glace, the largest glacier in France (7 km long).

At the top, the site includes the gondola down to the surface of the glacier (closed for maintenance until 29 May 2026), the ice cave carved each year inside the glacier (closed until 5 June 2026), the Glaciorium interpretation centre on glaciers, the Refuge-Hôtel du Montenvers and the Grand Hôtel du Montenvers, a historic 1880 building listed as a historic monument.

2026 prices: adult return EUR 31.50, Voyage pack EUR 49.70 (train + gondola + ice cave + Glaciorium). Season: year-round, daily in summer. Hours: 8:30 a.m. to 5 p.m., variable by season. The Montenvers is also a stage on the Balcon Nord, the famous high trail from the Plan de l'Aiguille (Aiguille du Midi cable car) to the Montenvers.

fields.src: Just-Jump Marnaz — Trampoline park & birthdays | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Just-Jump Marnaz — Trampoline park & birthdays | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Just-Jump Marnaz - Trampolina park & urodziny 124; Loisirs 74

src.q: From what age can you jump at Just-Jump Marnaz?
src.a: The park welcomes children from around 4-6 years depending on the zone, with a dedicated little-jumpers area. Teens and adults enjoy free-jump, dodge ball and trampoline basket. Check the minimum age on just-jump.fr.

src.q: How much does a session cost?
src.a: A 1-hour session is around €10-15 at full price. Multi-session passes, family/birthday/group deals and off-peak promos are available. 2026 prices to confirm on just-jump.fr.

src.q: Are special socks required?
src.a: Yes, Just-Jump non-slip socks are mandatory for jumping. They are sold on site and reusable from one visit to the next.

src.q: Do I need to book?
src.a: Online booking is strongly recommended on Wednesday afternoons, weekends and during school holidays. Sessions are timed and may sell out.

src.q: Can I host a birthday party?
src.a: Yes, dedicated birthday packages are offered: 1 h jumping + private room for cake/drinks subject to availability, personalised invitations. Book several weeks ahead.

src.q: Which groups are welcome?
src.a: All groups: families, teens, stag/hen, seminars, school groups outside school hours. Group rates from a minimum number. Evening privatisation possible.

src.q: Is there parking?
src.a: Yes, free parking in front of the park. Easy access from A40 Cluses exit.

src.what_is:
R'Jump Léman is a 1,300 m² indoor trampoline park near Lake Geneva, at 9 rue des Esserts, Annemasse / Vétraz-Monthoux, just 5 minutes from Geneva. Features include dozens of interconnected trampolines, a giant foam pit, tumbling tracks (inclined trampolines for acrobatics), a slackline over the foam pit, and basketball / dodgeball zones.

2026 prices: €14/hour from age 6, €10 per additional hour, €12 for groups of 10+. Mandatory non-slip socks are on sale on site. Booking recommended on weekends and during school holidays.

Summer hours: Tuesday to Sunday, 10 am-8 pm non-stop (varies off-season). Perfect for kids' energy, birthdays, bachelor parties or team-building. Juice bar and parents' area.

src.q: How do I book Mont Blanc Tramway tickets?
src.a: Buy at the ticket desk in Saint-Gervais or Le Fayet. A reduced rate is available online if purchased at least 3 days in advance. Wheelchair users must book by email (caisses.tmb@compagniedumonblanc.fr) several days ahead.

src.q: What does the Mont Blanc Tramway cost?
src.a: Adult return to Bellevue: €34. Child 5–14 or senior 65+: €28.90. Adult single: €22.20. Under 5 free. Disabled and companion: 50% off on presentation of card. Verified 28 April 2026.

src.q: What are the Mont Blanc Tramway timetables?
src.a: Summer 2026: 13 June to 13 September daily, departures from Le Fayet at 9 am, 11 am, 1 pm, 3 pm. Closed 7 April–12 June 2026. Winter: December–April, daily or weekends depending on snow. Verified 28 April 2026 — subject to change.

src.q: How high does the Mont Blanc Tramway go in summer?
src.a: In summer the terminus is the Nid d'Aigle at 2,412 m, at the foot of the Bionnassay glacier. The renovated platform offers secure access to the séracs and altitude trails. In winter the terminus is Bellevue (1,800 m).

src.q: Is skiing included in the Mont Blanc Tramway ticket?
src.a: No. The TMB ticket covers the train only. A separate ski pass for the Les Houches–Saint-Gervais area is available at the Le Fayet and Saint-Gervais stations.

src.q: Are dogs allowed on the Mont Blanc Tramway?
src.a: Yes, with restrictions: maximum 1 wheelchair per train, best accessed from Saint-Gervais station (not Le Fayet Thermal Park). Col de Voza is recommended for wheelchair users; Bellevue is difficult. Advance booking by email is mandatory.

segments.src: In summer the terminus is the Nid d'Aigle at 2,412 m, at the foot of the Bionnassay glacier. The renovated platform offers secure access to the séracs and altitude trails. In winter the terminus is Bellevue (1,800 m).
segments.mt: Latem terminal jest Nid d 'Aigle o długości 2,412 m, u podnóża lodowca Bionnase. Odnowiona platforma zapewnia bezpieczny dostęp do séraców i szlaków wysokościowych. W zimie terminal jest Bellevue (1800 m).

segments.src: Is skiing included in the Mont Blanc Tramway ticket?
segments.mt: Czy jazda na nartach jest uwzględniona w bilecie Mont Blanc Tramway?

segments.src: No. The TMB ticket covers the train only. A separate ski pass for the Les Houches–Saint-Gervais area is available at the Le Fayet and Saint-Gervais stations.
segments.mt: Nie. Bilet TMB obejmuje tylko pociąg. Na stacjach Le Fayet i Saint- Gervais dostępna jest osobna karta narciarska dla obszaru Les Houches- Saint- Gervais.

segments.src: Are dogs allowed on the Mont Blanc Tramway?
segments.mt: Czy psy są dozwolone na Mont Blanc Tramway?

segments.src: Yes, with restrictions: maximum 1 wheelchair per train, best accessed from Saint-Gervais station (not Le Fayet Thermal Park). Col de Voza is recommended for wheelchair users; Bellevue is difficult. Advance booking by email is mandatory.
segments.mt: Tak, z ograniczeniami: maksymalnie 1 wózek na pociąg, najlepiej dostępne ze stacji Saint- Gervais (nie Le Fayet Thermal Park). Pułkownik de Voza jest zalecany dla użytkowników wózków inwalidzkich; Bellevue jest trudne. Zaliczka przez e-mail jest obowiązkowa.

src.what_is:
Tropicaland is the only dinosaur park in Haute-Savoie, set between Geneva and Annecy a few kilometres from Saint-Julien-en-Genevois and Viry. On 1.5 hectares of dense jungle and green meadows, the park presents 35 life-size sculptures of dinosaurs and prehistoric creatures: tyrannosaurs, velociraptors, stegosaurs, triceratops, dimetrodons, spinosaurs, pterosaurs and other Jurassic giants, from baby dinos to the most imposing adults.

The park celebrates its 20th anniversary in 2026. It combines three themes: botany, with a jungle of original plants and trees; prehistoric animals (the 35 dinosaurs); and a mini-farm with domestic animals and exotic birds for the youngest visitors. A 'Dino Hunter' trail game invites children to spot the dinosaurs hidden in the vegetation and identify which are herbivores or carnivores.

2026 prices: adult EUR 10, child 3-11 EUR 7, under 3 free. Season: April to early November. Hours: 10 a.m. to 6 p.m. daily in season. Tropicaland is a family venue and does not host school groups or holiday-club outings - individual families only.

fields.src: Fantasticable de Châtel — Giant Zip-line in the Portes du Soleil (2026) | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Fantasticable de Châtel — Giant Zip-line in the Portes du Soleil (2026) | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Fantasticable de Châtel - Giant Zip- line w Portes du Soleil (2026)

src.what_is:
The Châtel Fantasticable is one of the most impressive giant zip-lines in the French Alps, set up on the Linga sector of the Portes du Soleil resort. The course consists of two successive cables: a first section of 1,200 metres, followed by a second of 1,325 metres, separated by an intermediate platform. You ride lying down in a flying-man position with a belly harness — close to paragliding without the pilot.

The sensation is powerful: heights reach up to 240 metres above the Plaine Dranse, and top speed approaches 100 km/h on the second cable. The panorama covers the Portes du Soleil peaks, the Abondance valley and, on clear days, the Mont Blanc massif in the background. Each ride lasts about a minute and a half — plan 1 h 30 on site with briefing, lift access and both descents.

The activity is operated by the resort's lift company. Entry conditions: solo weight between 35 and 120 kg; for duo flights, the lighter rider must be at least 35 kg, combined weight 90-150 kg, with a maximum gap of 40 kg between them. Limits may vary with weather. No technical skill is required.

Access to the start is via the Pierre Longue and Rochassons chairlifts, from the Linga sector in Châtel. The flat price is €43 per person for summer 2026, solo or duo. Booking advised in July-August via Châtel Réservation or directly at the Linga ticket office.

src.title: 400 m AD+ via ferrata route
src.description: 400 m of equipped cable including Tibetan bridges, wooden walkways, beam and monkey bridges above the Bérard torrent. AD+ grade, continuous lifeline throughout.

src.title: Starting wall crossing
src.description: First vertical step on rungs and handrails, the most physically and mentally engaging passage of the route. Escape route possible before continuing.

src.title: Aerial bridges and walkways
src.description: Three suspended structures cross the torrent at varying heights: main Tibetan bridge, wooden footbridge and monkey bridge, for thrills facing the waterfall.

src.title: Guided ascent with high-mountain guide
src.description: Several Chamonix and Vallorcine guide offices offer supervised outings (equipment included, early morning recommended). Indicative rate €80-120 per person.

src.title: Visit to the Grotte à Farinet
src.description: Small natural cave freely accessible on the return path, legendary refuge of the Valaisan counterfeiter Joseph-Samuel Farinet (1845-1880). Headlamp recommended.

src.title: Break at the Buvette de la Cascade de Bérard
src.description: Mountain refreshment bar open in season (May to September, 10am-6pm weather dependent) serving drinks, croque-monsieur and homemade tarts facing the waterfall. Cash payment.

src.title: Bérard valley discovery trail
src.description: The footpath alongside the via ferrata continues to the Pierre à Bérard refuge (2 h from the buvette), in the heart of the Aiguilles Rouges nature reserve.

src.title: Mid-route escape exit
src.description: Emergency exit laid out after the wooden footbridge on the right bank, allowing return to the hiking trail without continuing the higher aerial sections.

segments.src: 400 m of equipped cable including Tibetan bridges, wooden walkways, beam and monkey bridges above the Bérard torrent. AD+ grade, continuous lifeline throughout.
segments.mt: 400 m wyposażonego kabla, w tym mosty tybetańskie, drewniane chodniki, belki i mosty małpy nad torrentem Bérard. AD + gatunek, ciągła linia życia.

segments.src: First vertical step on rungs and handrails, the most physically and mentally engaging passage of the route. Escape route possible before continuing.
segments.mt: Pierwszy pionowy krok na szczeblach i poręczach, najbardziej fizycznie i psychicznie angażujący przejazd trasy. Droga ucieczki możliwa przed kontynuowaniem.

segments.src: Three suspended structures cross the torrent at varying heights: main Tibetan bridge, wooden footbridge and monkey bridge, for thrills facing the waterfall.
segments.mt: Trzy zawieszone konstrukcje przechodzą przez torrent na różnych wysokościach: główny most tybetański, drewniany most podnóżkowy i mostek małpy, dla dreszczy stojących przed wodospadem.

segments.src: Several Chamonix and Vallorcine guide offices offer supervised outings (equipment included, early morning recommended). Indicative rate €80-120 per person.
segments.mt: Kilka biur przewodników Chamonix i Vallorcine oferuje nadzorowane wyjścia (sprzęt w tym, rano zalecane). Wskaźnik indykatywny 80- 120 EUR za osobę.

segments.src: Visit to the Grotte à Farinet
Small natural cave freely accessible on the return path, legendary refuge of the Valaisan counterfeiter Joseph-Samuel Farinet (1845-1880). Headlamp recommended.
segments.mt: Mała naturalna jaskinia dostępna na ścieżce powrotnej, legendarne schronienie Valaisan fałszerza Joseph- Samuela Farineta (1845- 1880). Reflektor zalecany.

segments.src: Break at the Buvette de la Cascade de Bérard
segments.mt: Przerwa w Buvette de la Cascade de Bérard

segments.src: Mountain refreshment bar open in season (May to September, 10am-6pm weather dependent) serving drinks, croque-monsieur and homemade tarts facing the waterfall. Cash payment.
segments.mt: Mountain odświeżacz bar otwarty w sezonie (od maja do września, 10am- 6pm pogoda zależna) serwując napoje, croque- monsieur i domowe tarty z widokiem na wodospad. Gotówka.

segments.src: The footpath alongside the via ferrata continues to the Pierre à Bérard refuge (2 h from the buvette), in the heart of the Aiguilles Rouges nature reserve.
segments.mt: Ścieżka obok ferraty kontynuuje do schroniska Pierre à Bérard (2 h od buwety), w samym sercu rezerwatu przyrody Aiguilles Rouges.

segments.src: Emergency exit laid out after the wooden footbridge on the right bank, allowing return to the hiking trail without continuing the higher aerial sections.
segments.mt: Wyjście awaryjne położone po drewnianym moście na prawym brzegu, umożliwiające powrót na szlak turystyczny bez kontynuowania wyższych odcinków lotniczych.

src.q: Do I need a guide for the Bérard via ferrata?
src.a: Access is free, but a high-mountain guide is strongly advised if you are not self-sufficient, equipped and comfortable with exposure. Allow €80-120 per person equipment included from a Chamonix or Argentière guide office.

src.q: What fitness level is required for the Bérard via ferrata?
src.a: The via is graded AD+ (Fairly Difficult +): not very physically demanding, but the starting wall and aerial passages require managing exposure. Suitable for accompanied beginners with prior introduction to verticality.

src.q: What equipment should I bring for the Bérard via ferrata?
src.a: Mandatory: harness, double energy-absorbing lanyard with UIAA/CE certification, helmet, hiking or approach shoes. Recommended: ferrata gloves, water (1 L minimum), windproof jacket, headlamp for the Farinet Cave.

src.q: Is the Bérard via ferrata suitable for children?
src.a: Yes, from age 12, minimum 1.40 m and 40 kg recommended. Children under 40 kg must be roped with passage through safety anchors by an experienced adult. Always check the child's willingness regarding heights.

src.q: How long does the Bérard via ferrata take?
src.a: Allow 1 h to 1 h 30 for the via ferrata itself (depending on crowding), plus 15-20 min approach walk from Le Buet car park and 10-15 min return. Total: around 2 h to 2 h 30.

src.q: Can I get food at the Bérard via ferrata?
src.a: Yes, the Buvette de la Cascade de Bérard, at the end of the route, offers drinks, snacks, croque-monsieur and homemade tarts on a terrace facing the waterfall (open in season, generally 10am-6pm weather permitting, mainly cash payment).

src.q: Where can I rent via ferrata gear in Vallorcine?
src.a: In Vallorcine at Vallorcine Locaskis (by the station) or in Chamonix at Snell Sports, Zero G, Sanglard Sports and guide offices. Count €15-25 per day for the full kit (harness, lanyard, helmet).

src.q: Is the Bérard via ferrata busy?
src.a: Yes, due to its low grade and fun character, the via can be very busy in July-August (up to 200 passages/day). Choose an early morning (from 8am) or late afternoon start to avoid queuing at the initial wall.

src.q: What is the best season for the Bérard via ferrata?
src.a: June to October, peak in July-August. September offers the best conditions: cool but pleasant temperatures, reduced crowds, autumn colours. Avoid May (residual snow) and any wet weather (slippery rock).

src.q: Is there a car park for the Bérard via ferrata?
src.a: Yes, Le Buet station car park is the official parking: 2 h free, then €3 for 5 h or €5/day. Very limited in high season — prefer the Mont-Blanc Express train (Le Buet stop).

segments.src: What fitness level is required for the Bérard via ferrata?

segments.src: The via is graded AD+ (Fairly Difficult +): not very physically demanding, but the starting wall and aerial passages require managing exposure. Suitable for accompanied beginners with prior introduction to verticality.
segments.mt: System jest klasyfikowany jako AD + (Fairly Trudsult +): niezbyt wymagający fizycznie, ale ściany startowe i przejścia lotnicze wymagają zarządzania narażeniem. Nadaje się dla początkujących towarzyszy z wcześniejszym wprowadzeniem do wertykalności.

segments.src: What equipment should I bring for the Bérard via ferrata?
segments.mt: Jaki sprzęt powinienem przynieść dla Bérard przez ferratę?

segments.src: Mandatory: harness, double energy-absorbing lanyard with UIAA/CE certification, helmet, hiking or approach shoes. Recommended: ferrata gloves, water (1 L minimum), windproof jacket, headlamp for the Farinet Cave.
segments.mt: Obowiązkowe: uprząż, podwójna smycz pochłaniająca energię z certyfikacją UIAA / CE, kask, buty turystyczne lub podeszwy. Zalecane: rękawice ferrata, woda (minimum 1 L), żakiet przeciwwietrzny, reflektor do jaskini Farinet.

segments.src: Is the Bérard via ferrata suitable for children?
segments.mt: Czy Bérard przez ferrata nadaje się dla dzieci?

segments.src: Yes, from age 12, minimum 1.40 m and 40 kg recommended. Children under 40 kg must be roped with passage through safety anchors by an experienced adult. Always check the child's willingness regarding heights.
segments.mt: Tak, od 12 roku życia zaleca się minimum 1,40 m i 40 kg. Dzieci o masie ciała poniżej 40 kg muszą przejść przez kotwice bezpieczeństwa przez doświadczonego dorosłego. Zawsze sprawdzaj gotowość dziecka do wzrostu.

segments.src: How long does the Bérard via ferrata take?
segments.mt: Ile czasu zajmuje Bérard przez ferratę?

segments.src: Allow 1 h to 1 h 30 for the via ferrata itself (depending on crowding), plus 15-20 min approach walk from Le Buet car park and 10-15 min return. Total: around 2 h to 2 h 30.
segments.mt: Niech 1 h do 1 h 30 dla przez ferrata sam (w zależności od tłoczenia), plus 15- 20 min spacerem od parkingu Le Buet i 10- 15 min powrót. Razem: około 2 h do 2 h 30.

segments.src: Can I get food at the Bérard via ferrata?
segments.mt: Mogę dostać jedzenie w Bérard przez ferratę?

segments.src: Yes, the Buvette de la Cascade de Bérard, at the end of the route, offers drinks, snacks, croque-monsieur and homemade tarts on a terrace facing the waterfall (open in season, generally 10am-6pm weather permitting, mainly cash payment).
segments.mt: Tak, Buvette de la Cascade de Bérard, na końcu trasy, oferuje napoje, przekąski, croque- monsieur i domowe tarty na tarasie naprzeciwko wodospadu (otwarte w sezonie, zazwyczaj 10am- 6pm pogoda pozwala, głównie płatności pieniężne).

segments.src: Where can I rent via ferrata gear in Vallorcine?
segments.mt: Gdzie mogę wynająć za pomocą narzędzi ferrata w Vallorcine?

segments.src: In Vallorcine at Vallorcine Locaskis (by the station) or in Chamonix at Snell Sports, Zero G, Sanglard Sports and guide offices. Count €15-25 per day for the full kit (harness, lanyard, helmet).
segments.mt: W Vallorcine w Vallorcine Locaskis (przez stację) lub w Chamonix w Snell Sports, Zero G, Sanglard Sports i biura przewodników. Licz 15- 25 €dziennie za pełny zestaw (uprząż, smycz, hełm).

segments.src: Is the Bérard via ferrata busy?

Yes, due to its low grade and fun character, the via can be very busy in July-August (up to 200 passages/day). Choose an early morning (from 8am) or late afternoon start to avoid queuing at the initial wall.
segments.mt: Tak, ze względu na jego niskiej klasy i zabawny charakter, via może być bardzo zajęty w July- Sierpień (do 200 przejść / dzień). Wybierz wczesny poranek (od 8 rano) lub późnym popołudniem zacząć unikać kolejki na pierwszej ścianie.

segments.src: What is the best season for the Bérard via ferrata?

segments.src: June to October, peak in July-August. September offers the best conditions: cool but pleasant temperatures, reduced crowds, autumn colours. Avoid May (residual snow) and any wet weather (slippery rock).
segments.mt: Od czerwca do października, szczyt w lipcu - sierpień. Wrzesień oferuje najlepsze warunki: chłodne, ale przyjemne temperatury, zmniejszone tłumy, jesienne kolory. Unikać maja (szczątkowy śnieg) i mokrej pogody (śliska skała).

segments.mt: Czy istnieje parking dla Bérard przez ferratę?

segments.src: Yes, Le Buet station car park is the official parking: 2 h free, then €3 for 5 h or €5/day. Very limited in high season — prefer the Mont-Blanc Express train (Le Buet stop).
segments.mt: Tak, Le Buet stacja parking jest oficjalny parking: 2 godziny za darmo, a następnie €3 za 5 godzin lub €5 / dzień. Bardzo ograniczony w sezonie - preferuje pociąg Mont-Blanc Express (Le Buet stop).

src.title: 500 m AD+ via ferrata route
src.description: 500 m of cable, 180 m elevation gain on limestone rock in the Fiz massif. Fun yet sporty route, continuous lifeline with anchors.

src.title: Nepalese bridge
src.description: Emblematic structure of the route: three-cable suspension bridge (one walking, two handrails) requiring balance and exposure management. Highly photogenic facing Mont-Blanc.

src.title: Horizontal beam
src.description: Crossing of a metal beam in balance, secured by cable. Short but exposed passage that adds spice to the route.

src.title: Aerial walkway
src.description: Metal walkway overhanging the wall, crossed clipped to the lifeline. Plunging view over the Arve valley and Sallanches.

src.title: Mont-Blanc panorama
src.description: Throughout the route, open view facing the Mont-Blanc massif: Aiguille du Midi, Dôme du Goûter, Aiguilles de Chamonix, Aiguille Verte on clear days.

src.title: Guided supervision
src.description: Several Chamonix, Saint-Gervais and Passy guide offices offer supervision with equipment provided. Indicative rate €70-100/person for a half-day.

src.title: Forested approach walk
src.description: Steep 20-min approach through forest from the Chemin de Curalla car park. Marked, shaded trail, ideal for warming up before the via.

src.title: Loop with return path
src.description: Return to car park via marked footpath in ~40 min, making the itinerary a complete loop without need for shuttle.

segments.src: 500 m of cable, 180 m elevation gain on limestone rock in the Fiz massif. Fun yet sporty route, continuous lifeline with anchors.
segments.mt: 500 m kabla, 180 m wysokości na skale wapiennej w masywie Fiz. Zabawa jeszcze sportowa trasa, ciągła linia życia z kotwicami.

segments.src: Emblematic structure of the route: three-cable suspension bridge (one walking, two handrails) requiring balance and exposure management. Highly photogenic facing Mont-Blanc.
segments.mt: Konstrukcja emblematyczna trasy: most zawieszony na trzy kable (jeden spacer, dwie poręcze) wymagający zarządzania równowagą i narażeniem. Wysoce fotogeniczne w obliczu Mont-Blanc.

segments.src: Crossing of a metal beam in balance, secured by cable. Short but exposed passage that adds spice to the route.
segments.mt: Przekraczanie metalowej belki w równowadze, zabezpieczone kablem. Krótkie, ale odsłonięte przejście, które dodaje przyprawy do trasy.

segments.src: Metal walkway overhanging the wall, crossed clipped to the lifeline. Plunging view over the Arve valley and Sallanches.
segments.mt: Metal walkway overwinging the wall, crossed crupped to the lifeline. Widok na dolinę Arve i Sallanches.

segments.src: Throughout the route, open view facing the Mont-Blanc massif: Aiguille du Midi, Dôme du Goûter, Aiguilles de Chamonix, Aiguille Verte on clear days.
segments.mt: Na całej trasie, otwarty widok na masyw Mont-Blanc: Aiguille du Midi, Dôme du Goûter, Aiguilles de Chamonix, Aiguille Verte w dni jasne.

segments.src: Several Chamonix, Saint-Gervais and Passy guide offices offer supervision with equipment provided. Indicative rate €70-100/person for a half-day.
segments.mt: Kilka biur Chamonix, Saint- Gervais i Passy oferuje nadzór z wyposażeniem. Orientacyjna stawka 70-100 EUR / osobę za pół dnia.

segments.src: Steep 20-min approach through forest from the Chemin de Curalla car park. Marked, shaded trail, ideal for warming up before the via.
segments.mt: Steep 20- min podejście przez las z Chemin de Curalla parking. Naznaczony, zacieniony szlak, idealny do rozgrzewania się przed wejściem.

segments.src: Return to car park via marked footpath in ~40 min, making the itinerary a complete loop without need for shuttle.
segments.mt: Powrót do parkingu przez zaznaczoną ścieżkę w ~ 40 min, czyniąc trasę kompletną pętlą bez konieczności promu.

src.q: Do I need a guide for this via ferrata?
src.a: Access is free, but a guide is strongly recommended if you are a beginner or inexperienced. The Bureau des Guides du Grand-Bornand offers group outings (€122/pers) with equipment provided.

src.q: What level is required?
src.a: Graded AD to D+. The difficulty lies mainly in the length (1100 m) and commitment. Good physical fitness and stamina are required. Not recommended for complete beginners.

src.q: How long does it take?
src.a: Allow 5 to 6 hours in total: 30 min approach, 3 to 4 h via ferrata, and 1 h descent. A full day is necessary.

src.q: What equipment should I bring?
src.a: Essential: harness, double lanyard with energy absorber, helmet, mountain boots. Recommended: backpack with 1.5 L water, picnic, warm and windproof clothing, sunglasses, sun cream.

src.q: Can I turn back?
src.a: An escape route, the 'Sortie à Fred', allows you to exit after the first section (500 m, grade D). Note: this section also has cabled passages, so keep your equipment on. After the footbridge, you are fully committed.

src.q: Is it busy in summer?
src.a: Yes, this via ferrata is very popular in July–August. The car park can be full: arrive early in the morning or consider parking at the Col de la Colombière car park (10 min extra walk).

src.q: Is the via ferrata suitable for children?
src.a: Minimum age 13 years and minimum height 1.40 m recommended. Possible with younger children on private outings with a guide, provided they are fit and comfortable with heights.

src.q: When is the via ferrata open?
src.a: Theoretically from late May to late October, but check snow conditions at the start and end of season with the Tourist Office (+33 4 50 02 78 00). The route is freely accessible 24/7.

segments.src: Do I need a guide for this via ferrata?
Access is free, but a guide is strongly recommended if you are a beginner or inexperienced. The Bureau des Guides du Grand-Bornand offers group outings (€122/pers) with equipment provided.
segments.mt: Dostęp jest bezpłatny, ale przewodnik jest zdecydowanie zalecane, jeśli jesteś początkujący lub niedoświadczony. Biuro Przewodników du Grand- Bornand oferuje grupowe wyjazdy (122 EUR / osoby) z wyposażeniem.

segments.mt: Niezbędne: uprząż, podwójna smycz z pochłaniaczem energii, kask, buty górskie. Zalecane: plecak z 1,5 L wody, piknik, ciepłe i odporne na wiatr ubrania, okulary przeciwsłoneczne, krem do opalania.

segments.src: An escape route, the 'Sortie à Fred', allows you to exit after the first section (500 m, grade D). Note: this section also has cabled passages, so keep your equipment on. After the footbridge, you are fully committed.
segments.mt: Trasa ucieczki, 'Sortie à Fred', pozwala na wyjście po pierwszej sekcji (500 m, stopień D). Uwaga: w tej sekcji znajdują się także kanały kablowe, więc nie wyłączaj sprzętu. Po moście jesteś w pełni zaangażowany.

fields.src: Via ferrata Yves Pollet-Villard — Via ferrata La Clusaz (74) | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Via ferrata Yves Pollet-Villard — Via ferrata La Clusaz (74) | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Via ferrata Yves Pollet-Villard - Via ferrata La Clusaz (74)

src.q: Do I need a guide for the Pollet-Villard via ferrata?
src.a: Access is free, but a certified mountain guide is strongly recommended for beginners, families with children or anyone not fully self-sufficient. Bureau des Guides de La Clusaz: +33 4 50 63 35 99.

src.q: What is the difficulty level?
src.a: Graded D (difficult), with an optional D+ finish. The route is long (700 m, 2.5–3 h), physical and exposed. Good fitness and no vertigo required.

src.q: What gear is necessary?
src.a: Harness, energy-absorbing double lanyard, helmet and approach or hiking shoes with good grip. Water, snacks and warm clothes in a small backpack advised. Rental available from guides.

src.q: Can you do the via ferrata with children?
src.a: Possible from age 13 with supervision, but D rating not suited to beginners. Many reports advise against the route for children <12 or inexperienced climbers without a guide. Choose an easier via ferrata for a first experience.

src.q: What is the best departure time?
src.a: Early morning (7:30–8:00) recommended in summer to avoid intense heat (south-facing). In June, September or October, morning or early afternoon starts possible.

src.q: Are there escape routes?
src.a: Yes, several intermediate exits allow you to leave the route mid-way if needed (fatigue, weather, vertigo).

src.q: Where can I park?
src.a: Signposted via ferrata car park on Col des Aravis road (D909), 4 km past La Clusaz centre. 2nd car park on left going up. Free.

src.q: How long does the full route take?
src.a: 3–4 h total: 20 min approach + 2.5–3 h via ferrata + 40–60 min descent on trail.

segments.src: Do I need a guide for the Pollet-Villard via ferrata?

fields.src: Saix de Miolène via ferrata — La Chapelle-d'Abondance | Loisirs 74

segments.src: Saix de Miolène via ferrata — La Chapelle-d'Abondance | Loisirs 74
segments.mt: Saix de Miolène via ferrata - La Chapelle-d'Abondance X124; Loisirs 74

src.q: Do I need a guide?
src.a: Access is free, but a mountain guide is strongly recommended if you are not self-sufficient and experienced. The Bureau des Guides de la Vallée d'Abondance offers supervised outings (€50/person beginners, €60/person advanced).

src.q: What level is required?
src.a: The 1st section (Le Cabri, AD+) is accessible to accompanied beginner ferratists. Sections 2 and 3 (TD) demand arm strength, stamina and good exposure management. The full link represents a sporty 3–5-hour challenge.

src.q: Can I rent gear on site?
src.a: Yes. Harness, via ferrata lanyard and helmet rental at Bouvet Sport (Abondance), Trincaz Sports (La Chapelle-d'Abondance) or Arbr'acadabra adventure park. Booking advised in high season.

src.q: What's the best time?
src.a: May to October, in dry weather. Note: sections 2 and 3 may be closed 15 March to 10 July to protect peregrine falcon nesting. Check signs at the parking.

src.q: Can I do the via ferrata with children?
src.a: Minimum height required: 1.40 m. The 1st section (Le Cabri, 1 h) suits sporty children accompanied by a guide. Sections 2 and 3 are reserved for trained teens and adults.

src.q: Are there escape routes?
src.a: Yes, three escape routes allow you to exit after each section: after Le Cabri, after Le Chamois (Le Vionnet exit) and after Le Bouquetin. You can thus adapt the route to your fitness.

src.q: Where to park?
src.a: Free parking by the D22, at the entrance to La Chapelle-d'Abondance (via ferrata sign, at the foot of the quarry). Do not park on private property on the right side of the road.

src.q: What to bring?
src.a: Via ferrata equipment (harness, lanyard, helmet), hiking shoes with grippy sole, water (1.5 L minimum), energy snacks, sunscreen and cap. If in doubt, consult a guide before leaving.

segments.src: Access is free, but a mountain guide is strongly recommended if you are not self-sufficient and experienced. The Bureau des Guides de la Vallée d'Abondance offers supervised outings (€50/person beginners, €60/person advanced).
segments.mt: Dostęp jest bezpłatny, ale przewodnik górski jest zdecydowanie zalecane, jeśli nie są samowystarczalne i doświadczenie. Biuro Przewodników de la Vallée d 'Abondance oferuje pobyt pod nadzorem (50 EUR / osoby początkujące, 60 EUR / osoba zaawansowana).

src.what_is:
The Thônes via ferrata, also called Roche à l'Agathe, is a major equipped route that directly overlooks the center of Thônes. With its 600 meters of cable and a 235-meter elevation gain, this alpine route offers a vertical and athletic experience that attracts experienced climbers seeking thrills.

The route begins with an airy monkey bridge that immediately sets the tone, followed by steep and technical sections alternating metal rungs and natural holds on limestone rock. The highlight of the route is the Hermit's Overhang, a passage rated ED (extremely difficult) that severely tests arms and mental strength — an inverted ladder allows bypassing this difficulty. Two escape routes mark the route to allow exiting before the greatest difficulties.

Located at low altitude (start at 660 m, arrival at 895 m), the Thônes via ferrata differs from the alpine routes of La Clusaz or Grand-Bornand by its year-round accessibility (except when snow-covered) and its proximity to the village — the whole town can watch the climbers! The south-west exposure guarantees generous sunshine from 10 a.m. in summer, but can make the rock scorching in the afternoon. Access is free, but specialized equipment (harness, double energy-absorbing lanyard, helmet) is essential, as well as good physical condition and no fear of heights. For the less experienced, guidance by a mountain guide is highly recommended.

src.q: Do I need a guide?
src.a: Access is free, but a guide is highly recommended if you are not autonomous, equipped and experienced. Several guide offices offer supervised outings with equipment provided.

src.q: What is the difficulty level?
src.a: Route rated D (difficult) to ED (extremely difficult) depending on the exit chosen. The 1st part is D, the 2nd D+ and the final overhang ED (avoidable via ladder). Good physical condition and arm endurance essential.

src.q: How much time should I plan?
src.a: Allow 2–3 hours total: 10 min approach walk, 1h30 to 2h30 for the route depending on level, and 30 to 45 min for descent via trail.

src.q: Can equipment be rented on site?
src.a: Yes, Versant Nord shop (48 av. d'Annecy, Thônes, tel. 04 50 32 10 02) offers complete via ferrata equipment rental: harness, lanyards with absorber, helmet.

src.q: Is the via ferrata suitable for children?
src.a: Not recommended for children or beginners. The route is vertical, athletic and very exposed. Minimum recommended age: 13 years, with good physical condition and guide supervision.

src.q: Is it crowded?
src.a: The Thônes via ferrata is popular, especially on weekends and in high season. For more peace, start early in the morning on weekdays.

src.q: What to do in bad weather?
src.a: Never engage in storms, rain or wet rock (risk of slipping). Check weather before leaving. If in doubt, contact Thônes Tourist Office at 04 50 02 00 26.

src.q: Where to eat after the via ferrata?
src.a: Several restaurants in central Thônes: Hôtel de l'Hermitage (Savoyard specialties), Philtre (local and organic cuisine), brasseries and pizzerias. Thônes is also the capital of farmhouse Reblochon cheese!

src.what_is:
The Villa du Parc is one of the few contemporary art centres in Haute-Savoie to hold the 'Centre d'art contemporain d'intérêt national' label, awarded by the French Minister of Culture in 2020 (source CNAP - Centre national des arts plastiques). Set inside an 1865 villa in the heart of the Parc Montessuit, at 12 rue de Genève in Annemasse, it is a major cultural institution of the Greater Geneva area on the French side.

The programme features three to four temporary contemporary-art exhibitions a year, alongside artists' residencies, outreach activities for young audiences, talks and meet-the-artist events. The Villa du Parc defends an ambitious yet accessible contemporary art, with strong outreach work in local schools and a dedicated focus on emerging artists.

Free entry for all visitors. Season: year-round, closed between exhibitions. Hours: Wednesday to Sunday, 2 p.m. to 6 p.m. The Parc Montessuit setting makes the visit a pleasant outing, easily combined with a walk in the park. Accessible for visitors with reduced mobility (ground floor and exhibition rooms).

src.what_is:
The Chablais greenway, also called the Abondance Valley greenway, follows the former railway linking Évian to the upper Abondance Valley, closed in the 1980s. Its developed section descends from upper Abondance toward Lake Geneva over roughly 22 km, in several segments.

The very gentle profile — inherited from the railway — makes it one of the most family-friendly walks in the Chablais. The surface is partly asphalt, partly stabilised gravel. The greenway crosses villages, low pastures, wooden bridges and old tunnels, follows the Dranse d'Abondance and lets you discover the valley's heritage: Abondance and its abbey, La Chapelle-d'Abondance, Vacheresse, Bonnevaux.

Ideal for cycling (road, hybrid, e-bike rentals available locally), it also suits walking, roller-skating on the asphalt sections and prams. Picnic areas, fountains and benches dot the route. Free access; dogs on a leash welcome.

src.what_is:
The Cercle de la Voile de Thonon (CVT) is the historic French sailing club of Lake Geneva, based at the Port de Rives in Thonon-les-Bains. A member of the French Sailing Federation (FFV), it hosts a sailing school, organises club regattas on the lake and runs courses and training programmes for both adults and children.

The CVT covers all main disciplines: Optimist for children (from age 7), dinghies for teens and adults (Laser, 420), sport catamarans (Hobie Cat) and racing. Five-day courses run throughout the summer season, with private lessons and equipment hire also available. All instructors are FFV-qualified.

2026 prices: adult courses EUR 200-400 depending on discipline, child courses available. Season: April to October. Hours: lessons and rentals daily 9 a.m. to 6 p.m. in season; out of season by appointment. Partially accessible for visitors with reduced mobility (adapted sailing on request).

src.what_is:
The Ponton de l'Embarcadère Wakepark is the main boat-towed wakeboard spot on Lake Annecy, set on the western shore at Saint-Jorioz. The central location, with easy access from the bay of Saint-Jorioz out to open water, makes it the reference site for towed water sports on the lake, with a fleet of boats dedicated to water-skiing and wakeboarding.

The school covers every boat-towed discipline: classic water-skiing, wakeboarding, wakesurfing (recent addition), wakeskate and towed buoy for families. The instructors are FFSN-qualified. The early morning flat water is particularly well suited to technical wakeboarding, while afternoon boat waves and a more relaxed pace work well for family outings.

2026 prices: adult EUR 30-50 per session depending on the discipline, children's sessions available. Season: May to September. Hours: daily 9 a.m. to 7 p.m. in summer. Partially accessible for visitors with reduced mobility (pontoon access).

src.what_is:
The TNA Cable Park, at Arenthon, is the only true cable wakeboard park in Haute-Savoie. Riders are towed by a fixed overhead cable rather than by a boat, with two major advantages: the water is always flat, and sessions are significantly longer. The site is set on a lake at Arenthon and is equipped with modules for riders of every level.

Compared with the boat-towed spot at the Ponton de l'Embarcadère in Saint-Jorioz, the cable park offers a more affordable and more accessible alternative. A one-hour cable session typically allows several jumps and tricks, where a 15-minute boat tow only fits one or two runs. The cable park is therefore the go-to spot for progress-oriented wakeboarders looking for repetition.

2026 prices: adult EUR 25-40 per session, children's sessions available. Season: May to September. Hours: daily 10 a.m. to 7 p.m. in summer. Partially accessible for visitors with reduced mobility (poolside and bar areas).
